Genesis1:1 In the beginning God created heaven and earth.
Genesis1:2 The earth was void and empty
Genesis1:3 Then God said: let there be light and there was light.
Genesis1:4 And God saw the light that it was good: and divided the light from the darkness
Genesis1:5 and called the light day
Genesis1:6 And God said: let there be a firmament between the waters
Genesis1:7 Then God made the firmament and parted the waters which were under the firmament
Genesis1:8 And God called the firmament heaven. And so of the evening and morning was made the second day.
Genesis1:9 And God said
Genesis1:10 And God called the dry land the earth and the gathering together of waters called he the sea. And God saw that it was good.
Genesis1:11 And God said: let the earth bring forth herb and grass that sow seed
Genesis1:12 And the earth brought forth herb and grass sowing seed every one in his kind and trees bearing fruit and having their seed in them selves
Genesis1:13 and then of the evening and morning was made the third day.
Genesis1:14 Then said God: let there be lights in the firmament of heaven to divide the day from the night
Genesis1:15 And let them be lights in the firmament of heaven
Genesis1:16 And God made two great lights: A greater light to rule the day
Genesis1:17 And God put them in the firmament of heaven to shine upon the earth
Genesis1:18 and to rule the day and the night
Genesis1:19 and so of the evening and morning was made the fourth day.
Genesis1:20 And God said
Genesis1:21 And God created great whales and all manner of creatures that live and move
Genesis1:22 and God blessed them saying: Grow and multiply and fill the waters of the seas
Genesis1:23 And so of the evening and morning was made the fifth day.
Genesis1:24 And God said: let the earth bring forth living creatures in their kinds: cattle and worms and beasts of the earth in their kinds
Genesis1:25 And God made the beasts of the earth in their kinds
Genesis1:26 And God said: let us make man in our similitude and after our likeness: that he may have rule over the fish of the sea
Genesis1:27 And God created man after his likeness
Genesis1:28 And God blessed them
Genesis1:29 And God said: see
Genesis1:30 and for all beasts of the earth
Genesis1:31 And God beheld all that he had made
Genesis2:1 Thus was heaven and earth finished with all their apparel:
Genesis2:2 and in the seventh day God ended his work which he had made and rested in the seventh day from all his works which he had made.
Genesis2:3 And God blessed the seventh day
Genesis2:4 These are the generations of heaven and earth when they were created
Genesis2:5 and all the shrubs of the field before they were in the earth. And all the herbs of the field before they sprang: for the LORD God had yet sent no rain upon the earth
Genesis2:6 But there arose a mist out of the ground and watered all the face of the earth:
Genesis2:7 Then the LORD God shope man
Genesis2:8 The LORD God also planted a garden in Eden from the beginning
Genesis2:9 And the LORD God made to spring out of the earth
Genesis2:10 And there sprong a river out of Eden to water the garden
Genesis2:11 The name of the one is Phison
Genesis2:12 And the gold of that country is precious
Genesis2:13 The name of the second river is Gihon
Genesis2:14 And the name of the third river is Hidekell
Genesis2:15 And the LORD God took Adam and put him in the garden of Eden
Genesis2:16 And the LORD God commanded Adam saying: Of all the trees of the garden see thou eat:
Genesis2:17 But of the tree of knowledge of good and bad see that thou eat not: For even the same day thou eatest of it
Genesis2:18 And the LORD God said: It is not good that man should be alone
Genesis2:19 And after that the LORD God had made of the earth all manner beasts of the field
Genesis2:20 And Adam gave names unto all manner cattle
Genesis2:21 Then the LORD God cast a slumber on Adam
Genesis2:22 And the LORD God made of the rib which he took out of Adam
Genesis2:23 Then said Adam: This is once bone of my bones
Genesis2:24 For this cause shall a man leave father and mother and cleave unto his wife
Genesis2:25 And they were either of them naked
Genesis3:1 But the serpent was subtler than all the beasts of the field which the LORD God had made
Genesis3:2 And the woman said unto the serpent
Genesis3:3 but of the fruit of the tree that(yt) is in the midst(myddes) of the garden (said God) see that ye eat not
Genesis3:4 Then said the serpent unto the woman: tush ye shall not die:
Genesis3:5 but God doth know
Genesis3:6 And the woman saw that it was a good tree to eat of and lusty unto the eyes and a pleasant tree for to make wise. And took of the fruit of it and ate
Genesis3:7 And the eyes of both them were opened
Genesis3:8 And they heard the voice of the LORD God as he walked in the garden in the cool of the day. And Adam hid himself and his wife also from the face of the LORD God
Genesis3:9 And the LORD God called Adam and said unto him where art thou?
Genesis3:10 And he answered: Thy voice I heard in the garden
Genesis3:11 And he said: Who told thee that thou wast naked? hast thou eaten of the tree
Genesis3:12 And Adam answered: The woman which thou gavest to bear me company
Genesis3:13 And the LORD God said unto the woman: Wherefore didst thou so? And the woman answered: The serpent deceived me and I ate.
Genesis3:14 And the LORD God said unto the serpent because thou hast so done most cursed be thou of all cattle and of all beasts of the field: upon thy belly shalt thou go: and earth shalt thou eat all days of thy life.
Genesis3:15 Moreover I will put hatred between thee and the woman
Genesis3:16 And unto the woman he said: I will surely increase thy sorrow and make thee oft with child
Genesis3:17 And unto Adam he said: Forasmuch as thou hast obeyed the voice of thy wife
Genesis3:18 And it shall bear thorns and thistles unto thee. And thou shalt eat the herbs of the field:
Genesis3:19 In the sweat of thy face shalt thou eat bread
Genesis3:20 And Adam called his wife Heva
Genesis3:21 And the LORD God made Adam and his wife garments of skins
Genesis3:22 And the LORD God said: Lo
Genesis3:23 And the LORD God cast him out of the garden of Eden
Genesis3:24 And he cast Adam out
Genesis4:1 And Adam lay with Heva is wife
Genesis4:2 And she proceeded forth and bare his brother Abel: And Abel became a shepherd
Genesis4:3 And it fortuned in process of time
Genesis4:4 And Abel
Genesis4:5 but unto Cain and unto his offering
Genesis4:6 And the LORD said unto Cain: why art thou angry
Genesis4:7 But and if thou dost evil
Genesis4:8 And Cain talked with Abel his brother. And as soon as they were in the fields
Genesis4:9 And the LORD said unto Cain: where is Abel thy brother? And he said: I can not tell
Genesis4:10 And he said: What hast thou done? The voice of thy brother's blood cryeth unto me out of the earth.
Genesis4:11 And now cursed be thou as pertaining to the earth
Genesis4:12 For when thou tillest the ground she shall henceforth not give her power unto thee. A vagabond and a renegade shalt thou be upon the earth.
Genesis4:13 And Cain said unto the LORD: my sin is greater
Genesis4:14 Behold thou castest me out this day from off the face of the earth
Genesis4:15 And the LORD said unto him: Not so
Genesis4:16 And Cain went out from the face of the LORD
Genesis4:17 And Cain lay with his wife
Genesis4:18 And Henoch begat Irad. And Irad begat Mahuiael. And Mahuiael begat Mathusael. And Mathusael begat Lamech.
Genesis4:19 And Lamech took him two wives: the one was called Ada
Genesis4:20 And Ada bare Jabal
Genesis4:21 And his brother's name was Jubal: of him came all that exercise them selves on the harp and on the organs.
Genesis4:22 And Zilla she also bare Tubalcain a worker in metal and a father of all that grave in brass and iron. And Tubalcain's sister was called Naema.
Genesis4:23 Then said Lamech unto his wives Ada and Zilla: hear my voice ye wives of Lamech and hearken unto my words
Genesis4:24 For Cain shall be avenged sevenfold: but Lamech seventy times sevenfold.
Genesis4:25 Adam also lay with his wife yet again
Genesis4:26 And Seth begat a son and called his name Enos. And in that time began men to call on the name of the LORD.
Genesis5:1 This is the book of the generation of man
Genesis5:2 Male and female made he them
Genesis5:3 And when Adam was an hundred and thirty year old
Genesis5:4 And the days of Adam after he begat Seth
Genesis5:5 And all the days of Adam which he lived
Genesis5:6 And Seth lived an hundred and five years
Genesis5:7 And after he had begot Enos he lived eight hundred and seven year
Genesis5:8 And all the days of Seth were nine hundred and twelve year
Genesis5:9 And Enos lived ninety year
Genesis5:10 And Enos after he begat Kenan
Genesis5:11 and all the days of Enos were nine hundred and five year
Genesis5:12 And Kenan lived seventy year
Genesis5:13 And Kenan after he had begot Mahalaliel
Genesis5:14 and all the days of Kenan were nine hundred and ten year
Genesis5:15 And Mahalaliel lived sixty five year
Genesis5:16 And Mahalaliel after he had begot Jared lived eight hundred and thirty year
Genesis5:17 and all the days of Mahalaliel were eight hundred ninety and five year
Genesis5:18 And Jared lived an hundred and sixty two year
Genesis5:19 and Jared lived after he begat Henoch
Genesis5:20 And all the days of Jared were nine hundred and sixty two year
Genesis5:21 And Henoch lived sixty five year
Genesis5:22 And Henoch walked with God after he had begot Mathusalah
Genesis5:23 And all the days of Henoch were three hundred and sixty five year
Genesis5:24 and then Henoch lived a godly life
Genesis5:25 And Mathusala lived an hundred and eighty seven year and begat Lamech:
Genesis5:26 and Mathusala after he had begot Lamech
Genesis5:27 And all the days of Mathusala were nine hundred sixty nine year
Genesis5:28 And Lamech lived an hundred eighty two year and begat a son
Genesis5:29 and called him Noe saying: This same shall comfort us: as concerning our work and sorrow of our hands which we have about the earth that the LORD hath cursed.
Genesis5:30 And Lamech lived after he had begot Noe
Genesis5:31 And all the days of Lamech were seven hundred seventy seven year
Genesis5:32 And when Noe was five hundred year old
Genesis6:1 And it came to pass
Genesis6:2 the sons of God saw the daughters of men that they were fair
Genesis6:3 And the LORD(LORd) said: My spirit shall not alway strive with man
Genesis6:4 There were tyrants in the world in those days. For after that the children of God had gone in unto the daughters of men and had begotten them children
Genesis6:5 And when the LORD saw that the wickedness of man was increased upon the earth
Genesis6:6 he repented that he had made man upon the earth and sorrowed in his heart.
Genesis6:7 And said: I will destroy mankind which I have made
Genesis6:8 But yet Noe found grace in the sight of the LORD.
Genesis6:9 These are the generations of Noe. Noe was a righteous man and uncorrupt in his time
Genesis6:10 And Noe begat three sons: Sem
Genesis6:11 And the earth was corrupt in the sight of God
Genesis6:12 And God looked upon the earth
Genesis6:13 Then said God to Noe: the end of all flesh is come before me
Genesis6:14 Make thee an ark of pine tree
Genesis6:15 And of this fashion shalt thou make it. The length of the ark shall be three hundred cubits
Genesis6:16 A window shalt thou make above in the ark. And within a cubit compass shalt thou finish it. And the door of the ark shalt thou set in the side of it: and thou shalt make it with three lofts one above an other.
Genesis6:17 For behold I will bring in a flood of water upon the earth to destroy all flesh from under heaven
Genesis6:18 But I will make mine appointment with thee
Genesis6:19 And of all that liveth whatsoever flesh it be
Genesis6:20 And male and female see that they be
Genesis6:21 And take unto thee of all manner of meat that may be eaten and lay it up in store by thee
Genesis6:22 and Noe did according to all that God commanded him.
Genesis7:1 And the LORD said unto Noe: Go into the ark both thou and all thy household. For thee have I seen righteous before me in this generation.
Genesis7:2 Of all clean beasts take unto thee seven of every kind the male and his female
Genesis7:3 likewise of the birds of the air seven of every kind
Genesis7:4 For seven days hence will I send rain upon the earth forty days and forty nights and will destroy all manner of things that I have made
Genesis7:5 And Noe did according to all that the LORD(lorde) commanded him:
Genesis7:6 and Noe was six hundred year old
Genesis7:7 and Noe went and his sons and his wife and his sons' wives with him
Genesis7:8 And of clean beasts and of beasts that were unclean and of birds and of all that creepeth upon the earth
Genesis7:9 came in by couples of every kind unto Noe into the ark: a male and a female: even as God commanded Noe.
Genesis7:10 And the seventh day the waters of the flood came upon the earth.
Genesis7:11 In the six hundredth year of Noe's life
Genesis7:12 and there fell a rain upon the earth forty days and forty nights.
Genesis7:13 And the self same day went Noe
Genesis7:14 both they and all manner of beasts in their kind
Genesis7:15 And they came unto Noe into the ark by couples
Genesis7:16 And they that came
Genesis7:17 And the flood came forty days and forty nights upon the earth
Genesis7:18 And the water prevailed and increased exceedingly upon the earth: and the ark went upon he top of the waters.
Genesis7:19 And the waters prevailed exceedingly above measure upon the earth
Genesis7:20 even fifteen cubits high prevailed the waters
Genesis7:21 And all flesh that moved on the earth
Genesis7:22 so that all that had the breath of life in the nostrils of it thorow out all that was on dry land died.
Genesis7:23 Thus was destroyed all that was upon the earth
Genesis7:24 And the waters prevailed upon the earth
Genesis8:1 And God remembered Noe and all the beasts and all the cattle that were with him in the ark; And God made a wind to blow upon the earth
Genesis8:2 and the fountains of the deep and the windows of heaven were stopped and the rain of heaven was forbidden
Genesis8:3 and the waters returned from off the earth and abated after the end of an hundred and fifty days.
Genesis8:4 And the ark rested upon the mountains of Ararat
Genesis8:5 And the waters went away and decreased until the tenth month. And the first day of the tenth month
Genesis8:6 And after the end of forty days
Genesis8:7 and sent forth a raven
Genesis8:8 Then sent he forth a dove from him
Genesis8:9 And when the dove could find no resting place for her foot
Genesis8:10 And he abode yet seven days more
Genesis8:11 and the dove came to him again about eventide
Genesis8:12 And he tarried yet seven other days
Genesis8:13 And it came to pass
Genesis8:14 So by the twenty seventh day of the second month the earth was dry.
Genesis8:15 And God spake unto Noe saying:
Genesis8:16 Come out of the ark
Genesis8:17 And all the beasts that are with thee whatsoever flesh it be
Genesis8:18 And Noe came out
Genesis8:19 And all the beasts
Genesis8:20 And Noe made an altar unto the LORD
Genesis8:21 And the LORD(LORDE) smelled a sweet savour and said in his heart: I will henceforth no more curse the earth for man's sake
Genesis8:22 Neither shall sowing time and harvest
Genesis9:1 And God blessed Noe and his sons
Genesis9:2 The fear also and dread of yow be upon all beasts of the earth
Genesis9:3 And all that moveth upon the earth having life
Genesis9:4 Only the flesh with his life which is his blood
Genesis9:5 For verily the blood of yow wherein your lives are will I require: Even of the hand of all beasts will I require it
Genesis9:6 so that he which sheddeth man's blood
Genesis9:7 See that ye increase
Genesis9:8 Furthermore God spake unto Noe and to his sons with him saying:
Genesis9:9 See
Genesis9:10 and with all living things that is with you: both fowl and cattle
Genesis9:11 I make my bond with yow
Genesis9:12 And God said: This is the token of my bond which I make between me and yow
Genesis9:13 I will set my bow in the clouds
Genesis9:14 so that when I bring in clouds upon the earth
Genesis9:15 And then will I think upon my testament which I have made between me and yow
Genesis9:16 The bow shall be in the clouds
Genesis9:17 And God said unto Noe: This is the sign of the testament which I have made between me and all flesh that is on the earth.
Genesis9:18 The sons of Noe that came out of the ark were: Sem
Genesis9:19 These are the three sons of Noe
Genesis9:20 And Noe being an husbandman
Genesis9:21 and drank of the wine and was drunk
Genesis9:22 And Ham the father of Canaan saw his father's privates
Genesis9:23 And Sem and Japheth took a mantle
Genesis9:24 As soon as Noe was awaked from his wine and wist what his youngest son had done unto him
Genesis9:25 he said: Cursed be Canaan
Genesis9:26 And he said: Blessed be the LORD God of Sem
Genesis9:27 God increase Japheth that he may dwell in the tents of Sem. And Canaan be their servant.
Genesis9:28 And Noe lived after the flood three hundred and fifty year:
Genesis9:29 So that all the days of Noe were nine hundred and fifty year
Genesis10:1 These are the generations of the sons of Noe: of Sem
Genesis10:2 The sons of Japheth were: Gomir
Genesis10:3 And the sons of Gomir were: Ascenas
Genesis10:4 And the sons of Javan were: Elisa
Genesis10:5 Of these came the Isles of the gentiles in their countries
Genesis10:6 The sons of Ham were: Chus
Genesis10:7 The sons of Chus: were Seba
Genesis10:8 Chus also begot Nimrod
Genesis10:9 He was a mighty hunter in the sight of the LORD: Whereof came the proverb: he is as Nimrod that mighty hunter in the sight of the LORD.
Genesis10:10 And the beginning of his kingdom was Babell
Genesis10:11 Out of that land came Assur and builded Ninive
Genesis10:12 and Ressen between Nineve and Calah. That is a great city.
Genesis10:13 And Mizrim begat Ludim
Genesis10:14 Pathrusim and Casluhim: from whence came the Philistins
Genesis10:15 Canaan also begat Zidon his eldest son and Heth
Genesis10:16 Jebusi
Genesis10:17 Hivi
Genesis10:18 Arvadi
Genesis10:19 And the coasts of the Cananites were from Sidon till thou come to Gerara and to Asa
Genesis10:20 These were the children of Ham in their kindreds
Genesis10:21 And Sem the father of all the children of Eber and the eldest brother of Japheth
Genesis10:22 And his sons were: Elam Assur
Genesis10:23 And the children of Aram were: Ur
Genesis10:24 And Arphachsad begat Sala
Genesis10:25 And Eber begat two sons. The name of the one was Peleg
Genesis10:26 Jaketan begat Almodad
Genesis10:27 Hadoram
Genesis10:28 Obal
Genesis10:29 Ophir
Genesis10:30 And the dwelling of them was from Mesa until thou come unto Sephara a mountain of the east land.
Genesis10:31 These are the sons of Sem in their kindreds
Genesis10:32 These are the kindreds of the sons of Noe
Genesis11:1 And all the world was of one tongue and one language.
Genesis11:2 And as they came from the east
Genesis11:3 And they said one to another: come on
Genesis11:4 And they said: Come on
Genesis11:5 And the LORD came down to see the city and the tower which the children of Adam had builded.
Genesis11:6 And the LORD said: See
Genesis11:7 Come on
Genesis11:8 Thus the LORD scattered them from thence upon all the earth. And they left off to build the city.
Genesis11:9 Wherefore the name of it is called Babel
Genesis11:10 These are the generations of Sem: Sem was an hundred year old and begat Arphachsad two year after the flood.
Genesis11:11 And Sem lived after he had begot Arphachsad five hundred year and begat sons and daughters.
Genesis11:12 And Arphachsad lived thirty five year and begat Sala
Genesis11:13 and lived after he had begot Sala four hundred year and three and begat sons and daughters.
Genesis11:14 And Sala was thirty year old and begat Eber
Genesis11:15 and lived after he had begot Eber four hundred and three year
Genesis11:16 When Eber was thirty four year old
Genesis11:17 and lived after he had begot Peleg
Genesis11:18 And Peleg when he was thirty year old begat Regu
Genesis11:19 and lived after he had begot Regu two hundred and nine year
Genesis11:20 And Regu when he had lived thirty two year begat Serug
Genesis11:21 and lived after he had begot Serug two hundred and seven year
Genesis11:22 And when Serug was thirty year old
Genesis11:23 and lived after he had begot Nahot two hundred year
Genesis11:24 And Nahor when he was twenty nine year old
Genesis11:25 and lived after he had begot Terah
Genesis11:26 And when Terah was seventy year old
Genesis11:27 And these are the generations of Terah. Terah begat Abram
Genesis11:28 And Haran died before Terah his father in the land where he was born
Genesis11:29 And Abram and Nahor took them wives. Abram's wife was called Sarai. And Nahor's wife Milkah the daughter of Haran which was father of Milca and of Iisca.
Genesis11:30 But Sarai was barren and had no child.
Genesis11:31 Then took Terah Abram his son and Lot his son Haran's son
Genesis11:32 And when Terah was two hundred year old and five he died in Haran.
Genesis12:1 Then the LORD said unto Abram: Get thee out of thy country and from thy kindred
Genesis12:2 And I will make of thee a mighty people
Genesis12:3 And I will bless them that bless thee
Genesis12:4 And Abram went as the LORD bade him
Genesis12:5 And Abram took Sarai his wife and Lot his brother's son
Genesis12:6 Abram went forth into the land till he came unto a place called Sichem
Genesis12:7 Then the LORD appeared unto Abram and said: unto thy seed will I give this land. And he builded an altar there unto the LORD(LORDE) which appeared to him.
Genesis12:8 Then departed he thence unto a mountain that lieth on the east side of BETHEL and pitched his tent: BETHEL being on the west side
Genesis12:9 And then Abram departed and took his journey southward.
Genesis12:10 After this there came a dearth in the land. And Abram went down into Egypt to sojourn there
Genesis12:11 And when he was come nigh for to enter into Egypt
Genesis12:12 It will come to pass therefore when the Egyptians see thee
Genesis12:13 Say I pray thee therefore that thou art my sister
Genesis12:14 As soon as he came into Egypt
Genesis12:15 And Pharao's lords saw her also
Genesis12:16 which entreated Abram well for her sake
Genesis12:17 But GOD(The LORDE) plagued Pharao and his house with great plagues
Genesis12:18 Then Pharao called Abram and said: Why hast thou thus dealt with me? Wherefore toldest thou me not that she was thy wife?
Genesis12:19 Why saidest thou that she was thy sister
Genesis12:20 Pharao also gave a charge unto his men over Abram
Genesis13:1 Then Abram departed out of Egypt
Genesis13:2 Abram was very rich in cattle
Genesis13:3 And he went on his journey from the south even unto BETHEL
Genesis13:4 and unto the place of the altar which he made before. And there called Abram upon the name
Genesis13:5 Lot also which went with him had sheep
Genesis13:6 so that the land was not able to receive them that they might dwell together
Genesis13:7 And there fell a strife between the herdmen of Abram's cattle
Genesis13:8 Then said Abram unto Lot: Let there be no strife I pray thee between thee and me and between my herdmen and thine
Genesis13:9 Is not all the hole land before thee? Depart I pray thee from me. If thou wilt take the left hand
Genesis13:10 And Lot lift up his eyes and beheld all the country about Jordan
Genesis13:11 Then Lot chose all the coasts of Jordan and took his journey from the east. And so departed the one brother from the other.
Genesis13:12 Abram dwelled in the land of Canaan: And Lot in the cities of the plain
Genesis13:13 But the men of Sodom were wicked and sinned exceedingly against the LORD.
Genesis13:14 And the LORD said unto Abram
Genesis13:15 for all the land which thou seest will I give unto thee and to thy seed for ever.
Genesis13:16 And I will make thy seed
Genesis13:17 Arise and walk about in the land
Genesis13:18 Then Abram took down his tent
Genesis14:1 And it chanced within a while
Genesis14:2 made war with Bera king of Sodom
Genesis14:3 All these came together unto the vale of Siddim
Genesis14:4 Twelve year were they subject to king Kedorlaomer
Genesis14:5 Therefore in the fourteenth year came Kedorlaomer and the kings that were with him
Genesis14:6 and the Horims in their own mount Seir unto the plain of Pharan
Genesis14:7 And then turned they and came to the well of judgement which is Cades
Genesis14:8 Then went out the king of Sodom
Genesis14:9 with Kedorlaomer the king of Elam and with Thydeall king of the Nations
Genesis14:10 And that vale of Siddim was full of slime pits. And the kings of Sodom and Gomorra fled
Genesis14:11 And they took all the goods of Sodom and Gomorra and all their vitailles
Genesis14:12 And they took Lot also Abram's brother's son and his goods (for he dwelled at Sodom) and departed.
Genesis14:13 Then came one that had escaped
Genesis14:14 When Abram heard that his brother was taken
Genesis14:15 And set himself and his servants in array
Genesis14:16 and brought again all the goods and also his brother Lot
Genesis14:17 And as he returned again from the slaughter of Kedorlaomer and of the kings that were with him
Genesis14:18 Then Melchisedech king of Salem brought forth bread and wine. And he being the priest of the most highest God
Genesis14:19 Blessed be Abram unto the most highest God
Genesis14:20 And blessed be God the most highest
Genesis14:21 Then said the king of Sodom unto Abram: Give me the souls
Genesis14:22 And Abram answered the king of Sodom: I lift up my hand unto the LORD God most high possessor of heaven and earth
Genesis14:23 that I will not take of all that is thine
Genesis14:24 Save only that which the young men have eaten and the parts of the men which went with me. Aner
Genesis15:1 After these deeds
Genesis15:2 And Abram answered: Lord Jehovah(LORde Iehouah) what wilt thou give me: I go childless
Genesis15:3 And Abram said: See
Genesis15:4 And behold
Genesis15:5 And he brought him out at the doors and said: Look up unto heaven and tell the stars
Genesis15:6 And Abram believed the LORD
Genesis15:7 And he said unto him: I am the LORD that brought thee out of Ur in Chaldea to give thee this land to possess it.
Genesis15:8 And he said: Lord GOD
Genesis15:9 And he said unto him: take an heifer of three year old
Genesis15:10 And he took all these and divided them in the midst
Genesis15:11 And the birds fell on the carcasses
Genesis15:12 And when the sun was down
Genesis15:13 And he said unto Abram: know this of a surety
Genesis15:14 But the nation whom they shall serve
Genesis15:15 Nevertheless thou shalt go unto thy fathers in peace
Genesis15:16 and in the fourth generation they shall come hither again
Genesis15:17 When the sun was down and it was waxed dark: behold
Genesis15:18 And that same day the LORD made a covenant with Abram saying: unto thy seed will I give this land

Genesis15:20 the Hethites
Genesis15:21 the Amorites
Genesis16:1 Sarai Abram's wife bare him no children. But she had an hand maid an Egyptian
Genesis16:2 Wherefore she said unto Abram: Behold the LORD hath closed me
Genesis16:3 Then Sarai Abram's wife took Hagar her maid the Egyptian (after Abram had dwelled ten year in the land of Canaan) and gave her to her husband Abram
Genesis16:4 And he went in unto Hagar
Genesis16:5 Then said Sarai unto Abram: Thou dost me unright
Genesis16:6 Then said Abram to Sarai: behold

Genesis39:22 which committed to Joseph's hand all the prisoners that were in the prison house. And whatsoever was done there
Genesis39:23 And the keeper of the prison looked unto nothing that was under his hand
Genesis40:1 And it chanced after this
Genesis40:2 And Pharao was angry with them and put them in ward in his chief marshal's house:
Genesis40:3 even in the prison where Joseph was bound.
Genesis40:4 And the chief marshal gave Joseph a charge with them
Genesis40:5 And they dreamed either of them in one night: both the butler and the baker of the king of Egypt which were bound in the prison house
Genesis40:6 When Joseph came in unto them in the morning
Genesis40:7 And he asked them saying
Genesis40:8 They answered him
Genesis40:9 And the chief butler told his dream to Joseph and said unto him: In my dream me thought there stood a vine before me
Genesis40:10 and in the vine were three branches
Genesis40:11 And I had Pharao's cup in my hand
Genesis40:12 And Joseph said unto him
Genesis40:13 The three branches are three days: for within three days shall Pharao lift up thine head
Genesis40:14 But think on me with thee
Genesis40:15 for I was stolen out of the land of the Hebrews
Genesis40:16 When the chief baker saw that he had well interpreted it
Genesis40:17 And in the uppermost basket
Genesis40:18 Joseph answered and said: this is the interpretation thereof. The three baskets are three days
Genesis40:19 for this day three days shall Pharao take thy head from thee
Genesis40:20 And it came to pass the third day which was Pharao's birthday
Genesis40:21 And restored the chief butler unto his butlership again
Genesis40:22 and hanged the chief baker: even as Joseph had interpreted unto them.
Genesis40:23 Notwithstanding the chief butler remembered not Joseph
Genesis41:1 And it fortuned at two years' end
Genesis41:2 and that there came out of the river seven goodly kine and fat fleshed
Genesis41:3 And him thought that seven other kine came up after them out of the river evil favored and lean fleshed and stood by the other upon the brink of the river.
Genesis41:4 And the evil favored and lean fleshed kine: ate up the seven well favored and fat kine: and he awoke therewith.
Genesis41:5 And he slept again and dreamed the second time
Genesis41:6 And that seven thin ears blasted with the wind
Genesis41:7 and that the seven thin ears devoured the seven rank and full ears. And then Pharao awaked: and see
Genesis41:8 When the morning came
Genesis41:9 Then spake the chief butler unto Pharao saying: I do remember my fault this day.
Genesis41:10 Pharao was angry with his servants
Genesis41:11 And we dreamed both of us in one night and each man's dream of a sundry interpretation.
Genesis41:12 And there was with us a young man
Genesis41:13 And as he declared them unto us
Genesis41:14 Then Pharao sent and called Joseph. And they made him haste out of prison. And he shaved himself and changed his raiment
Genesis41:15 And Pharao said unto Joseph: I have dreamed a dream and no man can interpretate it
Genesis41:16 And Joseph answered Pharao saying: God shall give Pharao an answer of peace without me.
Genesis41:17 Pharao said unto Joseph: in my dream me thought I stood by a river's side
Genesis41:18 and there came out of the river seven fat fleshed and well favored kine
Genesis41:19 And then seven other kine came up after them
Genesis41:20 And the seven lean and evil favored kine ate up the first seven fat kine.
Genesis41:21 And when they had eaten them up
Genesis41:22 And I saw again in my dream seven ears spring out of one stalk full and good
Genesis41:23 and seven other ears withered
Genesis41:24 And the thin ears devoured the seven good ears. And I have told it unto the soothsayers
Genesis41:25 Then Joseph said unto Pharao: both Pharao's dreams are one. And God doth shew Pharao what he is about to do.
Genesis41:26 The seven good kine are seven year: and the seven good ears are seven year also
Genesis41:27 Likewise
Genesis41:28 This is that which I said unto Pharao
Genesis41:29 Behold there shall come seven year of great plenteousness throughout all the land of Egypt.
Genesis41:30 And there shall arise after them seven years of hunger. So that all the plenteousness shall be forgotten in the land of Egypt. And the hunger shall consume the land:
Genesis41:31 so that the plenteousness shall not be once a seen in the land by reason of that hunger that shall come after
Genesis41:32 And as concerning that the dream was doubled unto Pharao the second time
Genesis41:33 Now therefore let Pharao provide for a man of understanding and wisdom
Genesis41:34 And let Pharao make officers over the land
Genesis41:35 and let them gather all the food of these good years that come
Genesis41:36 and there let them keep it: that there may be food in store in the land
Genesis41:37 And the saying pleased Pharao and all his servants.
Genesis41:38 Then said Pharao unto his servants: where shall we find such a man as this is
Genesis41:39 wherefore Pharao said unto Joseph: forasmuch as God hath shewed thee all this
Genesis41:40 Thou therefore shalt be over my house
Genesis41:41 And he said unto Joseph: behold
Genesis41:42 And he took off his ring from his finger
Genesis41:43 and set him upon the best chariot that he had save one. And they cried before him Abrech
Genesis41:44 And Pharao said unto Joseph: I am Pharao
Genesis41:45 And he called Joseph's name Zaphnath Paenea. And he gave him to wife Asnath the daughter of Potiphar priest of On. Then went Joseph abroad in the land of Egypt.
Genesis41:46 And he was thirty year old when he stood before Pharao king of Egypt. And then Joseph departed from Pharao
Genesis41:47 And in the seven plenteous years they made sheaves
Genesis41:48 and gathered up all the food of the seven plenteous years which were in the land of Egypt and put it into the cities. And he put the food of the fields that grew round about every city: even in the same.

Genesis41:56 And when the dearth was thorow out all the land

Exodus2:3 And when she could no longer hide him
Exodus2:4 And his sister stood afar off
Exodus2:5 And the daughter of Pharao came down to the river to wash herself
Exodus2:6 And when she had opened it she saw the child
Exodus2:7 Then said his sister unto Pharao's daughter: shall I go and call unto thee a nurse of the Hebrew's women
Exodus2:8 And the maid ran and called the child's mother.
Exodus2:9 Then Pharao's daughter said unto her. Take this child away and nurse it for me
Exodus2:10 And when the child was grown
Exodus2:11 And it happened in these days when Moses was waxed great
Exodus2:12 And he looked round about: and when he saw that there was no man by
Exodus2:13 And he went out another day: and behold
Exodus2:14 And he answered: who hath made thee a ruler or a judge over us? Intendest thou to kill me
Exodus2:15 And Pharao heard of it and went about to slay Moses: but he fled from Pharao and dwelt in the land of Madian
Exodus2:16 The priest of Madian had seven daughters which came and drew water and filled the troughs
Exodus2:17 And the shepherds came and drove them away: But Moses stood up and helped them and watered their sheep.

Exodus2:19 And they answered: there was an Egyptian that delivered us from the shepherds
Exodus2:20 And he said unto his daughters: where is he? why have ye left the man? Go call him that he may eat bread.
Exodus2:21 And Moses was content to dwell with the man. And he gave Moses Zephora his daughter which bare a son
Exodus2:22 and he called him Gerson: for he said: I have been a stranger in a strange land.
Exodus2:23 And she bare yet another son
Exodus2:24 And their complaint came up unto God from the labour. And God remembered his promise with Abraham
Exodus2:25 And God looked upon the children of Israel and knew them.

Exodus12:1 And the LORD spake unto Moses and Aaron in the land of Egypt saying:
Exodus12:2 This month shall be your chief month: even the first month of the year shall it be unto you.
Exodus12:3 Speak ye unto all the fellowship of Israel saying: that they take the tenth day of this month to every household
Exodus12:4 If the household be too few for a sheep
Exodus12:5 A sheep without spot and a male of one year old shall it be
Exodus12:6 And ye shall keep him in ward
Exodus12:7 And they shall take of the blood and strike on the two side posts and on the upper doorpost of the houses
Exodus12:8 And they shall eat the flesh the same night
Exodus12:9 See that ye eat not thereof sodden in water
Exodus12:10 And see that ye let nothing of it remain unto the morning: if ought remain burn it with fire.
Exodus12:11 Of this manner shall ye eat it: with your loins girded
Exodus12:12 for I will go about in the land of Egypt this same night
Exodus12:13 And the blood shall be unto you a token upon the houses wherein ye are
Exodus12:14 And this day shall be unto you a remembrance
Exodus12:15 Seven days shall ye eat unleavened bread
Exodus12:16 The first day shall be a holy feast unto you
Exodus12:17 And see that ye keep you to unleavened bread. For upon that same day I will bring your armies out of the land of Egypt
Exodus12:18 The first month and the fourteenth day of the month at even
Exodus12:19 Seven days see that there be no leavened bread found in your houses. For whosoever eateth leavened bread
Exodus12:20 Therefore see that ye eat no leavened bread
Exodus12:21 And Moses called for the elders of Israel and said unto them: choose out and take to every household a sheep
Exodus12:22 And take a bunch of hyssop
Exodus12:23 For the LORD will go about and smite Egypt. And when he seeth the blood upon the upper door post and on the two side posts
Exodus12:24 Therefore see that thou observe this thing
Exodus12:25 And when ye be come into the land which the LORD will give you according as he hath promised
Exodus12:26 And when your children ask you what manner of service is this ye do.
Exodus12:27 Ye shall say: it is the sacrifice of the LORD's passover
Exodus12:28 And the children of Israel went and did as the LORD had commanded Moses and Aaron.
Exodus12:29 And at midnight the LORD smote all the firstborn in the land of Egypt: from the firstborn of Pharao that sat on his seat
Exodus12:30 Then Pharao arose the same night and all his servants and all the Egyptians
Exodus12:31 And he called unto Moses and Aaron by night saying: Rise up
Exodus12:32 And take your sheep and your oxen with you as ye have said
Exodus12:33 And the Egyptians were fierce upon the people and made haste to send them out of the land: for they said: we be all dead men.
Exodus12:34 And the people took the dough before it was soured which they had in store
Exodus12:35 And the children of Israel did according to the saying of Moses: and they borrowed of the Egyptians jewels of silver
Exodus12:36 And the LORD gat the people favour in the sight of the Egyptians: and so they borrowed and robbed the Egyptians.
Exodus12:37 Thus took the children of Israel their journey from Rameses to Sucoth six hundred thousand men of foot
Exodus12:38 And much common people went also with them
Exodus12:39 And they baked sweet cakes of the dough which they brought out of Egypt
Exodus12:40 And the time of the dwelling of the children of Israel which they dwelt in Egypt
Exodus12:41 And when the four hundred and thirty years were expired
Exodus12:42 This is a night to be observed to the LORD
Exodus12:43 And the LORD said unto Moses and Aaron: this is the manner of passover: there shall no stranger eat thereof
Exodus12:44 but all the servants that are bought for money shall ye circumcise
Exodus12:45 A stranger and a hired servant shall not eat thereof.
Exodus12:46 In one house shall it be eaten. Ye shall carry none of the flesh out at the doors: moreover
Exodus12:47 All the multitude of the children of Israel shall observe it.
Exodus12:48 If a stranger dwell among you
Exodus12:49 One manner of law shall be unto them that are born in the land
Exodus12:50 And all the children of Israel did as the LORD commanded Moses and Aaron.
Exodus12:51 And even the self same day did the LORD bring the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt with their armies.

Exodus13:5 When the LORD hath brought thee in to the land of the Cananites

Exodus13:9 Therefore it shall be a sign unto thee upon thine hand and a remembrance between thine eyes
Exodus13:10 see thou keep therefore this ordinance in his season from year to year.

Exodus13:20 And they took their journey from Sucoth: and pitched their tents in Etham in the edge of the wilderness.

Exodus13:22 And the pillar of the cloud never departed by day nor the pillar of fire by night out of the people's sight.

Exodus14:14 The LORD shall fight for you and ye shall hold your peace.
Exodus14:15 The LORD said unto Moses: wherefore criest thou unto me? speak unto the children of Israel that they go forward.
Exodus14:16 But lift thou up thy rod and stretch out thy hand over the sea and divide it asunder
Exodus14:17 And behold I will harden the hearts of the Egyptians that they may follow you. And I will get me honour upon Pharao and upon all his host
Exodus14:18 And the Egyptians shall know that I am the LORD when I have gotten me honour upon Pharao
Exodus14:19 And the angel of God which went before the host of Israel
Exodus14:20 and went between the host of the Egyptians and the host of Israel. It was a dark cloud
Exodus14:21 When now Moses stretched forth his hand over the sea
Exodus14:22 And the children of Israel went in thorow the middest of the sea upon the dry ground. And the water was a wall unto them
Exodus14:23 And the Egyptians followed and went in after them to the middest of the sea
Exodus14:24 And in the morning watch
Exodus14:25 and smote off their chariot wheels and cast them down to the ground. Then said the Egyptians: Let us flee from Israel
Exodus14:26 Then said the LORD unto Moses: stretch out thine hand over the sea
Exodus14:27 Then stretched forth Moses his hand over the sea
Exodus14:28 and the water returned and covered the chariots and the horsemen: so that of all the host of Pharao that came into the sea after them
Exodus14:29 But the children of Israel went upon dry land in the middest of the sea
Exodus14:30 Thus the LORD delivered Israel the self same day out of the hand of the Egyptians
Exodus14:31 And when Israel saw that mighty hand which the LORD had shewed upon the Egyptians
Exodus15:1 Then Moses and the children of Israel sang this song unto the LORD and said: Let us sing unto the LORD
Exodus15:2 The LORD is my strength and my song
Exodus15:3 The LORD is a man of war
Exodus15:4 Pharao's chariots and his host hath he cast into the sea. His jolly captains are drowned in the redde(reed) sea
Exodus15:5 the deep waters have covered them: they sank to the bottom as a stone.
Exodus15:6 Thine hand LORD is glorious in power
Exodus15:7 And with thy great glory thou hast destroyed thine adversaries
Exodus15:8 With the breath of thine anger the water gathered together and the floods stood still as a rock
Exodus15:9 The enemy said: I will follow and overtake them
Exodus15:10 Thou blewest with thy breath and the sea covered them
Exodus15:11 Who is like unto thee o LORD among gods: who is like thee
Exodus15:12 Thou stretchedest out thy right hand
Exodus15:13 And thou carriedest with thy mercy this people which thou deliveredest
Exodus15:14 The nations heard
Exodus15:15 Then the dukes of the Edomites were amazed
Exodus15:16 Let fear and dread fall upon them thorow the greatness of thine arm
Exodus15:17 Bring them in and plant them in the mountains of thine inheritance
Exodus15:18 The LORD reign ever and allway.
Exodus15:19 For Pharao went in on horseback with his chariots and horsemen into the sea
Exodus15:20 And Miriam a prophetess the sister of Aaron took a timbrel in her hand
Exodus15:21 And Miriam sang before them: sing ye unto the LORD
Exodus15:22 Moses brought Israel from the redde(reed) sea
Exodus15:23 At the last they came to Mara: but they could not drink of the waters for bitterness
Exodus15:24 Then the people murmured against Moses
Exodus15:25 And Moses cried unto the LORD
Exodus15:26 and said: If ye will hearken unto the voice of the LORD your God
Exodus15:27 And they came to Elim where were twelve wells of water and seventy date trees
Exodus16:1 And they took their journey from Elim
Exodus16:2 And the hole multitude of the children of Israel murmured against Moses and Aaron in the wilderness
Exodus16:3 and said unto them: would to God we had died by the hand of the LORD in the land of Egypt
Exodus16:4 Then said the LORD unto Moses: behold
Exodus16:5 The sixth day let them prepare that which they will bring in
Exodus16:6 And Moses and Aaron said unto all the children of Israel: at even ye shall know that it is the LORD
Exodus16:7 and in the morning ye shall see the glory of the LORD: because he hath heard your grudgings against the LORD: for what are we that ye should murmur against us?
Exodus16:8 And moreover spake Moses: At evening the LORD will give you flesh to eat
Exodus16:9 And Moses spake unto Aaron: Say unto all the company of the children of Israel
Exodus16:10 And as Aaron spake unto the hole multitude of the children of Israel
Exodus16:11 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying:
Exodus16:12 I have heard the murmuring of the children of Israel
Exodus16:13 And at evening the quails came and covered the ground where they lay. And in the morning the dew lay round about the host.
Exodus16:14 And when the dew was fallen: behold
Exodus16:15 When the children of Israel saw it
Exodus16:16 This is the thing which the LORD hath commanded
Exodus16:17 And the children of Israel did even so
Exodus16:18 and did mete it with a gomer. And unto him that had gathered much remained nothing over
Exodus16:19 And Moses said unto them: See that no man let ought remain of it till the morning.
Exodus16:20 Notwithstanding they hearkened not unto Moses: but some of the left of it until the mornynge
Exodus16:21 And they gathered it all mornings: every man as much as sufficed for his eating
Exodus16:22 And the sixth day they gathered twice so much bread
Exodus16:23 And he said unto them: this is that which the LORD hath said: tomorrow is the Sabbath of the holy rest of the LORD: bake that which ye will bake and fetch that ye will fetch
Exodus16:24 And they laid it up till the morning as Moses bade
Exodus16:25 And Moses said: that eat this day: for today it is the LORD's Sabbath: today ye shall find none in the field.
Exodus16:26 Sixth days ye shall gather it
Exodus16:27 Notwithstanding there went out of the people in the seventh day for to gather: but they found none.
Exodus16:28 Then the LORD said unto Moses: how long shall it be
Exodus16:29 See because the LORD hath given you a Sabbath
Exodus16:30 And the people rested the seventh day.
Exodus16:31 And the house of Israel called it Man. And it was like unto Coriander seed and white
Exodus16:32 And Moses said: this is that which the LORD commandeth: fill a gomer of it
Exodus16:33 And Moses spake unto Aaron: take a cruse and put a gomer full of Man therein
Exodus16:34 as the LORD commanded Moses. And Aaron laid it up before the testimony there to be kept.
Exodus16:35 And the children of Israel ate man forty year until they came unto a land inhabited. And so they ate Man
Exodus16:36 and a gomer is the tenth part of an Epha.

Exodus17:5 And the LORD said unto Moses: go before the people

Exodus17:12 When Moses' hands were weary

Exodus18:1 Jethro the priest of Madian Moses' father-in-law heard of all that God had done unto Moses and to Israel his people
Exodus18:2 And he took Zippora Moses' wife
Exodus18:3 and her two sons
Exodus18:4 And the other was called Eliesar: for the God of my father was mine help and delivered me from the sword of Pharao.
Exodus18:5 And Jethro Moses' father-in-law came with his two sons and his wife unto Moses into the wilderness: where he had pitched his tent by the mount of God.
Exodus18:6 And he sent word to Moses: I thy father-in-law Jethro am come to thee
Exodus18:7 And Moses went out to meet his father-in-law and did obeisance and kissed him
Exodus18:8 And Moses told his father-in-law all that the LORD had done unto Pharao and to the Egyptians for Israel's sake
Exodus18:9 And Jethro rejoiced over all the good which the LORD had done to Israel
Exodus18:10 And Jethro said: blessed be the LORD which hath delivered you out of the hand of the Egyptians and out of the hand of Pharao
Exodus18:11 Now I know that the LORD is greater than all gods
Exodus18:12 And Jethro Moses' father-in-law offered burnt offerings and sacrifices unto God. And Aaron and all the elders of Israel came to eat bread with Moses' father-in-law before God.
Exodus18:13 And it chanced on the morrow
Exodus18:14 When his father-in-law saw all that he did unto the people
Exodus18:15 And Moses said unto his father-in-law: because the people came unto me to seek counsel(councell) of God.
Exodus18:16 For when they have a matter
Exodus18:17 And his father-in-law said unto him: it is not well that thou doest.
Exodus18:18 Thou doest unwisely and also this people that is with thee: because the thing is too grievous for thee
Exodus18:19 But hear my voice
Exodus18:20 and provide them ordinances and laws
Exodus18:21 Moreover seek out among all the people
Exodus18:22 And let them judge the people at all seasons: If there be any great matter
Exodus18:23 If thou shalt do this thing
Exodus18:24 And Moses heard the voice of his father-in-law
Exodus18:25 and chose active men out of all Israel and made them heads over the people
Exodus18:26 And they judged the people at all seasons
Exodus18:27 And then Moses let his father-in-law depart
Exodus19:1 The third month after the children of Israel were gone out of Egypt: the same day they came into the wilderness of Sinai.
Exodus19:2 For they were departed from Raphidim
Exodus19:3 And Moses went up unto God. And the LORD called to him out of the mountain saying: thus say unto the house of Jacob
Exodus19:4 Ye have seen what I did unto the Egyptians and how I took you up upon Eagles' wings
Exodus19:5 Now therefore if ye will hear my voice and keep mine appointment: ye shall be mine own above all nations
Exodus19:6 Ye shall be unto me a kingdom of priests and an holy people: these are the words which thou shalt say unto the children of Israel.
Exodus19:7 And Moses came and called for the elders of Israel
Exodus19:8 And the people answered all together and said: All that the LORD hath said
Exodus19:9 And the LORD said unto Moses: Lo
Exodus19:10 And the LORD said unto Moses: Go unto the people and sanctify them today and tomorrow
Exodus19:11 that they may be ready against the third day. For the third day the LORD will come down in the sight of all the people upon mount Sinai.
Exodus19:12 And set marks round about the people and say: beware that ye go not up into the mount
Exodus19:13 There shall not an hand twich it
Exodus19:14 And Moses went down from the mount unto the people and sanctified them
Exodus19:15 And he said unto the people: be ready against the third day
Exodus19:16 And the third day in the morning there was thunder
Exodus19:17 And Moses brought the people out of the tents to meet with God
Exodus19:18 And mount Sinai was altogether on a smoke: because the LORD descended down upon it in fire. And the smoke thereof ascended up
Exodus19:19 And the voice of the horn blew and waxed louder
Exodus19:20 And the LORD came down upon mount Sinai
Exodus19:21 And the LORD said unto Moses: go down and charge the people that they prease not up unto the LORD for to see him
Exodus19:22 And let the priests also which come to the LORD's presence
Exodus19:23 Then Moses said unto the LORD: the people can not come up in to mount Sinai
Exodus19:24 And the LORD said unto him: away
Exodus19:25 And Moses went down unto the people and told them.
Exodus20:1 And God spake all these words and said:
Exodus20:2 I am the LORD thy God
Exodus20:3 Thou shalt have none other gods in my sight.
Exodus20:4 Thou shalt make thee no graven image
Exodus20:5 See that thou neither bow thyself unto them neither serve them: for I the LORD thy God
Exodus20:6 and yet shew mercy unto thousands among them that love me and keep my commandments.
Exodus20:7 Thou shalt not take the name of the LORD thy God in vain
Exodus20:8 Remember the Sabbath day that thou sanctify it.
Exodus20:9 Six days mayst thou labour and do all that thou hast to do:
Exodus20:10 but the seventh day is the Sabbath of the LORD thy God
Exodus20:11 For in six days the LORD made both heaven and earth and the sea and all that in them is and rested the seventh day: wherefore the LORD blessed the Sabbath day and hallowed it.
Exodus20:12 Honour thy father and thy mother
Exodus20:13 Thou shalt not kill.
Exodus20:14 Thou shalt not break wedlock.
Exodus20:15 Thou shalt not steal.
Exodus20:16 Thou shalt bear no false witness against thy neighbour.
Exodus20:17 Thou shalt not covet thy neighbour's house: neither shalt covet thy neighbour's wife
Exodus20:18 And all the people saw the thunder and the lightning and the noise of the horn
Exodus20:19 And when the people saw it
Exodus20:20 And Moses said unto the people fear not
Exodus20:21 And the people stood afar off
Exodus20:22 And the LORD said unto Moses: thus thou shalt say unto the children of Israel: Ye have seen how that I have talked with you from out of heaven.
Exodus20:23 Ye shall not make therefore with me gods of silver nor gods of gold: in no wise shall ye do it.
Exodus20:24 An altar of earth thou shalt make unto me and thereon offer thy burnt offerings and thy peace offerings
Exodus20:25 But and if thou wilt make me an altar of stone
Exodus20:26 Moreover thou shalt not go up with steps unto mine altar
Exodus21:1 These are the laws which thou shalt set before them.
Exodus21:2 If thou buy a servant that is an Hebrew
Exodus21:3 If he came alone
Exodus21:4 And if his master have given him a wife and she have borne him sons or daughters: then the wife and her children shall be her master's and he shall go out alone.
Exodus21:5 But and if the servant say I love my master and my wife and my children
Exodus21:6 Then let his master bring him unto the Gods and set him to the door or the doorpost
Exodus21:7 If a man sell his daughter to be a servant: she shall not go out as the menservants do.
Exodus21:8 If she please not her master
Exodus21:9 If he have promised her unto his son to wife
Exodus21:10 If he take him another wife
Exodus21:11 If he do not these three unto her
Exodus21:12 He that smiteth a man that he die
Exodus21:13 If a man lay not await but God deliver him into his hand
Exodus21:14 If a man come presumptuously upon his neighbour and slay him with guile
Exodus21:15 And he that smiteth his father or his mother
Exodus21:16 He that stealeth a man and selleth him (if it be proved upon him) shall be slain for it.
Exodus21:17 And he that curseth his father or mother
Exodus21:18 If men strive together and one smite another with a stone or with his fist
Exodus21:19 if he rise again and walk without upon his staff
Exodus21:20 If a man smite his servant or his maid with a staff that they die under his hand
Exodus21:21 But and if they continue a day or two
Exodus21:22 When men strive and smite a woman with child so that her fruit depart from her and yet no misfortune followeth: then shall he be merced
Exodus21:23 But and if any misfortune follow
Exodus21:24 eye for eye
Exodus21:25 burning for burning
Exodus21:26 If a man smite his servant or his maid in the eye and put it out
Exodus21:27 Also if he smite out his servant's or his maid's tooth
Exodus21:28 If an ox gore a man or a woman that they die
Exodus21:29 If the ox were wont to run at men in time past and it hath been told his master
Exodus21:30 If he be set to a sum of money
Exodus21:31 And whether he hath gored a son or a daughter
Exodus21:32 But if it be a servant or a maid that the ox hath gored
Exodus21:33 If a man open a well or dig a pit and cover it not
Exodus21:34 the owner of the pit shall make it good and give money unto their master
Exodus21:35 If one man's ox hurt another's that he die: then they shall sell the live ox and divide the money
Exodus21:36 But and if it be known that the ox hath used to push in time's past
Exodus22:1 If a man steal an ox or sheep and kill it or sell it
Exodus22:2 If a thief be found breaking up and be smitten that he die
Exodus22:3 except the son(sonne) be up when he is found
Exodus22:4 If the theft be found in his hand alive (whether it be ox
Exodus22:5 If a man do hurt field or vineyard
Exodus22:6 If fire break out and catch in the thorns
Exodus22:7 If a man deliver his neighbour money or stuff to keep
Exodus22:8 If the thief be not found
Exodus22:9 And in all manner of trespass
Exodus22:10 If a man deliver unto his neighbour to keep
Exodus22:11 then shall an oath of the LORD go between them
Exodus22:12 If it be stolen from him
Exodus22:13 If it be torn with wild beasts
Exodus22:14 When a man borroweth ought of his neighbour if it be hurt or else die
Exodus22:15 If the owner thereof be by
Exodus22:16 If a man beguile a maid that is not betrothed and lie with her
Exodus22:17 If her father refuse to give her unto him
Exodus22:18 Thou shalt not suffer a witch to live.
Exodus22:19 Whosoever lieth with a beast
Exodus22:20 He that offereth unto any gods save unto the LORD only
Exodus22:21 Vex not a stranger neither oppress him for ye were strangers in the land of Egypt.
Exodus22:22 Ye shall trouble no widow nor fatherless child:
Exodus22:23 If ye shall trouble them: they shall cry unto me
Exodus22:24 and then will my wrath wax hot and I will kill you with sword
Exodus22:25 If thou lend money to any of my people that is poor by thee
Exodus22:26 If thou take thy neighbour's raiment to pledge
Exodus22:27 For that is his coverlet only: even the raiment for his skin wherein he sleepeth: or else he will cry unto me and I will hear him
Exodus22:28 Thou shalt not rail upon the gods
Exodus22:29 Thy fruits (whether they be dry or moist) see thou keep not back. Thy firstborn son thou shalt give me:
Exodus22:30 likewise shalt thou do of thine oxen and of thy sheep. Seven days it shall be with the dam
Exodus22:31 Ye shall be holy people unto me
Exodus23:1 Thou shalt not accept a vain tale
Exodus23:2 Thou shalt not follow a multitude to do evil: neither answer in a matter of plea that thou wouldest to follow many turn aside from the truth
Exodus23:3 neither shalt thou paint a poor man's cause.
Exodus23:4 When thou meetest thine enemy's ox or ass going astray
Exodus23:5 If thou see thine enemy's ass sink under his burthen
Exodus23:6 Thou shalt not hinder the right of the poor that are among you in their suit.
Exodus23:7 Keep thee far from a false matter
Exodus23:8 Thou shalt take no gifts
Exodus23:9 Thou shalt not oppress a stranger
Exodus23:10 Six years thou shalt sow thy land and gather in the fruits thereof:
Exodus23:11 and the seventh year thou shalt let it rest and lie still
Exodus23:12 Six days thou shalt do thy work and the seventh day thou shalt keep holy day
Exodus23:13 And in all things that I have said unto you be circumspect. And make no rehearsal of the names of strange gods
Exodus23:14 Three feasts thou shalt hold unto me in a year.
Exodus23:15 Thou shalt keep the feast of sweet bread
Exodus23:16 And the feast of Harvest
Exodus23:17 Three times in a year shall all thy men children appear before the Lord Jehovah.(Lorde Iehouah)
Exodus23:18 Thou shalt not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leavened bread: neither shall the fat of my feast remain until the morning.
Exodus23:19 The first of the firstfruits of thy land thou shalt bring into the house of the LORD thy God thou shalt also not seethe a kid in his mother's milk.
Exodus23:20 Behold
Exodus23:21 Beware of him and hear his voice and anger him not: for he will not spare your misdeeds
Exodus23:22 But and if thou shalt hearken unto his voice and keep all that I shall tell thee
Exodus23:23 When mine angel goeth before thee and hath brought thee in unto the Amorites
Exodus23:24 see thou worship not their gods neither serve them
Exodus23:25 And see that ye serve the LORD your God
Exodus23:26 Moreover there shall be no woman childless or unfruitful in thy land
Exodus23:27 I will send my fear before thee and will kill all the people whither thou shalt go. And I will make all thine enemies turn their backs unto thee
Exodus23:28 and I will send hornets before thee
Exodus23:29 I will not cast them out in one year
Exodus23:30 But a little and a little I will drive them out before thee
Exodus23:31 And I will make thy coasts from the red sea unto the sea of the Philistines and from the desert unto the river. I will deliver the inhabiters of the land into thine hand
Exodus23:32 And thou shalt make none appointment with them nor with their gods.
Exodus23:33 Neither shall they dwell in thy land

Exodus25:1 And the LORD talked with Moses saying:
Exodus25:2 speak unto the children of Israel that they give me an heave offering
Exodus25:3 And this is the heave offering which ye shall take of them: gold
Exodus25:4 and Jacinth colour
Exodus25:5 ram's skins that are red
Exodus25:6 oil for lights
Exodus25:7 Onyx stones and set stones for the Ephod and for the breastlap.
Exodus25:8 And they shall make me a sanctuary that I may dwell among them.
Exodus25:9 And as I have shewed(And i shall shew) thee the fashion of the habitation and of all the ornaments thereof
Exodus25:10 And they shall make an ark of sethim wood two cubits and an half long
Exodus25:11 And thou shalt overlay it with pure gold: both within and without
Exodus25:12 And thou shalt cast four rings of gold for it and put them in the four corners thereof
Exodus25:13 And thou shalt make staves of sethim wood and cover them with gold
Exodus25:14 and put the staves in the rings along by the sides of the ark
Exodus25:15 And the staves shall abide in the rings of the ark
Exodus25:16 And thou shalt put in the ark
Exodus25:17 And thou shalt make a mercy seat of pure gold two cubits and an half long and a cubit and an half broad.
Exodus25:18 And make two cherubins of thick gold on the two ends of the mercy seat:
Exodus25:19 and set the one cherub on the one end and the other on the other end of the mercy seat: so see that thou make them on the two ends thereof.
Exodus25:20 And the cherubins shall stretch their wings abroad over on high
Exodus25:21 And thou shalt put the mercy seat above upon the ark
Exodus25:22 There I will meet thee and will commune(common) with thee from upon the mercy seat from between the two cherubins which are upon the ark of witness
Exodus25:23 Thou shalt also make a table of sethim wood of two cubits long and one cubit broad and a cubit and an half high.
Exodus25:24 And cover it with pure gold and make thereto a crown of gold round about.
Exodus25:25 And make unto that an hoop of four fingers broad
Exodus25:26 And make for it four rings of gold and put them in the corners that are on the four feet thereof:
Exodus25:27 even hard under the hoop shall the rings be
Exodus25:28 And thou shalt make staves of sethim wood and overlay them with gold
Exodus25:29 And thou shalt make his dishes
Exodus25:30 And thou shalt set upon the table
Exodus25:31 And thou shalt make a candlestick of pure thick gold with his shaft
Exodus25:32 Six branches shall proceed out of the sides of the candlestick
Exodus25:33 And there shall be three cups like unto almonds with knops and flowers upon every one of the six branches that proceed out of the candlestick:
Exodus25:34 and in the candlestick self four cups like unto almonds with their knops and flowers:
Exodus25:35 that there be a knop under every two branches of the six that proceed out of the candlestick.
Exodus25:36 And the knops and the branches shall be altogether
Exodus25:37 And thou shalt make seven lamps and put them an high(hye) thereon
Exodus25:38 with snuffers and firepans of pure gold.
Exodus25:39 An hundred pound weight of fine gold shall make it with all the apparel.
Exodus25:40 And see that thou make them after the fashion that was shewed thee in the mount.
Exodus26:1 And thou shalt make an habitation with ten curtains of twined byss
Exodus26:2 The length of a curtain shall be twenty eight cubits
Exodus26:3 five curtains shall be coupled together one to another: and the other five likewise shall be coupled together one to another.
Exodus26:4 Then shalt thou make loops of Jacinth colour
Exodus26:5 Fifty loops shalt thou make in the one curtain
Exodus26:6 And thou shalt make fifty buttons of gold
Exodus26:7 And thou shalt make eleven curtains of goat's hair
Exodus26:8 The length of a curtain shall be thirty cubits
Exodus26:9 And thou shalt couple five by them selves
Exodus26:10 And thou shalt make fifty loops in the edge of the utmost curtain on the one side: even in the coupling curtain
Exodus26:11 And thou shalt make fifty buttons of brass and put them on the loops
Exodus26:12 And the remnant that resteth in the curtains of the tent: even the breadth of half a curtain that resteth
Exodus26:13 a cubit on the one side and a cubit on the other side
Exodus26:14 And thou shalt make another covering for the tent of rams' skins dyed red: and yet another above all of taxus' skins.
Exodus26:15 And thou shalt make boards for the habitation of sethim wood to stand upright:
Exodus26:16 ten cubits long shall every board be
Exodus26:17 Two feet shall one board have to couple them together withal
Exodus26:18 And thou shalt make twenty boards for the habitation on the south side
Exodus26:19 and thou shalt make forty sockets of silver and put them under the twenty boards: two sockets under every board
Exodus26:20 In like manner in the north side of the habitation there shall be twenty boards
Exodus26:21 and forty sockets of silver: two sockets under every board.
Exodus26:22 And for the west end of the habitation
Exodus26:23 and two boards more for the two west corners of the habitation:
Exodus26:24 so that these two boards be coupled together beneath and likewise above with clamps. And so shall it be in both the corners.
Exodus26:25 And so there shall be eight boards in all
Exodus26:26 And thou shalt make bars of sethim wood five for the boards of the one side of the tabernacle
Exodus26:27 and five for the other side
Exodus26:28 And the middle bar shall go along thorow the midst(myddes) of the boards and bar them together from the one end unto the other.
Exodus26:29 And thou shalt cover the boards with gold and make golden rings for them to put the bars thorow
Exodus26:30 And rear up the habitation according to the fashion thereof that was shewed thee in the mount.
Exodus26:31 And thou shalt make a vail of Jacinth
Exodus26:32 And hang it upon four pillars of sethim wood covered with gold and that their knops be covered with gold also
Exodus26:33 And thou shalt hang up the vail with rings
Exodus26:34 And thou shalt put the mercy seat upon the ark of witness in the holiest place.
Exodus26:35 And thou shalt put the table without the vail and candlestick over against the table
Exodus26:36 And thou shalt make an hanging for the door of the tabernacle: of Jacinth
Exodus26:37 And thou shalt make for the hanging
Exodus27:1 And thou shalt make an altar of sethim wood: five cubits long and five cubits broad
Exodus27:2 And make it horns proceeding out in the four corners of it
Exodus27:3 And make his ashpans
Exodus27:4 after the fashion of a net
Exodus27:5 and put it beneath under the compass of the altar
Exodus27:6 And make staves for the altar of sethim wood
Exodus27:7 and let them be put in rings along by the sides of the altar
Exodus27:8 And make the altar hollow with boards: even as it was shewed thee in the mount
Exodus27:9 And thou shalt make a court unto the habitation
Exodus27:10 and twenty pillars thereof with their twenty sockets of brass: but the knops of the pillars and their hoops shall be silver.
Exodus27:11 In like wise on the north side there shall be hangings of an hundred cubits long and twenty pillars with their sockets of brass
Exodus27:12 And in the breadth of the court westward
Exodus27:13 And in the breadth of the court eastward toward the rising of the son
Exodus27:14 Hangings of fifteen cubits in the one side of it with three pillars
Exodus27:15 and likewise on the other side shall be hangings of fifteen cubits with three pillars and three sockets.
Exodus27:16 And in the gate of the court shall be a vail of twenty cubits: of jacinth
Exodus27:17 All the pillars round about the court shall be hooped with silver
Exodus27:18 The length of the court
Exodus27:19 And all the vessels of the habitation to all manner service and the pins thereof: yea and the pins also of the court
Exodus27:20 And command the children of Israel that they give the pure oil olive beaten for the lights to pour alway into the lamps.

Exodus28:1 And take thou unto thee
Exodus28:2 And thou shalt make holy raiment for Aaron thy brother
Exodus28:3 Moreover speak unto all that are wise hearted
Exodus28:4 These are the garments which they shall make: a breastlap
Exodus28:5 And they shall take thereto
Exodus28:6 And they shall make the Ephod of gold
Exodus28:7 The two sides shall come together
Exodus28:8 And the girdle of the Ephod shall be of the same workmanship and of the same stuff: even of gold
Exodus28:9 And thou shalt take two onyx stones and grave in them the names of the children of Israel:
Exodus28:10 fix in the one stone
Exodus28:11 After the work of a stone graver
Exodus28:12 And thou shalt put the two stones upon the two shoulders of the Ephod
Exodus28:13 And thou shalt make hooks of gold
Exodus28:14 and two chains of fine gold: link work and wreathed
Exodus28:15 And thou shalt make the breastlap of ensample(judgement) with broidered work: even after the work of the Ephod shalt thou make it: of gold
Exodus28:16 Foursquare it shall be and double
Exodus28:17 And thou shalt fill it with four rows of stones. In the first row shall be a Sardius
Exodus28:18 The second row
Exodus28:19 The third: Ligurius
Exodus28:20 The fourth: a Turcois Onyx and Jasper. And they shall be set in gold in their enclosures.
Exodus28:21 And the stones shall be graven as signets be graven: with the names of the children of Israel
Exodus28:22 And thou shalt make upon the breastlap two fastening chains of pure gold and wreathen work.
Exodus28:23 And thou shalt make likewise upon the breastlap two rings of gold and put them on the edges of the breastlap
Exodus28:24 and put the two wreathen chains of gold in the two rings which are in the edges of the breastlap.
Exodus28:25 And the two ends of the two chains thou shalt fasten in the two rings
Exodus28:26 And thou shalt yet make two rings of gold
Exodus28:27 And yet two other rings of gold thou shalt make
Exodus28:28 And they shall bind the breastlap by his rings unto the rings of the Ephod with a lace of jacinth
Exodus28:29 And Aaron shall bear the names of the children Israel in the breastlap of ensample upon his heart
Exodus28:30 And thou shalt put in the breastlap of ensample light and perfectness: that they be even upon Aaron's heart when he goeth in before the LORD and Aaron shall bear the example of the children of Israel upon his heart before the LORD alway.
Exodus28:31 And thou shalt make the tunicle unto the Ephod
Exodus28:32 And there shall be an hole for the head in the midst of it
Exodus28:33 And beneath upon the hem
Exodus28:34 and bells of gold between them round about: that there be ever a golden bell and a pomegranate
Exodus28:35 And Aaron shall have it upon him when he ministereth
Exodus28:36 And thou shalt make a plate of pure gold
Exodus28:37 and put it on a lace of Jacincth and tie it unto the mitre
Exodus28:38 upon the forefront of it
Exodus28:39 And thou shalt make an alb of byss
Exodus28:40 And thou shalt make for Aaron's sons also coats
Exodus28:41 and thou shalt put them upon Aaron thy brother and on his sons with him and shalt anoint them and fill their hands and consecrate them
Exodus28:42 And thou shalt make them linen breeches to cover their privates:(privities) from the loins unto the thighs shall they reach.
Exodus28:43 And they shall be upon Aaron and his sons
Exodus29:1 This is the thing that thou shalt do unto them when thou hallowest them to be my priests. Take one ox and two rams that are without blemish
Exodus29:2 and unleavened bread and cakes of sweet bread tempered with oil and wafers of sweet bread anointed with oil (of wheaten flour shalt thou make them)
Exodus29:3 and put them in a maund and bring them in the maund with the oil and the two rams.
Exodus29:4 And bring Aaron and his sons unto the door of the tabernacle of witness
Exodus29:5 and take the garments
Exodus29:6 And put the mitre upon his head and put the holy crown upon the mitre.
Exodus29:7 Then take the anointing oil and pour it upon his head and anoint him.
Exodus29:8 And bring his sons and put albes upon them
Exodus29:9 and girth them with girdles: as well Aaron as his sons. And put the bonnets on them that the priest's office may be theirs for a perpetual law. And fill the hands of Aaron and of his sons
Exodus29:10 and bring the ox before the tabernacle of witness. And let Aaron and his sons put their hands upon his head
Exodus29:11 and kill him before the LORD in the door of the tabernacle of witness.
Exodus29:12 And take of the blood of the ox and put it upon the horns of the altar with thy finger and pour all the blood upon the bottom of the altar
Exodus29:13 and take all the fat that covereth the inwards
Exodus29:14 But the flesh of the ox and his skin and his dung
Exodus29:15 Then take one of the rams
Exodus29:16 and cause him to be slain
Exodus29:17 and cut the ram in pieces and wash the inwards of him and his legs
Exodus29:18 and burn the whole ram upon the altar. For it is a burnt offering unto the LORD
Exodus29:19 And take the other ram and let Aaron and his sons
Exodus29:20 And take of his blood and put it upon the tip of the right ear of Aaron and of his sons
Exodus29:21 Then take of the blood that is upon the altar and of the anointing oil
Exodus29:22 Then take the fat of the ram and his rump and the fat that covereth the inwards and the caul of the liver and
Exodus29:23 and a simnel of bread and a cake of oiled bread and a wafer out of the basket of sweet bread that is before the LORD
Exodus29:24 and put all upon the hands of Aaron and on the hands of his sons: and wave them in and out a wave offering unto the LORD.
Exodus29:25 Then take it from off their hands and burn it upon the altar: even upon the burnt offering
Exodus29:26 Then take the breast of the ram that is Aaron's full offering and wave it a wave offering before the LORD
Exodus29:27 And sanctify the breast of the wave offering and the shoulder of the heave offering which is waved and heaved up of the ram which is the full offering of Aaron and of his sons.
Exodus29:28 And it shall be Aaron's and his sons' duty for ever
Exodus29:29 And the holy garments of Aaron shall be his sons' after him
Exodus29:30 And that son that is priest in his stead after him
Exodus29:31 Then take the ram that is the full offering and seethe his flesh in an holy place.
Exodus29:32 And Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of him
Exodus29:33 And they shall eat them
Exodus29:34 If ought of the flesh of the full offerings
Exodus29:35 And see thou do unto Aaron and his sons
Exodus29:36 and offer every day an ox for a sin offering for to reconcile with all. And thou shalt hallow the altar when thou reconcilest it
Exodus29:37 Seven days thou shalt reconcile the altar and sanctify it
Exodus29:38 This is that which thou shalt offer upon the altar: two lambs of one year old day by day for ever
Exodus29:39 the one thou shalt offer in the morning and the other at even.
Exodus29:40 And unto the one lamb take a tenth deal of flour mingled with the fourth part of an hin of beaten oil
Exodus29:41 And the other lamb thou shalt offer at even and shalt do thereto according to the meat offering and drink offering in the morning
Exodus29:42 And it shall be a continual burnt offering among your children after you
Exodus29:43 There I will meet with the children of Israel
Exodus29:44 And I will sanctify the tabernacle of witness and the altar: and I will sanctify also both Aaron and his sons to be my priests.
Exodus29:45 And moreover I will dwell among the children of Israel and will be their God.
Exodus29:46 And they shall know that I am the LORD their God that brought them out of the land of Egypt for to dwell among them: even I the LORD their God.
Exodus30:1 And thou shalt make an altar to burn cense therein
Exodus30:2 a cubit long
Exodus30:3 and thou shalt overlay it with fine gold both the roof and the walls round about
Exodus30:4 and two golden rings on either side
Exodus30:5 And thou shalt make the staves of sethim wood and cover them with gold.
Exodus30:6 And thou shalt put it before the vail that hangeth before the ark of witness
Exodus30:7 And Aaron shall burn thereon sweet cense every morning when he dresseth the lamps:
Exodus30:8 and likewise at even when he setteth up the lamps he shall burn cense perpetually before the LORD thorowout your generations.
Exodus30:9 Ye shall put no strange cense thereon
Exodus30:10 And Aaron shall reconcile his horns once in a year
Exodus30:11 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying:
Exodus30:12 when thou takest the sum of the children of Israel and tellest them
Exodus30:13 And thus much shall every man give that goeth in the number: half a sicle

Exodus30:15 The rich shall not pass
Exodus30:16 And thou shalt take the reconciling money of the children of Israel and shalt put it unto the use of the tabernacle of witness
Exodus30:17 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying:

Exodus30:19 that Aaron and his sons may wash both their hands and their feet thereout
Exodus30:20 when they go into the tabernacle of witness
Exodus30:21 And it shall be an ordinance for ever unto him and his seed among your children after you.

Exodus30:23 take principal spices: of pure myrrh five hundred sicles
Exodus30:24 of sweet calamite
Exodus30:25 And make of them holy anointing oil even an oil compound after the craft of the apothecary.
Exodus30:26 And anoint the tabernacle of witness therewith
Exodus30:27 and the table with all his apparel
Exodus30:28 and the altar of burnt sacrifice and all his vessels
Exodus30:29 And sacrify them that they may be most holy: so that no man twich them but they that be hallowed.
Exodus30:30 And anoint Aaron and his sons and consecrate them to minister unto me.
Exodus30:31 And thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel saying: this shall be an holy anointing oil unto me
Exodus30:32 No man's flesh shall be anointed therewith: neither shall ye make any other after the making of it for it is holy
Exodus30:33 Whosoever maketh like that
Exodus30:34 And the LORD said unto Moses: take unto thee sweet spices: stacte
Exodus30:35 and make cense of them compounded after the craft of the apothecary
Exodus30:36 And beat it to powder and put it before the witness in the tabernacle of witness
Exodus30:37 And see that ye make none after the making of that
Exodus30:38 And whosoever shall make like unto that

Exodus31:17 For it shall be a sign between me

Exodus32:8 have marred all they are turned at once out of the way which I commanded them

Exodus32:10 and now therefore suffer me that my wrath may wax hot upon them
Exodus32:11 Then Moses besought the LORD his God and said: O LORD

Exodus32:14 And the LORD refrained himself from that evil

Exodus32:24 And I said unto them: let them that have gold
