Exodus34:1 And the LORD said unto Moses: hew the two tables of stone like unto the first that I may write in them the words which were in the first two tables
Exodus34:2 And be ready against the morning that thou mayst come up early unto the mount of Sinai and stand(stode) me there upon the top of the mount.
Exodus34:3 But let no man come up with thee
Exodus34:4 And Moses hewed two tables of stone like unto the first and rose up early in the morning and went up unto the mount of Sinai as the LORD commanded him: and took in his hand the two tables of stone.
Exodus34:5 And the LORD descended in the cloud
Exodus34:6 And when the LORD walked before him
Exodus34:7 and keepest mercy in store for thousands
Exodus34:8 And Moses bowed himself to the earth quickly
Exodus34:9 and said: If I have found grace in thy sight o Lord
Exodus34:10 And he said: behold
Exodus34:11 keep all that I command thee this day
Exodus34:12 Take heed to thyself
Exodus34:13 But overthrow their altars and break their pillars
Exodus34:14 for thou shalt worship no strange god. For the LORD is called jealous
Exodus34:15 lest if thou make any agreement with the inhabiters of the land
Exodus34:16 And thou take of their daughters unto thy sons
Exodus34:17 they make thy sons go a whoring after their gods also. Thou shalt make thee no gods of metal.
Exodus34:18 The feast of sweet bread shalt thou keep
Exodus34:19 All that breaketh up the matrice shall be mine
Exodus34:20 But the first of the ass thou shalt buy out with a sheep
Exodus34:21 Six days thou shalt work
Exodus34:22 Thou shalt observe the feast of weeks with the first fruits of wheat harvest
Exodus34:23 Thrice in a year shall all your men children appear before the Lord Jehovah(Lorde Iehouah) God of Israel:
Exodus34:24 for I will cast out the nations before thee and will enlarge thy coasts
Exodus34:25 Thou shalt not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leavened bread: neither shall ought of the sacrifice of the feast of Passover
Exodus34:26 The first of the first fruits of thy land
Exodus34:27 And the LORD said unto Moses: write these words

Exodus36:4 And all the wise men that wrought all the holy work

Exodus36:8 And all the wise hearted men among them that wrought in the work of the habitation made: even ten curtains of twined byss
Exodus36:9 The length of one curtain was twenty eight cubits and the breadth four and were all of one size.

Exodus36:23 And they made twenty boards for the south side of the habitation

Exodus36:25 And for the other side of the dwelling toward the north

Exodus37:4 and made staves of Sethim wood

Exodus37:27 And he made two rings of gold unto it
Exodus37:28 and made staves of Sethim wood
Exodus37:29 And he made the holy anointing oil and the sweet pure incense after the apothecary's craft.
Exodus38:1 And he made the burnt offering altar of Sethim wood
Exodus38:2 And he made horns in the four corners of it proceeding out of it
Exodus38:3 And he made all the vessels of the altar: the cauldrons
Exodus38:4 And he made a brazen gridiron of network unto the altar round about alow beneath
Exodus38:5 so that it reached unto half the altar
Exodus38:6 And he made staves of sethim wood and covered them with brass
Exodus38:7 and put the staves in the rings along by the altar side to bear it withal
Exodus38:8 And he made the laver of brass and the foot of it also of brass
Exodus38:9 And he made the court with hangings of twined byss of an hundred cubits long upon the south side
Exodus38:10 and twenty pillars with twenty sockets of brass: but the knops of the pillars
Exodus38:11 And on the north side the hangings were an hundred cubits long with twenty pillars and twenty sockets of brass
Exodus38:12 And on the west side
Exodus38:13 And on the east side toward the son rising
Exodus38:14 the hangings of the one side of the gate were fifteen cubits long
Exodus38:15 And of the other side of the court gate
Exodus38:16 Now all the hangings of the court round about
Exodus38:17 and the sockets of the pillars were brass: but the knops and the hoops of the pillars were silver
Exodus38:18 And the hanging of the gate of the court was needlework: of jacinth
Exodus38:19 And the pillars were four with four sockets of brass
Exodus38:20 and all the pins of the tabernacle and of the court round about were brass.
Exodus38:21 This is the sum of the habitation of witness
Exodus38:22 And Bezaleel son of Uri son to Hur of the tribe of Juda
Exodus38:23 and with him Ahaliab son of Ahisamach of the tribe of Dan
Exodus38:24 All the gold that was occupied upon all the work of the holy place (which was the gold of the wave offering) was
Exodus38:25 And the sum of silver that came of the multitude
Exodus38:26 Every man offering half a sicle after the weight of the holy sicle among them that went to be numbered from twenty year old and above
Exodus38:27 And the five score hundred weight of silver went to the casting of the sockets of the sanctuary and the sockets of the vail: an hundred sockets of the five score hundred weight
Exodus38:28 And the thousand seven hundred and seventy five sicles
Exodus38:29 And the brass of the wave offering was seventy hundred weight and two thousand
Exodus38:30 And therewith he made the sockets to the door of the tabernacle of witness
Exodus38:31 and the sockets of the court round about
Exodus39:1 And of the jacinth
Exodus39:2 And they made the Ephod of gold
Exodus39:3 And they did beat the gold into thin plates
Exodus39:4 And they made the sides come together
Exodus39:5 And the broidering of the girdle that was upon it
Exodus39:6 And they wrought Onyx stones closed in ouches of gold and graved as signets are graven with the names of the children of Israel
Exodus39:7 and put them on the shoulders of the Ephod that they should be a remembrance of the children of Israel
Exodus39:8 And they made the brestlap of cunning work
Exodus39:9 And they made it four square and double
Exodus39:10 And they filled it with four rows of stones (the first row: Sardios
Exodus39:11 the second row: a Ruby
Exodus39:12 The third row: Ligurius
Exodus39:13 The fourth row: a Turquoise
Exodus39:14 And the twelve stones were graven as signets with the names of the children of Israel: every stone with his name
Exodus39:15 And they made upon the breastlap
Exodus39:16 And they made two hooks of gold and two gold rings
Exodus39:17 And they put the two chains of gold in the two rings
Exodus39:18 And the two ends of the two chains they fastened in the two hooks
Exodus39:19 And they made two other rings of gold and put them on the two other corners of the breastlap along upon the edge of it
Exodus39:20 And they made yet two other gold rings
Exodus39:21 and they strained the breastlap by his rings unto the rings of the Ephod
Exodus39:22 And he made the tunicle unto the Ephod of woven work and all together of jacinth
Exodus39:23 and the head of the tunicle was in the middest of it as the collar of a partlet
Exodus39:24 And they made beneath upon the hem of the tunicle: pomegranates of jacinth
Exodus39:25 And they made little bells of pure gold
Exodus39:26 a bell and a pomegranate round about the hems of the tunicle to minister in
Exodus39:27 And they made coats of byss of woven work for Aaron and his sons
Exodus39:28 and a mitre of byss
Exodus39:29 and a girdle of twined byss
Exodus39:30 And they made the plate of the holy crown of fine gold
Exodus39:31 and tied it to a lace of jacinth to fasten it on high(an hye) upon the mitre
Exodus39:32 Thus was all the work of the habitation of the tabernacle of witness
Exodus39:33 And they brought the habitation unto Moses: the tent and all his apparel thereof: the buttons
Exodus39:34 and the covering of rams' skins red
Exodus39:35 and the ark of witness with the staves thereof
Exodus39:36 the table and all the ordinance thereof
Exodus39:37 and the pure candlestick
Exodus39:38 and the golden altar
Exodus39:39 and the brasen altar
Exodus39:40 and the hangings of the court with his pillars and sockets
Exodus39:41 and the ministering vestments to serve in the holy place
Exodus39:42 according to all that the LORD commanded Moses: even so the children of Israel made all the work.
Exodus39:43 And Moses beheld all the work: and see
Exodus40:1 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying:
Exodus40:2 In the first day of the first month shalt thou set up the habitation of the tabernacle of witness
Exodus40:3 and put therein the ark of witness
Exodus40:4 and bring in the table and apparel it
Exodus40:5 and set the cense altar of gold before the ark of witness
Exodus40:6 And set the burnt offering altar before the door of the tabernacle of witness
Exodus40:7 and set the laver between the tabernacle of witness
Exodus40:8 and make the court round about
Exodus40:9 And take the anointing oil and anoint the habitation and all that is therein
Exodus40:10 And anoint the altar of the burnt offerings and all his vessels
Exodus40:11 And anoint also the laver and his foot
Exodus40:12 Then bring Aaron and his sons unto the door of the tabernacle of witness
Exodus40:13 And put upon Aaron the holy vestments.
Exodus40:14 And anoint him and sanctify him that he may minister unto me
Exodus40:15 that their anointing may be an everlasting priesthood unto them thorowout their generations.
Exodus40:16 And Moses did according to all that the LORD commanded him.
Exodus40:17 Thus was the tabernacle reared up the first month in the second year.
Exodus40:18 And Moses reared up the tabernacle and fastened his sockets
Exodus40:19 and spread abroad the tent over the habitation and put the covering of the tent on high(an hye) above it: as the LORD commanded Moses.
Exodus40:20 And he took and put the testimony in the ark
Exodus40:21 and brought the ark into the habitation and hanged up the vail and covered the ark of witness
Exodus40:22 And he put the table in the tabernacle of witness in the north side of the habitation without the vail
Exodus40:23 and set the bread in order before the LORD
Exodus40:24 And he put the candlestick in the tabernacle of witness over against the table in the south side of the habitation
Exodus40:25 and set up the lamps before the LORD: as the LORD commanded Moses.
Exodus40:26 And he put the golden altar in the tabernacle of witness before the vail
Exodus40:27 and brent sweet cense thereon as the LORD commanded Moses.
Exodus40:28 And set up the hanging in the door of the habitation
Exodus40:29 and set the burnt offering altar before the door of the tabernacle of witness
Exodus40:30 And he set the laver between the tabernacle of witness and the altar
Exodus40:31 And both Moses Aaron and his sons washed their hands and their feet thereat:
Exodus40:32 both when they went into the tabernacle of witness
Exodus40:33 And he reared up the court round about the habitation and the altar
Exodus40:34 And the cloud covered the tabernacle of witness
Exodus40:35 so that Moses could not enter into the tabernacle of witness
Exodus40:36 When the cloud was taken up from off the habitation
Exodus40:37 And if the cloud departed not
Exodus40:38 for the cloud of the LORD was upon the habitation by day
Leviticus1:1 And the LORD called Moses
Leviticus1:2 Speak unto the children of Israel
Leviticus1:3 If he bring a burntoffering of the oxen he shall offer a male without blemish
Leviticus1:4 And let him put his hand upon the head of the burntsacrifice
Leviticus1:5 and let him kill the ox before the LORD. And let the priests Aaron's sons bring the blood and let them sprinkle it round about upon the altar that is before the door of the tabernacle of witness.
Leviticus1:6 And let the burntofferings be stripped and hewed in pieces.
Leviticus1:7 And then let the sons of Aaron the priest put fire upon the altar
Leviticus1:8 and let them lay the pieces with the head and the fat
Leviticus1:9 But the inwards and the legs they shall wash in water
Leviticus1:10 If he will offer a burnt sacrifice of the sheep whether it be of the lambs or of the goats: he shall offer a male without blemish.
Leviticus1:11 And let him kill it on the north side of the altar
Leviticus1:12 And let it be cut in pieces: even with his head and his fat
Leviticus1:13 But let him wash the inwards and the legs with water
Leviticus1:14 If he will offer a burntoffering of the fowls (unto the LORD) he shall offer either of the turtle doves or of the young pigeons.
Leviticus1:15 And the priest shall bring it unto the altar
Leviticus1:16 and pluck away his crop and his feathers
Leviticus1:17 and break his wings
Leviticus2:1 If any soul will offer a meatoffering unto the LORD
Leviticus2:2 and shall bring it unto Aaron's sons the priests. And one of them shall take thereout his handful of the flour
Leviticus2:3 And the remnant of the meatoffering shall be Aaron's and his sons
Leviticus2:4 If any man bring a meatoffering that is baken in the oven
Leviticus2:5 If thy meatoffering be baken in the frying pan
Leviticus2:6 And thou shalt mince it small
Leviticus2:7 If thy meatoffering be a thing broiled upon the gridiron
Leviticus2:8 And thou shalt bring the meatoffering that is made of these things unto the LORD
Leviticus2:9 and shall heave up part of the meatoffering for a memorial
Leviticus2:10 And that which is left of the meatoffering shall be Aaron's and his sons
Leviticus2:11 All the meatofferings which ye shall bring unto the LORD
Leviticus2:12 Notwithstanding ye shall bring the firstlings of them unto the LORD: But they shall not come upon the altar to make a sweet savour.
Leviticus2:13 All thy meatofferings thou shalt salt with salt: neither shalt thou suffer the salt of the covenant of thy God to be lacking from thy meatoffering: but upon all thine offerings thou shalt bring salt.
Leviticus2:14 If thou offer a meatoffering of the first ripe fruits unto the LORD
Leviticus2:15 And then pour oil thereto
Leviticus2:16 And the priest shall burn part of the beaten corn and part of that oil
Leviticus3:1 If any man bring a peaceoffering of the oxen: whether it be male or female
Leviticus3:2 and let him put his hand upon the head of his offering
Leviticus3:3 And they shall offer of the peaceoffering to be a sacrifice unto the LORD: the fat that covereth the inwards and all the fat that is upon the inwards:
Leviticus3:4 and the two kidneys with the fat that lieth upon the loins: and the caul that is on the liver
Leviticus3:5 And Aaron's sons shall burn them upon the altar with the burntsacrifice which is upon the wood on the fire. That is a sacrifice of a sweet favour unto the LORD.
Leviticus3:6 If a man bring a peaceoffering unto the LORD from of the flock: whether it be male
Leviticus3:7 If he offer a lamb
Leviticus3:8 and put his hand upon his offering's head
Leviticus3:9 And of the peaceoffering they shall bring a sacrifice unto the LORD: the fat thereof and the rump altogether
Leviticus3:10 and the two kidneys with the fat that lieth upon them and upon the loins
Leviticus3:11 And the priest shall burn them upon the altar to feed the LORD's offering withall.
Leviticus3:12 If the offering be a goat
Leviticus3:13 and put his hand upon the head of it and kill it before the tabernacle of witness
Leviticus3:14 And he shall bring thereof his offering unto the LORD's sacrifice: the fat that covereth the inwards and all the fat that is upon the inwards
Leviticus3:15 and the two kidneys and the fat that lieth upon them and upon the loins
Leviticus3:16 And the priest shall burn them upon the alter to feed the LORD's sacrifice with all and to make a sweet savour. And thus shall all the fat be the LORD's
Leviticus3:17 and it shall be a law forever among your generations after you in your dwelling places: that ye eat neither fat nor blood.
Leviticus4:1 And the LORD talked with Moses saying:
Leviticus4:2 speak unto the children of Israel and say: when a soul sinneth thorow ignorance
Leviticus4:3 If the priest that is anointed sin and make the people to do amiss
Leviticus4:4 And he shall bring the ox unto the door of the tabernacle of witness before the LORD
Leviticus4:5 And the priest that is anointed shall take of the ox's blood
Leviticus4:6 and shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle thereof seven times before the LORD: even before the hanging of the holy place.
Leviticus4:7 And he shall put some of the blood upon the horns of the altar of sweet cense before the LORD which is in the tabernacle of witness
Leviticus4:8 And he shall take away all the fat of the ox that is the sinoffering: the fat that covereth the inwards and all the fat that is about them
Leviticus4:9 and the two kidneys with the fat that lieth upon them and upon the loins
Leviticus4:10 as it was taken from the ox of the peaceoffering
Leviticus4:11 But the skin of the ox and all his flesh with his head
Leviticus4:12 shall he carry altogether out of the host unto a clean place: even where the ashes are poured out
Leviticus4:13 If the whole commonalty of the children of Israel sin thorow ignorance
Leviticus4:14 and the sin which they have sinned be afterward known
Leviticus4:15 and the elders of the multitude shall put their hands upon his head before the LORD.
Leviticus4:16 And the priest that is anointed shall bring of his blood into the tabernacle of witness
Leviticus4:17 and shall dip his finger in the blood
Leviticus4:18 And shall put of the blood upon the horns of the altar which is before the LORD in the tabernacle of witness
Leviticus4:19 and shall take all his fat from him and burn it upon the altar
Leviticus4:20 and shall do with his ox as he did with the sinoffering ox. And the priest shall make an atonement for them
Leviticus4:21 And he shall bring the ox without the host
Leviticus4:22 When a lord sinneth and committeth thorow ignorance any of these things which the LORD his God hath forbidden to be done in his commandments and hath so offended:
Leviticus4:23 when his sin is shewed unto him which he hath sinned
Leviticus4:24 and lay his hand upon the head of it
Leviticus4:25 Then let the priest take of the blood of the sinoffering with his finger
Leviticus4:26 and burn all his fat upon the altar
Leviticus4:27 If one of the common people of the land sin thorow ignorance and commit any of the things which the LORD hath forbidden
Leviticus4:28 when his sin which he hath sinned is come to his knowledge
Leviticus4:29 and lay his hand upon the head of the sinoffering
Leviticus4:30 And the priest shall take of the blood with his finger
Leviticus4:31 and shall take away all his fat as the fat of the peaceofferings is taken away. And the priest shall burn it upon the altar for a sweet savour unto the LORD
Leviticus4:32 If he bring a sheep(lamb) and offer it for a sinoffering
Leviticus4:33 and lay his hand upon the head of the sinoffering
Leviticus4:34 And the priest shall take of the blood of the sinoffering with his finger
Leviticus4:35 And he shall take away all the fat thereof
Leviticus5:1 When a soul hath sinned and heard the voice of cursing and is a witness: whether he hath seen or known of it if he have not uttered it
Leviticus5:2 Either when a man toucheth any unclean thing: whether it be the carrion of an unclean beast or of unclean cattle or unclean worm and is not ware of it
Leviticus5:3 Either when he toucheth any uncleanness of man (whatsoever uncleanness it be that a man is defiled with all) and is not ware of it and afterward cometh to the knowledge of it
Leviticus5:4 Either when a soul sweareth: so that he pronounceth with his lips to do evil or to do good (whatsoever it be that a man pronounceth with an oath) and the thing be out of his mind and afterward cometh to the knowledge of it
Leviticus5:5 Then when he hath sinned in one of these things
Leviticus5:6 and shall bring his trespassoffering unto the LORD for his sin which he hath sinned. A female from the flock
Leviticus5:7 But if he be not able to bring a sheep
Leviticus5:8 And he shall bring them unto the priest
Leviticus5:9 And let him sprinkle of the blood of the sinoffering upon the side of the altar
Leviticus5:10 And let him offer the second for a burntoffering as the manner is: and so shall the priest make an atonement for him for the sin which he hath sinned
Leviticus5:11 And yet if he be not able to bring two turtle doves or two young pigeons
Leviticus5:12 And let him bring it to the priest
Leviticus5:13 And let the priest make an atonement for him for his sin (whatsoever of these he hath sinned) and it shall be forgiven. And the remnant shall be the priest's
Leviticus5:14 And the LORD communed with Moses saying:
Leviticus5:15 when a soul trespasseth and sinneth thorow ignorance in any of the holy things of the LORD
Leviticus5:16 And he shall make amends for the harm that he hath done in the holy thing
Leviticus5:17 When a soul sinneth and committeth any of these things which are forbidden to be done by the commandments of the LORD: though he wist it not
Leviticus5:18 and shall bring a ram without blemish out of the flock that is esteemed to be worth a sinoffering
Leviticus5:19 This is a trespassoffering
Leviticus6:1 And the LORD talked with Moses saying:
Leviticus6:2 when a soul sinneth and trespasseth against the LORD
Leviticus6:3 or hath found that which was lost and denieth it
Leviticus6:4 Then when he hath sinned or trespassed
Leviticus6:5 or whatsoever it be about which he hath sworn falsely
Leviticus6:6 and shall bring for his trespassoffering unto the LORD
Leviticus6:7 And the priest shall make an atonement for him before the LORD
Leviticus6:8 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying:
Leviticus6:9 Command Aaron and his sons saying: this is the law of the burntoffering. The burntoffering shall be upon the hearth of the altar all night unto the morning
Leviticus6:10 And the priest shall put on his linen alb and his linen breeches upon his flesh
Leviticus6:11 and then put off his raiment and put on other and carry the ashes out without the host unto a clean place.
Leviticus6:12 The fire that is upon the altar shall burn therein and not go out. And the priest shall put wood on the fire every morning
Leviticus6:13 The fire shall ever burn upon the altar
Leviticus6:14 This is the law of the meatoffering: Aaron's sons shall bring it before the LORD
Leviticus6:15 and one of them shall take his handful of the flour of the meatoffering and of the oil with all the frankincense which is thereon
Leviticus6:16 And the rest thereof
Leviticus6:17 Their part which I have given them of my sacrifice
Leviticus6:18 All the males among the children of Aaron
Leviticus6:19 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying:
Leviticus6:20 this is the offering of Aaron and of his sons which he shall offer unto the LORD in the day when they are anointed: the tenth part of an Epha of flour
Leviticus6:21 and in the frying pan it shall be made with oil. And when it is fried
Leviticus6:22 And that priest of his sons that is anointed in his stead
Leviticus6:23 For all the meatofferings of the priests shall be burnt altogether
Leviticus6:24 And the LORD talked with Moses saying:
Leviticus6:25 speak unto Aaron and unto his sons and say: This is the law of the sinoffering. In the place where the burntoffering is killed
Leviticus6:26 The priest that offereth it shall eat it in the holy place: even in the court of the tabernacle of witness.
Leviticus6:27 No man shall touch the flesh thereof
Leviticus6:28 and the earthen pot that it is sodden in shall be broken. If it be sodden in brass
Leviticus6:29 All the males among the children of Aaron(the priests) shall eat thereof
Leviticus6:30 Notwithstanding no sinoffering that hath his blood brought into the tabernacle of witness to reconcile with all in the holy place
Leviticus7:1 This is the law of the trespassoffering which is most holy.
Leviticus7:2 In the place where the burntoffering is killed
Leviticus7:3 And all the fat thereof shall be offered: the rump and the fat that covered the inwards
Leviticus7:4 and the two kidneys with the fat that lieth on them and upon the loins: and the caul on the liver shall be taken away with the kidneys:
Leviticus7:5 And the priest shall burn them upon the altar
Leviticus7:6 All the males among the priests shall eat thereof in the holy place
Leviticus7:7 As the sinoffering is
Leviticus7:8 And the priest that offered a man's burntoffering
Leviticus7:9 And all the meatofferings that are baken in the oven
Leviticus7:10 And all the meatofferings that are mingled with oil or dry
Leviticus7:11 This is the law of the peaceofferings which shall be offered unto the LORD.
Leviticus7:12 If he offer to give thanks
Leviticus7:13 and he shall bring his offering upon cakes made of leavened bread unto the thankoffering of his peaceofferings
Leviticus7:14 and of them all he shall offer one to be an heave offering unto the LORD
Leviticus7:15 And the flesh of the thankoffering of his peaceofferings shall be eaten the same day that it is offered
Leviticus7:16 If it be a vow or a freewill offering that he bringeth
Leviticus7:17 but as much of the offered flesh as remaineth unto the third day shall be burned with fire.
Leviticus7:18 For if any of the flesh of the peaceofferings be eaten the third day then shall he that offered it obtain no favour
Leviticus7:19 The flesh that twicheth any unclean thing shall not be eaten
Leviticus7:20 If any soul eat of the flesh of the peaceofferings
Leviticus7:21 Moreover if a soul twich any unclean thing
Leviticus7:22 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying:
Leviticus7:23 speak unto the children of Israel and say: Ye shall eat no manner fat of oxen
Leviticus7:24 neverthelater the fat of the beast that dieth alone and the fat of that which is torn with wild beasts
Leviticus7:25 For whosoever eateth the fat of the beast of which men bring an offering unto the LORD
Leviticus7:26 Moreover ye shall eat no manner of blood
Leviticus7:27 Whatsoever soul it be that eateth any manner of blood the same soul shall perish from his people.
Leviticus7:28 And the LORD talked with Moses saying:
Leviticus7:29 speak unto the children of Israel and say: He that offereth his peaceoffering unto the LORD
Leviticus7:30 his own hands shall bring the offering of the LORD: even the fat upon the breast he shall bring with the breast to wave it a waveoffering before the LORD.
Leviticus7:31 And the priest shall burn the fat upon the altar
Leviticus7:32 And the right shoulder they shall give unto the priest
Leviticus7:33 And the same that offereth the blood of the peaceofferings and the fat
Leviticus7:34 for the wavebreast and the heaveshoulder I have taken of the children of Israel
Leviticus7:35 This is the anointing of Aaron and of the sacrifices of the LORD
Leviticus7:36 which the LORD commanded to be given them in the day when he anointed them
Leviticus7:37 This is the law of burntofferings
Leviticus7:38 which the LORD commanded Moses in the mount of Sinai
Leviticus8:1 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying:
Leviticus8:2 take Aaron and his sons with him
Leviticus8:3 and gather all the community together unto the door of the tabernacle of witness.
Leviticus8:4 And Moses did as the LORD commanded him
Leviticus8:5 And Moses said unto the people: this is the thing which the LORD commanded to do.
Leviticus8:6 And Moses brought Aaron and his sons
Leviticus8:7 and put upon him the alb and gird him with a girdle
Leviticus8:8 And he put the breastlap thereon
Leviticus8:9 And he put the mitre upon his head
Leviticus8:10 And Moses took the anointing oil and anointed the habitation and all that was therein and sanctified them
Leviticus8:11 and sprinkled thereof upon the altar seven times
Leviticus8:12 And he poured of the anointing oil upon Aaron's head
Leviticus8:13 And he brought Aaron's sons and put albs upon them
Leviticus8:14 And the sinoffering was brought. And Aaron and his sons put their hands upon the head of the ox of the sinoffering.
Leviticus8:15 And when it was slain
Leviticus8:16 And he took all the fat that was upon the inwards and the caul that was on the liver and the two kidneys with their fat and burned it upon the altar.
Leviticus8:17 But the ox
Leviticus8:18 And he brought the ram of the burntoffering
Leviticus8:19 and it was killed. And Moses sprinkled the blood upon the altar round about
Leviticus8:20 and cut the ram in pieces and burnt the head
Leviticus8:21 and washed the inwards and the legs in water
Leviticus8:22 And he brought the other ram that was the fulloffering
Leviticus8:23 And when it was slain
Leviticus8:24 Then were Aaron's sons brought
Leviticus8:25 And he took the fat and the rump and all the fat that was upon the inwards
Leviticus8:26 And out of the basket of sweet bread that was before the LORD
Leviticus8:27 and put altogether upon Aaron's hands and upon his sons' hands
Leviticus8:28 And then Moses took them from of their hands again
Leviticus8:29 And Moses took the breast and waved it a waveoffering before the LORD
Leviticus8:30 And Moses took of the anointing oil and of the blood which was upon the altar
Leviticus8:31 Then Moses said unto Aaron and his sons: boil the flesh in the door of the tabernacle of witness
Leviticus8:32 and that which remaineth of the flesh and of the bread
Leviticus8:33 And see that ye depart not from the door of the tabernacle of witness seven days long: until the days of your fullofferings be at an end. For seven days must your hands be filled
Leviticus8:34 as they were this day: even so the LORD hath commanded to do
Leviticus8:35 See therefore that ye abide in the door of the tabernacle of witness day and night seven days long: and keep the watch of the LORD that ye die not: for so I am commanded.
Leviticus8:36 And Aaron and his sons did all things which the LORD commanded by the hand of Moses.
Leviticus9:1 And the eighth day Moses called Aaron and his sons
Leviticus9:2 and said unto Aaron: take a calf for a sinoffering
Leviticus9:3 And unto the children of Israel he spake saying: take ye an he goat for a sinoffering
Leviticus9:4 and an ox and a ram for peaceofferings
Leviticus9:5 And they brought that which Moses commanded unto the tabernacle of witness
Leviticus9:6 And Moses said: this is the thing which the LORD commanded that ye should do: and then the glory of the LORD shall appear unto you.
Leviticus9:7 And Moses said unto Aaron: go unto the altar and offer thy sinoffering
Leviticus9:8 And Aaron went unto the altar
Leviticus9:9 And the sons of Aaron brought the blood unto him
Leviticus9:10 And the fat and the two kidneys with the caul of the liver of the sinoffering
Leviticus9:11 but the flesh and the hide
Leviticus9:12 Afterward he slew the burntoffering
Leviticus9:13 And they brought the burntoffering unto him in pieces and the head also
Leviticus9:14 and did wash the inwards and the legs
Leviticus9:15 And then he brought the people's offering and took the goat that was the people's sinoffering
Leviticus9:16 And then brought the burntoffering and offered it as the manner was
Leviticus9:17 and brought the meatoffering and filled his hand thereof
Leviticus9:18 Then he slew the ox and the ram that were the people's peaceofferings
Leviticus9:19 and took the fat of the ox and of the ram: the rump and the fat that covereth the inwards and the kidneys and the caul of the liver:
Leviticus9:20 and put them upon the breasts and burnt it upon the altar:
Leviticus9:21 but the breasts and the right shoulders Aaron waved before the LORD
Leviticus9:22 And Aaron lift up his hand over the people and blessed them
Leviticus9:23 Then Moses and Aaron went into the tabernacle of witness and came out again and blessed the people
Leviticus9:24 And there came a fire out from before the LORD
Leviticus10:1 And Nadab and Abihu the sons of Aaron took either of them his censor
Leviticus10:2 and there went a fire out from the LORD
Leviticus10:3 Then Moses said unto Aaron: this is it that the LORD spake saying: I will be sanctified in them that come nigh me
Leviticus10:4 And Moses called Misael and Elisaphan the sons of Uriel the uncle of Aaron
Leviticus10:5 And they went to them and carried them in their albs out of the host
Leviticus10:6 And Moses said unto Aaron and unto Eleazar and Ithamar his eldest sons: uncover not your head neither rent your clothes
Leviticus10:7 But go ye not out from the door of the tabernacle of witness
Leviticus10:8 And the LORD spake unto Aaron saying:
Leviticus10:9 drink no wine nor strong drink
Leviticus10:10 that ye may put difference between holy and unholy
Leviticus10:11 and that ye may teach the children of Israel all the ordinances which the LORD hath commanded them by the hands of Moses.
Leviticus10:12 And Moses said unto Aaron and unto Eleazar and Ithamar his sons that were left: take the meatoffering that remaineth of the sacrifices of the LORD
Leviticus10:13 eat it therefore in the holy place
Leviticus10:14 And the wavebreast and heaveshoulder eat in a clean place: both thou and thy sons and thy daughters with thee. For it is thy duty
Leviticus10:15 For the heaveshoulder and the wavebreast which they bring with the sacrifices of the fat
Leviticus10:16 And Moses sought for the goat that was the sinoffering
Leviticus10:17 wherefore have ye not eaten the sinoffering in the holy place
