Numbers7:88 And all the oxen of the peaceofferings were twenty four the rams sixty the goats sixty and lambs of a year old apiece sixty and this was the dedication of the altar
Numbers7:89 And when Moses was gone into the tabernacle of witness to speak with him
Numbers8:1 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying:
Numbers8:2 speak unto Aaron and say unto him: when thou puttest on the lamps see that they light all seven upon the forefront of the candlestick.
Numbers8:3 And Aaron did even so
Numbers8:4 and the work of the candlestick was of stiff gold: both the shaft and the flowers thereof. And according unto the vision which the LORD had shewed Moses even so he made the candlestick.
Numbers8:5 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying:
Numbers8:6 take the Levites from among the children of Israel
Numbers8:7 And this do unto them when thou cleansest them
Numbers8:8 And let them take a bullock and his meatoffering
Numbers8:9 Then bring the Levites before the tabernacle of witness and gather the hole multitude of the children of Israel together.
Numbers8:10 And bring the Levites before the LORD
Numbers8:11 And let Aaron heave the Levites before the LORD
Numbers8:12 And let the Levites put their hands upon the heads of the bullocks
Numbers8:13 And make the Levites stand before Aaron and his sons
Numbers8:14 And thou shalt separate the Levites
Numbers8:15 and after that let them go and do the service of the tabernacle of witness. Cleanse them and wave them
Numbers8:16 for they are given unto me from among the children of Israel: for I have taken them unto me for all the firstborn that open any matrice among the children of Israel.
Numbers8:17 For all the firstborn among the children of Israel are mine both man and beast: because the same time that I smote the firstborn in the land of Egypt
Numbers8:18 and I have taken the Levites for all the firstborn among the children of Israel
Numbers8:19 and have given them unto Aaron and his sons from among the children of Israel
Numbers8:20 And Moses and Aaron and all the congregation of the children of Israel did unto the Levites according unto all that the LORD commanded Moses.
Numbers8:21 And the Levites purified them selves
Numbers8:22 And after that they went in to do their service in the tabernacle of witness
Numbers8:23 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying:
Numbers8:24 this shall be the manner of the Levites: from twenty five year upward they shall go in to wait upon the service in the tabernacle of witness
Numbers8:25 and at fifty they shall cease waiting upon the service thereof
Numbers8:26 but shall minister unto their brethren in the tabernacle of witness
Numbers9:1 And the LORD spake unto Moses in the wilderness of Sinai
Numbers9:2 let the children of Israel offer Passover in his season:
Numbers9:3 even the fourteenth day of this month at even they shall keep it in his season
Numbers9:4 And Moses bade the children of Israel that they should offer Passover
Numbers9:5 and they offered Passover the fourteenth day of the first month at even in the wilderness of Sinai: and did according to all that the LORD commanded Moses.
Numbers9:6 And it chanced that certain men which were defiled with a dead corpse that they might not offer Passover the same day
Numbers9:7 and said: we are defiled upon a dead corpse
Numbers9:8 And Moses said unto them: tarry
Numbers9:9 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying:
Numbers9:10 speak unto the children of Israel and say: If any man among you or your children after you be unclean by the reason of a corpse or is in the way far off
Numbers9:11 the fourteenth day of the second month at even
Numbers9:12 and let them leave none of it unto the morning nor break any bone of it. And according to all the ordinance of the Passover let them offer it.
Numbers9:13 But if a man be clean and not let in a journey
Numbers9:14 And when a stranger dwelleth among you and will offer Passover unto the LORD
Numbers9:15 And the same day that the habitation was reared up
Numbers9:16 And so it was alway
Numbers9:17 And when the cloud was taken up from of the tabernacle
Numbers9:18 At the mouth of the LORD the children of Israel journeyed
Numbers9:19 and when the cloud tarried still upon the habitation long time
Numbers9:20 If it chanced that the cloud abode any space of time upon the habitation
Numbers9:21 And if it happened that the cloud was upon the habitation from evening unto morning and was taken up in the morning
Numbers9:22 But when the cloud tarried two days or a month or a long season upon the habitation
Numbers9:23 At the mouth of the LORD they rested
Numbers10:1 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying:
Numbers10:2 Make thee two trumpets of hard(beaten) silver
Numbers10:3 When they blow with them
Numbers10:4 If but one trumpet blow only
Numbers10:5 And when ye trump the first time
Numbers10:6 And when ye trump the second time
Numbers10:7 And in gathering the congregation together
Numbers10:8 And the sons of Aaron the priests shall blow the trumpets and shall have them and it shall be a law unto you for ever and among your children after you.
Numbers10:9 And when ye shall go to war in your land against your enemies that vex you
Numbers10:10 Also when ye be merry in your feast days and in the first days of your months
Numbers10:11 And it came to pass the twentieth day of the second month in the second year
Numbers10:12 And the children of Israel took their journey out of the desert of Sinai
Numbers10:13 And the first took their journey at the mouth of the LORD
Numbers10:14 even the standard of the host of Juda removed first with their armies
Numbers10:15 And over the host of the tribe of the children of Isachar
Numbers10:16 And over the host of the tribe of the children of Zabulon
Numbers10:17 And the habitation was taken down: and the sons of Gerson and Merari went forth bearing the habitation.
Numbers10:18 Then the standard of the host of Ruben went forth with their armies
Numbers10:19 And over the host of the tribe of the children of Simeon
Numbers10:20 And over the host of the tribe of the children of Gad was Eliasaph the son of Deguel.
Numbers10:21 Then the Cahathites went forward and bare the holy things
Numbers10:22 Then the standard of the host of the children of Ephraim went forth with their armies
Numbers10:23 And over the host of the tribe of the sons of Manasse
Numbers10:24 And over the host of the tribe of the sons of BenJamin
Numbers10:25 And hindmost of all the host came the standard of the host of the children of Dan with their armies: whose captain was
Numbers10:26 And over the host of the tribe of the children of Asser
Numbers10:27 And over the host of the tribe of the children of Nephthali
Numbers10:28 of this manner were the journeys of the children of Israel
Numbers10:29 And Moses said unto Hobab the son of Raguel the Madianite
Numbers10:30 And he said unto him: I will not: but will go to mine own land and to my kindred.
Numbers10:31 And Moses said
Numbers10:32 And if thou go with us
Numbers10:33 And they departed from the mount of the LORD three days' journey
Numbers10:34 And the cloud of the LORD was over them by day
Numbers10:35 And when the ark went forth
Numbers10:36 And when the ark rested
Numbers11:1 And the people waxed unpatient
Numbers11:2 And the people cried unto Moses
Numbers11:3 And they called the name of the place Tabera because the fire of the LORD burnt among them.
Numbers11:4 And the rascal people that was among them fell a lusting. And the children of Israel also went to and wept and said: who shall give us flesh to eat?
Numbers11:5 we remember the fish which we should eat in Egypt for nought
Numbers11:6 But now our souls are dried away
Numbers11:7 The Manna was as it had been coriander seed
Numbers11:8 And the people went about and gathered it
Numbers11:9 And when the dew fell about the host in the night
Numbers11:10 And when Moses heard the people weep in their households every man in the door of his tent
Numbers11:11 And Moses said unto the LORD: wherefore dealest thou so cruelly with thy servant? wherefore do I not find favour in thy sight
Numbers11:12 have I conceived all this people
Numbers11:13 where should I have flesh to give unto all this people? For they weep unto me saying: give us flesh that we may eat.
Numbers11:14 I am not able to bear all this people alone
Numbers11:15 Wherefore if thou deal thus with me
Numbers11:16 And the LORD said unto Moses: gather unto me seventy of the elders of Israel
Numbers11:17 And I will come down and talk with thee there
Numbers11:18 And say unto the people: hallow your selves against tomorrow
Numbers11:19 Ye shall not eat one day only either two or five days
Numbers11:20 but even a month long
Numbers11:21 And Moses said: six hundred thousand footmen are there of the people
Numbers11:22 Shall the sheep and the oxen be slain for them to find them
Numbers11:23 And the LORD(Lorde) said unto Moses: is the LORD's(lordes) hand waxed short? Thou shalt see whether my word shall come to pass unto thee or not.
Numbers11:24 And Moses went out and told the people the saying of the LORD
Numbers11:25 And the LORD came down in a cloud and spake unto him
Numbers11:26 But there remained two of the men in the host: the one called Eldad
Numbers11:27 And there ran a young man and told Moses and said: Eldad and Medad do prophesy in the host.
Numbers11:28 And Josua the son of Nun the servant of Moses which he had chosen out
Numbers11:29 And Moses said unto him: enviest thou for my sake? would God that all the LORD's people could prophesy
Numbers11:30 And then both Moses and the elders of Israel
Numbers11:31 And there went forth a wind from the LORD and brought quails from the sea and let them fall about the host
Numbers11:32 And the people stood up all that night and on the morrow
Numbers11:33 And while the flesh was yet between their teeth
Numbers11:34 And they called the name of the place
Numbers11:35 And the people took their journey from the graves of lust(kibrath hathavah) unto Hazeroth
Numbers12:1 And Miriam and Aaron spake against Moses
Numbers12:2 And they said: doth the LORD speak only thorow Moses? doth he not speak also by us? And the LORD heard it.
Numbers12:3 But Moses was a very meek man above all the men of the earth.
Numbers12:4 And the LORD spake at once unto Moses unto Aaron and Miriam: come out ye three unto the tabernacle of witness: and they came out all three.
Numbers12:5 And the LORD came down in the pillar of the cloud and stood in the door of the tabernacle and called Aaron and Miriam. And they went out both of them.
Numbers12:6 And he said: hear my words. If there be a prophet of the LORD's among you
Numbers12:7 But my servant Moses is not so
Numbers12:8 Unto him I speak mouth to mouth and he seeth the sight and the fashion of the LORD

Numbers14:3 Wherefore hath the LORD brought us unto this land to fall upon the sword
Numbers14:4 And they said one to another: let us make a captain and return unto Egypt again.
Numbers14:5 And Moses and Aaron fell on their faces before all the congregation of the multitude of the children of Israel.
Numbers14:6 And Josua the son of Nun
Numbers14:7 and spake unto all the company of the children of Israel saying: The land which we walked thorow to search it
Numbers14:8 If the LORD have lust to us
Numbers14:9 But in any wise rebel not against the LORD. Moreover fear ye not the people of the land
Numbers14:10 And all the whole multitude bade stone them with stones. But the glory of the LORD appeared in the tabernacle of witness

Numbers14:16 because the LORD was not able to bring in this people into the land which he swore unto them
Numbers14:17 So now let the power of my Lord(LORDE) be great
Numbers14:18 the LORD is long yer he be angry
Numbers14:19 be merciful I beseech ye therefore
Numbers14:20 And the LORD said: I have forgiven it
Numbers14:21 But as truly as I live

Numbers14:24 But my servant Caleb

Numbers14:27 how long shall this evil multitude murmur against me? I have heard the murmurings of the children of Israel which they murmur against me.

Numbers14:34 after the number of the days in which ye searched out the land forty days
Numbers14:35 I the LORD have said that I will do it unto all this evil congregation that are gathered together against me: even in this wilderness ye shall be consumed

Numbers15:4 Then
Numbers15:5 and the fourth part of an hin of wine for a drinkoffering
Numbers15:6 And unto a ram thou shalt offer a meatoffering of two tenth deals of flour
Numbers15:7 and to a drinkoffering thou shalt offer the third part of an hin of wine
Numbers15:8 When thou offerest an ox to a burntoffering or in any special vow or peaceoffering unto the LORD
Numbers15:9 then thou shalt bring unto an ox
Numbers15:10 And thou shalt bring for a drinkoffering half an hin of wine
Numbers15:11 This is the manner that shall be done unto one ox
Numbers15:12 And according to the number of such offerings
Numbers15:13 All that are of your selves shall do these things after this manner
Numbers15:14 And if there be a stranger with you or be among you in your generations
Numbers15:15 One ordinance shall serve both for you of the congregation
Numbers15:16 One law and one manner shall serve

Numbers15:19 then when ye will eat of the bread of the land

Numbers15:21 Of the first of your dough ye must give unto the LORD an heaveoffering
Numbers15:22 If ye oversee your selves and observe not all these commandments which the LORD hath spoken unto Moses
Numbers15:23 and all that the LORD hath commanded you by the hand of Moses
Numbers15:24 when ought is committed ignorantly before the eyes of the congregation
Numbers15:25 And the priest shall make an atonement for all the multitude of the children of Israel
Numbers15:26 And it shall be forgiven unto all the multitude of the children of Israel

Numbers15:28 And the priest shall make an atonement for the soul that sinned ignorantly with the sinoffering before the LORD and reconcile him
Numbers15:29 And both thou that art born one of the children of Israel and the stranger that dwelleth among you shall have both one law

Numbers16:6 This do: take firepans
Numbers16:7 and do fire therein and put cense thereto before the LORD tomorrow: And then whomsoever the LORD doeth chose

Numbers16:9 Seemeth it but a small thing unto you

Numbers16:34 And all Israel that were about them

Numbers16:39 And Eleazar the priest took the brazen censers which they that were burnt had offered

Numbers19:8 And he that burneth her

Numbers19:10 And let him that gathereth the ashes of the cow
Numbers19:11 He that twicheth any dead person
Numbers19:12 And he shall purify himself with the ashes the third day and then he shall be clean the seventh day. And if he purify not himself the third day
Numbers19:13 Whosoever twicheth any person that dieth and sprinkleth not himself
Numbers19:14 This is the law of the man that dieth in a tent: all that come into the tent and all that is in the tent
Numbers19:15 And all the vessels that be open which have no lid nor covering upon them
Numbers19:16 And whosoever twicheth one that is slain with a sword in the fields
Numbers19:17 And they shall take for an unclean person
Numbers19:18 And a clean person shall take Hyssop and dip it in the water
Numbers19:19 And the clean person shall sprinkle upon the unclean the third day and the seventh day. And the seventh day he shall purify himself and wash his clothes and bathe himself in water
Numbers19:20 If any be unclean and sprinkle not himself
Numbers19:21 And he that twicheth the sprinkling water
Numbers19:22 And whatsoever the unclean person twicheth

Numbers23:13 And Balac said unto him: Come I pray thee with me unto another place
Numbers23:14 And he brought him into a plain field where men might see far
Numbers23:15 And he said unto Balac: stand here by thy sacrifice while I go yonder.
Numbers23:16 And the LORD met Balam and put words in his mouth and said: go again unto Balac and thus say.
Numbers23:17 And when he came to him: behold
Numbers23:18 And he took up his parable and said: rise up Balac and hear
Numbers23:19 The Lord(God) is not a man
Numbers23:20 behold
Numbers23:21 He beheld no wickedness in Jacob nor saw Idolatry in Israel: The LORD his God is with him
Numbers23:22 God that brought them out of Egypt
Numbers23:23 for there is no sorcerer
Numbers23:24 Behold
Numbers23:25 And Balac said unto Balam: neither curse them nor bless them.
Numbers23:26 And Balam answered and said unto Balac: told not I thee saying
Numbers23:27 And Balac said unto Balam: come I pray thee
Numbers23:28 And Balac brought Balam unto the top of Peor
Numbers23:29 And Balam said unto Balac: make me here seven altars
Numbers23:30 And Balac did as Balam had said
Numbers24:1 When Balam saw that it pleased the LORD that he should bless Israel
Numbers24:2 and lift up his eyes and looked upon Israel as he lay with his tribes
Numbers24:3 And he took up his parable and said: Balam the son of Beor hath said
Numbers24:4 and the man whose eye is open hath said: he hath said which heareth the words of God and seeth the visions of the almighty
Numbers24:5 How goodly are the tents of Jacob and thine habitations Israel
Numbers24:6 even as the broad valleys and as gardens by the river's side
Numbers24:7 The water shall flow out of his bucket and his seed shall be many waters
Numbers24:8 God that brought him out of Egypt is as the strength of an unicorn unto him
Numbers24:9 He couched himself and lay down as a lion and as a lioness
Numbers24:10 And Balac was wroth with Balam and smote his hands together
Numbers24:11 and now get thee quickly unto thy place. I thought that I would promote thee unto honour
Numbers24:12 And Balam said unto Balac: told I not thy messengers which thou sentest unto me saying:
Numbers24:13 If Balac would give me his house full of silver and gold
Numbers24:14 And now behold
Numbers24:15 And he began his parable and said: Balam the son of Beor hath said
Numbers24:16 and he hath said that heareth the words of God and hath the knowledge of the most high and beholdeth the vision of the almighty
Numbers24:17 I see him but not now
Numbers24:18 And Edom shall be his possession
Numbers24:19 And out of Jacob shall come he that shall destroy the remnant of the cities.
Numbers24:20 And he looked on Amalek and began his parable and said: Amalek is the first of the nations
Numbers24:21 And he looked on the Kenites
Numbers24:22 Neverthelater thou shalt be a burning to Kain
Numbers24:23 And he took his parable and said: Alas
Numbers24:24 The ships shall come out of the coast of Cittim and subdue Assur and subdue Eber
Numbers24:25 And Balam rose up and went and dwelt in his place: and Balac also went his way.
Numbers25:1 And Israel dwelt in Sittim
Numbers25:2 which called the people unto the sacrifice of their gods. And the people ate and worshipped their gods
Numbers25:3 and Israel coupled himself unto Baal Peor. Then the LORD was angry with Israel
Numbers25:4 and said unto Moses: take all the heads of the people
Numbers25:5 And Moses said unto the judges of Israel: go and slay those men that joined them selves unto Baal Peor.
Numbers25:6 And behold
Numbers25:7 And when Phineas the son of Eleazer the son of Aaron the priest saw it

Numbers25:11 Phineas the son of Eleazer the son of Aaron the priest
Numbers25:12 Wherefore say: behold
Numbers25:13 and he shall have it and his seed after him

Numbers27:1 And the daughters of Zelaphead the son of Heber the son of Gilead

Numbers27:3 our father died in the wilderness
Numbers27:4 Wherefore should the name of our father's be taken away from among his kindred
Numbers27:5 And Moses brought their cause before the LORD.
Numbers27:6 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying:
Numbers27:7 The daughters of Zelaphead speak right: thou shalt give them a possession to inherit among their father's brethren
Numbers27:8 And speak unto the children of Israel saying: If a man die and have no son
Numbers27:9 If he have no daughter
Numbers27:10 If he have no brethren
Numbers27:11 If he have no father's brethren

Numbers28:1 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying:
Numbers28:2 give the children of Israel a charge and say unto them
Numbers28:3 And say unto them. This is the offering which ye shall offer unto the LORD: two lambs of a year old without spot day by day to be a burntoffering perpetually.
Numbers28:4 One lamb thou shalt offer in the morning
Numbers28:5 And thereto the tenth part of an Epha of flour for a meatoffering mingled with beaten oil
Numbers28:6 which is a daily offering ordained in the mount Sinai unto a sweet savour in the sacrifice of the LORD.
Numbers28:7 And the drinkoffering of the same: the fourth part of an hin unto one lamb
Numbers28:8 And the other lamb thou shalt offer at even
Numbers28:9 And on the Sabbath day two lambs of a year old apiece and without spot
Numbers28:10 This is the burntoffering of every Sabbath
Numbers28:11 And in the first day of your months
Numbers28:12 and three tenth deals of flour for a meatoffering mingled with oil unto one bullock
Numbers28:13 And ever more
Numbers28:14 And their drink offerings shall be half an hin of wine unto one bullock
Numbers28:15 and one he goat for a sinoffering unto the LORD
Numbers28:16 And the fourteenth day of the first month shall be passover unto the LORD.
Numbers28:17 And the fifteenth day of the same month shall be a feast
Numbers28:18 The first day shall be an holy feast
Numbers28:19 And ye shall offer a burntoffering unto the LORD two bullocks
Numbers28:20 and their meatoffering of flour mingled with oil three tenth deals unto a bullock
Numbers28:21 and ever more one tenth deal unto a lamb
Numbers28:22 and an he goat for a sinoffering to make an atonement for you.
Numbers28:23 And ye shall offer these
Numbers28:24 And after this manner ye shall offer thorowout the seven days
Numbers28:25 And the seventh day shall be an holy feast unto you
Numbers28:26 And the day of your first fruits when ye bring a new meatoffering unto the LORD in your weeks
Numbers28:27 so that ye shall do no labourious work therein. And ye shall offer a burntoffering of a sweet savour unto the LORD: two young bullocks
Numbers28:28 with their meatofferings of flour mingled with oil three tenth deals unto a bullock two tenth deals to a ram
Numbers28:29 and ever more one tenth deal unto a lamb thorowout the seven lambs
Numbers28:30 and an he goat to make an atonement for you.
Numbers28:31 And this ye shall do besides the daily burntoffering
Numbers29:1 Also(and) the first day of the seventh month shall be an holy feast unto you
Numbers29:2 And ye shall offer a burntoffering of a sweet savour unto the LORD: one young bullock and one ram and seven lambs of a year old apiece that are pure.
Numbers29:3 And their meatofferings of flour mingled with oil: three tenth deals unto the bullock
Numbers29:4 and one tenth deal unto one lamb thorow the seven lambs.
Numbers29:5 And an he goat for a sinoffering to make an atonement for you
Numbers29:6 beside the burntoffering of the month and his meatoffering and beside the daily burntoffering and his meatoffering
Numbers29:7 And the tenth day of that same seventh month shall be an holy feast unto you
Numbers29:8 And ye shall offer a burntoffering unto the LORD of a sweet savour: one bullock
Numbers29:9 and their meatofferings of flour mingled with oil: three tenth deals to a bullock
Numbers29:10 and always a tenth deal unto a lamb
Numbers29:11 And one he goat for a sinoffering
Numbers29:12 And the fifteenth day of the seventh month shall be holy day and ye shall do no laborious work therein
Numbers29:13 And ye shall offer a burntoffering of a sweet savour unto the LORD: thirteen bullocks two rams and fourteen lambs which are yearlings and pure
Numbers29:14 with oil
Numbers29:15 and one tenth deal unto each of the fourteen lambs.
Numbers29:16 And one he goat unto a sinoffering
Numbers29:17 And the second day twelve young bullocks
Numbers29:18 and their meatofferings and drinkofferings unto the bullocks
Numbers29:19 And an he goat for a sinoffering
Numbers29:20 And the third day eleven bullocks two rams and fourteen yearling lambs without spot:
Numbers29:21 and their meat and drinkofferings unto the bullocks
Numbers29:22 And an he goat for a sinoffering
Numbers29:23 And the fourth day ten bullocks two rams and fourteen lambs
Numbers29:24 and their meat and drinkofferings unto the bullocks
Numbers29:25 And an he goat for a sinoffering
Numbers29:26 And the fifth day nine bullocks two rams and fourteen lambs of one year old apiece without spot.
Numbers29:27 And their meat and drinkofferings unto the bullocks
Numbers29:28 And an he goat for a sinoffering
Numbers29:29 And the sixth day eight bullocks two rams and fourteen yearling lambs without spot.
Numbers29:30 And their meat and drinkofferings unto the bullocks
Numbers29:31 And an he goat for a sinoffering
Numbers29:32 And the seventh day seven bullocks two rams and fourteen lambs that are yearlings and pure.
Numbers29:33 And their meat and drinkofferings unto the bullocks
Numbers29:34 And an hegoat for a sinoffering
Numbers29:35 And the eighth day shall be the conclusion of the feast unto you
Numbers29:36 And ye shall offer a burntoffering of a sweet savour unto the LORD: one bullock
Numbers29:37 And the meat and drinkofferings unto the bullock
Numbers29:38 And an he goat for a sinoffering beside the daily burntoffering and his meat and drinkofferings.
Numbers29:39 These things ye shall do unto the LORD in your feasts: beside your vows and freewill offerings
Numbers29:40 And Moses told the children of Israel
Numbers30:1 And Moses spake unto the heads of the tribes of the children of Israel saying: this is the thing which the LORD commandeth.
Numbers30:2 If a man vow a vow unto the LORD or swear an oath and bind his soul
Numbers30:3 If a damsel vow a vow unto the LORD and bind herself being in her father's house and unmarried:
Numbers30:4 If her father hear her vow and bond which she hath made upon her soul
Numbers30:5 But and if her father forbid her the same day that he heareth it
Numbers30:6 If she had an husband when she vowed or pronounced ought out of her lips wherewith she bound her soul
Numbers30:7 and her husband heard it and held his peace thereat the same day he heard it: Then her vows and her bonds wherewith she bound her soul
Numbers30:8 But and if her husband forbade her the same day that he heard it
Numbers30:9 The vow of a widow and of her that is divorced
Numbers30:10 If she vowed in her husband's house or bound her soul with an oath
Numbers30:11 and her husband heard it and held his peace and forbade her not: then all her vows and bonds wherewith she bound her soul
Numbers30:12 But if her husband disannulled them the same day that he heard them
Numbers30:13 All vows and oaths that bind to humble the soul
Numbers30:14 But if her husband hold his peace from one day unto another
Numbers30:15 And if he afterward break them
Numbers30:16 These are the ordinances which the LORD commanded Moses
Numbers31:1 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying:
Numbers31:2 avenge the children of Israel of the Madianites
Numbers31:3 And Moses spake unto the folk saying: Harness some of you unto war
Numbers31:4 Ye shall send unto the war a thousand of every tribe thorowout all the tribes of Israel.
Numbers31:5 And there were taken out of the thousands of Israel twelve thousand prepared unto war
Numbers31:6 And Moses sent them a thousand of every tribe
Numbers31:7 And they warred against the Madianites
Numbers31:8 And they slew the kings of Madian among other that were slain: Eui
Numbers31:9 And the children of Israel took all the women of Madian prisoners and their children
Numbers31:10 And they burnt all their cities wherein they dwelt
Numbers31:11 And they took all the spoil and all they could catch
Numbers31:12 And they brought the captives and that which they had taken and all the spoil unto Moses and Eleazer the priest and unto the company of the children of Israel
Numbers31:13 And Moses and Eleazer the priest and all the lords of the congregation went out of the host against them.
Numbers31:14 And Moses was angry with the officers of the host
Numbers31:15 and said unto them: Have ye saved the women alive?
Numbers31:16 behold
Numbers31:17 Now therefore slay all the men children and the women that have lien with men fleshly:
Numbers31:18 But all the women children that have not lien with men
Numbers31:19 And lodge without the host seven days all that have killed any person and all that have twiched any dead body
Numbers31:20 And sprinkle all your raiments and all that is made of skins
Numbers31:21 And Eleazer the priest said unto all the men of war which went out to battle: this is the ordinance of the law which the LORD commanded Moses:
Numbers31:22 Gold
Numbers31:23 and all that may abide the fire
Numbers31:24 And wash your clothes the seventh day
Numbers31:25 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying:
Numbers31:26 take the sum of the prey that was taken
Numbers31:27 And divide it into two parts
Numbers31:28 And take a portion unto the LORD of the men of war which went out to battle one of five hundred
Numbers31:29 and ye shall take it of their half and give it unto Eleazer the priest
Numbers31:30 And of the half of the children of Israel
Numbers31:31 And Moses and Eleazer the priest did as the LORD commanded Moses.
Numbers31:32 And the booty and the prey which the men of war had caught
Numbers31:33 and seventy two thousand oxen:
Numbers31:34 and sixty one thousand asses:
Numbers31:35 and thirty two thousand women that had lien by no man.
Numbers31:36 And the half which was the part of them that went out to war
Numbers31:37 And the LORD's part of the sheep was six hundred and seventy five.
Numbers31:38 And the oxen were thirty six thousand
Numbers31:39 And the asses were thirty thousand and five hundred
Numbers31:40 And the women were sixteen thousand
Numbers31:41 And Moses gave that sum which was the LORD's heave offering unto Eleazer the priest: as the LORD commanded Moses.
Numbers31:42 And the other half of the children of Israel which Moses separated from the men of war
Numbers31:43 (that is to wete
Numbers31:44 and thirty six thousand oxen:
Numbers31:45 and thirty thousand asses and five hundred:
Numbers31:46 and sixteen thousand women.
Numbers31:47 And Moses took of this half that pertained unto the children of Israel: one of every fifty
Numbers31:48 And the officers of thousands of the host
Numbers31:49 Thy servants have taken the sum of the men of war
Numbers31:50 We have therefore brought a present unto the LORD what every man found of Jewels of gold
Numbers31:51 And Moses and Eleazer took the gold of them: Jewels of all manner fashions.
Numbers31:52 And all the gold of the heave offering of the LORD
Numbers31:53 which(for) the men of war had spoiled
Numbers31:54 And Moses and Eleazer the priest took the gold of the captains over the thousands and over the hundreds
Numbers32:1 The children of Ruben and the children of Gad
Numbers32:2 they came and spake unto Moses and Eleazer the priest and unto the lords of the congregation saying:
Numbers32:3 The land of Ataroth
Numbers32:4 which country the LORD smote before the congregation of Israel: is a land for cattle and we thy servants have cattle
Numbers32:5 wherefore (said they) if we have found grace in thy sight
Numbers32:6 And Moses said unto the children of Gad and of Ruben: shall your brethren go to war and ye tarry here?
Numbers32:7 Wherefore discourage ye the hearts of the children of Israel for to go over into the land which the LORD hath given them?
Numbers32:8 This did your fathers
Numbers32:9 And they went up even unto the river of Escol and saw the land

Numbers32:13 And the LORD was angry with Israel

Numbers33:1 These are the journeys of the children of Israel which went out of the land of Egypt with their armies under Moses and Aaron.
Numbers33:2 And Moses wrote their going out by their journeys at the commandment of the LORD: even these are the journeys of their going out.
Numbers33:3 The children of Israel departed from Rameses the fifteenth day of the first month
Numbers33:4 while the Egyptians buried all their firstborn which the LORD had smoten among them. And upon their gods also the LORD did execution.
Numbers33:5 And the children of Israel removed from Rameses and pitched in Sucoth.
Numbers33:6 And they departed from Sucoth and pitched their tents in Ethan
Numbers33:7 And they removed from Ethan and turned unto the entering of Hiroth which is before Baal Zephon
Numbers33:8 And they departed from before Hiroth and went thorow the midst of the sea into the wilderness
Numbers33:9 And they removed from Marah and went unto Elim where were twelve fountains and seventy date(palm) trees and they pitched there.
Numbers33:10 And they removed from Elim and lay fast by the red sea.
Numbers33:11 And they removed from the red sea and lay in the wilderness of Zin.
Numbers33:12 And they took their journey out of the wilderness of Zin

Numbers33:14 And they removed from Alus
Numbers33:15 And they departed from Raphedim
Numbers33:16 And they removed from the desert of Sinai

Numbers33:23 And they went from Kehelatha
Numbers33:24 And they removed from mount Sapher
Numbers33:25 And they removed from Harada
Numbers33:26 And they removed from Makeheloth
Numbers33:27 and they departed from Tahath and pitched at Tharath.

Numbers33:50 And the LORD spake unto Moses in the fields of Moab by Jordan nigh unto Jericho
Numbers33:51 speak unto the children of Israel and say unto them: when ye are come over Jordan into the land of Canaan
Numbers33:52 see that ye drive out all the inhabiters of the land before you
Numbers33:53 And possess the land and dwell therein
Numbers33:54 And ye shall divide the inheritance of the land by lot among your kindreds
Numbers33:55 But and if ye will not drive out the inhabiters of the land before you
Numbers33:56 Moreover it will come to pass
Numbers34:1 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying:
Numbers34:2 command the children of Israel and say unto them: when ye come into the land of Canaan
Numbers34:3 And your south quarter shall be from the wilderness of Zin along by the coast of Edom
Numbers34:4 and shall fetch a compass from the south up to Acrabim
Numbers34:5 And shall fetch a compass from Azmon unto the river of Egypt
Numbers34:6 And your west quarter shall be the great sea
Numbers34:7 And this shall be your north quarter: ye shall compass from the great sea unto mount Hor.
Numbers34:8 And from mount Hor
Numbers34:9 and the coast shall reach out to Ziphron and go out at Hazor enan. And this shall be your north quarter.
Numbers34:10 And ye shall compass your east quarter from Hazar Enan to Sepham.
Numbers34:11 And the coast shall go down from Sepham to Ribla on the east side of Ain. And then descend and go out at the side of the sea of Cenereth eastward.
Numbers34:12 And then go down along by Jordan
Numbers34:13 And Moses commanded the children of Israel
Numbers34:14 for the tribe of the children of Ruben
Numbers34:15 that is to wete two tribes and a half have received their inheritance on the other side of Jordan by Jericho eastward
Numbers34:16 And the LORD spake to Moses saying:
Numbers34:17 These are the names of the men
Numbers34:18 And ye shall take also a lord of every tribe to divide the land
Numbers34:19 whose names are these: in the tribe of Juda
Numbers34:20 And in the tribe of the children of Simeon
Numbers34:21 and in the tribe of BenJamin
Numbers34:22 And in the tribe of the children of Dan
Numbers34:23 And among the children of Joseph: in the tribe of the children of Manasse
Numbers34:24 And in the tribe of the children of Ephraim
Numbers34:25 And in the tribe of the sons of Zabulon
Numbers34:26 And in the tribe of the children of Isachar
Numbers34:27 And in the tribe of the sons of Aser
Numbers34:28 And in the tribe of the children of Naphthali

Numbers35:1 And the LORD spake unto Moses in the fields of Moab by Jordan Jericho saying:
Numbers35:2 command the children of Israel
Numbers35:3 The cities shall be for them to dwell in
Numbers35:4 And the suburbs of the cities which ye shall give unto the Levites
Numbers35:5 And ye shall measure without the city
Numbers35:6 And among the cities which ye shall give unto the Levites
Numbers35:7 so that all the cities which ye shall give the Levites shall be forty eight with their suburbs.
Numbers35:8 And of the cities which ye shall give out of the possessions of the children of Israel
Numbers35:9 And the LORD spake unto Moses saying:
Numbers35:10 speak unto the children of Israel and say unto them: when ye be come over Jordan into the land of Canaan
Numbers35:11 ye shall build cities which shall be privileged towns for you: that he which slayeth a man unawares
Numbers35:12 And the cities shall be to flee from the executer of blood
Numbers35:13 And of these six free cities
Numbers35:14 which ye shall give three ye shall give on this side Jordan and three in the land of Canaan.
Numbers35:15 And these six free cities shall be for the children of Israel and for the stranger and for him that dwelleth among you
Numbers35:16 If any man smite another with a weapon of iron that he die
Numbers35:17 If he smite him with a throwing stone that he die therewith
Numbers35:18 If he smite him with a hand weapon of wood that he die therewith
Numbers35:19 The judge(justice) of blood shall slay the murderer
Numbers35:20 If he thrust him of hate
Numbers35:21 or smite him with his hand of envy that he die
Numbers35:22 But and if he pushed him by chance and not of hate or cast at him with any manner of thing and not of laying of wait:
Numbers35:23 or cast any manner of stone at him that he die therewith
Numbers35:24 Then the congregation shall judge between the slayer and the executer of blood in such cases.
Numbers35:25 And the congregation shall deliver the slayer out of the hand of the judge of blood
Numbers35:26 But and if he came without the borders of his privileged city whither he was fled
Numbers35:27 if the blood avenger find him without the borders of his free town
Numbers35:28 because he should have bidden in his free town until the death of the high priest
Numbers35:29 And this shall be an ordinance and a law unto you
Numbers35:30 Whosoever slayeth
Numbers35:31 Moreover ye shall take none amends for the life of the murderer which is worthy to die: But he shall be put to death.
Numbers35:32 Also ye shall take none atonement for him that is fled to a free city
Numbers35:33 And see that ye pollute not the land which ye are in
Numbers35:34 Defile not therefore the land which ye inhabit

Numbers36:2 and said: The LORD commanded my lord to give the land to inherit by lot to the children of Israel. And then my lord commanded in the name of the LORD to give the inheritance of Zelaphead our brother unto his daughters.
Numbers36:3 Now when any of the sons of the tribes of Israel take them to wives
Numbers36:4 And when the free year(of jubilee) cometh unto the children of Israel
Numbers36:5 And Moses commanded the children of Israel at the mouth of the LORD saying: the tribe of the children of Joseph have said well.
Numbers36:6 This therefore doeth the LORD: command the daughters of Zelaphead saying: let them be wives to whom they themself think best
Numbers36:7 that the inheritance of the children of Israel roll not from tribe to tribe. But that the children of Israel may abide
Numbers36:8 And every daughter that possesseth any inheritance among the tribes of the children of Israel
Numbers36:9 and that the inheritance go not from one tribe to another: but that the tribes of the children of Israel
Numbers36:10 And as the LORD commanded Moses even so did the daughters of Zelaphead:
Numbers36:11 Mahela
Numbers36:12 of the kindred of the children of Manasse the son of Joseph: and so they had their inheritance in the tribe of the kindred of their father.

Deuteronomy1:1 These be the words which Moses spake unto all Israel
Deuteronomy1:2 twelve days journey from Horeb unto Cades barne
Deuteronomy1:3 And it fortuned the first day of the eleventh month in the fortieth year
Deuteronomy1:4 after that he had smote Sehon the king of the Amorites which dwelt in Hesbon
Deuteronomy1:5 On the other side Jordan in the land of Moab
Deuteronomy1:6 the LORD our God spake unto us in Horeb saying: Ye have dwelt long enough in this mount:
Deuteronomy1:7 depart therefore and take your journey and go unto the hills of the Amorites and unto all places nigh there unto: both fields
Deuteronomy1:8 Behold
Deuteronomy1:9 And I said unto you the same season: I am not able to bear you myself alone.
Deuteronomy1:10 For the LORD your God hath multiplied you: so that ye are this day as the stars of heaven in number
Deuteronomy1:11 (the LORD God of your fathers make you a thousand times so many more as ye are
Deuteronomy1:12 how (said I) can I myself alone
Deuteronomy1:13 bring therefore men of wisdom and of understanding and expert
Deuteronomy1:14 And ye answered me and said: that which thou hast spoken is good to be done.
Deuteronomy1:15 And then I took the heads of your tribes
Deuteronomy1:16 And I charged your judges the same time saying: hear your brethren and judge righteously between every man and his brother and the stranger that is with him.
Deuteronomy1:17 See that ye know no man in judgement: but hear the small as well as the great and be afraid of no man
Deuteronomy1:18 And I commanded you the same season
Deuteronomy1:19 And then we departed from Horeb and walked thorow all that great and terrible wilderness as ye have seen along by the way that leadeth unto the hills of the Amorites
Deuteronomy1:20 And there I said unto you: Ye are come unto the hills of the Amorites
Deuteronomy1:21 Behold the LORD thy God hath set the land before thee
Deuteronomy1:22 And then ye came unto me every one and said: Let us send men before us
Deuteronomy1:23 And the saying pleased me well
Deuteronomy1:24 And they departed and went up into the high country and came unto the river Escol
Deuteronomy1:25 and took of the fruit of the land in their hands and brought it down unto us and brought us word again and said: it is a good land which the LORD our God doeth give us.
Deuteronomy1:26 Notwithstanding ye would not consent to go up
Deuteronomy1:27 and murmured in your tents and said: because the LORD hateth us
Deuteronomy1:28 How shall we go up? Our brethren have discouraged our hearts saying: the people is greater and taller than we
Deuteronomy1:29 And I said unto you: dread not nor be afraid of them:
Deuteronomy1:30 The LORD your God which goeth before you
Deuteronomy1:31 and in the wilderness: as thou hast seen how that the LORD thy God bare thee as a man should bear his son
Deuteronomy1:32 And yet for all this saying ye did not believe the LORD your God
Deuteronomy1:33 which goeth the way before you
Deuteronomy1:34 And the LORD heard the voice of your words and was wroth and swore saying
Deuteronomy1:35 there shall not one of these men of this froward generation see that good land which I sware to give unto your fathers
Deuteronomy1:36 save Caleb the son of Jephune
Deuteronomy1:37 Likewise the LORD was angry with me for your sakes saying: thou also shalt not go in thither.
Deuteronomy1:38 But Josua the son of Nun which standeth before thee
Deuteronomy1:39 Moreover your children which ye said should be a prey
Deuteronomy1:40 But as for you
Deuteronomy1:41 Then ye answered and said unto me: We have sinned against the LORD: we will go up and fight
Deuteronomy1:42 the LORD said unto me: say unto them
Deuteronomy1:43 And when I told you ye would not hear: but disobeyed the mouth of the LORD
Deuteronomy1:44 Then the Amorites which dwelt in those hills
Deuteronomy1:45 And ye came again and wept before the LORD: but the LORD would not hear your voice nor give you audience.
Deuteronomy1:46 And so ye abode in Cades a long season
Deuteronomy2:1 Then we turned and took our journey into the wilderness
Deuteronomy2:2 Then the LORD spake unto me saying:
Deuteronomy2:3 Ye have compassed these mountains long enough
Deuteronomy2:4 And warn the people saying: Ye shall go thorow the coasts of your brethren the children of Esau which dwell in Seir
Deuteronomy2:5 that ye provoke them not
Deuteronomy2:6 Ye shall buy meat of them for money to eat
Deuteronomy2:7 For the LORD thy God hath blessed thee in all the works of thine hand
Deuteronomy2:8 And when we were departed from our brethren the children of Esau which dwelt in Seir by the field way from Elath and Ezion Gaber
Deuteronomy2:9 Then the LORD said unto me: see that thou vex not the Moabites
Deuteronomy2:10 The Emims dwelt therein in times past
Deuteronomy2:11 which also were taken for giants as the Enakims: And the Moabites called them Emims.
Deuteronomy2:12 In like manner the Horims dwelt in Seir before time which the children of Esau cast out
Deuteronomy2:13 Now rise up (said I) and get you over the river Zared: and we went over the river Zared.
Deuteronomy2:14 The space in which we came from Cades barne until we were come over the river Zared was thirty eight years: until all the generation of the men of war were wasted out of the host as the LORD sware unto them.
Deuteronomy2:15 For indeed the hand of the LORD was against them
Deuteronomy2:16 And as soon as all the men of war were consumed and dead from among the people

Deuteronomy2:18 Thou shalt go thorow Ar the coast of Moab this day

Deuteronomy2:20 That also was taken for a land of giants and giants dwelt therein in old time
Deuteronomy2:21 A people that was great
Deuteronomy2:22 as he did for the children of Esau which dwell in Seir: even as he destroyed the Horims before them
Deuteronomy2:23 And the Avims which dwelt in Hazarim even unto Aza
Deuteronomy2:24 Rise up

Deuteronomy2:27 Let me go thorow thy land. I will go always along by the high way and will neither turn unto the right hand nor to the left.
Deuteronomy2:28 Sell me meat for money for to eat
Deuteronomy2:29 (as the children of Esau did unto me which dwell in Seir and the Moabites which dwell in Ar) until I be come over Jordan
Deuteronomy2:30 But Sihon the king of Hesbon would not let us pass by him
Deuteronomy2:31 And the LORD said unto me: behold

Deuteronomy4:5 Behold

Deuteronomy4:17 or any manner beast that is on the earth or of any manner feathered fowl that flyeth in the air

Deuteronomy4:21 Furthermore
Deuteronomy4:22 For I must die in this land
Deuteronomy4:23 Take heed unto yourselves therefore
Deuteronomy4:24 For the LORD thy God is a consuming fire

Deuteronomy4:26 I call heaven and earth to record unto you this day
Deuteronomy4:27 And the LORD shall scatter you among nations
Deuteronomy4:28 and there ye shall serve gods which are the works of man's hand
Deuteronomy4:29 Neverthelater ye shall seek the LORD your God even there
Deuteronomy4:30 In thy tribulation and when all these things are come upon thee
Deuteronomy4:31 For the LORD thy God is a pitiful God: he will not forsake thee neither destroy thee
Deuteronomy4:32 For ask I pray thee of the days that are past which were before thee
Deuteronomy4:33 that a nation hath heard the voice of God speaking out of fire as thou hast heard
Deuteronomy4:34 either whether God assayed to go and take him a people from among nations
Deuteronomy4:35 Unto thee it was shewed
Deuteronomy4:36 Out of heaven he made thee hear his voice to nurture thee
Deuteronomy4:37 And because he loved thy fathers
Deuteronomy4:38 to thrust out nations greater and mightier than thou before thee
Deuteronomy4:39 Understand therefore this day and turn it to thine heart
Deuteronomy4:40 keep therefore his ordinances

Deuteronomy5:1 And Moses called unto all Israel and said unto them: Hear Israel the ordinances and laws which I speak in thine ears this day
Deuteronomy5:2 The LORD(Loode) our God made an appointment with us in Horeb.
Deuteronomy5:3 The LORD made not this bond with our fathers
Deuteronomy5:4 The LORD talked with you face to face in the mount out of the fire.
Deuteronomy5:5 And I stood between the LORD and you the same time
Deuteronomy5:6 I am the LORD thy God which brought thee out of the land of Egypt the house of bondage.
Deuteronomy5:7 Thou shalt have therefore none other gods in my presence.
Deuteronomy5:8 Thou shalt make thee no graven Image of any manner likeness that is in heaven above
Deuteronomy5:9 Thou shalt neither bow thyself unto them nor serve them
Deuteronomy5:10 and shew mercy upon thousands among them that love me and keep my commandments.
Deuteronomy5:11 Thou shalt not take the name of the LORD thy God in vain: for the LORD will not hold him guiltless
Deuteronomy5:12 Keep the Sabbath day that thou sanctify it
Deuteronomy5:13 Six days thou shalt labour and do all that thou hast to do
Deuteronomy5:14 but the seventh day is the Sabbath of the LORD thy God: thou shalt do no manner work
Deuteronomy5:15 And remember that thou wast a servant in the land of Egypt and how that the LORD God
Deuteronomy5:16 Honour thy father and thy mother
Deuteronomy5:17 Thou shalt not slee.
Deuteronomy5:18 Thou shalt not break wedlock.
Deuteronomy5:19 Thou shalt not steal.
Deuteronomy5:20 Thou shalt not bear false witness against thy neighbour.
Deuteronomy5:21 Thou shalt not lust after thy neighbour's wife: thou shalt not covet thy neighbour's house
Deuteronomy5:22 These words the LORD spake unto all your multitude in the mount out of the fire
Deuteronomy5:23 But as soon as ye heard the voice out of the darkness and saw the hill burn with fire
Deuteronomy5:24 and ye said: behold
Deuteronomy5:25 And now wherefore should we die that this great fire should consume us: If we should hear the voice of the LORD our God any more
Deuteronomy5:26 For what is any flesh that he should hear the voice of the living God speaking out of the fire as we have done and should yet live:
Deuteronomy5:27 Go thou and hear all that the LORD our God sayeth
Deuteronomy5:28 And the LORD heard the voice of your words when ye spake unto me
Deuteronomy5:29 Oh that they had such an heart with them to fear me and keep all my commandments alway
Deuteronomy5:30 Go and say unto them: get you into your tents again
Deuteronomy5:31 but stand thou here before me and I will tell thee all the commandments
Deuteronomy5:32 Take heed therefore that ye do as the LORD your God hath commanded you

Deuteronomy7:8 But because the LORD loved you and because he would keep the oath which he had sworn unto your fathers

Deuteronomy8:4 Thy raiment waxed not old upon thee
Deuteronomy8:5 Understand therefore in thine heart

Deuteronomy8:8 a land of wheat and of barley
Deuteronomy8:9 a land wherein thou shalt not eat bread in scarceness

Deuteronomy9:1 Hear Israel
Deuteronomy9:2 and people great and tall
Deuteronomy9:3 But understand this day that the LORD thy God which goeth over before thee a consuming fire
Deuteronomy9:4 Speak not in thine heart
Deuteronomy9:5 It is not for thy righteousness' sake and right heart that thou goest to possess their land: But partly for the wickedness of these nations
Deuteronomy9:6 Understand therefore that it is not for thy righteousness' sake
Deuteronomy9:7 Remember and forget not how thou provokedest the LORD thy God in the wilderness: for since the day that thou camest out of the land of Egypt until ye came unto this place
Deuteronomy9:8 Also in Horeb ye angered the LORD so that the LORD was wroth with you
Deuteronomy9:9 after that I was gone up into the mount
Deuteronomy9:10 And the LORD delivered me two tables of stone written with the finger of God
Deuteronomy9:11 And when the forty days and forty nights were ended
Deuteronomy9:12 and said unto me: Up
Deuteronomy9:13 Furthermore the LORD spake unto me saying: I see this people how that it is a stiffnecked people
Deuteronomy9:14 let me alone that I may destroy them and put out the name of them from under heaven
Deuteronomy9:15 And I turned away and came down from the hill (and the hill burnt with fire) and had the two tables of the appointment in my hands.
Deuteronomy9:16 And when I looked and saw that ye had sinned against the LORD your God and had made you a calf of metal and had turned at once out of the way which the LORD had commanded you.
Deuteronomy9:17 Then I took the two tables and cast them out of my two hands
Deuteronomy9:18 And I fell before the LORD: even as at the first time forty days and nights
Deuteronomy9:19 For I was afraid of the wrath and fierceness wherewith the LORD was angry with you
Deuteronomy9:20 The LORD was very angry with Aaron also
Deuteronomy9:21 And I took your sin
Deuteronomy9:22 Also at Thabeera and at Masa and at the sepulchres of lust ye angered the LORD
Deuteronomy9:23 yea and when the LORD sent you from Cades Barnea saying: go up and conquer the land which I have given you

Deuteronomy11:22 For if ye shall keep all these commandments which I command you
Deuteronomy11:23 Then will the LORD cast out all these nations (and ye shall conquer them which are) both greater and mightier than your selves.

Deuteronomy33:27 that is the dwelling place of God from the beginning and from under the arms of the world: he hath cast out thine enemies before thee and said: destroy.
Deuteronomy33:28 And Israel shall dwell in safety alone. And the eyes of Jacob shall look upon a land of corn and wine
Deuteronomy33:29 Happy art thou Israel
Deuteronomy34:1 And Moses went from the fields of Moab up into mount Nebo which is the top of Pisga
Deuteronomy34:2 and all Naphthali and the land of Ephraim and Manasse
Deuteronomy34:3 and the south and the region of the plain of Jericho the city of datetrees(palm trees) even unto Zoar.
Deuteronomy34:4 And the LORD said unto him. This is the land which I sware unto Abraham
Deuteronomy34:5 So Moses the servant of the LORD died there in the land of Moab at the commandment of the LORD.
Deuteronomy34:6 And he buried him in a valley in the land of Moab beside Beth Peor: but no man wist of his sepulchre unto this day.
Deuteronomy34:7 And Moses was an hundred and twenty year old when he died
Deuteronomy34:8 And the children of Israel wept for Moses in the fields of Moab thirty days. And the days of weeping and mourning for Moses were ended.
Deuteronomy34:9 And Josua the son of Nun was full of the spirit of wisdom: for Moses had put his hand upon him. And all the children of Israel hearkened unto him and did as the LORD commanded Moses.
Deuteronomy34:10 But there arose not a prophet since in Israel like unto Moses
Deuteronomy34:11 in all the miracles and wonders which the LORD sent him to do in the land of Egypt
Deuteronomy34:12 and in all the mighty deeds and great terrible things which Moses did in the sight of all Israel. [The end of the fifth book of Moses.]
Joshua1:1 After the death of Moses the servant of the LORD: the LORD spake unto Josua the son of Nun Moses' minister saying:
Joshua1:2 Moses my servant is dead. Now therefore up and go over Jordan
Joshua1:3 All the places that the soles of your feet shall tread upon
Joshua1:4 from the wilderness and this Libanon unto the great river Euphrates: and all the land of the Hethites
Joshua1:5 There shall not a man be able to withstand thee all the days of thy life. For as I was with Moses
Joshua1:6 Be strong and bold: for unto this people shalt thou divide the land which I sware unto their fathers to give them.
Joshua1:7 Above all thing be strong and harden thyself
Joshua1:8 Let not the book of this law depart out of thy mouth: but record therein day and night that thou mayest be circumspect to do according to all that is written therein. For then shalt thou make thy way prosperous
Joshua1:9 Behold
Joshua1:10 Then Josua commanded the officers of the people saying:
Joshua1:11 Go thorow the midst of the host
Joshua1:12 And unto the Rubenites
Joshua1:13 Remember that which Moses the servant of the LORD commanded you saying: the LORD your God hath given you rest
Joshua1:14 Let your wives
Joshua1:15 until the LORD hath given your brethren rest
Joshua1:16 And they answered Josua saying: All that thou biddest us
Joshua1:17 According as we obeyed Moses in all things
Joshua1:18 And whosoever disobey thy mouth
Joshua2:1 Then Josua the son of Nun sent out of Settim two spies secretly saying: go and view the land and also Jericho. And they went
Joshua2:2 And it was told the king of Jericho saying: Behold there came men in hither tonight
Joshua2:3 And the king of Jericho sent unto Rahab saying: bring forth the men that are come to thee
Joshua2:4 And the woman took the two men and hid them. And said
Joshua2:5 And about the time of the shutting of the gate when it was dark
Joshua2:6 And she brought them up upon the roof of the house
Joshua2:7 And the men pursued after them
Joshua2:8 And or ever they were asleep
Joshua2:9 and said unto the men: I know that the LORD hath given you the land
Joshua2:10 For we have heard how the LORD dried up the water of the red sea before you
Joshua2:11 And as soon as we had heard these things
Joshua2:12 Now therefore swear unto me by the LORD because I have shewed you mercy
Joshua2:13 And that ye shall save alive
Joshua2:14 And the men answered her
Joshua2:15 And then she let them down with a cord thorow the window. For her house stood in the town wall. And she dwelt in the town wall.
Joshua2:16 And she said unto them
Joshua2:17 And the men said unto her
Joshua2:18 Behold
Joshua2:19 And then whosoever go out at the doors of thy house
Joshua2:20 And even so if thou utter these our words
Joshua2:21 And she said according unto your words so be it: and so sent them away
Joshua2:22 And they departed and got them into the mountains
Joshua2:23 And the two men returned and descended from the mountain and passed over
Joshua2:24 And they said unto Josua the LORD hath delivered into our hands all the land
Joshua3:1 And Josua rose early: And they removed from Settim
Joshua3:2 And after three days the officers went thorowout the host
Joshua3:3 and commanded the people saying: when ye see the ark of the testament of the LORD your God
Joshua3:4 So yet that there be a space between you and it
Joshua3:5 And Josua said unto the people
Joshua3:6 Then Josua spake unto the priests saying: Take up the ark of the testament
Joshua3:7 And the LORD said unto Josua: this day will I begin to magnify thee
Joshua3:8 And command thou the priests that bear the ark of the testament
Joshua3:9 Then spake Josua to the children of Israel
Joshua3:10 And Josua said: Hereby ye shall know
Joshua3:11 Behold
Joshua3:12 And now take you twelve men of the children of Israel
Joshua3:13 And as soon as the soles of the feet of the priests that bear the ark of Jehovah(Iehouah) (the Lord of all the world) tread in the water of Jordan
Joshua3:14 And when the people were departed from their tents to go over Jordan
Joshua3:15 as soon as they that bare the ark came unto Jordan
Joshua3:16 the water that came down from above did stop and stood upon an heap
Joshua3:17 And the priests that bare the ark of the appointment of the LORD stood still upon dry land
Joshua4:1 And as soon as the people were all gone over Jordan
Joshua4:2 Take you twelve men out of the people
Joshua4:3 And command you them saying: take you hence out of the midst of Jordan even out of the place where the priests stood in a readiness
Joshua4:4 And Josua called unto the twelve men which he had prepared of the children of Israel
Joshua4:5 and said unto them: get you before the ark of the LORD your God even into the midst of Jordan
Joshua4:6 that this may be a sign among you. And when your children ask their fathers in time to come saying: what mean ye with these stones?
Joshua4:7 then say unto them
Joshua4:8 And the children of Israel did even so as Josua commanded
Joshua4:9 And thereto Josua set up twelve stones in the midst of Jordan in the place where the feet of the priests which bare the ark of the testament stood. And there they be even unto this day.
Joshua4:10 For the priests that bare the ark stood in the midst of Jordan
Joshua4:11 When all the people were clean over
Joshua4:12 And the children of Ruben
Joshua4:13 Upon a forty thousand trimmed for war
Joshua4:14 That day the LORD magnified Josua in the sight of all Israel and they feared him
Joshua4:15 And the LORD spake unto Josua saying:
Joshua4:16 command the priests that bear the ark of witness to come up out of Jordan.
Joshua4:17 And Josua commanded the priests saying: Ascend up out of Jordan.
Joshua4:18 And when the priests that bare the ark of the appointment of the LORD were ascended out of Jordan: as soon as the soles of the priests' feet were brought to dry land
Joshua4:19 And the people came up out of Jordan the tenth day of the first month and pitched in Gilgal even in the east borders of Jericho.
Joshua4:20 And the twelve stones which they took out of Jordan
Joshua4:21 And he spake unto the children of Israel saying: when your children ask you in time to come saying: what mean these stones
Joshua4:22 shew your children and say: Israel came over this Jordan on dry land.
Joshua4:23 For the LORD your God dried up the water of Jordan before you
Joshua4:24 that all the people of the world may know the hand of the LORD how mighty it is
Joshua5:1 And when all the kings of the Amorites which are on this side Jordan to the seaward
Joshua5:2 That same time the LORD said unto Josua: Make thee knives of stone
Joshua5:3 And Josua made him knives of stone and circumcised the children of Israel in the hill of foreskins.
Joshua5:4 And this is the cause why Josua circumcised. All the people that came out of Egypt that were males
Joshua5:5 Now all the people that came out were circumcised. But all the people that were born in the wilderness by the way after they came out of Egypt
Joshua5:6 For the children of Israel walked forty year in the wilderness
Joshua5:7 And their children he set up in their stead: them Josua circumcised: for they were uncircumcised
Joshua5:8 And when all the people were full circumcised
Joshua5:9 And the LORD said unto Josua
Joshua5:10 And the children of Israel pitched their tents in Gilgal
Joshua5:11 And they ate of the corn of the land on the morrow after passover
Joshua5:12 And Manna ceased on the morrow
Joshua5:13 And when Josua was come to Jericho
Joshua5:14 And he answered
Joshua5:15 And the captain of the LORD's host said unto Josua: do thy shoes off thy feet
Joshua6:1 And Jericho shut in her people
Joshua6:2 And the LORD said into Josua: behold
Joshua6:3 And ye shall compass the city
Joshua6:4 And seven priests shall bear seven trumpets of rams' horns before the ark. And the seventh day
Joshua6:5 And when there is a long blast blown with the ram's horn
Joshua6:6 And Josua the son of Nun
Joshua6:7 And he said unto the people
Joshua6:8 And when Josua had spoken unto the people
Joshua6:9 And the men of arms went before the priests that blew with the horns
Joshua6:10 And Josua commanded the people saying
Joshua6:11 And so the ark of the LORD compassed the city and went about it once: and then they returned unto the host and lodged there.
Joshua6:12 And Josua rose early in the morning
Joshua6:13 and seven priests bare seven trumpets of rams' horns before the ark of the LORD
Joshua6:14 And the second day they compassed the city once
Joshua6:15 And the seventh day they rose early: even with the dawning of the day
Joshua6:16 And at the seventh time
Joshua6:17 But the city shall be excommunicate
Joshua6:18 And in any wise beware of the excommunicate things
Joshua6:19 But of the silver
Joshua6:20 And the people cried
Joshua6:21 And they utterly destroyed all that was in the city
Joshua6:22 Then Josua said to the two men that went to spy out the country: go into the harlot's house
Joshua6:23 And the young men that were spies went in and brought out Rahab
Joshua6:24 And they burnt the city with fire
Joshua6:25 And Josua saved Rahab the harlot
Joshua6:26 And Josua cursed at that time
Joshua6:27 And the LORD was with Josua
Joshua7:1 But yet the children of Israel trespassed in the excommunicate things: for Acan the son of Carmi
Joshua7:2 Then Josua sent men from Jericho to Ai which is beside Bethaven
Joshua7:3 And returned unto Josua and said unto him
Joshua7:4 And so there went up thither about a three thousand men
Joshua7:5 And the men of Ai smote of them upon a thirty and six men
Joshua7:6 And Josua rent his clothes and fell to the earth upon his face before the ark of the LORD until the eventide
Joshua7:7 And Josua said
Joshua7:8 Oh Lord(LORDE) what shall I say
Joshua7:9 Moreover the Cananites
Joshua7:10 And the LORD said unto Josua
Joshua7:11 Israel hath sinned and also transgressed mine appointment
Joshua7:12 And therefore the children of Israel can not stand before their enemies. They shall turn their backs before their enemies
Joshua7:13 Up and sanctify the people and bid them to sanctify themselves against the morrow: for so sayeth the LORD God of Israel
Joshua7:14 Tomorrow morning ye shall come by your tribes. And the tribe which the LORD shall find guilty
Joshua7:15 And he that is found in the excommunication shall be burnt with fire
Joshua7:16 And so Josua rose up early in the morning and brought Israel by their tribes: and the tribe of Juda was caught.
Joshua7:17 Then he brought the kindreds of Juda
Joshua7:18 And he brought the kindred of the Zarehites by householders
Joshua7:19 And Josua said unto Acan: my son
Joshua7:20 And Acan answered Josua and said: of a truth I have sinned against the LORD God of Israel
Joshua7:21 I saw among the spoil a goodly Babylonish garment
Joshua7:22 And then Josua sent messengers which ran unto the tent. And behold
Joshua7:23 And they took them out of the midst of his tent and brought them unto Josua and unto all the children of Israel
Joshua7:24 And Josua took Acan the son of Zareh
Joshua7:25 And Josua said: Inasmuch as thou hast troubled us: the LORD shall trouble thee this day. And all Israel stoned him with stones
Joshua7:26 And they cast upon him a great heap of stones that remaineth unto this day. And so the LORD turned from his fierce wrath. Wherefore the name of the place is called the valley of Acor unto this day.
Joshua8:1 And the LORD said unto Josua: fear not
Joshua8:2 And thou shalt do to Ai and her king
Joshua8:3 And then Josua arose and all the men of war
Joshua8:4 And he commanded them saying: See that ye lie await unto the town on the backside thereof. Go not very far from the city
Joshua8:5 And I and all the people that are with me
Joshua8:6 And they will come out after us
Joshua8:7 Then shall ye rise up from lying await and destroy the city: For the LORD your God will deliver it into your hands.
Joshua8:8 As soon therefore as ye have taken the city
Joshua8:9 And Josua sent them forth
Joshua8:10 And he rose up early in the morning and numbered the people and went up
Joshua8:11 And all the men of war that were with him
Joshua8:12 and he took upon a five thousand men and put them to lie await
Joshua8:13 And he put the people even all the host
Joshua8:14 And when the king of Ai saw that they hasted and rose up early
Joshua8:15 And Josua and Israel were put to the worse before them
Joshua8:16 And all the people of the town cried to follow after them. And they followed after Josua till they were drawn away from the city:
Joshua8:17 So that there was not a man left in Ai
Joshua8:18 Then the LORD said unto Josua stretch out the buckler that is in thine hand toward Ai
Joshua8:19 And the lyers await rose quickly out of their places
Joshua8:20 And when the men of Ai looked back after them and saw. Behold
Joshua8:21 And when Josua and all Israel saw that the lyers await had taken the city and that the smoke of it ascended. They turned again and laid on the men of Ai.
Joshua8:22 And the other issued out of the city against them. And so were they in the midst of Israel
Joshua8:23 And the king of Ai they took alive and brought him to Josua.
Joshua8:24 And when Israel had made an end of slaying all the inhabiters of Ai in the fields and in the wilderness by which they chased them
Joshua8:25 And all that fell that day
Joshua8:26 For Josua plucked not his hand back again which he stretched out with the buckler
Joshua8:27 Only the cattle and the spoil of the said city
Joshua8:28 And Josua set Ai on fire and made it an heap for ever and a wilderness
Joshua8:29 And the king of Ai he hanged on tree
Joshua8:30 Then Josua built an altar unto the LORD God of Israel
Joshua8:31 as Moses the servant of the LORD commanded the children of Israel
Joshua8:32 And he wrote there upon the stones the second law of Moses which he wrote in the presence of the children of Israel.
Joshua8:33 And all Israel and the elders thereof
Joshua8:34 And after that he read all the words of the law
Joshua8:35 so that there was not one word of all that Moses commanded
Joshua9:1 And when all the kings that dwell on this side Jordan
Joshua9:2 they gathered themselves together to fight against Josua and Israel with one accord.
Joshua9:3 But when the Inhabiters of Gibeon heard what Josua had done unto Jericho
Joshua9:4 they played wilily
Joshua9:5 and old clouted shoes upon their feet
Joshua9:6 And they came unto Josua unto the host
Joshua9:7 And the men of Israel said unto the Hevites
Joshua9:8 And they said unto Josua
Joshua9:9 And they answered him. From a very far country thy servants are come in the name of the LORD thy God: for we have heard the fame of him
Joshua9:10 and all that he did to the two kings of the Amorites beyond Jordan
Joshua9:11 Wherefore our elders and all the Enhabiters of our country spake to us saying: take vitailles with you to serve by the way
Joshua9:12 This our provision of bread
Joshua9:13 And these bottles of wine which we filled were new: and see
Joshua9:14 And the men took of their vitailles
Joshua9:15 And Josua made peace with them
Joshua9:16 But three days after they had made peace with them
Joshua9:17 For the children of Israel took their Journey and came unto their cities the third day: and their cities were Gabaon
Joshua9:18 And the children of Israel slew them not

Joshua9:20 But this we will do to them and let them live: and so shall no wrath be upon us because of the oath which we sware unto them.

Joshua9:22 Then Josua sent for them
Joshua9:23 and now therefore cursed be you
Joshua9:24 And they answered Josua and said: it was told thy servants
Joshua9:25 and now behold
Joshua9:26 And he dealt as it is said
Joshua9:27 And Josua made them that same day hewers of wood and drawers of water unto the congregation and unto the altar of GOD unto this day

Joshua10:2 then they feared exceedingly

Joshua10:12 Then spake Josua unto the LORD
Joshua10:13 And the sun abode
Joshua10:14 And there was no day like that
Joshua10:15 And Josua returned and all Israel with him
Joshua10:16 but the five kings fled and hid themselves in a cave at Makeda.
Joshua10:17 And it was told Josua
Joshua10:18 And Josua said
Joshua10:19 And stand ye not still
Joshua10:20 And when Josua and the children of Israel had made an end of slaying them with a great slaughter till they were wasted
Joshua10:21 And all the people returned to the host
Joshua10:22 Then said Josua: open the mouth of the cave
Joshua10:23 And they did so
Joshua10:24 And when they had brought out those kings unto Josua: Josua called for all the men of Israel
Joshua10:25 And Josua said unto them fear not nor dread: But be strong and pluck up your hearts
Joshua10:26 And then Josua smote them and slew them and hanged them on five trees till evening.
Joshua10:27 And at the going down of the son
Joshua10:28 And that same day Josua took Makeda
Joshua10:29 Then Josua went from Makeda
Joshua10:30 And the LORD delivered it also into their hands
Joshua10:31 After that Josua departed from Libna
Joshua10:32 And the LORD delivered into the hands of Israel Lachis
Joshua10:33 Then Horam king of Geser came up to help Lachis: But Josua smote him and his people
Joshua10:34 And from Lachis Josua departed
Joshua10:35 and took it the same day

Joshua10:37 and took it
Joshua10:38 Then Josua returned and all Israel with him to Dabir
Joshua10:39 and took it

Joshua10:41 And Josua smote them from Cades Barne
Joshua10:42 And all those kings and their land
Joshua10:43 And then Josua returned and all Israel with him unto the host to Gilgal.
Joshua11:1 And when Jabin king of Hazor had heard that: he sent to Jobab king of Madon
Joshua11:2 And unto the kings that are by north in the mountains
Joshua11:3 and unto the Cananites both by East and west: and unto the Amorites
Joshua11:4 which came out and all their hosts with them a multitude of folk even as the sands of the sea in number with horses and chariots exceeding many.
Joshua11:5 And all these kings met together
Joshua11:6 And the LORD said unto Josua: be not afraid of them: for tomorrow about this time I will deliver them all slain
Joshua11:7 And Josua came and all the men of war with him against them
Joshua11:8 And the LORD delivered them into the hands of Israel: and they smote them and chased them
Joshua11:9 And Josua served them as the LORD bade him
Joshua11:10 Then Josua at that time turned back and took Hazor and smote her king with the sword. For Hazor before time was the head of all those kingdoms.
Joshua11:11 And they smote all the souls that were therein with the edge of the sword
Joshua11:12 and all the cities of those kings
Joshua11:13 But Israel burnt none of the cities that stood upon hills
Joshua11:14 but all the spoil of the said cities and the cattle
Joshua11:15 and had left no breath remaining. As the LORD commanded Josua
Joshua11:16 And so Josua took all the land
Joshua11:17 even from mount Halak that goeth up to Seir
Joshua11:18 Josua made war long time with those kings:
Joshua11:19 for there was no city that made peace with the children of Israel
Joshua11:20 for it came of the LORD to harden their hearts: that they should come against Israel in battle: even to destroy them utterly
Joshua11:21 And that same season
Joshua11:22 So that there was not one Enakite left in the land of the children of Israel: only in Asah
Joshua11:23 And Josua took the whole land according to all that the LORD said unto Moses

Joshua13:1 When Josua was waxen old
Joshua13:2 This is the land that remaineth: all the coasts of the Philistines: and all the Gesurites:
Joshua13:3 from Nile in Egypt unto the borders of Akaron northward
Joshua13:4 from the south. All the land of the Cananites
Joshua13:5 And the land of the Giblites
Joshua13:6 all the inhabiters of the hill country
Joshua13:7 Now therefore divide this land to inherit
Joshua13:8 For the other half with the Rubenites and the Gaddites have received their inheritance already which Moses gave them beyond Jordan Eastward: even as Moses the servant of the LORD gave them:
Joshua13:9 from Aroer that lieth on the brim of the river Arnon: with the city in the midst of the river. And all the plain of Midba unto Dibon:
Joshua13:10 and all the cities of Sehon
Joshua13:11 and Gilead
Joshua13:12 even all the kingdom of Og in Basan which reigned in Astharoth and Edrai which same remained yet of the Rest of the Raphaites. But Moses smote them and destroyed them.
Joshua13:13 Nevertheless the children of Israel expelled not the Gesurites nor the Machathites: But the Gesurites and the Machathites dwelt among the Israelites even unto this day.
Joshua13:14 Onely unto the tribe of Levi
Joshua13:15 And Moses gave unto the tribe of the children of Ruben by their kindreds.
Joshua13:16 And their coasts were from Aroer that lieth on the bank of the river Arnon
Joshua13:17 Hesbon
Joshua13:18 Jahazah
Joshua13:19 Kiriathaim
Joshua13:20 and Beth Pheor and the springs of Phasgah
Joshua13:21 and all the cities in the plain. And all the kingdom of Sehon king of the Amorites which reigned in Hesbon
Joshua13:22 And Balam also the son of Beor the soothsayer
Joshua13:23 And the borders of the children of Ruben was Jordan with the country that lieth thereon. This is the inheritance of the children of Ruben in their kindreds
Joshua13:24 And Moses gave unto the tribe of Gad
Joshua13:25 And their coasts were Jazer
Joshua13:26 And from Hesbon unto Ramoth mazphah
Joshua13:27 And in the valley they had Betharam
Joshua13:28 This is the inheritance of the children of Gad in their kindreds
Joshua13:29 And Moses gave unto the half tribe of Manasses. And the half tribe of Manasses had their part by their kindreds.
Joshua13:30 And their coasts were from Mahanaim
Joshua13:31 and half Gilead and Astaroth
Joshua13:32 These are they to which Moses gave inheritance in the fields of Moab
Joshua13:33 But unto the tribe of Levi Moses gave none inheritance
Joshua14:1 And these are the countries which the children inherited in the land of Canaan
Joshua14:2 gave them their inheritance by lot
Joshua14:3 For Moses had given inheritance unto two tribes and an half on the other side Jordan. But unto the Levites he gave none inheritance among them.
Joshua14:4 For the children of Joseph were two tribes
Joshua14:5 As the LORD commanded Moses: even so the children of Israel did when they divided the land.
Joshua14:6 And the children of Juda came unto Josua in Gilgal. And Caleb the son of Jephune the Kenesite said unto him: thou wotest what the LORD said unto Moses the man of God
Joshua14:7 Forty year old was I when Moses the servant of the LORD sent me from Cades Barne to spy out the land. And I brought them word again even as I thought in mine heart.
Joshua14:8 Nevertheless my brethren that went up with me discouraged the hearts of the people. But I followed the LORD my God even unto the end.
Joshua14:9 And Moses sware the same season saying the land whereon thy feet have trodden
Joshua14:10 And now behold
Joshua14:11 and yet am as strong at this time
Joshua14:12 Now therefore give me this mountain which the LORD at that time promised
Joshua14:13 And Josua blessed Caleb the son of Jephune and gave him Hebron to inherit.
Joshua14:14 And Hebron became the inheritance of Caleb the son of Jephune the Kenesite
Joshua14:15 And the name of Hebron was called in old time
Joshua15:1 And the lot of the tribe of the children of Juda in their kindreds was the wilderness of Zin that stretched to the coasts of Edom southward
Joshua15:2 And their south coasts were from the brink of the salt sea and from a certain point of the sea that leaneth southward.
Joshua15:3 And it went out on the south side of the going up to Acrabim
Joshua15:4 and went along to Azmon
Joshua15:5 And their east coasts are the salt sea even unto the edge of Jordan. And their borders in the north quarter were from another point of the sea where Jordan endeth.
Joshua15:6 And went up to Bethhagla and went along by the north side of Betharabah and went up to the stone of Bohen the son of Ruben.
Joshua15:7 And then went up to Dabir from the valley of Acor
Joshua15:8 And then went up to the valley of the son of Hennom

Joshua16:1 And the lot of the children of Joseph fell from Jordan by Jericho
Joshua16:2 and then goeth out from Bethel to Lus
Joshua16:3 and goeth down westward
Joshua16:4 And so the children of Joseph
Joshua16:5 And the children of Ephraim took their coasts by kindreds. And the coasts of their inheritance Eastward were
