Joshua17:1 And the tribe of Manasses which was the eldest son of Joseph received a lot. And Machir the eldest son of Manasses which was the father of Gilead (and a man of war) had Gilead and Basan.
Joshua17:2 And the rest of the children of Manasses received by their kindreds: which were the children of Abiezer: the children of Nelek: the children of Asriel: the children of Sichen
Joshua17:3 But Zelaphead the son of Hepher the son of Gilead
Joshua17:4 which came before Eleazar the priest
Joshua17:5 And there fell ten portions to Manasses beside the land of Gilead and Basan which are on the other side Jordan:
Joshua17:6 because the daughters of Manasses did inherit among his sons. And Manasses' other sons had the land of Gilead.
Joshua17:7 And the coasts of Manasses reached from Aser to Machmathah that lieth before Sichem and went along on the right hand
Joshua17:8 and the land of Thaphuah belonged to Manasses
Joshua17:9 And the coasts descended unto the river Canah on whose south side Ephraim hath cities among the cities of Manasses. For the coasts of Manasses were on the north side of the river and the ends of them was the sea:
Joshua17:10 so that the south pertained to Ephraim
Joshua17:11 And Manasses had in Isacar and in Aser
Joshua17:12 yet the children of Manasses could not overcome those cities: But the Cananites began to dwell in the same land.
Joshua17:13 Nevertheless as soon as the children of Israel were waxed strong
Joshua17:14 And the children of Joseph spake unto Josua saying: why hast thou given us but one lot and one portion to inherit
Joshua17:15 and Josua answered them: if ye be much people
Joshua17:16 Then the children of Joseph said again the hill will not be gotten of us: for all the Cananites that dwell in the low country have chariots of iron
Joshua17:17 And Josua answered the house of Joseph
Joshua17:18 But the hill shall be yours

Joshua18:2 But there remained among the children of Israel seven tribes which had not their inheritance divided out.

Joshua18:5 Divide it into seven parts. For Juda shall abide by their coasts in the south
Joshua18:6 Describe ye the land therefore into seven parts
Joshua18:7 The Levites have no part among you for the priestship of the LORD is their inheritance. And Gad

Joshua20:4 And the slayer shall flee unto one of those Cities and shall stand in the entering of the gate of the city and shall shew his cause in the ears of the elders of the said city. And they shall take him into the city unto them
Joshua20:5 And when the avenger of blood followeth after him they shall not deliver the slayer unto his hand: because he smote his friend ignorantly

Joshua22:1 Then Josua called unto the Rubenites
Joshua22:2 and said unto them: ye have kept all that Moses the servant of the LORD commanded you
Joshua22:3 ye have not left your brethren of a long season unto this day but have kept the commandment of the LORD your God.
Joshua22:4 And now the LORD hath given rest unto your brethren as he promised them. Wherefore return and go unto your tents and unto the land of your possession
Joshua22:5 But in any wise take exceeding good heed that ye do the commandment and law which Moses the servant of the LORD charged you: which is
Joshua22:6 And so Josua blessed them and sent them away. And they went unto their tents.

Joshua22:13 And the children of Israel sent unto the children of Ruben

Joshua22:15 And they went unto the children of Ruben
Joshua22:16 Thus say the whole congregation of the LORD: what transgression is this that ye have transgressed against the God of Israel
Joshua22:17 Is the wicked deed of Peor too little for us
Joshua22:18 But that ye also should turn away this day from following the LORD: ye shall rebel today against the LORD
Joshua22:19 Notwithstanding if the land of your possession be unclean
Joshua22:20 Did not Acan the son of Zareh trespass in the excommunicate things
Joshua22:21 Then the children of Ruben and of Gad
Joshua22:22 The mighty God Jehovah(Iehouah) he knoweth
Joshua22:23 Or else if we have built us an altar to turn from following the LORD
Joshua22:24 And have not rather done it for fear of this: lest in time to come your children should say unto ours: what have ye to do with the LORD God of Israel
Joshua22:25 saying: that the LORD hath made Jordan a border between us and you ye children of Ruben and of Gad: ye have no part therefore in the LORD: and so shall your children make our children cease from fearing the LORD.
Joshua22:26 And therefore we said: Let us cause an altar to be made
Joshua22:27 but it shall be a witness between us and you and our generations after us
Joshua22:28 And we thought if they should so say to us
Joshua22:29 God forbid that we should rebel against the LORD and that we should turn this day from after him

Joshua22:31 And Phinehes the son of Eleazar the priest said unto the children of Ruben
Joshua22:32 And Phinehes the son of Eleazar the priest and the lords returned from the children of Ruben and of Gad out of the land of Gilead unto the land of Canaan
Joshua22:33 And the answer pleased the children of Israel well
Joshua22:34 And the children of Ruben and the children of Gad called the altar Ed
Joshua23:1 And it came to pass a long season after that the LORD had given rest unto Israel from all their enemies round about
Joshua23:2 Wherefore he sent for all Israel and for their elders
Joshua23:3 And ye have seen all that the LORD your God hath done unto all these nations before your faces: for the LORD your God he fought for you.
Joshua23:4 Behold
Joshua23:5 And the LORD your God shall expel and cast them out before you
Joshua23:6 Be therefore exceeding strong that ye take heed to do all that is written in the book of the law of Moses
Joshua23:7 and that ye go not unto these nations that remain with you: and that ye neither make mention or swear by the names of their gods: and that ye neither serve them nor bow yourselves unto them.
Joshua23:8 But that ye stick fast unto the LORD your God
Joshua23:9 So shall the LORD cast out before you great nations and mighty
Joshua23:10 One of you shall chase a thousand: for the LORD your God he fighteth for you
Joshua23:11 Take good heed therefore unto your souls
Joshua23:12 But and if ye shall go back and cleave unto the rest of these nations that remain with you and shall make Marriages with them
Joshua23:13 be sure that the LORD your God will not cast out all these nations before you. But they shall be snares and traps unto you
Joshua23:14 Behold I walk this day
Joshua23:15 And as all good things are come upon you
Joshua23:16 when ye have transgressed the appointment of the LORD your God
Joshua24:1 And Josua gathered all the tribes of Israel to Sichem
Joshua24:2 And Josua said unto all the people
Joshua24:3 But I took your father Abraham from the other side of the flood
Joshua24:4 And I gave unto Isaac Jacob and Esau. And I gave unto Esau mount Seir to possess it. But Jacob and his children went down into Egypt.
Joshua24:5 Then I sent Moses and Aaron. And I plagued Egypt
Joshua24:6 and I brought your fathers out of Egypt. And ye came unto the sea: and the Egyptians followed after your fathers with chariots and horsemen
Joshua24:7 And they cried unto the LORD. And he put darkness between you and the Egyptians and brought the sea upon them and covered them. And your eyes have seen what I have done in Egypt. And ye dwelt in the wilderness a long season.
Joshua24:8 And I brought you into the land of the Amorites which dwelt on the other side Jordan. And they fought with you: and I gave them into your hands. And ye conquered their country. And I destroyed them in your sight.
Joshua24:9 Then Balak the son of Ziphor
Joshua24:10 But I would not agree to hearken unto Balam
Joshua24:11 And when ye went over Jordan
Joshua24:12 And I sent hornets before you and ye cast out before you: even the two kings of the Amorites: but not with your own sword or with your own bow.
Joshua24:13 And I gave you a land in which ye did not labor
Joshua24:14 And now fear the LORD and serve him in pureness and truth: And put away the gods which your fathers served on the other side of the flood
Joshua24:15 But if it seem evil unto you to serve the LORD
Joshua24:16 And the people answered and said
Joshua24:17 For the LORD our God he it is that brought us and our fathers out of the land of Egypt
Joshua24:18 And the LORD did cast out before us all the nations with the Amorites which dwelt in the land
Joshua24:19 But Josua said unto the people
Joshua24:20 But when ye have forsaken the LORD and have served strange gods
Joshua24:21 And the people answered Josua: nay
Joshua24:22 Then Josua said unto the folk
Joshua24:23 Then put away
Joshua24:24 And the people answered Josua: the LORD our God we will serve and his voice we will obey.
Joshua24:25 And so Josua made a covenant with the people the same day and set ordinances and laws before them in Sichem.
Joshua24:26 And Josua wrote these words in the book of the law of God
Joshua24:27 And Josua said unto all the people: behold
Joshua24:28 And so Josua let the people depart every man unto his inheritance.
Joshua24:29 And after these things the time came that Josua the son of Nun and the servant of the LORD died being an hundredth and ten years old.
Joshua24:30 Whom they buried in the country of his inheritance
Joshua24:31 And Israel served the LORD all the days of Josua
Joshua24:32 And the bones of Joseph
Joshua24:33 And Eleazar the son of Aaron died whom they buried in Gabaath that pertained to Phinehes his son and was given him in Mount Ephraim. [The end of the book of Iosua.]
Judges1:1 After the death of Josua the children of Israel asked the LORD saying: Who shall go up first unto the Cananites to fight against them?
Judges1:2 And the LORD said: Juda shall go up: behold I have delivered the land into his hands.
Judges1:3 Then Juda said unto Simeon his brother: come with me into my lot
Judges1:4 And Juda went up
Judges1:5 And they found Adonibezek in Bezek. And they fought against him
Judges1:6 But Adonibezek fled
Judges1:7 Then Adonibezek said: three score and ten kings having their thumbs and great toes cut off
Judges1:8 The children of Juda fought against Jerusalem and took it
Judges1:9 And after that the children of Juda went even to fight against the Cananites that dwelt in the mountain
Judges1:10 And Juda went unto the Cananites that dwelt in Hebron
Judges1:11 And from thence they went to the inhabiters of Dabir
Judges1:12 And Caleb said: he that smiteth Kariathsepher
Judges1:13 And Othoniel the son of Kenez Caleb's younger brother took it: to whom he gave Acsah his daughter to wife
Judges1:14 and as they went she counseled him to ask of her father a field. And then she lighted off her ass: and Caleb said unto her: What aileth thee?
Judges1:15 She said unto him: give me a blessing: for thou hast given me a Southward and a dry land
Judges1:16 And the children of the Kenite
Judges1:17 And Juda went and Simeon with him
Judges1:18 And Juda took Azah with the coasts thereof
Judges1:19 And the LORD was with Juda that he conquered the mountains
Judges1:20 And they gave Hebron unto Caleb as Moses said. And he expelled thence the three sons of Enack.
Judges1:21 And the children of BenJamin did not cast out the Jebusites that inhabited Jerusalem
Judges1:22 And in like manner the house of Joseph went up to Bethel and the LORD with them
Judges1:23 and the house of Joseph searched out Bethel which before time was called Lus.
Judges1:24 And the spies saw a man come out of the city and they said unto him shew us the way into the city. And we will shew thee mercy.
Judges1:25 And he shewed them the way into the city
Judges1:26 And the man went into the land of the Hethites
Judges1:27 Neither did Manasses expel Bethsean with her towns
Judges1:28 But as soon as Israel was waxed mighty
Judges1:29 In like manner Ephraim expelled not the Cananites that dwelt in Gazer
Judges1:30 Neither did Zabulon expel the inhabiters of Ketron
Judges1:31 Neither did Aser cast out the inhabiters of Acho
Judges1:32 but the Aserites dwelt among the Cananites the inhabiters of the land
Judges1:33 Neither did Nephthalim drive out the enhabiters of Bethsames
Judges1:34 And the Amorites kept the children of Dan in the mountains
Judges1:35 And so the Amorites went and dwelled in mount Heres in Hailon and in Salabim. Neverthelater the hand of Joseph waxed heavy upon them
Judges1:36 And the coasts of the Amorites was from the going up to Acrabim
Judges2:1 And the angel of the LORD came up from Gilgal to Bocim
Judges2:2 but ye should have made no covenant with the inhabiters of this land
Judges2:3 Wherefore I have likewise determined that I will not cast them out before you: abut they shall be in the sides of you
Judges2:4 And when the angel of the LORD had spoken these words unto all the children of Israel
Judges2:5 And called the name of the said place
Judges2:6 And when Josua had sent the people away
Judges2:7 And the people served the LORD all the days of Josua
Judges2:8 And Josua the son of Nun the servant of the LORD died when he was an hundredth and ten years old:
Judges2:9 whom they buried in the coasts of his inheritance: even in Thamnath hares in mount Ephraim on the north side of the hill Gaas.
Judges2:10 And even so all that generation were put unto their fathers
Judges2:11 And then the children of Israel did wickedly in the sight of the LORD
Judges2:12 and forsook the LORD God of their fathers
Judges2:13 And so they forsook the LORD and served Baal and Astharoth.
Judges2:14 Wherefore the LORD waxed angry with Israel
Judges2:15 But unto whatsoever thing they went
Judges2:16 Nevertheless the LORD raised up judges
Judges2:17 and yet for all that they would not hearken unto their judges: But went a whoring after strange gods and bowed themselves unto them
Judges2:18 And when the LORD raised them up judges
Judges2:19 yet for all that as soon as the judge was dead they turned and did worse than their fathers in following strange gods
Judges2:20 Wherefore the LORD was angry with Israel and said: because this people hath transgressed mine appointment which I commanded their fathers and have not obeyed my voice
Judges2:21 therefore henceforth I will not cast out one man before them of the nations which Josua left when he died
Judges2:22 and that to prove Israel through them whether they will keep the way of the LORD to walk therein as their fathers did or not.
Judges2:23 And so the LORD left those nations alone
Judges3:1 These are the nations which the LORD left to tempt Israel: even as many of Israel as had not known all the wars of Canaan:
Judges3:2 Onely for the learning of the generation of the children of Israel: which before knew nothing of war
Judges3:3 he left the five lords of the Philistines
Judges3:4 Those remained to prove Israel by
Judges3:5 And as the children of Israel dwelt among the Cananites
Judges3:6 they took the daughters of them to be their wives
Judges3:7 And so the children of Israel did wickedly in the sight of the LORD and forgot the LORD their God and served Baalim and Aseroth.
Judges3:8 Therefore the LORD was angry with Israel and delivered them into the hands of Chusan Rasathaim king of Mesopotamia. So that the children of Israel served Chusan Rasathaim eight years.
Judges3:9 And the children of Israel cried unto the LORD. And the LORD stirred them up a saver and saved them: one Othoniel the son of Kenes
Judges3:10 And the spirit of the LORD came upon him. And he judged Israel
Judges3:11 And the land had rest forty year. And Othoniel the son of Kenes died.
Judges3:12 And then the children of Israel went to again
Judges3:13 And this Eglon gathered unto him the children of Ammon
Judges3:14 And the children of Israel served Eglon the king of the Moabites eighteen year.
Judges3:15 And then they cried unto the LORD. And the LORD stirred them up a saver
Judges3:16 which Ahud made him a dagger with two edges
Judges3:17 and carried the present unto Eglon the king of the Moabites
Judges3:18 And when he had presented the present
Judges3:19 but he himself turned again from the Idols by Gilgal
Judges3:20 And Ahud came in unto him in a summer parlor
Judges3:21 And Ahud put forth his left hand and took the dagger from his right thigh and thrust it into his belly
Judges3:22 so that the haft went in after the blade. And the haft stopped in the fat
Judges3:23 But Ahud gat him out at a postern door and shut the doors of the parlor upon him and locked them.
Judges3:24 When he was gone out
Judges3:25 And when they had tarried till they were ashamed
Judges3:26 But Ahud escaped while they tarried and was gone beyond the Idols and escaped into Seirath.
Judges3:27 And when he was come he blew a trumpet in mount Ephraim. And the children of Israel went down with him off the hill and he before them.
Judges3:28 And he said unto them
Judges3:29 And they slew of the Moabites
Judges3:30 and so the Moabites were subdued that day

Judges6:3 For when Israel had sown then came the Madianites the Amalekites and they of the East country upon them and pitched their tents against them
Judges6:4 and destroyed the increase of the earth even unto Azah
Judges6:5 for they came with their cattle and households even as grasshoppers in multitude: so that both they and also their camels were without number. And they entered the land to destroy it.

Judges6:7 And when the children of Israel cried unto the LORD because of the Madianites

Judges6:17 And he answered him
Judges6:18 Depart not hence until I come again unto thee and bring mine offering

Judges6:21 Then the angel of the LORD put forth the end of the staff that was in his hand and touched the flesh and the cakes. And there arose up fire out of the rock and consumed the flesh and the cakes. And the angel of the LORD vanished out of his sight.

Judges6:27 Then Gedeon took ten men of his servants and did as the LORD bade him. But because he durst not do it by day for fear of his father's household and of the men of the city

Judges6:40 And God did so that same night: so that it was dry upon the fleece onely

Judges21:1 And the men of Israel sware in Mazphah saying: there shall none of us give his daughter unto any of Benjamin to wife.
Judges21:2 And the people came to Bethel and abode there till evening
Judges21:3 and said: O LORD God of Israel
Judges21:4 And on the morrow the people rose up be time and made there an altar and offered burntofferings and peaceofferings.
Judges21:5 And the children of Israel asked
Judges21:6 And the children of Israel had pity on Benjamin their brethren
Judges21:7 what shall we do unto the remnant of them
Judges21:8 Then they said
Judges21:9 And when the people were viewed: behold there were none of the inhabiters of Jabes Gilead there.
Judges21:10 And the congregation sent thither twelve thousand men of the strongest of them
Judges21:11 And this is that ye shall do: utterly destroy all the males and the women that have lien by men.
Judges21:12 And they found among the inhabiters of Jabes Gilead four hundredth damsels virgins that had known no man by lying with any male. And they brought them unto the host to Siloh which is in the land of Canaan.
Judges21:13 And the whole congregation sent and spake with the children of Benjamin that were in the Rock of pomegranates
Judges21:14 and Benjamin came again at that time
Judges21:15 And the people had compassion on Benjamin
Judges21:16 And then the elders of the congregation said: what shall we do to the remnant of them
Judges21:17 And they said there must be an inheritance for them that be escaped of Benjamin that a tribe be not destroyed out of Israel:
Judges21:18 how be it we may not give them wives of our daughters. For the children of Israel had made an adjuration saying: acursed be he that giveth a wife to Benjamin.
Judges21:19 Then they said: behold
Judges21:20 And they commanded the children of Benjamin saying: go and lie in wait in the vineyards.
Judges21:21 And when ye see that the daughters of Siloh come out to dance in a row
Judges21:22 And if their fathers or brethren come to us to complain
Judges21:23 And the children of Benjamin did even so: and took them wives according to the number of them of the dancers which they caught. And then they went and returned unto their inheritance
Judges21:24 And the children of Israel departed thence at that time and went every man to his tribe
Judges21:25 In those days there was no king in Israel: but every man did what seemed him right. [The end of the book of Judges.]

Ruth1:6 Then she stood up with her daughters-in-law and returned from the country of Moab: for she had heard say
Ruth1:7 Wherefore she departed out of the place where she was and her two daughters with her. And as they went by the way returning unto the land of Juda
Ruth1:8 Noemi said unto her two daughters-in-law: go and return each of you unto your mother's house: the LORD deal as kindly with you
Ruth1:9 And the LORD give(grant) you that you may find rest either of you in the house of her husband: and so she kissed them.
Ruth1:10 Then they cried and wept and said unto her we will go with thee unto thy folk.
Ruth1:11 But Noemi said
Ruth1:12 Turn again my daughters and go: for I am too old to have a husband. If I said I have hope

Ruth1:14 Therewith they cried and wept again: howbeit Orphah kissed her mother-in-law but Ruth abode still by her

Ruth1:20 But she said unto them
Ruth1:21 I went out full: but the LORD hath brought me home empty. Why should ye then call me Noemi: seeing the LORD hath humbled me

1Samuel1:1 There was a man of Ramathaim Zophim
1Samuel1:2 which had two wives
1Samuel1:3 And the said man went out of his city every feastful day
1Samuel1:4 And it fell on a day as Elkanah had offered that he gave to Phenennah his wife and to all her sons and daughters portions.
1Samuel1:5 But unto Hannah he gave a portion with a heavy cheer
1Samuel1:6 And thereto her enemy Phenennah vexed her a good in casting her in the teeth how the LORD had made her barren.
1Samuel1:7 And so did she year by year as oft as she went up to the house of the LORD. And so chafed her: wherefore Hannah wept and ate not.
1Samuel1:8 Then said Elkanah her husband to her: Hannah why weepest thou? and why eatest thou not? and why is thine heart so troubled? am not I better to thee
1Samuel1:9 Then Hannah rose up after that they had eaten and drunk in Siloh. And Eli the priest sat upon a stool by one of the side posts of the temple of the LORD.
1Samuel1:10 And she was troubled in her spirits
1Samuel1:11 and vowed a vow and said: LORD of Hosts
1Samuel1:12 And as she continued praying before the LORD
1Samuel1:13 For Hannah she spake in her heart
1Samuel1:14 and said unto her
1Samuel1:15 Hannah answered and said: not so my lord
1Samuel1:16 Count not thine handmaid to be like a daughter of unthriftiness: for out of the abundance of my meditation and grief have I spoken hitherto.
1Samuel1:17 Eli answered and said: go in peace
1Samuel1:18 Then she said
1Samuel1:19 And they rose up early and bowed themselves before the LORD
1Samuel1:20 And in process of time she conceived and bare a son and called his name Samuel: because she had asked him of the LORD.
1Samuel1:21 And Elkanah and all his house went up to offer unto the LORD
1Samuel1:22 but Hannah went not up for she said unto her husband: I will tarry until the lad be weaned
1Samuel1:23 And Elkanah her husband said to her: do what seemeth thee best: tarry until thou hast weaned him
1Samuel1:24 And then she took him with her
1Samuel1:25 And they slew the bullocks and brought in the lad to Eli
1Samuel1:26 and then she said: Oh my lord: as truly as thy soul liveth my lord: I am the woman that stood by thee here
1Samuel1:27 and for this lad I prayed
1Samuel1:28 and therefore I also lend him the LORD
1Samuel2:1 And Hannah prayed and said: mine heart rejoiceth in the LORD
1Samuel2:2 There is none so holy as the LORD: no there is none save thou. Neither is there any strength like unto our God.
1Samuel2:3 Talk not too much proudly: let old things depart out of your mouths
1Samuel2:4 The bows of the strong are broken: and the weak are endued with strength.
1Samuel2:5 They that were full
1Samuel2:6 The LORD killeth and maketh alive
1Samuel2:7 The LORD maketh poor
1Samuel2:8 He raiseth up the poor out of the dust
1Samuel2:9 He will keep the feet of his saints but the wicked shall keep silence in darkness. For in his own might shall no man be strong.
1Samuel2:10 The LORD's adversaries shall be made to fear him: and out of heaven he shall thunder upon them. The LORD shall judge the ends of the world
1Samuel2:11 And Elkanah went to Ramath to his house
1Samuel2:12 But the sons of Eli were unthrifty children and knew not the LORD. For the manner of the priests with the people was
1Samuel2:13 whensoever any man offered any offerings the priest's lad came
1Samuel2:14 and thrust it into the pan
1Samuel2:15 Yea: and thereto before they had offered the fat
1Samuel2:16 And if any man said unto him

1Samuel2:18 But the lad Samuel ministered before the LORD girded about with a linen Ephod.
1Samuel2:19 Moreover his mother made him a little coat
1Samuel2:20 And Eli blessed Elkanah and his wife
1Samuel2:21 And the LORD visited Hannah for that she conceived and bare three sons and two daughters. But the boy Samuel grew dwelling still with the LORD.
1Samuel2:22 Eli was very old and heard all that his sons did unto all Israel
1Samuel2:23 and said unto them: why do ye such things? For I hear your wicked deeds of all these people.

1Samuel2:28 And I chose thy father out of all the tribes of Israel to be my priest for to offer upon mine altar and to burn incense
1Samuel2:29 Wherefore kick ye against my sacrifice and against mine offering which I command in the tabernacle
1Samuel2:30 Wherefore the LORD God of Israel sayeth: I said that thine house and the house of thy father should have walked before me for ever. But now the LORD sayeth that be far from me: for them that worship(honoureth) me I will worship
1Samuel2:31 Behold the days will come that I will cut off thine arm and the arm of thy father's house

1Samuel2:33 Nevertheless I will not destroy all thy males from my altar
1Samuel2:34 And this shall be a sign unto thee
1Samuel2:35 And I will stir me up a faithful priest that shall do as it is in mine heart and in my mind. And I will build him a sure house. And he shall walk before mine anointed for ever.

1Samuel3:11 And the LORD said to Samuel: behold
1Samuel3:12 In that day I will perform to Eli
1Samuel3:13 For I have told him that I will judge his house for ever. For the wickedness which he knoweth
1Samuel3:14 And therefore I have sworn unto the house of Eli
1Samuel3:15 And Samuel lay till morning and then opened the doors of the house of the LORD. But Samuel feared
1Samuel3:16 Then Eli called Samuel and said: Samuel my son. And he answered here I am.
1Samuel3:17 And he said what is that he said unto thee? See ye hide it not from me. The LORD do so and so to thee
1Samuel3:18 And Samuel told him every whit and hid it not from him. And he answered: it is the LORD
1Samuel3:19 And Samuel grew
1Samuel3:20 And all Israel from Dan to Bersabe wist that Samuel was truly made the LORD's prophet.
1Samuel3:21 And the LORD appeared again in Siloh: for the LORD opened himself to Samuel in Siloh
1Samuel4:1 And Samuel spake unto all Israel. And Israel went out against the Philistines to battle
1Samuel4:2 And put themselves in array against Israel. And in the end of the battle Israel was put to the worse before the Philistines. And the Philistines slew in array along by the fields
1Samuel4:3 And when the people were come into their tents
1Samuel4:4 And the people sent to Siloh
1Samuel4:5 And when the ark of the appointment of the LORD came into the host
1Samuel4:6 When the Philistines heard the noise of the shout they said: what meaneth the sound of this mighty shout in the host of the Hebrews? And they understood how that the ark of the LORD was come into the host.
1Samuel4:7 And the Philistines were afraid
1Samuel4:8 Woe unto us
1Samuel4:9 Be strong and quite yourselves like men
1Samuel4:10 And the Philistines fought
1Samuel4:11 And the ark of God was taken
1Samuel4:12 And there ran a man of Ben Jamin out of the array and came to Siloh the same day
1Samuel4:13 And when the man came in
1Samuel4:14 When Eli heard the noise of the crying
1Samuel4:15 Eli was four score and eighteen year old
1Samuel4:16 And the man said unto Eli: I am he that came out of the array and fled out of the host this day. And he said how is it fortuned
1Samuel4:17 And the messenger answered and said: Israel is fled before the Philistines
1Samuel4:18 And when he made mention of the ark of God
1Samuel4:19 And his daughter-in-law Phinehes' wife was with child and nigh the birth. And when she heard the tidings of the taking of the ark of God
1Samuel4:20 And about the time of her death
1Samuel4:21 And she named the child Ichabod saying: honour is departed from Israel. Because the ark of God was taken
1Samuel4:22 And therefore she said
1Samuel5:1 And the Philistines took the ark of God and carried it from the help stone
1Samuel5:2 and brought it unto the house of Dagon
1Samuel5:3 And when they of Asdod were up in the morning behold
1Samuel5:4 And when they were up early in the next morning behold
1Samuel5:5 Wherefore neither the priests of Dagon
1Samuel5:6 But the hand of the LORD was heavy upon them of Asdod
1Samuel5:7 And when the men of Asdod saw that it was so
1Samuel5:8 And so they sent and gathered all the lords of the Philistines unto them and said: what shall we do with the ark of the God of Israel? Then said they of Geth let the ark of the God of Israel be carried about. And they carried the ark of the God of Israel about.
1Samuel5:9 And when they had carried it about
1Samuel5:10 Then they sent the ark of God to Akaron. And as soon as the ark of God came to Akaron
1Samuel5:11 Then they sent and fetched all the lords of the Philistines unto them and said: send away the ark of the God of Israel
1Samuel5:12 insomuch that they which died not
1Samuel6:1 And so when ark of the LORD had been in the country of the Philistines seven months
1Samuel6:2 the Philistines called for the priests and the soothsayers saying: what shall we do with the ark of the LORD? tell us wherewith we shall send it home again.
1Samuel6:3 They answered if you send the ark of the God of Israel home again send it not empty: But reward it with a trespass offering: and then ye shall be whole
1Samuel6:4 Then said they
1Samuel6:5 Wherefore ye shall make images like to your arses with Emerods and images like to your mice that destroyed your land
1Samuel6:6 Wherefore should ye harden your hearts as the Egyptians and Pharao hardened their hearts
1Samuel6:7 Now therefore make a new cart and take two milch kine
1Samuel6:8 Then take the ark of the LORD and put it in the cart
1Samuel6:9 And mark if he go up by the way that leadeth unto his own coast
1Samuel6:10 And the men did even so: they took two kine that gave milk and tied them in the cart
1Samuel6:11 and they laid the ark of the LORD upon the cart and the forcer with the mice of gold and the images of their arses with Emerods.
1Samuel6:12 And the kine took the straight way to Bethsames
1Samuel6:13 And they of Bethsames were reaping their wheat harvest in the valley. And they lifted up their eyes and spied the ark. And rejoiced when they saw it.
1Samuel6:14 And the cart came into the grove of one Jehosua a Bethsamite
1Samuel6:15 And the Levites took down the ark of the LORD and the forcer that was thereby
1Samuel6:16 And when the five lords of the Philistines had seen it
1Samuel6:17 These are the golden arses with Emerods which the Philistines gave to amends for a trespass offering to the LORD: for Asdod one: for Gaza one: for Askalon one: for Geth one: and for Akaron one.
1Samuel6:18 And the golden mice were according to the number of all the cities of the Philistines thorow the five lordships: both of walled towns and of towns unwailed
1Samuel6:19 And he plagued the men of Bethsames
1Samuel6:20 And the men of Bethsames said who is able to stand before the LORD so holy a God and to whom shall he go from us?
1Samuel6:21 And they sent messengers to the inhabiters of Kariath Jarim saying: The Philistines have brought home again the Ark of the LORD: come down and fetch it up to you.
1Samuel7:1 And the men of Kariathjarim came and set up the ark of the LORD
1Samuel7:2 And while the ark abode in Kariathjarim
1Samuel7:3 And Samuel spake unto all Israel saying: if ye be come again unto the LORD with all your hearts
1Samuel7:4 And then the children of Israel did put away Baalim and Astharoth
1Samuel7:5 Then said Samuel: gather all Israel to Mazphah
1Samuel7:6 And they gathered together to Mazphah
1Samuel7:7 When the Philistines heard
1Samuel7:8 and said to Samuel: cease not to cry unto the LORD our God for us
1Samuel7:9 And Samuel took a sucking lamb and offered it altogether for a burnt offering
1Samuel7:10 And as Samuel offered the burnt offering
1Samuel7:11 And the men of Israel issued out of Mazphah and pursued the Philistines and slew them
1Samuel7:12 And the Samuel took a stone and pitched it between Mazphah and Sen
1Samuel7:13 And so the Philistines were brought under that they came no more into the coasts of Israel: for the hand of the LORD was upon the Philistines all the days of Samuel.
1Samuel7:14 Thereto the cities which the Philistines had taken from Israel came again to Israel
1Samuel7:15 And Samuel judged Israel all the days of his life
1Samuel7:16 and went about year by year
1Samuel7:17 for there was his house
1Samuel8:1 When Samuel was old
1Samuel8:2 The name of his eldest son was Joel
1Samuel8:3 Nevertheless his sons followed not his steps: but turned aside after lucre and took rewards
1Samuel8:4 Then all the elders of Israel gathered them together and came to Samuel unto Kamath
1Samuel8:5 and said unto him: behold
1Samuel8:6 But the thing displeased Samuel
1Samuel8:7 And the LORD said unto Samuel
1Samuel8:8 And as they have ever done (since I brought them out of Egypt unto this day
1Samuel8:9 Now therefore hearken unto their voice: howbeit yet
1Samuel8:10 And Samuel told all the words of the LORD unto the people that asked a king of him
1Samuel8:11 and he said: this shall be the duty of the king that shall reign over you: he will take your sons and put them to his chariots and make his horsemen of them and they must run before his chariot
1Samuel8:12 and will make him captains of them over thousands and over fifties
1Samuel8:13 And he will take your daughters and make them the dressers of his ointments
1Samuel8:14 And he shall take the best of your fields
1Samuel8:15 And he shall take the tenth of your seed and of your vines
1Samuel8:16 And he shall take the best of your menservants and maidservants and young men
1Samuel8:17 And he shall take the tenth of your sheep
1Samuel8:18 And when ye cry out at that time upon your king which ye shall have chosen you
1Samuel8:19 Nevertheless the people would not hear the voice of Samuel
1Samuel8:20 and we will be like all other nations. And our king shall judge us
1Samuel8:21 And Samuel heard all the words of the people and rehearsed them in the ears of the LORD.
1Samuel8:22 And the LORD said to Samuel: hearken unto their voice and make them a king. Then said Samuel unto the men of Israel: go every man to his city.
1Samuel9:1 Now there was a man of Benjamin named Cis the son of Abiel
1Samuel9:2 the same had a son called Saul
1Samuel9:3 And it chanced that the Asses of this Cis Saul's father were lost. Then said Cis to Saul his son: Take one of the lads with thee and up and go seek the Asses.
1Samuel9:4 And they went thorow mount Ephraim and thorow the land of Salisa
1Samuel9:5 Then when they were come to the land of Zuph
1Samuel9:6 behold there is in this city a worshipful man of God
1Samuel9:7 Then said Saul to his lad: if we go what shall we bring the man? For our bread is all spent out of our hampers and there is none other present to bring the man of God
1Samuel9:8 And the young man answered Saul again and said: I have found about me the fourth part of a Sicle
1Samuel9:9 Before time in Israel when a man went to seek an answer of God
1Samuel9:10 Then said Saul to his young man: well said of thee: come let us go. And so they went unto the city where the man of God was.
1Samuel9:11 And as they were going up into the city
1Samuel9:12 And the maidens answered them
1Samuel9:13 When ye be come into the city
1Samuel9:14 And they went up into the city. And when they were come into the midst of the city: behold
1Samuel9:15 But the LORD had told Samuel a day before Saul came
1Samuel9:16 tomorrow this time I will send thee a man out of the land of Benjamin
1Samuel9:17 When Samuel saw Saul
1Samuel9:18 Then went Saul to Samuel in the middle of the gate
1Samuel9:19 and Samuel answered Saul and said: I am the sear
1Samuel9:20 and as for thine asses that were lost this day three days
1Samuel9:21 But Saul answered and said: am not I the son of a Jeminite of the smallest tribe of Israel
1Samuel9:22 And Samuel took Saul and his lad and brought them into the parlour and made them sit in the chiefest place among them that were bidden: which were upon a thirty persons.
1Samuel9:23 Then said Samuel unto the cook: bring forth the portion which I gave thee
1Samuel9:24 And the cook took up the shoulder and brought it
1Samuel9:25 And when they were come down from the hill unto the city
1Samuel9:26 and they arose early. And about the spring of the day Samuel called Saul upon the top of the house saying: up that I may send thee away. And Saul arose. And they went out at the doors both of them
1Samuel9:27 And when they were come almost out of the town
1Samuel10:1 And then Samuel took a box of oil and poured it upon his head and kissed him and said: the LORD hath anointed thee to be a captain over his inheritance.
1Samuel10:2 And now when thou art departed from me
1Samuel10:3 Then thou shalt go forth from thence and shalt come to the oak of Thabor. And there shalt thou meet three men going to God to Bethel: one carrying three kids: and another carrying three loaves of bread: and the third carrying a bottle of wine.
1Samuel10:4 And they will salute thee and give thee two loaves of bread: which thou shalt receive of their hands.
1Samuel10:5 After that thou shalt come to the hill of God
1Samuel10:6 And the spirit of the LORD will come upon thee
1Samuel10:7 And when these signs are chanced thee
1Samuel10:8 And thou shalt also go before me to Gilgal. And behold I will come unto thee to sacrifice burnt sacrifice and peace offerings. Tarry for me seven days till I come to thee and shew thee what thou shalt do.
1Samuel10:9 And as soon as he had turned his shoulder to go from Samuel
1Samuel10:10 When they came to the hill: behold the company of prophets met him
1Samuel10:11 And all that knew him before
1Samuel10:12 And one of the same place answered and said: who is their father? And thereof sprang a proverb: what is Saul also among the prophets?
1Samuel10:13 And when he had made an end of prophesying
1Samuel10:14 Saul's father's brother said unto him and his lad: whither went ye? And he answered: to seek the asses
1Samuel10:15 Then said Saul's uncle: tell me what Samuel said unto you?
1Samuel10:16 And Saul answered his uncle: he told us that the asses were found. But of the kingdom whereof Samuel spake told he him not.
1Samuel10:17 After that Samuel called the people together unto the LORD to Mazphah
1Samuel10:18 and said unto the children of Israel: thus sayeth the LORD God of Israel
1Samuel10:19 And ye have this day cast away your God that holp you out of all your adversities and tribulations. And ye have said unto him
1Samuel10:20 And when Samuel had brought all the tribes of Israel the tribe of Benjamin was caught.
1Samuel10:21 When he had brought the tribe of Benjamin by their kindreds
1Samuel10:22 Then they asked the LORD further: whither the man should come thither. And the LORD answered: behold
1Samuel10:23 And they ran and fetched him thence. And when he stood among the people
1Samuel10:24 And Samuel said to all the people: there see ye whom the LORD hath chosen and how there is none like him among all the company. And all the people shouted and said: God send the king life.
1Samuel10:25 Then Samuel told the people the duty of the kingdom
1Samuel10:26 And Saul also went home to Gabaah. And there went with him an host
1Samuel10:27 But the children of unthriftiness said: how shall he save us? and despised him and brought him not presents. And he made as though he had not heard it.
1Samuel11:1 Then Nahas the Ammonite came and beseiged Jabes in Gilead. And all the people of Jabes said unto Nahas:
1Samuel11:2 make a covenant with us and we will be thy servants. And Nahas the Ammonite said: hereto will I make a covenant with you
1Samuel11:3 Then said the elders of Jabes: give us seven days respite that we may send messengers unto all the coasts of Israel. And then if there come no man to help us
1Samuel11:4 Then came the messengers to Gabaah where Saul dwelt
1Samuel11:5 And behold Saul came following his oxen out of the field
1Samuel11:6 Then came the spirit of God upon Saul
1Samuel11:7 And took a yoke of oxen and hewed them in pieces
1Samuel11:8 And when they were numbered in Bezek the children of Israel were three hundredth thousand
1Samuel11:9 And they said unto the messengers that came
1Samuel11:10 And then the men of Jabes said: tomorrow we will come out unto you
1Samuel11:11 And on the morrow Saul put the people in three parts. And they came in upon the host in the morning watch
1Samuel11:12 Then said the people to Samuel: what are they that said: shall Saul reign over us? bring them that we may slay them.
1Samuel11:13 But Saul said: there shall no man die this day
1Samuel11:14 Then said Samuel unto the people: come and let us go to Gilgal
1Samuel11:15 And the people went to Gilgal and made Saul king there
1Samuel12:1 Then said Samuel unto all Israel: behold I have obeyed your voice in all that ye said unto me
1Samuel12:2 And see your king walketh before you. But I am old and gray headed: and behold my sons are with you and I have walked before you from my childhood unto this day.
1Samuel12:3 Behold here I am: answer me before the LORD and before his anointed
1Samuel12:4 And they said thou hast done us no wrong
1Samuel12:5 Then said he to them: the LORD is witness unto you
1Samuel12:6 Then said Samuel unto the people it is the LORD that made Moses and Aaron
1Samuel12:7 Now therefore stand still
1Samuel12:8 After that Jacob was come into Egypt
1Samuel12:9 Neverthelater they forgot the LORD their God. And he sold them into the hand of Sisara chief captain of Jabin king of Nazor
1Samuel12:10 Then they cried unto the LORD and said: we have sinned
1Samuel12:11 And the LORD sent Jerobaal
1Samuel12:12 And for all that when you saw that Nahas the king of the children of Ammon came against you
1Samuel12:13 Now therefore behold the king
1Samuel12:14 Oh that ye would fear the LORD and serve him and hear his voice and not disobey the mouth of the LORD: and that both ye and the king that reigneth over you would follow the LORD your God.
1Samuel12:15 For if ye shall not hearken unto the voice of the LORD
1Samuel12:16 Now also stand and see this great thing which the LORD will do before your eyes:
1Samuel12:17 is it not now wheat harvest. And yet for all that
1Samuel12:18 And when Samuel called unto the LORD
1Samuel12:19 Then said all the people unto Samuel: pray for thy servants unto the LORD thy God
1Samuel12:20 Then said Samuel unto the people: fear not. And though ye have done all this wickedness yet depart not from the LORD in any case. But serve him with all your hearts.
1Samuel12:21 Neither turn ye after vain things which can not deliver you
1Samuel12:22 But the LORD will not forsake his people
1Samuel12:23 Moreover God forbid that I should sin against the LORD in leaving praying for you and to shew you the good and right way.
1Samuel12:24 Only fear you the LORD and serve him truly with all your hearts: for he hath done great things for you.
1Samuel12:25 But and if ye shall do wickedly: then shall both ye and your king thereto perish.
1Samuel13:1 Saul was as a child of a year old
1Samuel13:2 he chose him three thousand men out of Israel. Two thousand were with Saul in Machmas and mount Bethel
1Samuel13:3 And Jonathas slew the Philistines in an hold they had in Gabaah
1Samuel13:4 And all Israel heard say
1Samuel13:5 Then the Philistines gathered themselves together to fight with Israel
1Samuel13:6 And when the men of Israel saw themselves in a strait
1Samuel13:7 And the Hebrews went over Jordan unto the land of Gad and Gilead. But Saul was yet in Gilgal
1Samuel13:8 And he tarried seven days
1Samuel13:9 Wherefore Saul said: bring burnt sacrifice to me and peace offerings. And he offered burnt sacrifice.
1Samuel13:10 And as soon as he had made an end of offering burnt offerings behold
1Samuel13:11 And Saul said
1Samuel13:12 then said I
1Samuel13:13 Then said Samuel to Saul: thou hast done foolishly and hast not kept the commandment of the LORD thy God which he commanded thee. For at this time would the LORD have stablished thy kingdom upon Israel for ever.
1Samuel13:14 But now thy kingdom shall not continue. The LORD hath sought him a man after his own heart
1Samuel13:15 And Samuel arose and gat him from Gilgal to Gabaah Benjamin. And Saul numbered the people that were found with him
1Samuel13:16 And Saul and Jonathas his son and the people that were found with them
1Samuel13:17 And there came out of the host of the Philistines three companies
1Samuel13:18 And another company turned the way to Bethoron. And the third company turned to the way of the coast that turneth to the valley of Zeboim toward the wilderness.
1Samuel13:19 But there was no smith thorowout the land of Israel. For the Philistines thought that then the Hebrews might make them swords or spears.
1Samuel13:20 And therefore must all Israel go down to the Philistines
1Samuel13:21 as oft as the edges of the sickles
1Samuel13:22 And so in time of battle there was neither sword nor spear found in the hands of any of the people that were with Saul and Jonathas: save for Saul and Jonathas his son was there somewhat found.
1Samuel13:23 And the Garrison of the Philistines came out and stood on the other side before Machmas.
1Samuel14:1 And it fell on a day
1Samuel14:2 But Saul tarried in the utmost part of Gabaah under a pomegranate tree
1Samuel14:3 And Ahiah the son of Ahitob Ichabod's brother
1Samuel14:4 And in the way over by which Jonathas sought to go over unto the garrison of the philistines
1Samuel14:5 And the one leaneth northward toward Machmas
1Samuel14:6 Then said Jonathas to the young man that bare his harness: come and let us go over unto the standing of these uncircumcised
1Samuel14:7 And his harness bearer said unto him do all that is in thine heart: set thee forward: and see I am with thee
1Samuel14:8 Then said Jonathas: behold when we go over unto the men
1Samuel14:9 if they say on this wise to us: tarry until we come to you
1Samuel14:10 But and if they so say unto us: come up unto us
1Samuel14:11 And when they had both shewed themselves unto the garrison of the Philistines
1Samuel14:12 And the men of the garrison answered Jonathas and his harness bearer and said: come up to us
1Samuel14:13 And Jonathas clame upon hands and feet
1Samuel14:14 And that first slaughter which Jonathas and his harness bearer made was upon a twenty men
1Samuel14:15 And there was a fear in the host that was in the field
1Samuel14:16 And the watchmen of Saul in Gabaah Benjamin
1Samuel14:17 Then said Saul unto the people that was with him: number and see who is gone away from us. And when they had told: behold
1Samuel14:18 And then Saul said unto Ahia: bring hither the ark of God. For the ark of God was at the time with the children of Israel.
1Samuel14:19 And while Saul talked unto the priest
1Samuel14:20 And Saul cried and all the people that were with him
1Samuel14:21 Moreover the Hebrews that were with the Philistines before that time
1Samuel14:22 And they also which had hid themselves in mount Ephraim
1Samuel14:23 And so GOD holp Israel that day. And the battle continued until they came unto Bethaven.
1Samuel14:24 And the men of Israel joined themselves together that day: and Saul adjured the people saying: Cursed be he that eateth any food until night
1Samuel14:25 And all the land came to a wood where honey lay upon the ground.
1Samuel14:26 And when the people were come into the wood: Behold
1Samuel14:27 But Jonathas heard not when his father adjured the people
1Samuel14:28 Then spake one of the people and said: thy father adjured the people saying: Cursed be the man that eateth any sustenance this day
1Samuel14:29 Then said Jonathas: my father hath troubled the land: for see
1Samuel14:30 how then if all the people had eaten of the spoil of their enemies which they found
1Samuel14:31 And they laid on the Philistines that day
1Samuel14:32 Then the people gat them to the spoil and took sheep oxen and calves
1Samuel14:33 Then men told Saul saying: Behold the people sin against the LORD
1Samuel14:34 and go abroad among the people and bid them bring every man his ox and every man his sheep
1Samuel14:35 And Saul made an altar unto the LORD. And that was the first altar that he made unto the LORD.
1Samuel14:36 And Saul said: let us go down after the Philistines by night
1Samuel14:37 And Saul asked of God: shall I go down after the Philistines? and wilt thou deliver them into mine hands? But he answered him not at that time.
1Samuel14:38 Then said Saul let the people come hither out of all quarters
1Samuel14:39 for as truly as the LORD liveth which hath saved Israel
1Samuel14:40 Then he said unto all Israel: be ye on one side
1Samuel14:41 And Saul said unto the LORD God of Israel: give perfect knowledge. And Saul and Jonathas were caught
1Samuel14:42 Then said Saul: cast lots between me and Jonathas my son. And Jonathas was caught.
1Samuel14:43 Then Saul said to Jonathas: tell me what thou hast done. And Jonathas told him and said: I tasted a little honey upon the end of my staff that was in mine hand
1Samuel14:44 Then said Saul God do so and so to me
1Samuel14:45 But the people said unto Saul: shallJonathas die which hath so mightily holp Israel? God forbid. As truly as the LORD liveth
1Samuel14:46 And then Saul departed from following the Philistines. And the Philistines went to their own place.
1Samuel14:47 And so Saul took the kingdom over Israel
1Samuel14:48 And whether soever he turned himself
1Samuel14:49 The sons of Saul were
1Samuel14:50 And the name of Saul's wife was Ahinoam the daughter of Ahimaaz. And the name of his chief captain was Abner the son of Ner Saul's uncle.
1Samuel14:51 And Cis was Saul's father. And Ner the father of Abner was the son of Abiel.
1Samuel14:52 And there was sore war with the Philistines
1Samuel15:1 Then said Samuel unto Saul: the LORD sent me to anoint thee king over his people Israel. Now therefore obey thou the voice of the words of the LORD.
1Samuel15:2 Thus sayeth the LORD of Hosts: I have called to remembrance that which Amalek did to Israel how they lay in wait for them in the way
1Samuel15:3 Now therefore go and smite the Amalekites
1Samuel15:4 And Saul told it the people
1Samuel15:5 And Saul came unto a city of the Amalekites
1Samuel15:6 But Saul said unto the Kenites go and depart and get you down from among the Amalekites
1Samuel15:7 And Saul slew the Amalekites from Hevila to Sur that lieth before Egypt
1Samuel15:8 and took Agag the king of the Amalekites alive
1Samuel15:9 But Saul and the people spared Agag
1Samuel15:10 Then came the word of the LORD unto Samuel saying:
1Samuel15:11 it repenteth me that I have made Saul king. For he is turned from me and hath not performed my commandments
1Samuel15:12 And Samuel rose early
1Samuel15:13 And when Samuel was come to Saul
1Samuel15:14 And Samuel answered: what meaneth then the bleating of the sheep in mine ears
1Samuel15:15 And Saul said: they have brought them from the Amalekites. For the people spared the best of the sheep and of the oxen
1Samuel15:16 But Samuel said to Saul: cease and let me tell thee what the LORD hath said to me this night. And he said unto him: say on.
1Samuel15:17 And Samuel said when thou wast little in thine own sight
1Samuel15:18 And then the LORD sent thee on a journey
1Samuel15:19 And wherefore hast thou now not obeyed the voice of the LORD: But didst turn to the prey and hast wrought wickedness in the sight of the LORD?
1Samuel15:20 And Saul said unto Samuel: I have obeyed the voice of the LORD
1Samuel15:21 And the people took of the spoil
1Samuel15:22 Then said Samuel: hath the LORD as great pleasure in burnt sacrifices and offerings
1Samuel15:23 For rebelliousness is as the sin of witchcraft
1Samuel15:24 Then said Saul to Samuel: I have sinned for I have trespassed the mouth of the LORD
1Samuel15:25 But now take away my sin
1Samuel15:26 Then said Samuel unto Saul: I will not return with thee: for thou hast cast away the bidding of God
1Samuel15:27 And as Samuel turned to go away
1Samuel15:28 Then Samuel said
1Samuel15:29 And thereto he that giveth victory to Israel
1Samuel15:30 Then he said: I have sinned. But yet honour me before the elders of my people
1Samuel15:31 And Samuel turned again and followed Saul. And Saul prayed unto the LORD.
1Samuel15:32 Then said Samuel: Bring ye hither to me
1Samuel15:33 And Samuel said: As thy sword hath made women childless
1Samuel15:34 And then Samuel departed to Ramath. And Saul went home to his house to Gabaah Saul.
1Samuel15:35 But Samuel came no more to see Saul until the day of his death. Nevertheless Samuel mourned for Saul
1Samuel16:1 And then the LORD said unto Samuel: How long wilt thou mourn for Saul
1Samuel16:2 But Samuel answered: how shall I go? for Saul shall hear it and will kill me. And the LORD said: Take an heifer with thee
1Samuel16:3 And call Isai to the offering
1Samuel16:4 And Samuel did as the LORD bade him. And when he came to Bethlehem
1Samuel16:5 and he said yea
1Samuel16:6 And when they were come
1Samuel16:7 But the LORD said unto Samuel: look not on his fashion nor on the height of his stature
1Samuel16:8 Then Isai called Abinadab and made him come before Samuel. And he said: neither hath the LORD chosen this.
1Samuel16:9 Then Isai made Samah come
1Samuel16:10 Then made Isai seven of his sons come before Samuel. And Samuel said
1Samuel16:11 Then said Samuel to Isai: are here all thy children? and he said: the youngest is yet behind: Behold
1Samuel16:12 And he sent and brought him in. And he was brown with goodly eyes
1Samuel16:13 And Samuel took the horn with the ointment and anointed him in the presence of his brethren. And the spirit of the LORD came upon David
1Samuel16:14 But the spirit of the LORD departed from Saul
1Samuel16:15 Then said his servants unto him: Behold
1Samuel16:16 let our lord therefore command his servants to seek a man that is a cunning player with an harp. And then when the evil spirit sent of God
1Samuel16:17 And Saul said unto his servants: seek me a man that can well play
1Samuel16:18 Then answered one of his servants and said: Behold
1Samuel16:19 whereupon Saul sent messengers unto Isai and said: send me David thy son which is with the sheep.
1Samuel16:20 And Isai took an ass laden with bread
1Samuel16:21 And David went to Saul and came before him
1Samuel16:22 And Saul sent to Isai saying: let David remain with me
1Samuel16:23 And when the spirit of God came upon Saul
1Samuel17:1 The Philistines gathered their host to battle
1Samuel17:2 And Saul and the men of Israel came and pitched in Oakdale and put themselves in array
1Samuel17:3 And the Philistines stood on an hill on the one side
1Samuel17:4 And then came a man and stood in the midst
1Samuel17:5 and had an helmet of brass upon his head
1Samuel17:6 And he had harness of brass upon his legs
1Samuel17:7 And the shaft of his spear was like a weaver's beam. And his spearhead weighed six hundredth sicles of iron. And one bearing a shield went before him.
1Samuel17:8 And he stood and called unto the host of Israel
1Samuel17:9 if he be able to fight with me and to beat me
1Samuel17:10 And the Philistine said: I have defied the host of Israel this day give me a man and let us fight together.
1Samuel17:11 When Saul and all Israel heard those words of the Philistine
1Samuel17:12 And this David was the son of an Ephrathite of Bethlehem Juda
1Samuel17:13 And the three eldest sons of Isai went and followed Saul to battle. And the names of his three sons that went to battle were: Eliab the eldest
1Samuel17:14 and David was the youngest. And when the three eldest were gone after Saul
1Samuel17:15 David went and departed from Saul
1Samuel17:16 And the Philistine came forth every morning and evening
1Samuel17:17 And Isai said unto David his son: take for thy brethren this Epha of parched corn
1Samuel17:18 And carry these ten fresh cheeses unto the captain
1Samuel17:19 And Saul and they and all the men of Israel were in oak valley fighting with the Philistines.
1Samuel17:20 And David rose up early in the morning and left the sheep with a keeper
1Samuel17:21 for Israel and the Philistines had put themselves in array
1Samuel17:22 Then David put the pannier from him
1Samuel17:23 And as he talked with them: Behold
1Samuel17:24 And all the men of Israel
1Samuel17:25 And every man of Israel said: See ye this man that is come forth
1Samuel17:26 Then spake David to the men that stood by and said: What shall be done to the man that beateth this Philistine and taketh away the shame from Israel? for what is this uncircumcised Philistine
1Samuel17:27 And the people answered as it is rehearsed saying: so shall it be done to the man that beateth him.
1Samuel17:28 And Eliab his eldest brother heard when he spake unto the man and was angry with David and said: Why camest thou away
1Samuel17:29 And David answered
1Samuel17:30 And departed from by him into another front
1Samuel17:31 And they that heard the words which David spake
1Samuel17:32 And David said to Saul: Let no man's heart fail him because of him. Thy servant will go and fight with his Philistine.
1Samuel17:33 And Saul said to David again
1Samuel17:34 Then said David unto Saul
1Samuel17:35 And I went out after him and smote him
1Samuel17:36 For both a Lion and also a Bear hath thy servant slain. And this uncircumcised Philistine shall be as one of them
1Samuel17:37 And David spake moreover
1Samuel17:38 And Saul put his raiment upon David
1Samuel17:39 and gird David with his own sword upon his raiment. And he assayed to go
1Samuel17:40 and took his staff in his hand
1Samuel17:41 And the Philistine came and drew near to David
1Samuel17:42 And when the Philistine looked and saw David: he disdained him
1Samuel17:43 And the Philistine said unto David: am I a dog
1Samuel17:44 And he said to David: come to me and I will give thy flesh unto the fowls of the air
1Samuel17:45 Then said David to the Philistine thou comest to me with a sword a spear and a shield: But I come to thee in the name of the LORD of Hosts
1Samuel17:46 This day shall the LORD deliver thee into my hand
1Samuel17:47 And all this congregation shall know
1Samuel17:48 And when the Philistine arose and came and drew nigh unto David: David hasted and ran in array even against the Philistine.
1Samuel17:49 And David put his hand in his poke and took out a stone and slung it
1Samuel17:50 And so David overcame the Philistine with a sling and a stone
1Samuel17:51 he ran and stood upon the Philistine
1Samuel17:52 And the men of Israel and of Juda arose and shouted and followed after the Philistines
1Samuel17:53 And then the children of Israel returned from chasing after the Philistines and robbed their tents.
1Samuel17:54 And David took the head of the Philistine and brought it to Jerusalem: But he put his armour in his tent.
1Samuel17:55 When Saul saw David go forth against the Philistine
1Samuel17:56 Then said the king: Enquire thou
1Samuel17:57 And so when David was returned from the slaughter of the Philistine
1Samuel17:58 And Saul said to him: whose son art thou
1Samuel18:1 And when he had made an end of speaking unto Saul
1Samuel18:2 And Saul took him that day and would let him go no more home to his father's house.
1Samuel18:3 And Jonathas and David bound themselves the one to the other
1Samuel18:4 And Jonathas put off his own coat that was upon him
1Samuel18:5 And David went out to all that Saul sent him
1Samuel18:6 And it happened as they went
1Samuel18:7 And the women that played sang thereto
1Samuel18:8 Then was Saul exceeding wroth and that saying displeased him
1Samuel18:9 Wherefore Saul looked on side of David from that day forward.
1Samuel18:10 And it happened on the morrow
1Samuel18:11 And Saul had a spear in his hand
1Samuel18:12 For Saul was afeard of David
1Samuel18:13 And then Saul put David from him and made him a captain over a thousand
1Samuel18:14 And David was wise in all that he took in hand
1Samuel18:15 Wherefore when Saul saw that he was so exceeding wise
1Samuel18:16 But all Israel and Juda loved David
1Samuel18:17 Then said Saul to David. Behold my eldest daughter Merob
1Samuel18:18 And David answered Saul: what am I? and what is my life or the kindred of my father in Israel
1Samuel18:19 How be it when the time was come that Merob Saul's daughter should have been given to David she was given unto Adziel a Neholothite
1Samuel18:20 How be it Michol Saul's daughter loved David. And when it was shewed Saul: the thing pleased him well.
1Samuel18:21 And he said: I will give him her that she may be a snare to him
1Samuel18:22 And Saul commanded his servants to commune with David secretly and say: Behold the king hath a favour to thee
1Samuel18:23 And Saul's servants spake those words in the ears of David. But David answered: seemeth it to you a light thing to be the king's son-in-law
1Samuel18:24 And Saul's servants told him again saying: of this manner answered David.
1Samuel18:25 Then said Saul: this wise say to David: the king careth for no other dowry but for an hundredth foreskins of the Philistines
1Samuel18:26 Then his servants told David these words
1Samuel18:27 And shortly after that David arose with his men
1Samuel18:28 And when Saul saw and understood
1Samuel18:29 he was the more afraid of David
1Samuel18:30 And when the Philistines went out to war
1Samuel19:1 Then Saul communed with Jonathas his son
1Samuel19:2 and told David saying: Saul my father goeth about to slay thee. Now therefore take heed to thyself betimes and abide in some secret place and hide thyself.
1Samuel19:3 And I will go out and stand by my father in the field where thou art
1Samuel19:4 And Jonathas spake the best of David unto Saul his father and said unto him: let not the king sin against his servant David
1Samuel19:5 For he did put his life in his hand and slew the Philistine
1Samuel19:6 And Saul hearkened unto the voice of Jonathas and sware: as truly as the LORD liveth
1Samuel19:7 Then Jonathas called David and shewed him all those words
1Samuel19:8 And the war began again
1Samuel19:9 And the evil spirit of the LORD was upon Saul as he sat in his house having a Javelin in his hand
1Samuel19:10 And Saul intended to nail David to the wall with the Javelin: But David rid himself out of Saul's presence and he smote the spear into the wall. But David fled and saved himself that same night.
1Samuel19:11 Then Saul sent messengers unto David's house
1Samuel19:12 And so Michol let David down thorow a window
1Samuel19:13 And then she took an Image and laid it in the bed
1Samuel19:14 And when Saul sent messengers to fetch David
1Samuel19:15 Then Saul sent the messengers to see David saying: bring him to me
1Samuel19:16 And when the messengers were come in: Behold there lay an Image in the bed
1Samuel19:17 Then said Saul to Michol: why hast thou mocked me so
1Samuel19:18 And so David fled and escaped and went to Samuel to Ramath and told him all that Saul had done to him. And he
1Samuel19:19 And it was told Saul saying: Behold
1Samuel19:20 then Saul sent messengers to fetch David. And when they saw a company of prophets prophesying and Samuel standing fast by them
1Samuel19:21 And when it was told Saul
1Samuel19:22 Then went he himself to Ramath
1Samuel19:23 and as he went thither to Naioth in Ramath the spirit of God came upon him also and he went prophesying
1Samuel19:24 And he stript off his clothes and prophesied before Samuel in like manner
1Samuel20:1 And David fled from Naioth in Ramath and went and said before Jonathas: What have I done? wherein am I faulty? what is the sin that I have committed before thy father that he seeketh my life?
1Samuel20:2 And Jonathas answered him: God forbid
1Samuel20:3 And David sware again and said: thy father knoweth that I have found grace in thine eyes and therefore he thinketh
1Samuel20:4 Then said Jonathas unto David
1Samuel20:5 And David said unto Jonathas: Behold
1Samuel20:6 If thy father miss me
1Samuel20:7 And if thy father say thus: it is well done
1Samuel20:8 And then thou shalt shew mercy unto thy servant
1Samuel20:9 And Jonathas answered: God keep that from thee
1Samuel20:10 Then said David
1Samuel20:11 Then said Jonathas to David
1Samuel20:12 And Jonathas said unto David: O LORD God of Israel
1Samuel20:13 And in like manner
1Samuel20:14 And thou shalt perform unto me the mercy of the LORD
1Samuel20:15 No not when the LORD hath destroyed the enemies of David every one from the face of the earth.
1Samuel20:16 And so Jonathas made a bond with the house of David
1Samuel20:17 And with other words Jonathas adjured David
1Samuel20:18 Then said Jonathas to David: tomorrow shall be the first day of the moon. And thou shalt be missed
1Samuel20:19 But this day three days come in any wise unto the place where thou shalt hide thy self
1Samuel20:20 And I will shoot three arrows by the one side thereof
1Samuel20:21 If I say unto the lad: see the arrows are on this side thee
1Samuel20:22 But and if I say thus unto the young fellow
1Samuel20:23 And of this which thou and I have spoke: behold the LORD is witness between thee and me for ever.
1Samuel20:24 And so David hid himself in the field. And when the new moon was come
1Samuel20:25 And the king sat him down after the old manner
1Samuel20:26 Nevertheless yet Saul said nothing at all that day. For he thought something had chanced him that he is not clean.
1Samuel20:27 But on the morrow which was the second day of the moon
1Samuel20:28 And Jonathas answered unto Saul
1Samuel20:29 let me go I pray thee
1Samuel20:30 Then was Saul angry with Jonathas and said unto him: O froward and rebellious
1Samuel20:31 For as long as the son of Isai lieth upon the earth
1Samuel20:32 But Jonathas answered Saul his father and said to him: wherefore should he die? what hath he done?
1Samuel20:33 Then Saul cast a spear at him to hit him
1Samuel20:34 And so Jonathas arose from the table in a great anger and did eat no meat the second day of the month
1Samuel20:35 On the next morning Jonathas went out unto the field
1Samuel20:36 And he said unto the boy: run and find out mine arrows which I shoot. And as the boy ran
1Samuel20:37 And when the lad was come to the place whither Jonathas had shot the arrow
1Samuel20:38 And he cried after the lad: haste
1Samuel20:39 But the lad wist(knew) nothing of the matter: Onely Jonathas and David wist it.
1Samuel20:40 Then Jonathas gave his weapons unto the lad and said unto him: go and carry them to the town.
1Samuel20:41 And as soon as the lad was gone
1Samuel20:42 And Jonathas said to David: go in peace which we have sworn both of us in the name of the LORD saying: the LORD be between thee and me
1Samuel21:1 Then came David to Nob to Ahimelech the priest. And Ahimelech was astonied at his coming and said unto him: Why cometh thou thyself alone
1Samuel21:2 And David said to Ahimelech the priest: the king hath commanded me to do a certain thing and said unto me
1Samuel21:3 And now what hast thou under thine hand? give me five loaves of bread or what cometh to hand.
1Samuel21:4 And the priest answered David and said: there is no common bread under mine hand
1Samuel21:5 And David answered the priest and said unto him: of a truth women hath been locked up from us about a three days
1Samuel21:6 And so the priest gave him hallowed bread
1Samuel21:7 And there was there the same day a certain man of the servants of Saul abiding before the LORD named Doeg an Edomite
1Samuel21:8 And David said unto Ahimelech: is not here under thine hand other spear or sword? for I have neither brought my sword nor mine harness with me
1Samuel21:9 Then the priest answered: the sword of Goliath the Philistine whom thou slewest in Oakdale
1Samuel21:10 And David arose and fled the same day from the presence of Saul
1Samuel21:11 And the servants of Achis said of him: is not this David the king of the land? did they not sing unto this fellow in dances saying: Saul hath slain his thousand
1Samuel21:12 And David put those words into his heart and was sore afraid of Achis the king of Geth.
1Samuel21:13 And he changed his countenance before them
1Samuel21:14 Then said Achis unto his servant. Lo ye saw that this man was beside himself
1Samuel21:15 lack I madmen
1Samuel22:1 And David departed thence and escaped
1Samuel22:2 And there gathered unto him all men that were in cumbrance and in debt and troubled in their hearts
1Samuel22:3 And David went thence to Mazphah in the land of Moab
1Samuel22:4 And he left them with the king of Moab
1Samuel22:5 And the prophet Gad said unto David: Abide not in castles
1Samuel22:6 And Saul heard of it: for David was known and also the men that were with him. And as Saul sat in Gabaah under a grove upon a high bank with his spear in his hand and all his men about him
1Samuel22:7 he said unto his servants that stood about him: Hear I pray you
1Samuel22:8 that ye have all conspired against me
1Samuel22:9 Then answered Doeg the Edomite
1Samuel22:10 which asked counsel of the LORD for him
1Samuel22:11 Then the king sent to call Ahimelech the priest the son of Ahitob
1Samuel22:12 And Saul said: hear thou son of Ahitob. And he said: here I am my lord.
1Samuel22:13 Then said Saul to him: why have ye conspired against me
1Samuel22:14 And Ahimelech answered the king and said: who is so faithful among all thy servants as David and thereto the king's son-in-law
1Samuel22:15 have I this day begun first to ask counsel of God for him? God forbid that from me: let not the king put such a thing unto his servant and on all the house of my father. For thy servant knoweth nothing of all this
1Samuel22:16 But for all that the king said: thou shalt surely die Ahimelech
1Samuel22:17 Then said the king unto his footmen that stood about him: turn and slay the priests of the LORD
1Samuel22:18 Then said the king to Doeg: turn thou and smite the priests. And Doeg the Edomite turned and ran upon the priests and slew that same day four score and five persons that did wear each man a linen Ephod.
1Samuel22:19 And Nob the city of the priests he smote with the edge of the sword
1Samuel22:20 But yet one of the sons of Ahimelech the son of Ahitob
1Samuel22:21 and shewed David
1Samuel22:22 And David said unto Abiathar: I wist it the same day
1Samuel22:23 Abide with me and fear not: he that seeketh thy soul
1Samuel23:1 Then men told David saying: Behold
1Samuel23:2 Then David asked the LORD's advice saying: shall I go and smite the Philistines? And the LORD said unto David: go and smite the Philistines and save Keilah.
1Samuel23:3 Then said David's men unto him: see we be afraid here in Juda. What shall we then be
1Samuel23:4 Then David asked the LORD again. And the LORD answered him and said: up and go to Keilah
1Samuel23:5 And so David and his men went to Keilah and fought with the Philistines
1Samuel23:6 And it chanced when Abiathar the son of Ahimelech fled to David to Keilah that he brought an Ephod in his hand.
1Samuel23:7 And it was told Saul
1Samuel23:8 And Saul called all the people to war
1Samuel23:9 But David had knowledge that Saul imagined mischief against him
1Samuel23:10 Then said David
1Samuel23:11 will the men of Keilah deliver me into his hand? or will Saul come as thy servant heareth say? LORD God of Israel tell thy servant. And the LORD said: he will come.
1Samuel23:12 Then said David: will the men of Keilah deliver me and my men into the hand of Saul? and the LORD said they will deliver you.
1Samuel23:13 Then David and his men which were upon a six hundredth
1Samuel23:14 And David abode in the wilderness in strong holds
1Samuel23:15 And David saw that Saul was come out
1Samuel23:16 And Jonathas Saul's son arose and went to David to the thicket
1Samuel23:17 and said unto him: fear not
1Samuel23:18 And they made a bond both of them together before the LORD. And David tarried still in the thicket
1Samuel23:19 Then came the Ziphites to Saul
1Samuel23:20 Now therefore sir king
1Samuel23:21 Then said Saul: Blessed are ye in the LORD: for ye have compassion on me.
1Samuel23:22 Go I pray you and mark more diligently
1Samuel23:23 See therefore and know all the lurking places where he lurketh
1Samuel23:24 And they arose and went to Ziph before Saul. But David and his men were in the wilderness of Maon
1Samuel23:25 For when Saul was gone with his men to seek
1Samuel23:26 And Saul and his men went on the one side of the mountain
1Samuel23:27 But there came a messenger to Saul saying: Haste thee and come
1Samuel23:28 Wherefore Saul returned from persecuting David
1Samuel23:29 And then David went thence and dwelt in holds at Engadi.
1Samuel24:1 When Saul was come again from the Philistines
1Samuel24:2 Then Saul took three thousand men out of all Israel
1Samuel24:3 And he came to the flocks of the sheep by a way's side where was a cave. And Saul went in to cover his feet. And David and his men sat a long by the sides of the cave.
1Samuel24:4 And the men of David said unto him: see the day is come
1Samuel24:5 And immediately David's heart smote him
1Samuel24:6 And he said unto his men: the LORD keep me from doing that thing unto my master that is the LORD's anointed
1Samuel24:7 And so David kept off his servants with words
1Samuel24:8 And when Saul was up out of the cave and gone away
1Samuel24:9 and said to Saul: wherefore giveth thou an ear to men's words
1Samuel24:10 Behold this day thine eyes have seen
1Samuel24:11 And moreover my father
1Samuel24:12 The LORD be judge between thee and me
1Samuel24:13 According to the old proverb
1Samuel24:14 After whom art thou come out
1Samuel24:15 The LORD be judge and judge between thee and me
1Samuel24:16 When David had made an end of speaking all these words to Saul
1Samuel24:17 and said to David: thou art righteouser than I
1Samuel24:18 And thou hast shewed this day how that thou hast dealt lovingly with me
1Samuel24:19 For who shall find his enemy
1Samuel24:20 And now I wot well that thou shalt be king
1Samuel24:21 Swear therefore unto me by the LORD
1Samuel24:22 And David sware unto Saul
1Samuel25:1 And then Samuel died
1Samuel25:2 And there was a man in Maon whose cattle was in Carmel
1Samuel25:3 The name of the man was Nabal
1Samuel25:4 And when David heard in the wilderness
1Samuel25:5 he sent out ten of his young men
1Samuel25:6 And thus wise say unto my friend: peace be to thee
1Samuel25:7 I have heard say that thou hast shearers. Now the shepherds were with us
1Samuel25:8 ask thy lads
1Samuel25:9 And David's young men came and told Nabal all those words in the name of David and then stopped.
1Samuel25:10 And Nabal answered David's servants and said: What is David? and what is the son of Isai? there is plenty of servants now a days
1Samuel25:11 I should take my bread
1Samuel25:12 And David's servants turned their way and went again
1Samuel25:13 Then David said unto his men: gird every man his sword about him. And they girded every man his sword on
1Samuel25:14 But one of the lads told Abigail Nabal's wife saying: see David sent messengers unto our master out of the wilderness to salute him
1Samuel25:15 And yet the men were very good unto us and did us no displeasure
1Samuel25:16 But they were a wall of defence unto us both by night and also by day
1Samuel25:17 Now take heed and see what thou hast to do
1Samuel25:18 Then Abigail made haste and took two hundredth loaves and two bottles of wine and five sheep ready dressed and five measures of parched corn
1Samuel25:19 and said unto her young men: go before me
1Samuel25:20 And as she rode on her ass and was coming down in a slade of the hill
1Samuel25:21 And David said: in vain have I kept all that this fellow had in the wilderness: so that nought was missed that pertained unto him
1Samuel25:22 So and so do God unto the enemies of David
1Samuel25:23 When Abigail saw David
1Samuel25:24 and fell at his feet and said: Let this unhappy deed be counted mine
1Samuel25:25 Let not my lord regard this unthrifty man Nabal
1Samuel25:26 And now my lord as sure as the LORD liveth and as thy soul liveth
1Samuel25:27 And now this blessing which thine handmaid hath brought
1Samuel25:28 Forgive the trespass of thine handmaid that the LORD may make my lord a sure house
1Samuel25:29 And if any man rise to persecute thee and to seek thy soul
1Samuel25:30 And moreover when the LORD shall have done to my lord all the good that he hath promised thee
1Samuel25:31 then shall it be no grudge of conscience unto thee or discourage of heart unto my lord
1Samuel25:32 Then said David to Abigail: blessed be the LORD God of Israel which sent thee this day to meet me.
1Samuel25:33 And blessed be thy behaviour
1Samuel25:34 For in very deed as sure as the LORD God of Israel liveth which hath kept me back from hurting thee
1Samuel25:35 And so David received of her hand that she brought him
1Samuel25:36 And when Abigail came to Nabal: behold
1Samuel25:37 But in the morning
1Samuel25:38 and upon a ten days after the LORD smote Nabal
1Samuel25:39 And when David heard that Nabal was dead
1Samuel25:40 And when the servants of David were come to Abigail to Carmel
1Samuel25:41 And she arose and bowed herself on her face to the earth
1Samuel25:42 And Abigail hasted and arose and gat her up upon an Ass
1Samuel25:43 David also took Ahinoam of Jezrahel
1Samuel25:44 But Saul gave Michol his daughter David's wife to Phalti the son of Lais of Gallin.
1Samuel26:1 After that came the Ziphites unto Saul to Gabaah saying: David hideth himself in the hill of Hachilah even before the wilderness.
1Samuel26:2 Then Saul arose and went to the wilderness of Ziph and three thousand chosen men of Israel with him
1Samuel26:3 And Saul pitched in the hill of Hachilah which lieth before the wilderness
1Samuel26:4 he sent out spies and understood that Saul was come of surety.
1Samuel26:5 Wherefore David arose and went to the place where Saul had pitched
1Samuel26:6 Then answered David and spake to Ahimelech the Hethite and to Abisai the son of Zaruiah and brother to Joab saying: who will go down with me to Saul to the host? Abisai said: I will go down with thee.
1Samuel26:7 And so David and Abisai came to the people by night. And behold
1Samuel26:8 The said Abisai to David: God hath closed in thine enemy unto thine hand this day. Now therefore let me smite him a fellowship with my spear to the earth
1Samuel26:9 But David said to Abisai: destroy him not
1Samuel26:10 And David said furthermore: as sure as the LORD liveth
1Samuel26:11 but the LORD keep me from laying mine hand upon the LORD's anointed. Now then take a fellowship the spear that is at his head
1Samuel26:12 And David took the spear and the cruse of water that were at Saul's head
