1Samuel26:13 Then David went over to the other side and stood on the top of an hill afar off (a great space being between them)
1Samuel26:14 and cried to the people and to Abner the son of Ner saying: Answerest thou not Abner? and Abner answered and said: What art thou that criest to the king?
1Samuel26:15 and David said to Abner: art not thou a man
1Samuel26:16 It is not good that thou hast done. As truly as the LORD liveth ye are worthy to die
1Samuel26:17 Then Saul knew David's voice and said: is this thy voice my son David? and David said: it is my voice my lord king.
1Samuel26:18 And he said thereto wherefore doth my lord persecute his servant? for what have I done? or what evil is in mine hand?
1Samuel26:19 Now hear therefore (my lord king) the words of thy servant. If the LORD have stirred thee up against me
1Samuel26:20 And yet I hope my blood shall not fall to the earth before the face of the LORD
1Samuel26:21 Then said Saul: I have sinned
1Samuel26:22 And David answered and said: Behold the king's spear
1Samuel26:23 The LORD reward every man's righteousness and faith: for the LORD delivered thee into my hand this day
1Samuel26:24 And as thy life was much set by this time in mine eyes: so be my life set by in the eyes of the LORD
1Samuel26:25 And Saul said to David: Blessed art thou my son David: for thou shalt be a doer and also able to bring to an end. And so David went his way
1Samuel27:1 Then thought David in his heart: I may perish one day or other by the hands of Israel. There is no better for me
1Samuel27:2 And David arose and he and the six hundredth men that were with him went unto Achis
1Samuel27:3 And David dwelt with Achis at Geth
1Samuel27:4 And when it was told Saul that David was fled to Geth
1Samuel27:5 And David said unto Achis: If I have found grace in thine eyes
1Samuel27:6 Then Achis gave him Zikeleg the same day for which cause Zikeleg pertaineth unto the kings of Juda unto this day.
1Samuel27:7 And the time that David dwelt in the country of the Philistines
1Samuel27:8 And David and his men went and ran upon the Gesurites
1Samuel27:9 And David smote the land and left neither man nor woman alive
1Samuel27:10 And Achis said: have ye not been a roving this day? And David answered: yes in the south of Juda
1Samuel27:11 And David saved neither man nor woman alive to bring to Geth
1Samuel27:12 And Achis believed David saying: he hath made him self to stink unto his people Israel
1Samuel28:1 And it chanced in those days
1Samuel28:2 And David said again to Achis: then thou shalt know
1Samuel28:3 Samuel was then dead
1Samuel28:4 And the Philistines gathered together and came and pitched in Sunam. And Saul and all Israel gathered together and pitched in Gelboe.
1Samuel28:5 And when Saul saw the host of the Philistines
1Samuel28:6 And Saul asked counsel of the LORD: But the LORD answered him not
1Samuel28:7 Then said Saul unto his servants: seek me a woman that is mistress of a spirit of prophecy that I may go to her and ask of her. And his servants said to him: see
1Samuel28:8 And Saul changed his clothes and put on other raiment
1Samuel28:9 And the wife said unto him: Behold
1Samuel28:10 And Saul swore to her by the LORD saying: As surely as the LORD liveth
1Samuel28:11 Then said the wife: whom shall I fetch up unto thee? and he said: Bring me up Samuel.
1Samuel28:12 When the woman saw Samuel she cried with a loud voice and spake to Saul saying: why hast thou mocked me? for thou art Saul.
1Samuel28:13 And the king said unto her
1Samuel28:14 And he said: what fashion is he of? And the woman said: there cometh up an old man with a mantle upon him. And Saul perceived that it was Samuel
1Samuel28:15 And Samuel said to Saul: why hast thou unquieted me
1Samuel28:16 Then said Samuel: wherefore dost thou ask of me? while the LORD is gone from thee and is thine enemy
1Samuel28:17 the LORD will do to thee as he said by my hand. For the LORD will rent the kingdom out of thine hand
1Samuel28:18 because thou obeyedst not the voice of the LORD
1Samuel28:19 And moreover the LORD will deliver Israel with thee
1Samuel28:20 Then Saul fell straightway flat on the earth as long as he was
1Samuel28:21 And the woman came unto Saul and saw that he was sore troubled
1Samuel28:22 Now therefore hearken thou also unto the voice of thine handmaid
1Samuel28:23 But he refused it and said: I will not eat. But his servants and the wife together compelled him
1Samuel28:24 The woman had a fat calf in the house
1Samuel28:25 and brought them before Saul and before his servants. And when they had eaten
1Samuel29:1 The Philistines gathered all their hosts together unto Aphec: And Israel pitched by a fountain in Jezrahel.
1Samuel29:2 And the lords of the Philistines went forth by hundreds and by thousands. But David and his men came behind with Achis.
1Samuel29:3 Then said the lords of the Philistines: what are yonder Hebrews? And Achis said unto the lords of the Philistines: Is not this David the servant of Saul the king of Israel
1Samuel29:4 Nevertheless the lords of the Philistines were wroth with him and said unto him: Make this fellow return
1Samuel29:5 Is not this David to whom they sang in dances: Saul slew his thousand
1Samuel29:6 Then Achis called David and said unto him: As sure as the LORD liveth thou art honest
1Samuel29:7 wherefore return and go in peace
1Samuel29:8 And David said again to Achis: Why what have I done? and what hast thou found in thy servant as long as I have been with thee unto this day? that I may not go fight against the enemies of my lord the king?
1Samuel29:9 Achis answered and said to David: I wot well thou pleasest me
1Samuel29:10 Wherefore rise up early in the morning with thy master's servants that are come with thee. And when ye be up early as soon as ye have light
1Samuel29:11 And so David and his men rose early to depart in the morning
1Samuel30:1 But yer David and his men were come to Zikeleg the third day
1Samuel30:2 and had taken the women that were therein prisoners
1Samuel30:3 When David and his men came to the city: behold
1Samuel30:4 Then David and the people that was with him lifted up their voices and wept
1Samuel30:5 And David's two wives were taken prisoners also: Ahinoam the Jezrahelite and Abigail the wife of Nabal the Carmelite.
1Samuel30:6 And David was in a shrewd strait: for the people intended to stone him because the hearts of the people were vexed for their sons and their daughters.
1Samuel30:7 But David took a good courage to him in the LORD his God and said to Abiathar the priest Ahimelech's son: bring me the Ephod. And Abiathar brought the Ephod to David.
1Samuel30:8 And David asked the LORD saying: shall I follow after this company? and shall I overtake them? And he said to him: follow
1Samuel30:9 And he went and the six hundredth men that were with him
1Samuel30:10 But David and four hundredth men followed: But two hundredth abode behind being too weary to go over the river Besor.
1Samuel30:11 Then they found an Egyptian in the field
1Samuel30:12 and gave him a few figs and two clusters of raisins. And when he had eaten his spirits came again to him: for he had eaten no bread nor drunk no water in three days and three nights.
1Samuel30:13 Then David said unto him: to whom belongest thou and whence art thou? and the lad answered: I am an Egyptian and servant to an Amalekite: and my master left me behind
1Samuel30:14 We came a roving upon the south of Cerethis
1Samuel30:15 And David said to him: canst thou bring me to this company? And he said: swear unto me by God
1Samuel30:16 And when he had brought him: see
1Samuel30:17 And David laid upon them from the twilight until the evening on the morrow: so that there escaped not a man
1Samuel30:18 And David recovered all the Amalekites had carried away
1Samuel30:19 so that there was no person lacking small or great
1Samuel30:20 And David took all the sheep
1Samuel30:21 And then David came to the two hundredth men that were too weary for to follow David which they made to abide at the river Besor. And they came to meet David and the people that were with him. And when David came to the people
1Samuel30:22 Then answered all the wicked and the unthrifts of the men that went with David
1Samuel30:23 Then said David: ye shall not do so (my brethren) with that the LORD hath given us
1Samuel30:24 For who should hearken unto you in this matter? But as his part is that goeth and fighteth
1Samuel30:25 And so from that day forward was that made a law and a custom in Israel
1Samuel30:26 When David came to Zikeleg
1Samuel30:27 He sent to them of Bethel: to them of south Ramath: to them of Gether:
1Samuel30:28 to them of Aroer: to them of Sephamoth: to them of Esthamo:
1Samuel30:29 to them of Rachal: to them of the cities of the Jerhameelites: to them of the cities of the Kenites:
1Samuel30:30 to them of Haramah: to them of Borasan: to them of Athach:
1Samuel30:31 to them of Hebron
1Samuel31:1 And as the Philistines fought against Israel
1Samuel31:2 And the Philistines followed after Saul and his sons
1Samuel31:3 And the battle went sore against Saul
1Samuel31:4 Then said Saul unto his harness bearer: draw out thy sword and thrust me thorow therewith lest these uncircumcised come thrust me thorow and make a mocking stock of me. But his harness bearer would not
1Samuel31:5 And when his harness bearer saw that Saul was dead
1Samuel31:6 And so Saul died and his three sons and his harness bearer
1Samuel31:7 When the men of Israel that were of the other side of the valley
1Samuel31:8 On the morrow when the Philistines were come to strip them that were slain
1Samuel31:9 And they cut off his head and stripped him out of his harness
1Samuel31:10 And they hanged up his harness in the house of Astharoth
1Samuel31:11 When the inhabiters of Jabes in Gilead heard thereof what the Philistines had done to Saul
1Samuel31:12 they arose as many as were men of war and went all night and took the carcase of Saul and the carcases of his sons from the walls of Bethsan and brought them to Jabes and burnt them there
1Samuel31:13 and took their bones and buried them under a Tree at Jabes
2Samuel1:1 After the death of Saul
2Samuel1:2 Behold
2Samuel1:3 To whom David said: whence comest thou? And the other answered him: Out of the host of Israel am I escaped.
2Samuel1:4 And David said to him again: How hath it chanced? tell me. And he said: the people fled from the battle: and many of the people are overthrown and dead: and Saul and Jonathas his son are dead thereto.
2Samuel1:5 And David said unto the young man that told him: how knowest thou that Saul and Jonathas his son be dead?
2Samuel1:6 and the young man that told him
2Samuel1:7 And Saul looked back and called me. And I answered: here am I.
2Samuel1:8 And he said unto me: what art thou? and I said unto him: I am an Amalekite.
2Samuel1:9 And he said unto me: come on me and slay me: For anguish is come upon me and my life is yet all in me.
2Samuel1:10 And I went on him and slew him: for I was sure that he could not live
2Samuel1:11 Then David took his clothes and rent them
2Samuel1:12 And they mourned
2Samuel1:13 Then said David unto the young man that brought him tidings. Whence art thou? And he said: I am the son of an alien an Amalekite.
2Samuel1:14 And David said unto him: How is it that thou wast not afraid to lay thine hand on the LORD's anointed
2Samuel1:15 And David called one of his young men and said: Go and run upon him. And he smote him that he died.
2Samuel1:16 Then said David unto him: thy blood upon thine own head: for thine own mouth hath testified against thee saying: I have slain the LORD's anointed.
2Samuel1:17 And David sang this song of mourning over Saul and Jonathas his son
2Samuel1:18 and bade to teach the children of Israel the staves thereof. And behold it is written in the book of the righteous.
2Samuel1:19 The glory of Israel is slain upon the high hills: Oh how were the mighty overthrown?
2Samuel1:20 Tell it not in Geth: nor publish it in the streets of Askalon: lest the daughters of the Philistines rejoice
2Samuel1:21 Ye mountains of Gelboe
2Samuel1:22 The bow of Jonathas and the sword of Saul turned never back again empty
2Samuel1:23 Saul and Jonathas lovely and pleasant in their lives
2Samuel1:24 Ye daughters of Israel
2Samuel1:25 How were the mighty slain in battle? Jonathas on the high hills was wounded to death:
2Samuel1:26 Woe is me for thee my brother Jonathas: delectable to me wast thou exceeding. Thy love to me was wonderful
2Samuel1:27 How were the mighty overthrown
2Samuel2:1 After this David asked the LORD saying: shall I go up into any of the cities of Juda? And the LORD said: go. And David answered whither shall I go? He answered
2Samuel2:2 And so David went thither with his two wives also
2Samuel2:3 And the men that were with him
2Samuel2:4 And the men of Juda came and there anointed David king over the house of Juda. When it was told David
2Samuel2:5 he sent messengers unto them and said unto them: blessed are ye unto the LORD
2Samuel2:6 Wherefore the LORD shew you mercy and truth again. And I will do you good also
2Samuel2:7 And now let your hands stir them and play ye the men
2Samuel2:8 But Abner the son of Ner that was captain of Saul's host
2Samuel2:9 and made him king over Gilead and over the Assurites
2Samuel2:10 And Isboseth Saul's son was forty year old when he began to reign over Israel
2Samuel2:11 And the time which David reigned in Hebron over the house of Juda was seven years and six months.
2Samuel2:12 And Abner the son of Ner and the servants of Isboseth the son of Saul went out of Mahanaim
2Samuel2:13 And Joab the son of Zaruiah and the servants of David went out and met them by the Pool of Gabaon. And they sat down
2Samuel2:14 And Abner said to Joab: let the young men arise and play before us. And Joab answered: be it.
2Samuel2:15 Then there arose and went over: twelve of Benjamin by number which pertained to Isboseth the son of Saul
2Samuel2:16 And they caught each his fellow that came against him
2Samuel2:17 And there began an exceeding cruel battle that same day. But Abner and the men of Israel were put to the worse of the servants of David.
2Samuel2:18 And there was three sons of Zaruiah there: Joab
2Samuel2:19 and followed after Abner and turned neither to the right hand nor to the left from Abner.
2Samuel2:20 Then Abner looked behind him and said: art thou Asahel? And he said yea.
2Samuel2:21 Then said Abner: turn thee either to the right hand or to the left and catch one of the young men and take thee his spoil. But Asahel would not depart from him.
2Samuel2:22 And Abner said again to Asahel: turn from me
2Samuel2:23 howbeit he would in no wise depart. Then Abner with the hinder end of the spear smote him under the short ribs
2Samuel2:24 But Joab and Abisai followed Abner till the son went down. And when they were come to the hill Amah that lieth before Giah in the way that goeth thorow the wilderness of Gabaon
2Samuel2:25 the children of Benjamin gathered themselves together after Abner on a plump and stood still on the top of an hill.
2Samuel2:26 Then Abner called to Joab and said: shall the sword devour without end? knowest thou not that bitterness is wont to come in the latter end? how long shall it be
2Samuel2:27 And Joab answered: as truly as God liveth
2Samuel2:28 And therewith Joab blew a trumpet
2Samuel2:29 And Abner and his men walked all that night by the wild fields
2Samuel2:30 And Joab returned from after Abner and gathered all the people together. And there lacked of David's servants nineteen persons and Asahel.
2Samuel2:31 But the servants of David had slain of Benjamin and of Abner's men
2Samuel2:32 And they took up Asahel and buried him in the sepulchre of his father in Bethlehem. And Joab and his men went all night
2Samuel3:1 There was long strife between the house of Saul and the house of David. But David waxed stronger and stronger
2Samuel3:2 And David had children born him in Hebron: his eldest son was Amnon of Ahinoam the Jezrahelite:
2Samuel3:3 the second Cheleab of Abigail the wife of Nabal the Carmelite: the third Absalom the son of Maachah the daughter of Tholmai the king of Gessur:
2Samuel3:4 the fourth Adoniah the son of Hagith: the fifth Saphaitiah the son of Abital:
2Samuel3:5 the sixth Jethraam by Egla David's wife. These were born to David in Hebron.
2Samuel3:6 And as long as there was battle between the house of Saul and the house of David
2Samuel3:7 And Saul had a concubine named Riphah the daughter of Aiah. And Isboseth said to Abner: Wherefore liest thou with my father's concubine?
2Samuel3:8 Then was Abner very wroth for the words of Isboseth and said: Am I not a dog's head
2Samuel3:9 So do God to Abner and so thereto: except that as the LORD hath sworn to David
2Samuel3:10 and bring the kingdom from the house of Saul
2Samuel3:11 And he could give Abner never a word to answer because he feared him.
2Samuel3:12 And Abner sent messengers to David forthwith
2Samuel3:13 And David answered well said
2Samuel3:14 And David sent messengers to Isboseth Saul's son saying: deliver me my wife Michol which I married with an hundredth foreskins of the Philistines.
2Samuel3:15 And Isboseth sent and took her from her husband Phaltiel the son of Lais.
2Samuel3:16 And her husband went with her
2Samuel3:17 And Abner had communication with the elders of Israel
2Samuel3:18 Now then do it: for the LORD hath said of David
2Samuel3:19 And like words had he with Benjamin
2Samuel3:20 And when Abner was come to David to Hebron and twenty men with him: David made him and the men that were with him a feast.
2Samuel3:21 Then Abner said unto David: I will up and go gather all Israel unto my lord the king
2Samuel3:22 And see
2Samuel3:23 When Joab and all the host that was with him were come
2Samuel3:24 Then Joab went to the king and said: what hast thou done? See
2Samuel3:25 Thou mightest know Abner the son of Ner
2Samuel3:26 And when Joab was come out from the king
2Samuel3:27 And when Abner was come again to Hebron
2Samuel3:28 And when it afterward came to David's ear
2Samuel3:29 It fall therefore on the head of Joab and on all his father's house: that the house of Joab be never without one or other that hath running issues or without lepers and goers on crutches
2Samuel3:30 And the cause why Joab and Abisai slew Abner was
2Samuel3:31 And David said to Joab and to all the people that were with him
2Samuel3:32 And when they had buried Abner in Hebron
2Samuel3:33 And the king lamented over Abner and said: Abner died not as a wretch dieth.
2Samuel3:34 Thy hands were not manacled nor thy feet brought into gyves: but as men fall before wicked children
2Samuel3:35 And when all the people came to eat meat with David
2Samuel3:36 And the people wist it
2Samuel3:37 And all the people and all Israel thereto understood that day how that it was not the king's mind
2Samuel3:38 And the king said unto his servants: know ye not
2Samuel3:39 And I am this day tender
2Samuel4:1 When Saul's son heard that Abner was dead in Hebron
2Samuel4:2 Now this Saul's son had two men that were become captains over the soldiers
2Samuel4:3 And these Berothites fled to Gethaim and sojourned there until the same time.
2Samuel4:4 And Jonathas Saul's son had a son that was lame on his feet: five years old was he when the tidings came of Saul and Jonathas out of Jezrahel. And his nurse took him up and fled away. And as she made haste to flee and was amazed
2Samuel4:5 And these sons of Remon the Berothite
2Samuel4:6 And behold they came into the house as though they would have bought wheat
2Samuel4:7 For they came into the house as he slept on his bed in his resting chamber
2Samuel4:8 And they brought the head of Isboseth unto David
2Samuel4:9 And David answered Rechab and Baanah his brother
2Samuel4:10 him that told me how that Saul was dead
2Samuel4:11 And how much more ought I to deal with wicked men that have slain a righteous person in his own house upon his bed? Now therefore think ye that I will not require his blood of your hands and take you from the earth?
2Samuel4:12 And David commanded his young men
2Samuel5:1 Then came all the tribes of Israel to David to Hebron and said: see
2Samuel5:2 And thereto in time past when Saul was king over us
2Samuel5:3 And so all the elders of Israel came to the king to Hebron. And king David made a covenant with them in Hebron before the LORD. And they anointed David king over Israel.
2Samuel5:4 David was thirty year old when he began to reign
2Samuel5:5 In Hebron he reigned over Juda seven years and six months. And in Jerusalem he reigned thirty and three year over all Israel and Juda.
2Samuel5:6 Then went the king and his men to Jerusalem
2Samuel5:7 Nevertheless David took the hold of Sion
2Samuel5:8 Then said David the same day: whosoever winneth the walls and the lame and the blind hated of David's soul. Wherefore the blind and the lame shall not come into the house.
2Samuel5:9 And David dwelt in the tower and called it the city of David. And David built round about it from Mello inward.
2Samuel5:10 And David waxed great and the LORD God of hosts was with him.
2Samuel5:11 And Hiram king of Tyre sent messengers to David with Cedar trees and carpenters and Masons
2Samuel5:12 Whereby David perecived that the LORD had stablished him king over Israel and had exalted his kingdom for his people Israel's sake.
2Samuel5:13 And David took him more concubines and wives out of Jerusalem
2Samuel5:14 And these be the names of the sons that were born him in Jerusalem: Samua
2Samuel5:15 Jebahar
2Samuel5:16 Eliada
2Samuel5:17 But when the Philistines heard that they had anointed David king over Israel
2Samuel5:18 And the Philistines came and laid them along in the valley of Raphaim.
2Samuel5:19 And David asked of the LORD saying: shall I go to the Philistines? and wilt thou deliver them into my hands? And the LORD said unto David: go for I will deliver the Philistines into thy hands.
2Samuel5:20 And David came to Baal Pharazim and smote them there and said: the LORD hath divided mine enemies asunder before me
2Samuel5:21 And there they left their Images and David and his men took them up.
2Samuel5:22 And the Philistines came yet again and laid them in the valley Raphaim.
2Samuel5:23 And David asked the LORD
2Samuel5:24 And when thou hearest the noise of a thing going in the tops of the Peretrees
2Samuel5:25 And David did as the LORD commanded him and smote the Philistines from Gabaah to Gezer.
2Samuel6:1 After that
2Samuel6:2 and arose and went and all the folk that were with him of the men of Juda
2Samuel6:3 And they put the Ark of God upon a new cart and brought it out of the house of Abinadab that dwelt at Gabaah. And Ozah and Ahio the sons of Abinadab drave the new cart.
2Samuel6:4 And when they brought it out of that house of Abinadab that dwelt at Gabaah with the Ark of God
2Samuel6:5 And David and all the house of Israel played before the LORD with all manner instruments of fir wood
2Samuel6:6 And when they came to Nachon's threshing floor
2Samuel6:7 And the LORD was wrath with Ozah and God smote him in the same place for his fault
2Samuel6:8 And David was displeased because the LORD had rent Ozah. And the name of the place was called Perez Ozah until this day.
2Samuel6:9 And David was then afraid of the LORD and said: how should the Ark of the LORD come to my house?
2Samuel6:10 And so David would not bring the Ark of the LORD with him into the city of David. But carried it into the house of Obed Edom a Gethite.
2Samuel6:11 And the Ark of the LORD continued in the house of Obed Edom the Gethite
2Samuel6:12 And when it was told king David how that the LORD had blessed the house of Obed Edom and all that pertained unto him
2Samuel6:13 And ever when they that bare the Ark of the LORD
2Samuel6:14 And David danced before the LORD with all his might in a linen Ephod gird unto him.
2Samuel6:15 And David and all the house of Israel brought the ark of the LORD with shouting and trumpet blowing.
2Samuel6:16 And as the ark of the LORD came into the city of David
2Samuel6:17 And when they had brought in the Ark of the LORD
2Samuel6:18 and as soon as David had made and end of offering burntofferings and peaceofferings he blessed the people in the name of the LORD of Hosts
2Samuel6:19 and gave among all the folk
2Samuel6:20 Then David returned to salute his household. And Michol the daughter of Saul came out against him and said: Oh how glorious was the king of Israel today
2Samuel6:21 But David said again to Michol
2Samuel6:22 And I will be yet more vile than so
2Samuel6:23 But the said Michol daughter to Saul had no child unto the day of her death.
2Samuel7:1 And in process as the king dwelt in his house after that the LORD had given him rest round about from all his enemies
2Samuel7:2 he said unto Nathan the prophet: behold
2Samuel7:3 Then said Nathan unto the king: go and do all that is in thine heart
2Samuel7:4 But that same night the word of the LORD came unto Nathan saying:
2Samuel7:5 go and tell my servant David
2Samuel7:6 For I have not dwelt in any house since the time I brought the children of Israel out of Egypt
2Samuel7:7 In any way where I went among the children of Israel
2Samuel7:8 Now therefore so say unto my servant David
2Samuel7:9 And I was with thee in all that thou wentest to
2Samuel7:10 And I will appoint a place for my people Israel
2Samuel7:11 and since the time I commanded judges to be over my people Israel
2Samuel7:12 And when thy days be fulfilled and thou laid to rest with thy fathers
2Samuel7:13 And he shall build an house for my name
2Samuel7:14 I will be his father and he shall be my son: insomuch that if he sin
2Samuel7:15 But my mercy will I not take away from him
2Samuel7:16 And thine house and thy kingdom shall endure without end after thee
2Samuel7:17 And when Nathan had told David all these words and all this vision:
2Samuel7:18 Then went king David and set him down before the LORD and said: what am I Lord Jehovah
2Samuel7:19 And is this a small thing in thy sight Lord Jehovah
2Samuel7:20 And what can David say more unto thee
2Samuel7:21 Even for thy word's sake and according to thine own heart hast thou done all these great things to make them known unto thy servant.
2Samuel7:22 Wherefore thou art great O LORD God and there is none like thee
2Samuel7:23 And what one people in the earth is like thy people Israel
2Samuel7:24 And thou hast ordained thy people Israel to be thy people for ever. And thou LORD art their God.
2Samuel7:25 And now LORD God the thing thou hast said of thy servant and of his house make it good for ever and do as thou hast said.
2Samuel7:26 And let thy name be great for ever
2Samuel7:27 For thou LORD of Hosts God of Israel hast told in the ear of thy servant saying: I will build thee an house. And therefore hath thy servant found in his heart to pray this prayer unto thee.
2Samuel7:28 And now Lord Jehovah(Lorde Iehouah) thou art the God
2Samuel7:29 And now go to and bless the household of thy servant
2Samuel8:1 After that
2Samuel8:2 And he beat the Moabites and measured them with a line
2Samuel8:3 David smote also Adadezer the son of Rehob king of Zobah as he went to make the end of his coasts at the river Euphrates.
2Samuel8:4 And David took a thousand and seven hundredth horsemen of his
2Samuel8:5 Then came the Sirians of Damasco to succour Adadezer king of Zobah. And David slew of them two and twenty thousand men and put soldiers in Siria Damasco.
2Samuel8:6 And the Sirians became servants to David paying tribute. And thus the LORD saved David in all that he went to.
2Samuel8:7 And David took the shields of gold that were upon the servants of Adadezer and brought them to Jerusalem.
2Samuel8:8 And thereto out of Betah and Berathai cities of Adadezer he brought exceeding great abundance of brass.
2Samuel8:9 When Thoi king of Hemath had heard how David had discomforted all the host of Adadezer
2Samuel8:10 he sent Joram his son unto king David to salute him with peace and to bless him because he had fought against Adadezer and beaten him: for Thoi kept war with Adadezer
2Samuel8:11 And them also king David did dedicate unto the LORD with the silver and gold that he consecrated of all nations which he subdued:
2Samuel8:12 of the Sirians
2Samuel8:13 And David made him a name after he returned from the slaughter of the Sirians in the valley of Salt where he slew eighteen thousand men.
2Samuel8:14 And he put keepers in Edom
2Samuel8:15 And David reigned over all Israel and executed right and Justice unto all his people.
2Samuel8:16 And Joab the son of Zaruiah was over the host
2Samuel8:17 And Sadoch the son of Ahitob and Ahimelech the son of Abiathar were the priests
2Samuel8:18 And Banaiah the son of Jehoiada was over the Cerethites and the Phelethites and David's sons were chief rulers.
2Samuel9:1 Then said David: is there yet any man left of the house of Saul
2Samuel9:2 And there was of the household of Saul a servant named Zibah which was called unto David. And the king said unto him: art thou Zibah? And he said: thy servant is the man.
2Samuel9:3 Then said the king: remaineth there yet any man of the kindred of Saul
2Samuel9:4 And the king said unto him: where is he? And Zibah said unto the king: behold he is in the house of Machir the son of Amiel in Lodaber.
2Samuel9:5 Then king David sent and fetched him out of the house of Machir the son of Amiel
2Samuel9:6 Now when Miphiboseth the son of Jonathas the son of Saul was come unto David
2Samuel9:7 And David said: fear not
2Samuel9:8 And the other bowed himself and said: what is thy servant that thou shouldst vouchsafe to look upon such a dead dog as I am.
2Samuel9:9 Then the king called unto Zibah Saul's young man saying unto him: I give unto thy master's son all that pertained to Saul and to all his kin.
2Samuel9:10 See therefore that thou and thy sons and thy servants till the land for him
2Samuel9:11 Then said Zibah unto the king: all that my lord hath said unto his servant
2Samuel9:12 This Miphiboseth had a son that was young named Micha
2Samuel9:13 And Miphiboseth dwelt in Jerusalem
2Samuel10:1 It happened after this
2Samuel10:2 Then said David: I will shew kindness unto Hanon the son of Nahas
2Samuel10:3 the lords of the children of Ammon said unto Hanon their lord: thinkest thou that David doth honour thy father
2Samuel10:4 Wherefore Hanon took David's servants and shaved off the one half of every man's beard and cut off their garments even in the middle
2Samuel10:5 When it was told David he sent against them (for they were men exceedingly ashamed) and said: tarry at Jericho until your beards be grown
2Samuel10:6 And when the children of Ammon saw that they stank unto David
2Samuel10:7 And when David heard of it
2Samuel10:8 And the children of Ammon came out and waged battle before the gate and the Sirians of Zobah
2Samuel10:9 When Joab saw that the front of the battle was before and after
2Samuel10:10 And the rest of the people he delivered in the hand of Abisai his brother
2Samuel10:11 And he said: if the Sirians overcome me
2Samuel10:12 quite thee like a man
2Samuel10:13 And forthwith Joab proceeded and the people that was with him into battle against the Sirians
2Samuel10:14 And when the children of Ammon saw that the Sirians were fled
2Samuel10:15 And when the Sirians saw that they were put to the worse before Israel
2Samuel10:16 And Adadezer sent and brought out the Sirians that were beyond the river. And the host of them came
2Samuel10:17 And when it was shewed David
2Samuel10:18 how be it the Sirians fled before Israel. And David destroyed seven hundredth chariots of them and forty thousand horsemen
2Samuel10:19 And when all the kings that were servants to Hadadezer saw that they were put to the worse before Israel
2Samuel11:1 And it came to pass in the beginning of a new year
2Samuel11:2 And it chanced in an evening that David arose from his couch and walked upon the roof of the king's palace
2Samuel11:3 And it was answered again
2Samuel11:4 And David sent messengers and fetched her
2Samuel11:5 And when the woman had perceived that she had conceived
2Samuel11:6 Then David sent to Joab
2Samuel11:7 And when Urias was come unto him he demanded how Joab did
2Samuel11:8 And David said moreover to Urias: go down to thine house and wash thy feet. And Urias departed out of the king's palace
2Samuel11:9 But Urias slept at the door of the king's palace with all the servants of his lord
2Samuel11:10 Then they told David saying: Urias descended not into his house. Then said David unto Urias: seeing that thou art come from journeying
2Samuel11:11 And Urias said unto David: the ark and Israel and Juda dwell in pavilions: and my lord Joab and the servants of my lord lie in tents upon the flat earth: and should I then go into mine house
2Samuel11:12 Then said David unto Urias: tarry here this day also
2Samuel11:13 And David called him
2Samuel11:14 Wherefore on the morrow David wrote a letter to Joab and sent it by Urias.
2Samuel11:15 And he wrote in the letter saying: put Urias in the forefront of the battle where it is most strong
2Samuel11:16 And as Joab beseiged the city he assigned Urias unto a place where he wist that strong men were.
2Samuel11:17 And the men of the city came out and fought with Joab. And there were certain overthrown of the people and of the servants of David
2Samuel11:18 Then Joab sent and told David all the process of the war
2Samuel11:19 and charged the messengers saying: when thou hast made an end of telling the story of war unto the king
2Samuel11:20 if he begin to fume and say unto thee: wherefore approached ye so nigh unto the city to fight? wist ye not that men would hurl and shoot from the walls?
2Samuel11:21 who smote Abimelech son of Jerobeseth? did not a woman cast a piece of a millstone upon him from off the walls
2Samuel11:22 And the messengers went and came and shewed David all that Joab had sent him with
2Samuel11:23 and said unto David: the men prevail against us
2Samuel11:24 And the shooters shot from the walls
2Samuel11:25 Then said David unto the messenger
2Samuel11:26 And when the wife of Urias heard that her husband was dead
2Samuel11:27 and she was his wife and bare him a son. But the deed that David had committed
2Samuel12:1 And the LORD sent Nathan unto David. And he came unto him and said unto him: there were two men in one city
2Samuel12:2 And the rich had exceeding great abundance of sheep and oxen.
2Samuel12:3 But the poor had nothing save one little lamb which he bought and nourished up. And it grew up with him and his children
2Samuel12:4 And there came a stranger unto the rich man. And he could not find in his heart to take of his own sheep nor of his beasts to dress for the stranger that was come unto him. But took the poor man's lamb and dressed it for the man that was come to him.
2Samuel12:5 And David was exceeding wroth with the man
2Samuel12:6 and shall restore the lamb fourfold
2Samuel12:7 Then Nathan said to David: thou art the man. Thus sayeth the LORD God of Israel: I anointed thee king over Israel and rid thee out of the hands of Saul.
2Samuel12:8 And I gave thee thy master's house and thy master's wives into thy bosom
2Samuel12:9 Wherefore hast thou despised the commandment of the LORD
2Samuel12:10 Now therefore the sword shall never depart from thine house
2Samuel12:11 Thus sayeth the LORD: behold I will stir up evil against thee even of thine own house and will take thy wives before thine eyes
2Samuel12:12 And thou didst it secretly
2Samuel12:13 Then said David unto Nathan: I have sinned against the LORD. And Nathan said again to David: the LORD hath put away thy sin
2Samuel12:14 Howbeit because in doing this deed thou hast given the enemies of the LORD a cause to rail
2Samuel12:15 And Nathan departed unto his house. And the LORD struck the child that Urias' wife bare David and it sickened.
2Samuel12:16 And David besought God for the boy and fasted and went and lay all night upon the earth.
2Samuel12:17 And the elders of his house arose and went to him to take him up from the earth. But he would not
2Samuel12:18 And it happened the seventh day that the child died. But the servants of David durst not tell him that the child was dead. For they said: see
2Samuel12:19 But David saw his servants whispering and thereby perceived that the child was dead
2Samuel12:20 Then David arose from the earth and washed and anointed himself and changed his apparel
2Samuel12:21 Then said his servants unto him: what is this that thou hast done? Thou fastedest and weepest for thy child while it was alive
2Samuel12:22 And he answered: while the child was alive
2Samuel12:23 But now seeing it is dead wherefore should I fast? can I bring him again any more? I shall go to him: but he shall not come again to me.
2Samuel12:24 And David comforted Bethsabe his wife
2Samuel12:25 And he sent by the hand of Nathan the prophet and called his name Jedidiah
2Samuel12:26 And as Joab fought against Rabah the city of the children of Ammon he took the king's Tower
2Samuel12:27 and sent messengers to David saying: I have made assault to Rabah
2Samuel12:28 Now therefore gather the rest of the people together
2Samuel12:29 And David gathered all the people together
2Samuel12:30 And he took the king's crown from off his head which weighed an hundredth weight of gold
2Samuel12:31 And he brought out the people that was therein and sawed them and drew harrows of iron upon them
2Samuel13:1 And it chanced that Absalom David's son had a fair sister named Thamar
2Samuel13:2 And this Amnon was so vexed that he fell sick for the love of his sister Thamar: for it thought him hard to come by his purpose of her
2Samuel13:3 But Amnon had a companion called Jonadab the son of Samah the brother of David: which Jonadab was a very wise man.
2Samuel13:4 And he said unto him: how cometh it that thou being the king's son art thus consumed every morning? mayst thou not tell it me? And Amnon answered him: I love Thamar my brother Absalom's sister.
2Samuel13:5 Then said Jonadab unto him: lie on thy bed and feign thyself sick. And when thy father is come to see thee
2Samuel13:6 And Amnon lay down and made himself sick. And when the king was come to see him Amnon said unto him: let Thamar my sister come and make me a couple of fritters in my sight
2Samuel13:7 And David sent home to Thamar saying: go to thy brother Amnon's house and dress him meat.
2Samuel13:8 When Thamar came to her brother Amnon's house he lay. And she took flour and made paste and made fritters in his sight and did bake them
2Samuel13:9 and took a platter and poured them out before him. And Amnon would not eat
2Samuel13:10 Then said Amnon unto Thamar: bring the meat into the chamber that I may eat it of thine hand. And Thamar took the fritters which she had made
2Samuel13:11 and set them before him to eat. And he took her and said unto her
2Samuel13:12 And she answered him: nay
2Samuel13:13 For whither shall I go with my shame? And thou shalt be as one of the fools in Israel. But speak unto the king
2Samuel13:14 How be it he would not hearken unto her voice but was too strong for her and forced her and lay with her.
2Samuel13:15 And then Amnon hated her exceedingly: so that the hate wherewith he hated her was greater than the love with which he before loved her. And he said unto her: up and away.
2Samuel13:16 Then she said unto him
2Samuel13:17 but called his lad that waited upon him and said: put her out at the doors from me and bolt the door after her.
2Samuel13:18 And she had a kirtle of divers colours upon her: for with such were the king's daughters that were virgins apparelled
2Samuel13:19 And Thamar put ashes on her head and rent her gay kirtle that was on her and put her hand on her head and so went
2Samuel13:20 Then Absalom her brother said unto her: hath Amnon thy brother been with thee? Now then be still my sister: for he is thy brother. And let not this thing grieve thine heart. And so Thamar remained discomforted in her brother Absalom's house.
2Samuel13:21 And king David heard of all these things and was very wroth.
2Samuel13:22 And Absalom said unto his brother Amnon neither good nor bad. How be it Absalom hated Amnon because he had forced his sister Thamar.
2Samuel13:23 And it happened two year after that Absalom had a sheep shearing in Baal Hazor besides the tribe of Ephraim
2Samuel13:24 and he came to the king and said: behold thy servant hath a sheep shearing
2Samuel13:25 And the king said unto Absalom: Oh nay my son let us not go every one of us that we be not chargeable unto thee. And Absalom lay sore upon him: how be it he would not go
2Samuel13:26 Then said Absalom: then let my brother Amnon go with us. And the king answered: what needeth it that he go with thee?
2Samuel13:27 But Absalom made such instance that he let Amnon go with him
2Samuel13:28 Then Absalom commanded his young men saying: mark when Amnon's heart is merry with wine
2Samuel13:29 And the young men of Absalom served Amnon even as Absalom commanded them. And all the king's sons arose and took each man his Mule and fled.
2Samuel13:30 And while they were yet in the way
2Samuel13:31 Then the king arose and tare his garments and lay along on the earth: and all his servants stood by with their clothes rent.
2Samuel13:32 Then Jonadab the son of Samah David's brother answered and said: let not my lord suppose that they have slain all the young men the king's sons
2Samuel13:33 Now therefore let not my lord the king take the thing so grievously to think that all the king's sons were dead
2Samuel13:34 But Absalom fled. And the young man that kept the watch lifted up his eyes and looked about. And behold
2Samuel13:35 Then said Jonadab unto the king: behold
2Samuel13:36 And as soon as he had left speaking: behold
2Samuel13:37 But Absalom escaped and went to Tholmai the son of Amihud king of Gesur. And the king mourned for his son continually.
2Samuel13:38 And so Absalom escaped and went to Gesur
2Samuel13:39 And by that time the king turned his mind from pursuing Absalom. For he had left mourning for the death of Amnon.
2Samuel14:1 When Joab the son of Zaruiah perceived that the king's heart was turned again to Absalom
2Samuel14:2 he sent to Thekoa
2Samuel14:3 And go to the king and speak of thus manner unto him. And so Joab taught her what she should say.
2Samuel14:4 And when the wife of Thekoa was come to speak with the king
2Samuel14:5 And the king said unto her: what aileth thee? and she answered. I am a widow
2Samuel14:6 And thy handmaid had two sons which fought together in the fields where was no man to go between them
2Samuel14:7 And behold
2Samuel14:8 And the king said unto the wife: go home to thine house
2Samuel14:9 And the woman of Thekoa said unto the king: My lord king
2Samuel14:10 And then said the king: if any man say ought unto thee
2Samuel14:11 Then said she: let the king remember his LORD God
2Samuel14:12 Then the woman said: let thine handmaid speak one word more unto my lord the king and he said: say on.
2Samuel14:13 And the woman said: wherefore then hast thou determined on this same manner against the people of God? that the king should speak this thing and be faulty therein
2Samuel14:14 For we must needs die and perish as water spilt on the ground which can not be gathered up again and God will not take away the life but find the means that the banished be not utterly expelled from him.
2Samuel14:15 And now concerning that I am come to speak of this thing unto the king my lord in the presence of the people: thy handmaid thought: surely I will speak unto the king
2Samuel14:16 And the king shall hear his handmaid and deliver her out of the hand of the man that would have destroyed me and my son also out of the inheritance of God.
2Samuel14:17 Then thine handmaid said: I pray God that the word of my lord the king may be immutable. For my lord the king is as an Angel of God
2Samuel14:18 Then the king answered and said unto the woman: hide not from me I pray thee: the thing that I shall ask thee. And the woman said: let my lord the king say on.
2Samuel14:19 Then said the king: is not the hand of Joab with thee in all this matter? And the woman answered and said: as sure as thy soul liveth my lord king
2Samuel14:20 And the turning of my tale another way
2Samuel14:21 Then said the king to Joab: behold
2Samuel14:22 And Joab fell to the ground on his face and bowed himself and blessed the king and said: now thy servant knoweth that I have found grace in the sight of my lord the king in that he hath fulfilled the request of his servant.
2Samuel14:23 And so Joab arose and went to Gesur and brought Absalom to Jerusalem.
2Samuel14:24 Then said the king: let him turn to his own house
2Samuel14:25 Moreover in all Israel there was not so goodly a man as Absalom

2Samuel14:27 And this Absalom had three sons born him and one daughter named Thamar

2Samuel14:29 But he would not come to him. And he sent again
2Samuel14:30 Then he said unto his servants: behold

2Samuel15:1 After this Absalom gat him chariots and horses and fifty men to run before him.
2Samuel15:2 And thereto he used to rise up early in the mornings and to stand by the way's side that led to the gate of the city. And all the men of Israel that had complaints and came to the king for judgement
2Samuel15:3 Then said Absalom unto him: see
2Samuel15:4 Then Absalom said moreover: Oh
2Samuel15:5 And also when any man came nigh to him to do him obeisance
2Samuel15:6 And on this manner did Absalom to all Israel that came to the king for judgement
2Samuel15:7 And at forty years' end Absalom said unto the king: let me go I pray thee to Hebron
2Samuel15:8 for thy servant vowed a vow when I was in Gesur in the land of Siria saying: if the LORD shall bring me again to Jerusalem
2Samuel15:9 And the king said: go in peace. And so he arose and went to Hebron.
2Samuel15:10 Then Absalom sent spies thorowout all the tribes of Israel saying: as soon as ye hear the voice of the trumpet blow
2Samuel15:11 And with Absalom went two hundredth men out of Jerusalem that were called. And they went with pure hearts(went on simply) and wist(knew) nothing of the matter.
2Samuel15:12 And Absalom sent also for Ahitophel the Gilonite David's counsellor
2Samuel15:13 And there came a messenger to David and said: the hearts of the men of Israel are to follow Absalom.

2Samuel15:15 And the king's servants said to him: behold thy servants are ready to whatsoever my lord(Lorde) the king shall appoint.
2Samuel15:16 And the king and all his household departed afoot. And he left behind him ten wives that were his concubines
2Samuel15:17 And so the king and all the people went out afoot and tarried far from the house.
2Samuel15:18 And all his servants went by his side. And all the Cerethites and all the Phelethites
2Samuel15:19 Then said the king to Ethai the Gethite
2Samuel15:20 Thou camest but yesterday
2Samuel15:21 And Ethai answered the king and said: as surely as the LORD liveth
2Samuel15:22 Then said the king to Ethai
2Samuel15:23 And all the country wept with a loud voice. And all the people went forward the straight way to the wilderness.
2Samuel15:24 And behold
2Samuel15:25 Then said the king unto Sadock: Carry the Ark of God again into the city. If I shall find favour in the eyes of the LORD
2Samuel15:26 But and if the Lord thus say: I have no lust unto thee. Behold

2Samuel16:8 The LORD hath brought upon thee all the blood of the house of Saul
2Samuel16:9 Then said Abisai the son of Zaruiah unto the king: why doth this dead dog curse my lord the king? let me go and take off the head of him.
2Samuel16:10 And the king said: what have I to do with you ye sons of Zaruiah: let him curse: for the LORD hath bid him curse David. And who dare presume to say wherefore doth he so?
2Samuel16:11 And David said to Abisai
2Samuel16:12 haply the LORD will look on my wretchedness
2Samuel16:13 And thus as David and his men went by the way
2Samuel16:14 And the king and all that were with him came weary
2Samuel16:15 And Absalom and all the people of the men of Israel came to Jerusalem and Ahitophel with him.
2Samuel16:16 And as soon as he was come: Husai the Arachite went unto Absalom and said unto him: God save the king
2Samuel16:17 And Absalom said again to Husai: is this the kindness thou owest to thy friend? Why wentest thou not with him?
2Samuel16:18 And Husai said unto Absalom: nay not so
2Samuel16:19 And furthermore unto whom shall I do service but even to his son? And as I was servant before with thy father
2Samuel16:20 Then spake Absalom to Ahitophel give counsel what is best for us to do.
2Samuel16:21 And Ahitophel said unto Absalom: get thee in unto thy father's concubines which he hath left to keep the house. For when all Israel shall hear that thou hast made thy father to stink
2Samuel16:22 And so they pitched Absalom a tent upon the top of the house. And he went in unto his father's concubines in the sight of all Israel.
2Samuel16:23 And the counsel of Ahitophel which he counselled in those days
2Samuel17:1 Then Ahitophel said unto Absalom: let me choose out
2Samuel17:2 And I will come upon him while he is weary and weak handed
2Samuel17:3 and will bring again all the people unto thee. And when all these men which thou seekest are turned to thee
2Samuel17:4 And the saying pleased Absalom well and all the elders of Israel.
2Samuel17:5 Then said Absalom: call also Husai the Arachite and let us hear his sentence.
2Samuel17:6 When Husai was come to Absalom
2Samuel17:7 Then said Husai to Absalom: the counsel that Ahitophel hath given is not good at this time.
2Samuel17:8 For (said Husai) thou knowest thy father and the men that are with him
2Samuel17:9 Behold he lurketh now in some cave or in some other place. And thereto though some of his men be overthrown at the first brunt
2Samuel17:10 By the reason whereof the best men thou hast whose hearts are as the hearts of Lions
2Samuel17:11 But my counsel is
2Samuel17:12 And we will come upon him in one place or other
2Samuel17:13 Moreover if he take a town then shall all the men of Israel bring ropes to that city
2Samuel17:14 And Absalom and all the men of Israel said: that the counsel of Husai the Arachite was better than the counsel of Ahitophel. For it was even the LORD's determination to destroy the counsel of Ahitophel: which was good
2Samuel17:15 Then said Husai unto Sadock and Abiathar the priests: of this and that manner did Ahitophel and the elders of Israel counsel Absalom. And thus and thus have I counselled.
2Samuel17:16 Now therefore send quickly and shew David saying: tarry not all night in the fields of the wilderness
2Samuel17:17 Now Jonathas and Ahimaaz abode by the well rogell: for they might not be seen to come in to the city. And a wench went and told them. And they to go and shewed king David.
2Samuel17:18 Nevertheless there was a lad saw them which told it to Absalom. But they went both of them away quickly and came to a man's house in Bahurim
2Samuel17:19 And the wife took and spread a coverlet on the top of the well and strawed thereon steeped barley to dry. And the thing was not spied.
2Samuel17:20 And when Absalom's servants came to the wife to the house and asked where one Ahimaaz and Jonathas were

2Samuel17:23 And when Ahitophel saw that his counsel was not followed
