2Samuel18:1 And the king numbered the people that were with him
2Samuel18:2 And he sent one part of them with Joab
2Samuel18:3 And the people answered: nay
2Samuel18:4 Then said the king: what seemeth you best
2Samuel18:5 And the king commanded Joab
2Samuel18:6 And the people went out into the field against Israel
2Samuel18:7 Where the people of Israel were put to the worse before the servants of David
2Samuel18:8 And the field was fought in divers places
2Samuel18:9 And it chanced Absalom to come before the servants of David riding upon a Mule which carried him under the thick boughs of a great Oak. And his head caught in the Oak
2Samuel18:10 And one that saw it told Joab saying: behold
2Samuel18:11 and Joab said unto him that told him: lo
2Samuel18:12 And the man answered Joab: though I had a thousand sicles of silver told in my hand yet would I not stretch out mine hand against the king's son. For we heard with our ears when the king charged thee
2Samuel18:13 Moreover though I had jeoparded my life and done falsely thereto
2Samuel18:14 Then said Joab I may not stand tarrying with thee. And therewith he took three spears in his hand and thrust them into the heart of Absalom
2Samuel18:15 And ten servants that bare Joab's weapons
2Samuel18:16 Then Joab blew a trumpet
2Samuel18:17 And they took Absalom and cast him into a great pit that was in the wood
2Samuel18:18 And this Absalom yet in his lifetime
2Samuel18:19 Then said Ahimaaz the son of Sadock: let me run I pray thee
2Samuel18:20 And Joab said unto him: thou art no man to bear tidings today: thou shalt bear tidings another time: but today thou shalt bear none
2Samuel18:21 Then said Joab to Chusi: go and tell the king what thou hast seen. And Chusi bowed himself unto Joab and ran.
2Samuel18:22 Then said Ahimaaz the son of Sadock again to Joab: come what come will
2Samuel18:23 And he said unto him: run. Then Ahimaaz ran by the plain and overran Chusi.
2Samuel18:24 And David sat between the two gates. And the watchman went up to the roof over the gate unto the wall
2Samuel18:25 And the watchman called and told the king. And the king said: if he come alone
2Samuel18:26 And the watchman saw another man running
2Samuel18:27 And the watchman said: me thinketh the running of the foremost is like the running of Ahimaaz the son of Sadock. And the king said: he is a good man and cometh with good tidings.
2Samuel18:28 And Ahimaaz called and said to the king: good tidings
2Samuel18:29 And the king said: is the lad Absalom safe? And Ahimaaz answered I saw a great ado
2Samuel18:30 And the king said: turn and stand here. And he turned and stood.
2Samuel18:31 And behold Chusi came and said: tidings my lord the king
2Samuel18:32 And the king said to Chusi: is the lad Absalom safe? And Chusi answered: the enemies of my lord the king and all that rise against thee
2Samuel18:33 And the king was moved and went up to a chamber over the gate and wept. And as he went thus he said: my son Absalom
2Samuel19:1 And it was told Joab
2Samuel19:2 And the victory was turned that day into mourning unto all the people. For the people heard say that day
2Samuel19:3 and the people went stealing into the city
2Samuel19:4 And the king hid his face and cried with a loud voice: my son Absalom
2Samuel19:5 And Joab went into the house to the king and said: thou hast shamed this day the faces of all thy servants
2Samuel19:6 in that thou lovest thine enemies and hatest thy friends. Thou hast declared this day that thou camest neither for thy lords nor servants. For this day I do perceive
2Samuel19:7 Now therefore up and come out
2Samuel19:8 Then the king arose and sat down in the Gate. And it was told unto all the people
2Samuel19:9 And all the people were at strife thorowout all the tribes of Israel saying: the king delivered us out of the hand of our enemies. And he delivered us out of the hand of the Philistines.
2Samuel19:10 And now he is fled out of the land for Absalom. But Absalom whom we anointed over us is dead in battle.
2Samuel19:11 Now therefore why are ye so still
2Samuel19:12 ye are my brethren
2Samuel19:13 And say to Amasa: art thou not my bone and my flesh? And God do so to me and so thereto: except thou be captain of the Host to me for ever in the room of Joab.
2Samuel19:14 And he bowed the hearts of all the men of Juda
2Samuel19:15 And the king returned and came to Jordan. And Juda came to Gilgal for to go against the king to convey him over Jordan.
2Samuel19:16 And Semei the son of Gera the son of Jemini which was of Bahurim
2Samuel19:17 and a thousand men of Benjamin with him
2Samuel19:18 And there went over a Boat to carry over the king's household and to do him pleasure. And Semei the son of Gera fell before the king
2Samuel19:19 and said unto him: let not my lord impute wickedness unto me
2Samuel19:20 For thy servant doth know how that I have done amiss. And therefore behold
2Samuel19:21 But Abisai the son of Zaruiah answered and said: shall Semei not die for that cause: which cursed the LORD's anointed?
2Samuel19:22 And the king said: what matter is between you and me ye sons of Zaruiah
2Samuel19:23 And the king said unto Semei: thou shalt not die
2Samuel19:24 And Miphiboseth the son of Saul came to meet the king
2Samuel19:25 And when he was come to Jerusalem toward the king
2Samuel19:26 And he said: my lord king
2Samuel19:27 And he hath thereto complained on thy servant unto my lord the king. But my lord the king is as an Angel of God: do therefore what seemeth best in thine eyes.
2Samuel19:28 For all my father's house were but dead men unto my lord the king: and yet didst thou put me among them that eat at thine own table. What right therefore have I yet
2Samuel19:29 And the king said: it needeth thee not to speak any more
2Samuel19:30 And Miphiboseth said unto the king: yea let him take all: for so much as my lord the king is come in peace unto his own house.
2Samuel19:31 And Berselai the Gileadite came from Rogelim and went over Jordan with the king
2Samuel19:32 which Berselai was a very aged man
2Samuel19:33 And the king said unto Berselai: come thou with me
2Samuel19:34 But Berselai said unto the king: of what age am I of
2Samuel19:35 I am this day four score year old: and therefore can not discern savoury from unsavoury
2Samuel19:36 let thy servant go a little way over Jordan with the king: for why should the king render me such a reward?
2Samuel19:37 But let thy servant turn back again
2Samuel19:38 And the king answered: Chamaam shall go with me. And I will do to him that thou shalt be content with. And thereto whatsoever thou shalt require of me
2Samuel19:39 And all the people went over Jordan. And when the king was come over Jordan
2Samuel19:40 And then the king went to Gilgal
2Samuel19:41 Wherefore all the men of Israel came to the king and said unto him: why have our brethren the men of Juda stolen thee away
2Samuel19:42 And all the men of Juda answered the men of Israel: for the king is near of kin to us:(nigh kindred) wherefore be ye angry for that matter? think ye that we eat of the king's cost
2Samuel19:43 And the men of Israel answered the men of Juda and said: we have ten parts in the king
2Samuel20:1 And there happened to be an unthrifty fellow named Seba the son of Bochri a man of Gemini which blew a trumpet and said: we have no part in David
2Samuel20:2 let us depart therefore every man to his tent. And all Israel went from David and followed Seba the son of Bochri. But the men of Juda clave fast unto the king
2Samuel20:3 And when David was come to his house to Jerusalem
2Samuel20:4 Then said the king to Amasa: call me the men of Juda together within three days and present thyself here.
2Samuel20:5 And Amasa went to gather the men of Juda together: but tarried longer than the time which was appointed him.
2Samuel20:6 Wherefore the king said to Abisai: now shall Seba the son of Bochri be worse to us than Absalom. Take thou therefore thy lord's servants and follow after him: lest he get him walled cities and escape us.
2Samuel20:7 And there went out after him Joab's men and the Cerethites and the Phelethites and the men of might. And they departed out of Jerusalem to follow after Seba the son of Bochri.
2Samuel20:8 And when they were come to the great stone in Gabaon
2Samuel20:9 And Joab said to Amasa: are all things in rest and peace my brother? And Joab took Amasa by the chin with the right hand
2Samuel20:10 And Amasa took no heed to the knife that was in Joab's hand
2Samuel20:11 And one of Joab's men that stood by him said: what is he that he would be as Joab
2Samuel20:12 And Amasa lay wallowing in blood in the midst of the way. And there was a man that saw all the people stand still. And when he saw that all that came by him stood still
2Samuel20:13 And as soon as he was tumbled out of the way
2Samuel20:14 And he went thorow all the tribes of Israel unto Abel
2Samuel20:15 And they came and besieged him in Abel Bethmaacah. And they cast up a bank against the city. And it was besieged. All the people that was with Joab laboured busily to overthrow the wall.
2Samuel20:16 Then cried a wise woman out of the city
2Samuel20:17 When Joab was come the woman said: art thou Joab? And Joab answered that am I. And she said to him: hear the words of thy handmaid? And he said: I am ready to hear.
2Samuel20:18 And she said: it hath been a common saying in the old time
2Samuel20:19 Abel is one of the quietest and faithfullest cities that are in Israel. And thou goest about to destroy a city that is as a mother in Israel. Why devourest thou the inheritance of the LORD?
2Samuel20:20 And Joab answered and said: God forbid
2Samuel20:21 The matter is not so: But there is a man of mount Ephraim
2Samuel20:22 And then the woman went unto all the people in her wisdom. And they smote off the head of Seba son of Bochri
2Samuel20:23 And Joab was over all the host of Israel. And Banaiah the son of Jehoiada was over the Cerethites and the Phelethites.
2Samuel20:24 And Aduram was over the tribute. And Jehosaphat the son of Ahilud was Recorder.
2Samuel20:25 Sema was scribe. And Sadock and Abiathar were priests.
2Samuel20:26 And Ira the Jairite was likewise David's priest.
2Samuel21:1 Then there fell an hunger in the days of David three years
2Samuel21:2 And the king called the Gabeonites and said unto them. Now these Gabeonites are not of the children of Israel
2Samuel21:3 Wherefore David said unto them: what shall I do for you? and wherewith shall I content you that ye may bless the inheritance of the LORD?
2Samuel21:4 And the Gabaonites said unto him: we have no matter of silver or of gold with Saul or with his house: neither have we any man that we would kill in Israel. Then he said: what say ye that I shall do for you?
2Samuel21:5 and they said unto the king: the man that consumed and imagined to bring us to nought
2Samuel21:6 let seven men of his sons be delivered unto us
2Samuel21:7 But the king had compassion on Miphiboseth the son of Jonathas
2Samuel21:8 But he took the two sons of Rezphah the daughter of Aiah which she bare unto Saul
2Samuel21:9 and delivered them unto the hands of the Gabeonites
2Samuel21:10 And Rezphah the daughter of Aiah took Sackcloth and spread it under her upon the Rock
2Samuel21:11 And when it was told David what Rezphah the daughter of Aiah the concubine of Saul had done
2Samuel21:12 he went and took the bones of Saul
2Samuel21:13 And he brought thence the bones of Saul and the bones of Jonathas his son
2Samuel21:14 and buried them with the bones of Saul and Jonathas his son in the country of Benjamin
2Samuel21:15 And the Philistines had yet again war with Israel. And David went down and his servants with him
2Samuel21:16 And David waxed fainty
2Samuel21:17 But Abisai the son of Zaruiah succoured him and smote the Philistine and killed him. Then the servants of David sware unto him saying: Thou shalt go no more out with us to battle
2Samuel21:18 And there was yet after this battle with the Philistines at Nob
2Samuel21:19 And there was yet the third battle in Gob
2Samuel21:20 And there was yet battle in Geth
2Samuel21:21 And Jonathas the son of Samach the brother of David slew him.
2Samuel21:22 These four were of the sons of Haraphah in Geth
2Samuel22:1 And David spake the words of this song unto the LORD
2Samuel22:2 And he said: The LORD is my rock
2Samuel22:3 God is my strength
2Samuel22:4 I will praise and call on the LORD
2Samuel22:5 For the waves of death have closed me about
2Samuel22:6 The cords of hell have compassed me about
2Samuel22:7 In my tribulation I called to the LORD
2Samuel22:8 And the earth trembled and quoke
2Samuel22:9 Smoke went up out of his nostrils
2Samuel22:10 And he bowed heaven and came down
2Samuel22:11 And he rode upon Cherub and flew: and appeared upon the wings of the wind.
2Samuel22:12 And he made darkness a tabernacle round about him
2Samuel22:13 Of the brightness
2Samuel22:14 The LORD thundered from heaven
2Samuel22:15 And he shot arrows and scattered them
2Samuel22:16 And the bottom of the sea appeared
2Samuel22:17 He sent from on high and fetched me
2Samuel22:18 He delivered me from my mighty enemy
2Samuel22:19 When they had compassed me in the day of my tribulation
2Samuel22:20 And he brought me out into roomth
2Samuel22:21 The LORD rewarded me according to my righteousness
2Samuel22:22 For I kept the ways of the LORD
2Samuel22:23 But I had all his laws in my sight
2Samuel22:24 But was pure unto him ward
2Samuel22:25 And the LORD did to me again
2Samuel22:26 With the holy thou shalt be holy
2Samuel22:27 With the pure thou shalt be pure
2Samuel22:28 And the people that are in adversity
2Samuel22:29 And on the proud shalt thou cast thine eyes. Thou art my light
2Samuel22:30 With thy help I will run thorow an Host of men
2Samuel22:31 The way of God is undefiled
2Samuel22:32 Who is a God save the LORD
2Samuel22:33 God is my strength in war
2Samuel22:34 And maketh my feet as swift as an hind's
2Samuel22:35 And teacheth my hands to fight
2Samuel22:36 And thou hast saved me with thy shield
2Samuel22:37 And thou madest me space to walk in
2Samuel22:38 I followed mine enemies and destroyed them
2Samuel22:39 I wasted them and so clouted them
2Samuel22:40 And thou compassedest me about with might to battle
2Samuel22:41 And thou madest mine enemies to turn their backs to me
2Samuel22:42 They looked for help: But none came to save them: unto the LORD they cried
2Samuel22:43 And I will beat them as small as the dust of the earth
2Samuel22:44 And thou deliveredst me from the dissension of my people
2Samuel22:45 And the aliens crouched unto me
2Samuel22:46 And the aliens that shrink away shall tremble for fear in their defended places.
2Samuel22:47 The LORD live
2Samuel22:48 It is God that avengeth me
2Samuel22:49 And delivereth me from mine enemies: thou liftest me up on high from them that rise against me
2Samuel22:50 And therefore I will praise thee O LORD among the heathen
2Samuel22:51 For thy great and manifold saving of thy king
2Samuel23:1 These be the last sayings of David. David the son of Isai said: And the man is lifted up on high
2Samuel23:2 the spirit of the LORD spake in me
2Samuel23:3 The God of Israel spake unto me
2Samuel23:4 And he shall be as the morning light
2Samuel23:5 And is not mine house so with God? in that he hath made with me an everlasting covenant perfect in all points: and in that he shall fulfil all that is healthsome unto me
2Samuel23:6 When the unthrifty men are all of them as thorns taken out of their places
2Samuel23:7 But the man that shall touch them
2Samuel23:8 These be the names of the mighty men that were about David: Joseb of Sebeth the Thachmonite
2Samuel23:9 And next to him Eleazar the son of Dodi the son of Ahohi
2Samuel23:10 arose and laid on the Philistines until his hand ached and clave unto the sword. And the LORD made a great victory the same day. And the people returned and went after him only to spoil.
2Samuel23:11 After him was Sema the son of Ageh the Hararite (which when the Philistines were gathered together in Lahaia where was a parcel of land full of rice
2Samuel23:12 stood in the midst of the said ground
2Samuel23:13 And these three of the thirty of the Lord's went down and came to David in the harvest time unto the cave Odolom. And the Host of the Philistines had pitched in the valley of Giants.
2Samuel23:14 And David was then in an hold. And the Soldiers of the Philistines were then in Bethlehem.
2Samuel23:15 And David longed and said: Oh
2Samuel23:16 And anon the three mighty brake thorow the Host of the Philistines and fetched water out of the well of Bethlehem that was in the gate
2Samuel23:17 and said: the LORD forbid that I should do so
2Samuel23:18 Then Abisai the brother of Joab
2Samuel23:19 For he was nobler man than the three and was their captain. How be it he attained not unto those three in acts.
2Samuel23:20 Then Banaiah the son of Jehoiada the son of a lusty man valiant in acts of Rabzeel
2Samuel23:21 And he slew an Egyptian a goodly man
2Samuel23:22 Such things did Banaiah the son of Jehoiada and was the noblest of thirty: But not like to any of the three in facts of war.
2Samuel23:23 And David made him of his counsel.
2Samuel23:24 Asahel the brother of Joab was one of the thirty. Then Elehanan the son of Dodo of Bethlehem:
2Samuel23:25 Semah the Harodite: Elica the Harodite:
2Samuel23:26 Helez the Palitite: Ira the son of Akes the Thekoite:
2Samuel23:27 Abiezer of Anathoth: Mobonai the Husathite:
2Samuel23:28 Zelmon an Ahohite: Maharai the Netophathite:
2Samuel23:29 Heleb the son of Baanah an Netophathite: Ithai the son of Ribai of Gabaah a city of the children of Benjamin:
2Samuel23:30 Banaiah the Pharathonite: Hedai of the river of Gaas:
2Samuel23:31 Abialbon the Arbathite: Asmaueth a Berhomite:
2Samuel23:32 Elithaba a Salabonite: Of the sons of Jasen Jonathas:
2Samuel23:33 Semah the Harorite: Ahiam the son of Sarar an Ararite:
2Samuel23:34 Eliphelet the son of Aasbai the son of a Maachathite: Eliam the son of Ahitophel the Gilonite:
2Samuel23:35 Hezrai the Carmelite: Pharai the Arbite:
2Samuel23:36 Igaal the son of Nathan of Zobah: Bani the Gadite:
2Samuel23:37 Zelec an Ammonite: Naharai a Berothite the Harnessbearer of Joab the son of Zaruiah:
2Samuel23:38 Ira the Jethrite: Gareb the Jethrite:
2Samuel23:39 Uriah the Hethite: in all thirty and seven.
2Samuel24:1 And the LORD was wroth again against Israel
2Samuel24:2 And forthwith the king said to Joab the captain of his Host: go abroad I pray thee
2Samuel24:3 And Joab said unto the king: I beseech that the LORD thy God make the people as many more as they be: yea and an hundredth times so many more
2Samuel24:4 Notwithstanding the king's words prevailed against Joab and against all the captains of the Host. Then Joab and the captains of the Host went out from the king
2Samuel24:5 And they passed over Jordan and pitched in Aroer on the right side of the city that lieth in the midst of the valley of Gad and so forth to Jazer.
2Samuel24:6 And then they went to Gilead and to the nether land of Hodesi
2Samuel24:7 and came to the strong town of Tyre
2Samuel24:8 And so when they had been abroad thorowout all the land they returned to Jerusalem again at the end of nine months and twenty days.
2Samuel24:9 And then Joab delivered up the reckoning of the number of the people unto the king. And there were found in Israel nine hundredth thousand men of might that drew swords. And the men of Juda were five hundredth thousand men.
2Samuel24:10 And David's heart smote him after that he had numbered the people. And he said unto the LORD: I have sinned exceedingly in that I have done. And now LORD take away the trespass of thy servant: for I have done foolishly.
2Samuel24:11 And when David was up in a morning
2Samuel24:12 go and say unto David
2Samuel24:13 And Gad came to David and shewed him and said unto him: whether wilt thou have seven years hunger in thy land
2Samuel24:14 Then said David unto Gad: I am in a marvellous strait. But let me fall I pray thee
2Samuel24:15 And the LORD sent a pestilence in Israel from the morning unto the end of the time appointed. And there died of the people between Dan and Bersabe seventy thousand men.
2Samuel24:16 And when the Angel stretched out his hand unto Jerusalem to have destroyed it
2Samuel24:17 Then spake David unto the LORD when he saw the Angel that smote the people
2Samuel24:18 And Gad came the same day to David and said unto him: go up and rear an altar unto the LORD in the threshing floor of Areunah the Jebusite.
2Samuel24:19 And David according to the saying of Gad went up as the LORD commanded.
2Samuel24:20 And when Areunah looked and saw the king and his servants coming toward him
2Samuel24:21 and said: wherefore is my lord the king come to his servant? And David said: to buy the threshing floor of thee
2Samuel24:22 And Areunah said unto the king: let my lord the king take and offer what seemeth him good in his eyes: Behold oxen for sacrifice
2Samuel24:23 And Areunah the king's friend gave all to the king
2Samuel24:24 But the king said unto Areunah: not so
2Samuel24:25 And David built there an altar unto the LORD and offered burnt sacrifices and peace offerings. And so the LORD was agreed with the land. And the plague ceased from Israel. [The end of the second book of Samuel
1Kings1:1 When king David was waxen old and stricken in year
1Kings1:2 Wherefore his servants said unto him: let there be sought for my lord the king a young maiden to wait upon him and to cherish him. And let her lie in his bosom
1Kings1:3 And so they sought for a fair Damsel thorowout all the coasts of Israel and found one Abisag a Sunamite and brought her to the king.
1Kings1:4 And the Damsel was exceeding fair
1Kings1:5 But Adoniah the son of Hagith exalted himself saying: I will be king. And he gat him a chariot and horsemen and fifty men to run before him.
1Kings1:6 And his father rebuked him not at any time nor said so much to him as why dost thou so. And he was thereto a goodly man and his mother bare him next after Absalom.
1Kings1:7 And he communed with Joab the son of Zaruiah and with Abiathar the priest. And they holp Adoniah.
1Kings1:8 But Sadock the priest
1Kings1:9 And Adoniah sacrificed sheep and oxen and fatlings at the stone of Zoheleth which is fast by the well of Rogel
1Kings1:10 But Nathan the prophet and Banaiah and the mighty men and Salomon his brother he bade not.
1Kings1:11 Whereupon Nathan spake unto Bethsabe the mother of Salomon saying: hast thou not heard how that Adoniah the son of Hagith doth reign unwitting unto our master David?
1Kings1:12 Now therefore come that I may give thee counsel
1Kings1:13 Hence and get thee unto king David and say unto him: didst thou not my lord king sware unto thy handmaid saying? Salomon thy son shall reign after me
1Kings1:14 And see
1Kings1:15 And Bethsabe went in unto the king into the chamber. And the king was very old and Abisag the Sunamite ministered unto him.
1Kings1:16 And Bethsabe stooped and made obeisance unto the king. And the king said: what is thy matter?
1Kings1:17 And she answered and said: My lord thou swearest by the LORD thy God unto thine handmaid: Salomon thy son shall reign after me and he shall sit upon my seat.
1Kings1:18 But now see
1Kings1:19 And he hath offered oxen
1Kings1:20 And now my lord king the eyes of all Israel wait on thee
1Kings1:21 For else when my lord the king is laid to rest with his fathers
1Kings1:22 And behold
1Kings1:23 And they told the king saying: here cometh Nathan the prophet. And when he was come before the king
1Kings1:24 and said: My lord king
1Kings1:25 For he is gone down this day and hath offered oxen
1Kings1:26 But me thy servant and Sadock the priest and Banaiah the son of Jehoiada
1Kings1:27 Is this thing done of my lord the king and yet wouldest not shew me thy servant
1Kings1:28 Then king David answered and said: call me Bethsabe. And she came in and stood before him.
1Kings1:29 And the king sware saying: As sure as the LORD (that hath rid my soul out of all adversity) liveth:
1Kings1:30 even as I sware unto thee by the LORD God of Israel saying: Salomon shall reign after me
1Kings1:31 And Bethsabe bowed on her face to the earth and did obeisance unto the king and said: I pray God my lord king David may live ever.
1Kings1:32 Then said king David call me Sadock the priest and Nathan the prophet and Banaiah the son of Jehoiada. And when they came before the king.
1Kings1:33 The king said unto them: take with you the servants of your lord
1Kings1:34 And let Sadock the priest and Nathan the prophet anoint him there
1Kings1:35 And then come up after him
1Kings1:36 And Banaiah the son of Jehoiada answered the king and said
1Kings1:37 And as the LORD hath been with my lord the king
1Kings1:38 And Sadock the priest and Nathan the prophet and Banaiah the son of Jehoiada and the Cerethites and Phelethites went down
1Kings1:39 And Sadock the priest took an horn of oil out of the tabernacle and anointed Salomon. And they blew a trumpet
1Kings1:40 And all the people came up after him piping with pipes and rejoicing greatly
1Kings1:41 And Adoniah and all the guests that were with him
1Kings1:42 And as he yet spake
1Kings1:43 And Jonathas answered and said to Adoniah: Verily our lord king David hath made Salomon king.
1Kings1:44 And the king sent with him
1Kings1:45 And Sadock the priest and Nathan the Prophet have anointed him king in Gihon. And as they came up again they rejoiced that the city did sound again. And that is the noise that ye have heard.
1Kings1:46 And thereto Salomon sitteth on the seat of the kingdom.
1Kings1:47 And moreover the king's servants went in to bless our lord king David saying: thy God make the name of Salomon more favour than thine
1Kings1:48 And last of all thus said the king: blessed be the LORD God of Israel which hath made one to sit on my seat this day
1Kings1:49 And all the guests that were with Adoniah were afraid and rose up and went every man his way.
1Kings1:50 And Adoniah fearing Salomon arose and went and caught hold on the horns of the altar.
1Kings1:51 And it was told Salomon
1Kings1:52 And Salomon said
1Kings1:53 And thereupon king Salomon sent and fetched him down from the altar. And he came and did obeisance unto king Salomon. And Salomon said to him: get thee to thine house.
1Kings2:1 When the days of David were drawn nigh
1Kings2:2 I must walk by the way of the world
1Kings2:3 And see that thou keep the appointment of the LORD thy God
1Kings2:4 That the LORD may make good his promise which he hath promised me saying: if thy children shall take heed to their ways
1Kings2:5 Moreover thou wottest how Joab the son of Zaruiah hath served me
1Kings2:6 Deal with him therefore according to thy wisdom and see that thou bring not his hoar head down to the grave in peace.
1Kings2:7 And shew kindness unto the sons of Berselai the Gileadite
1Kings2:8 And see
1Kings2:9 But pardon thou him not. Thou art a man of wisdom and shalt know what to do to him
1Kings2:10 And so David rested with his fathers
1Kings2:11 And the days which David reigned upon Israel
1Kings2:12 And Salomon sat upon the seat of David his father and his kingdom was stablished mightily.
1Kings2:13 After that
1Kings2:14 And he said: it betokeneth peace. Then said he
1Kings2:15 And he said: thou knowest that the kingdom was mine
1Kings2:16 And she said unto him: tell what it is.
1Kings2:17 Then he said: speak I pray thee
1Kings2:18 And Bethsabe said: well
1Kings2:19 And thereupon Bethsabe went unto king Salomon to speak unto him for Adoniah. And the king rose up against her and bowed himself unto her
1Kings2:20 Then she said: I must desire a little petition of thee: say me not nay. And the king said unto her: Ask on my mother: for I will not say thee nay.
1Kings2:21 And she said: let Abisag the Sunamite be given to Adoniah thy brother to wife.
1Kings2:22 And king Salomon answered and said unto his mother: why dost thou ask Abisag the Sunamite for Adoniah? but rather ask for him the kingdom
1Kings2:23 Then king Salomon sware by the LORD saying: God do so to me and so thereto if Adoniah have not spoken this word against himself.
1Kings2:24 Now therefore as surely as the LORD liveth which hath stablished me and set me on the seat of David my father
1Kings2:25 And king Salomon sent by the hand of Banaiah the son of Jehoiada
1Kings2:26 And unto Abiathar the priest said the king: get thee to Anatoth unto thine own fields
1Kings2:27 And so Salomon put away Abiathar from being priest unto the LORD: to fulfil the words of the LORD which he spake over the house of Eli in Siloh.
1Kings2:28 Then tidings came to Joab: for Joab had turned after Adoniah but not after Salomon. Whereupon he fled unto the tabernacle of the LORD and caught handfast on the horns of the altar.
1Kings2:29 And it was told king Salomon how that Joab was fled unto the tabernacle of the LORD and stood by the altar. Then Salomon sent Banaiah the son of Jehoiada saying: go and smite him.
1Kings2:30 And when Banaiah was come to the tabernacle of the LORD he said unto him: thus sayeth the king
1Kings2:31 And the king said: do even as he hath said: smite him and bury him
1Kings2:32 And the LORD bring his blood upon his own head
1Kings2:33 And their blood return upon the head of Joab and on the head of his seed for ever. And prosperity be unto David
1Kings2:34 And Banaiah the son of Jehoiada went up and smote him and slew him and buried him in his own house in the wilderness.
1Kings2:35 And the king put Banaiah the son of Jehoiada in his room over the Host
1Kings2:36 Then the king sent and called Semei
1Kings2:37 For be thou sure of it
1Kings2:38 And Semei said unto the king: is well said: as my lord the king hath said
1Kings2:39 And it chanced at the end of three years that two of the servants of Semei ran away unto Achis son of Maacah king of Geth. And one told Semei saying: see
1Kings2:40 And Semei stood up and saddled his Ass and gat him to Geth to Achis
1Kings2:41 But it was told Salomon how that Semei had gone from Jerusalem to Geth and was come again.
1Kings2:42 And the king sent and called Semei and said unto him: Did I not adjure thee by the LORD
1Kings2:43 Why then hast thou not kept the oath of the LORD
1Kings2:44 And then the king said to Semei: thou rememberest all the wickedness which thine heart knoweth that thou didst to David my father. The LORD therefore render again thy wickedness upon thine own head:
1Kings2:45 and king Salomon be blessed
1Kings2:46 And the king commanded Banaiah the son of Jehoiada: which went out and smote him that he died. And so was the kingdom settled in the hand of Salomon.
1Kings3:1 Then Salomon drew affinity with Pharao king of Egypt
1Kings3:2 Only the people sacrificed in altars made on hills
1Kings3:3 And Salomon loved the LORD and walked in the ordinances of David his father
1Kings3:4 And the king went to Gabaon
1Kings3:5 And in Gabaon the LORD appeared to Salamon in a dream by night. And God said: Ask what I shall give thee.
1Kings3:6 And Salomon said: thou hast shewed unto thy servant David my father great mercy
1Kings3:7 And now LORD
1Kings3:8 And thy servant is in the midst of thy people which thou hast chosen
1Kings3:9 Give therefore unto thy servant an understanding heart to judge thy people
1Kings3:10 And it pleased the Lord(Lorde)(LORDE) well
1Kings3:11 Wherefore God said unto him: because thou hast asked this thing and hast not asked long life
1Kings3:12 See
1Kings3:13 And thereto I have given thee that thou askedst not
1Kings3:14 And if thou wilt walk in my ways
1Kings3:15 When Salomon awoke
1Kings3:16 Then came there two women that were harlots unto the king and stood before him.
1Kings3:17 And the one of them said: Oh my lord I and this woman dwell in one house. And I was delivered of a child with her in the said house.
1Kings3:18 And the third day after that I was delivered
1Kings3:19 And this wife's child died in a night
1Kings3:20 And then she arose at midnight and took my son from my side
1Kings3:21 And when I rose up in the morning to give my child suck: see
1Kings3:22 And the other woman said it is not so: But the living is my son
1Kings3:23 Then said the king: the one sayeth
1Kings3:24 Then said the king: bring me a sword. And they brought a sword before the king.
1Kings3:25 And then the king said: Divide the living child in two
1Kings3:26 Then spake the woman whose the living child was
1Kings3:27 Then the king answered and said: give her the living child and slay it not
1Kings3:28 And all Israel heard of the judgement which the king had judged and feared the king: for they saw that the wisdom of God was in him to do justice.
1Kings4:1 And so king Salomon was king over all Israel.
1Kings4:2 And these were his lords: Azariah the son of Sadock the priest:
1Kings4:3 Elihoreph and Ahiah the sons of Sisa
1Kings4:4 And Banaiah the son of Jehoiada was over the Host. And Sadock and Abiathar were the priests.
1Kings4:5 And Azariah the son of Nathan was over the general receivers. And Zabud the son of Nathan was a waiter and the king's companion. And Ahisar the steward of household:
1Kings4:6 And Adiran the son of Abda over the tribute.
1Kings4:7 And Salomon had twelve general receivers over all Israel which sustained the king and his household: each man his month in a year.
1Kings4:8 Whose names are these: The son of Hur in mount Ephraim:
1Kings4:9 the son of Dekar in Makez
1Kings4:10 the son of Hesed in Aruboth
1Kings4:11 the son of Abinadab
1Kings4:12 Baanah the son of Ahilud had Thanach Magedo and all Bethsan which is by Zarhanah beneath Jezrahel
1Kings4:13 The son of Gaber had Ramoth Gilead
1Kings4:14 Abinadab the son of Ado had Mahanaim:
1Kings4:15 Ahimaaz had Nephthalim
1Kings4:16 Baanah the son of Husai had Aser and Aloth:
1Kings4:17 Jehosaphat the son of Pharuah had Isachar
1Kings4:18 Semei the son of Elah had Benjamin:
1Kings4:19 Gaber the son of Uri had the country of Gilead and the land of Sehon king of the Amorites and of Og king of Basan
1Kings4:20 And Juda and Israel were many even as the sand of the sea in number
1Kings4:21 And Salomon reigned over all kingdoms from the river thorowout all the land of the Philistines unto the borders of Egypt
1Kings4:22 And Salomon's food was in one day thirty quarters of manchet flour and three score quarters of meal:
1Kings4:23 ten stalled oxen
1Kings4:24 For he ruled in all the regions on the other side Euphrates from Thaphsah to Gaza and also over all the kings on the other side the said Euphrates. And he had peace with all his servants on every side.
1Kings4:25 And Juda and Israel dwelt without fear
1Kings4:26 And Salomon had forty thousand stalls of horses for chariots and twelve thousand horsemen.
1Kings4:27 And the foresaid general receivers made purveyance for king Salomon and for all that came to king Salomon's table every man his month
1Kings4:28 And as for barley and straw for the horses and beasts they brought unto the places where the officers were
1Kings4:29 And God gave Salomon wisdom and understanding exceeding much and a large heart
1Kings4:30 so that Salomon's wisdom exceeded the wisdom of all them of the East country and all the wisdom of the Egyptians.
1Kings4:31 And he excelled all men in wisdom
1Kings4:32 And Salomon wrote three thousand proverbs. And his songs were a thousand and five.
1Kings4:33 And he disputed of trees
1Kings4:34 And there came of all nations to hear the wisdom of Salomon
1Kings5:1 And Hiram king of Tyre sent his servants unto Salomon
1Kings5:2 And Salomon sent again to Hiram saying:
1Kings5:3 thou knowest of David my father
1Kings5:4 But now the LORD my God hath given me rest on every side
1Kings5:5 And therefore I am disposed to build an house unto the name of the LORD my God
1Kings5:6 Now then command that they hew me Cedar trees in Libanon. And let my servants be with thine
1Kings5:7 When Hiram heard the words of Salomon
1Kings5:8 And Hiram sent again to Salomon saying: I have accepted the request which thou sentest to me for
1Kings5:9 My servants shall bring them from Libanon to the sea. And I will convey them by ship unto the place that thou shalt send me word
1Kings5:10 And so Hiram gave Salomon Cedar trees and fir trees
1Kings5:11 And Salomon gave Hiram twenty thousand quarters of wheat to feed his household withal
1Kings5:12 And the LORD gave Salomon wisdom as he promised him. And there was peace between Hiram and Salomon and they were confedered together.
1Kings5:13 And king Salomon raised a tribute thorowout all Israel. And the tribute was thirty thousand men
1Kings5:14 which he sent to Libanon
1Kings5:15 And Salomon had three score and ten thousand that bare burthens
1Kings5:16 besides the lords he had
1Kings5:17 And at the commandment of the king
1Kings5:18 And Salomon's masons and the masons of Hiram did hew them
1Kings6:1 And it came to pass the four hundredth and fourscore year after the children of Israel were come out of the land of Egypt
1Kings6:2 And the house which Salomon built for the LORD
1Kings6:3 And the porch that was before the body of the temple
1Kings6:4 And he made unto the house windows to open and shut.
1Kings6:5 And under the walls of the house he made galleries round about
1Kings6:6 And the nethermost gallery was five cubits broad and the middle gallery six. And the third seven cubits broad. For he made the walls without whereon the beams lay
1Kings6:7 And the house was built of stone made perfect all ready yer it was brought thither
1Kings6:8 And the door of the middle gallery was in the end of the house on the right side. And men went up with winding stairs into the middle gallery
1Kings6:9 And so he built the house and finished it
1Kings6:10 And then he built chambers over all the temple of five cubits height
1Kings6:11 And the word of the LORD came to Salomon
1Kings6:12 concerning the house which thou art in building
1Kings6:13 And I will dwell among the children of Israel and will not forsake my people Israel.
1Kings6:14 And so therefore Salomon built the house and finished it
1Kings6:15 and sealed the walls of the house within
1Kings6:16 And he sealed twenty cubits in the end of the temple both floor and walls with Cedar
1Kings6:17 And the first house
1Kings6:18 And the Cedar of the house within was carved with knops and graven with flowers
1Kings6:19 And the quere that was within the temple
1Kings6:20 And the quere was twenty cubits long
1Kings6:21 And Salomon sealed the house within also with pure gold. And he made golden bars run along the quere
1Kings6:22 And the whole house he overlaid with gold until he had ended it. And the altar that was in the quere he overlaid with gold also.
1Kings6:23 And within the quere he made two Cherubins of olive tree
1Kings6:24 and every wing five cubits long: so that from the uttermost part of one wing unto the uttermost part of the other were ten cubits.
1Kings6:25 And the other Cherub was ten cubits high also: so that both the Cherubs were of one measure and one size.
1Kings6:26 The height of the one Cherub was ten cubits
1Kings6:27 And he put the Cherubs in the middle of the inner house. And the Cherubs stretched their wings
1Kings6:28 And he overlaid the Cherubs with gold.
1Kings6:29 And all the walls of the house round about
1Kings6:30 And the floor of the house he covered with gold both within in the quere and also without in the temple.
1Kings6:31 And in the entering of the quere he made two doors of olive tree
1Kings6:32 And the two doors of olive tree he graved with graving of Cherubs and Paulme trees and graved flowers
1Kings6:33 And in like manner unto the door of the temple he made posts of olive tree four square
1Kings6:34 and two doors of fir tree
1Kings6:35 and graved thereon Cherubs
1Kings6:36 And then he built the inner court with three rows of hewed stone and one row of Cedar wood.
1Kings6:37 In the fourth year of his reign was the foundation of the house of the LORD laid
1Kings6:38 and in the eleventh year in the month Bul which is the eighth month
1Kings7:1 And Salomon built his own house in thirteen year space and full finished it.
1Kings7:2 And he built the house of the wood of Libanon
1Kings7:3 And the roof was Cedar above on high upon the beams that lay an high(hye) on the pillars
1Kings7:4 and the spaces between the pillars were one against another three fold.
1Kings7:5 And all the doors with the side posts were four square one against another three fold.
1Kings7:6 And he made a porch of pillars fifty cubits long and thirty cubits broad: and yet a porch before that with pillars
1Kings7:7 Then he made a porch to sit and judge in
1Kings7:8 And then Salomon made an house for Pharao's daughter which he had taken to wife
1Kings7:9 And all these things were of rich stones hewed after a measure and sawed with saws within and without
1Kings7:10 And the foundation was of rich stones and the mighty great stones of ten cubits and of eight. And above were rich stones hewed according to the same size
1Kings7:11 And the great court round about was with three rows of hewed stone and one row of Cedar planks.
1Kings7:12 And likewise was it in the inner court of the house of the LORD and of the porch of the temple.
1Kings7:13 And king Salomon sent and fetched one Hiram out of Tyre
1Kings7:14 a widow's son of the tribe of Nephthalim
1Kings7:15 He cast two pillars of brass of eighteen cubits apiece high
1Kings7:16 And he made two head pieces of molten brass to set on the tops of the pillars
1Kings7:17 with garlands of braided work and hoops of chain work for the head pieces that were on the pillars
1Kings7:18 And he made the pillars
1Kings7:19 And the head pieces that were on the tops of the pillars were wrought with lilies in the porch the space of four cubits of them.
1Kings7:20 And the pomegranates above and beneath on the wreathen chains that compassed the middle of the head pieces were in number two hundredth on either head piece.
1Kings7:21 And he set up the pillars in the porch of the temple
1Kings7:22 And the tops of the pillars were thus wrought with lilies
1Kings7:23 Then he cast a sea of brass
1Kings7:24 and under the brim of it as it were apples compassed and embrased the sea of ten cubits wide in two rows cast with it when it was cast.
1Kings7:25 And it stood on twelve oxen: of which three looked North
1Kings7:26 And it was an hand breadth thick
1Kings7:27 And he made ten bottoms of brass
1Kings7:28 And the works of the bottoms was on this manner that the sides were as it were flat boards between the ledges.
1Kings7:29 And on the flat boards between the ledges were lions
1Kings7:30 And every bottom had four brazen wheels
1Kings7:31 And the stalk of the lavatory was in the middle of the bottom one cubit high
1Kings7:32 And under the sides were four wheels and their axletrees joined fast to the bottom. And the height of every wheel was a cubit and an half.
1Kings7:33 And the workmanship of the wheels was like the work of a chariot wheel. And the axletrees
1Kings7:34 And the four undersetters in the four corners were of the very bottoms.
1Kings7:35 And under the stalk of the lavatory in the midst of the bottom was there a round foot of half a cubit high. And thereon plain places and ledges of the self.
1Kings7:36 And he graved in the plain and also on the ledges
1Kings7:37 And on this manner he made the ten bottoms cast work: all of one measure and one size.
1Kings7:38 Then he made ten lavers of brass containing forty Baths apiece
1Kings7:39 And he put five of those bottoms on the right corner of the temple
1Kings7:40 And Hiram made pots
1Kings7:41 That is to say two pillars and two scalps of the heads that were on the tops of the two pillars
1Kings7:42 and four hundredth pomegranates upon two wreaths
1Kings7:43 And the ten bottoms of brass with ten lavers upon them:
1Kings7:44 and a sea with twelve oxen under it:
1Kings7:45 And pots
1Kings7:46 And in the plain of Jordan did the king cast them: even in the clay of the earth between Socoh and Zarthan.
1Kings7:47 And Salomon left all the vessels unweighed
1Kings7:48 And Salomon made all the vessels that pertained unto the house of the LORD: the Altar of gold
1Kings7:49 And five candlesticks
1Kings7:50 and Bowls
1Kings7:51 And so was ended all the work that king Salomon made for the house of the LORD. And then Salomon brought in the things which David his father had dedicated in silver
1Kings8:1 Then Salomon gathered the elders of Israel
1Kings8:2 And all the men of Israel assembled unto king Salomon to the feast that falleth in the month Ethanim which is the seventh month.
1Kings8:3 And when all the elders of Israel were come
1Kings8:4 and brought it and also the tabernacle of witness and all the holy vessels that were therein. And the priests and the Levites brought them up.
1Kings8:5 And king Salomon and all the congregation of Israel that were assembled
1Kings8:6 And so the priests brought the Ark of the appointment of the LORD unto his place: even into the quere of the temple and place most holy
1Kings8:7 For the Cherubs stretched out their wings over the Ark
1Kings8:8 Howbeit the staves were so long that the ends of them appeared out of the holy place before the quere
1Kings8:9 And there was nothing in the Ark save the two tables of stone which Moses put there at Horeb
1Kings8:10 And when the priests were come out of the Holy place
1Kings8:11 that the priests could not endure to minister because of the cloud: for the glory of the LORD had filled the house of the LORD.
1Kings8:12 Then spake Salomon: the LORD said
1Kings8:13 I have built thee an house to dwell in
1Kings8:14 And the king turned his face and blessed all the congregation of Israel
1Kings8:15 And he said: Blessed be the LORD God of Israel
1Kings8:16 from the day I brought my people Israel out of Egypt
1Kings8:17 And it was in the heart of David my father
1Kings8:18 But the LORD God said unto David my father: in that it was in thine heart to build an house for my name
1Kings8:19 Nevertheless thou shalt not build the house
1Kings8:20 And the LORD hath made good his word that he spake. For I stood up in the room of David my father
1Kings8:21 And I have prepared therein a place for the Ark wherein the covenant of the LORD is
1Kings8:22 And Salomon stood before the altar of the LORD in the sight of all the congregation of Israel and stretched out his hands to heaven
1Kings8:23 and said: LORD God of Israel
1Kings8:24 which also hast kept with thy servants David my father
1Kings8:25 And now LORD God of Israel
1Kings8:26 Now then O Lord God of Israel
1Kings8:27 But indeed can God dwell on the earth? Behold neither heaven
1Kings8:28 but look to the prayer of thy servant and his supplication O LORD my God
1Kings8:29 that thine eyes may be open upon this house night and day
1Kings8:30 And hearken unto the supplication of thy servant and of thy people Israel which they shall pray in this place: And hear thou up unto heaven thy dwelling place
1Kings8:31 And if any man trespass against his neighbour
1Kings8:32 then hearken thou up to heaven
1Kings8:33 When thy people Israel be put to the worse before their enemies
1Kings8:34 Then hear thou up to heaven and be merciful unto the sin of thy people Israel
1Kings8:35 If heaven be shut up
1Kings8:36 then hear thou up to heaven
1Kings8:37 If there chance dearth in the land
1Kings8:38 then hear thou up to heaven thy dwelling place
1Kings8:39 and be merciful
1Kings8:40 that they may fear thee as long as they live upon the earth which thou gavest unto their fathers.
1Kings8:41 And likewise if a stranger that is not of thy people Israel come out of a far country for thy name's sake
1Kings8:42 (for they shall hear of thy great name and of thy mighty hand and stretched out Arm) and therefore if he come and pray at this place
1Kings8:43 hear thou up to heaven thy dwelling place and do according to all that the stranger calleth to thee for: that all nations of the earth may know thy name
1Kings8:44 And when they go out to battle against their enemies whither soever thou shalt send them
1Kings8:45 hear thou their prayers and supplications
1Kings8:46 And finally when they shall have sinned against thee (for there is no man that sinneth not) and thou art angry with them and hast delivered them to their enemies
1Kings8:47 yet if they turn again unto their hearts in the land where they be in captivity
1Kings8:48 and so turn again unto thee with all their hearts and all their souls in the land of their enemies which hold them captive
1Kings8:49 then hear thou their prayer and supplication up to heaven thy dwelling place
1Kings8:50 and be merciful unto thy people that have sinned against thee
1Kings8:51 For they be thy people and thine inheritance which thou broughtest out of Egypt
1Kings8:52 And let thine eyes be open unto the prayer of thy servant and unto the prayer of thy people Israel
1Kings8:53 For thou didst separate them to be thine inheritance
1Kings8:54 And when Salomon had made an end of praying all this prayer and supplication unto the LORD
1Kings8:55 and stood and blessed all the congregation of Israel with a loud voice saying:
1Kings8:56 Blessed be the LORD that hath given rest unto his people Israel according to all that he promised: so that there is not one word escaped of all the good promises which he promised by the hand of Moses his servant.
1Kings8:57 And the LORD our God be with us
1Kings8:58 but that he bow our hearts unto him
1Kings8:59 And these my words which I have prayed before the LORD
1Kings8:60 that all nations of the earth may know
1Kings8:61 And I pray God that your hearts may be sound with the LORD our God
1Kings8:62 And the king and all Israel with him
1Kings8:63 And the peaceofferings that Salomon offered unto the LORD
1Kings8:64 And the same day the king hallowed the middle of the court that is before the house of the LORD: for there he offered burntofferings and meatofferings and the fat of the peaceofferings
1Kings8:65 And Solomon held that same time an high feast and all Israel with him
1Kings8:66 And the eighth day he sent the people away. And they blessed the king and went unto their tents joyous and glad in heart for all the goodness that the LORD had shewed unto David his servant and to Israel his people.
1Kings9:1 And when Salomon had finished the building of the house of the LORD and of the king's palace
1Kings9:2 the LORD appeared unto him again
1Kings9:3 And the LORD said unto him: I have heard thy prayer and intercession that thou madest before me
1Kings9:4 And if thou wilt walk before me
1Kings9:5 then I will stablish the seat of thy kingdom upon Israel for ever
1Kings9:6 But and if ye and your children shall turn away from after me
1Kings9:7 then I will weed Israel out of the land which I have given them. And this house which I have hallowed for my name
1Kings9:8 And this house which is so high
1Kings9:9 And it shall be answered then
1Kings9:10 Then at the end of twenty year in which Salomon had built the two houses
1Kings9:11 because Hiram the king of Tyre had supported him with Cedar
1Kings9:12 And when Hiram was come from Tyre to see the cities which Salomon had given him
1Kings9:13 Wherefore he said: what cities are these which thou hast given me
1Kings9:14 This Hiram had sent the king six score hundredweight of gold.
1Kings9:15 And this is the sum of the tribute
1Kings9:16 For Pharao king of Egypt went up and took Gazer and burnt it with fire
1Kings9:17 And Salomon built Gazer and Beth Horon the nether:
1Kings9:18 and Baalath and Thamar in the wilderness that is in the land:
1Kings9:19 and all the store cities that Salomon had
1Kings9:20 And all the people that were left of the Amorites
1Kings9:21 the children of the said nations that were left in the land
1Kings9:22 And of the children of Israel did Salomon make no bondmen. But they were men of war and his servants and his lords and Captains and rulers of his chariots and of his Horsemen.
1Kings9:23 And these many lords that oversaw the work had Salomon
1Kings9:24 And Pharao's daughter came up out of the city of David unto the house which Salomon had built for her. And after that he built Mello.
1Kings9:25 And thrice a year did Salomon offer burntofferings and peaceofferings upon the altar which he had built unto the LORD
1Kings9:26 he made ships in Azion Gaber which is beside Eloth
1Kings9:27 And Hiram sent by ship also of his servants
1Kings9:28 And they went to Ophir and fetched from thence gold
1Kings10:1 And the queen of Saba heard of the wisdom of Salomon concerning the name of the LORD
1Kings10:2 And she came to Jerusalem with a mighty great multitude of Camels that bare sweet odours and gold exceeding much
1Kings10:3 And Salomon expounded her all her questions
1Kings10:4 And when the queen of Saba had seen all Salomon's wisdom and the house that he had built
1Kings10:5 and the meat of his table
1Kings10:6 Then she said to the king: the word I heard in mine own land of thy deeds and wisdom
1Kings10:7 How be it I believed it not
1Kings10:8 Happy are thy men: and happy are these thy servants
1Kings10:9 And blessed be the LORD thy God which had a lust to thee
1Kings10:10 And she gave the king an hundredth and twenty talents of gold
1Kings10:11 And thereto the ships of Hiram that brought gold from Ophir
1Kings10:12 And king Salomon made of the Almuge trees pillars in the house of the LORD in the king's palace
1Kings10:13 And king Salomon gave unto the Queen of Saba all her desire that she asked of him: besides that he gave her with his own hand. And so she returned unto her own country with her servants.
1Kings10:14 The weight of gold that came to Salomon in one year
1Kings10:15 besides that he had of chapmen and merchants of Poticaries and of all the kings of Araby and of the lords of the country.
1Kings10:16 And king Salomon made two hundredth bucklers of beaten gold
1Kings10:17 And he made three hundredth shields of beaten gold
1Kings10:18 And the king made a great seat of Ivory and covered it with precious gold.
1Kings10:19 And the seat had six steps. And the top of the seat was round behind his back with pommels on either side on the place where he sat
1Kings10:20 And there stood twelve lions on the steps
1Kings10:21 And all king Salomon's drinking vessels were of gold
1Kings10:22 For the sea ships which the king had in the sea
1Kings10:23 And so king Salomon exceeded all the kings of the earth in riches and wisdom.
1Kings10:24 And all the world resorted to Salomon
1Kings10:25 And brought him every man his present
1Kings10:26 And Salomon gathered together chariots and horsemen: so that he had a thousand and four hundredth chariots
1Kings10:27 And the king made silver in Jerusalem as plenteous as stones
1Kings10:28 And Salomon's horses came out of Egypt from Keva: the merchants fetched them from Keva at a price.
1Kings10:29 A chariot came out of Egypt for six hundredth sicles of silver
1Kings11:1 But king Salomon loved many outlandish women: the daughter of Pharao: and of the Moabites
1Kings11:2 even of nations of which the LORD said unto the children of Israel
1Kings11:3 And he had seven hundredth very Queens and three hundredth concubines which turned always his heart.
1Kings11:4 For when Salomon was old
1Kings11:5 And Salomon followed Astaroth the God of the Zidons
1Kings11:6 And Salomon wrought wickedness in the sight of the LORD and continued not after the LORD as did David his father.
1Kings11:7 After that Salomon built an altar for Chamos the abomination of the Moabites in the hill that standeth before Jerusalem
1Kings11:8 And likewise did he for all his outlandish wives which burnt cense and offered unto their gods.
1Kings11:9 Then was the LORD angry with Salomon because his heart turned from the LORD God of Israel which appeared unto him twice
1Kings11:10 and gave him a charge of this thing that he should not follow other gods. But he kept not that which the LORD commanded him:
1Kings11:11 whereupon the LORD said to Salomon: for as much as this is done of thee
1Kings11:12 Notwithstanding in thy days I will not do it
1Kings11:13 How be it I will not take away all: but will give one tribe to thy son
1Kings11:14 And the LORD stirred up an adversary unto Salomon
1Kings11:15 For when David was in Edom
1Kings11:16 for six months did Joab remain there and all Israel
1Kings11:17 And this Hadad fled and certain other Edomites of his father's servants with him
1Kings11:18 And they arose out of Madian and went to Pharan
1Kings11:19 And Hadad gat great favour in the sight of Pharao
1Kings11:20 And the sister of Thahaphnes bare him Senubath his son. And Thahaphnes weaned him in Pharao's house. And he was in Pharao's house among the sons of Pharao.
1Kings11:21 And when Hadad heard say in Egypt that David was laid to sleep with his fathers
1Kings11:22 Then said Pharao unto him: what lackest thou here with me
1Kings11:23 And God stirred up another adversary
1Kings11:24 Which Razon gathered men unto him and became captain over the company
1Kings11:25 And he was an adversary to Israel all the days of Salomon
1Kings11:26 And Jeroboam the son of Nabat an Ephrathite of Zaredah (whose mother was called Zeruah and was a widow and Salomon's servant) lifted up his hand against the king. And hereof came it
1Kings11:27 Salomon built Mello and mended broken places in the city of David his father.
1Kings11:28 And this fellow Jeroboam was an active man. And when Salomon saw the young man
1Kings11:29 And it chanced at that season as Jeroboam was gone out of Jerusalem
1Kings11:30 And Ahiah caught the new mantle that was on him and rent it in twelve pieces
1Kings11:31 and said to Jeroboam: take thee ten pieces. For this sayeth the LORD God of Israel: behold
1Kings11:32 and he shall have one
1Kings11:33 because they have forsaken me
1Kings11:34 How be it I will take none of the kingdom out of his hand: but will make him chief all his life long
1Kings11:35 But I will take the kingdom out of his son's hand and will give unto thee ten tribes of it:
1Kings11:36 and will give his son one
1Kings11:37 And I will take thee
1Kings11:38 And if thou shalt hearken unto all that I command thee
1Kings11:39 And therewith will I vex the seed of David
1Kings11:40 And Salomon sought to kill Jeroboam: wherefore Jeroboam arose and fled into Egypt unto Sesak king of Egypt and continued there until the death of Salomon.

1Kings11:42 And the time that Salomon reigned in Jerusalem upon all Israel
1Kings11:43 And then he laid him to rest with his fathers
1Kings12:1 And Rehoboam went to Sichem: for all Israel were come to Sichem to make him king.
1Kings12:2 And when Rehoboam the son of Nabat heard of it being yet in Egypt: for he fled to Egypt for fear of Salomon
1Kings12:3 And they sent and called him: and so Jeroboam and all the congregation of Israel came and spake unto Rehoboam saying:
1Kings12:4 thy father made our yoke grievous
1Kings12:5 And he said unto them: depart yet for the space of three days
1Kings12:6 And king Rehoboam took counsel with the old men that stood before Salomon his father
1Kings12:7 And they said unto him: If thou shalt be a servant unto this people this day and serve them and answer them and speak kind words to them: they will be thy servants for ever.
1Kings12:8 But he left the counsel that the old men had given him
1Kings12:9 And he said unto them: what counsel give ye
1Kings12:10 And the young men that were brought up with him
1Kings12:11 And now where my father put a grievous yoke upon you
1Kings12:12 And when Jeroboam: and all the people were come to Rehoboam the third day
1Kings12:13 the king answered the people churlishly and left the advice that old men gave him:
1Kings12:14 and spake to them after the counsel of the young men saying: My father made your yoke grievous
1Kings12:15 And so the king hearkened not unto the people: for the turning away was of the LORD
1Kings12:16 And when all Israel saw that the king regarded them not
1Kings12:17 How be it over the children of Israel which dwelt in the city of Juda
1Kings12:18 Then king Rehoboam sent Aduram the receiver of the tribute. And all Israel stoned him to death with stones. But king Rehoboam made speed and gat him up to his chariot and fled to Jerusalem.
1Kings12:19 And so Israel departed from the house of David unto this day.
1Kings12:20 And when all Israel heard that Jeroboam was come again
1Kings12:21 And when Rehoboam was come to Jerusalem
1Kings12:22 Then came the word of the Lord(God) unto Semeiah the man of God saying:
1Kings12:23 speak unto Rehoboam the son of Salomon king of Juda
1Kings12:24 Thus sayeth the LORD: go not nor yet fight against your brethren the children of Israel
1Kings12:25 Then Jeroboam built Sichem in mount Ephraim
1Kings12:26 And Jeroboam thought in his heart: now might the kingdom return to the house of David again.
1Kings12:27 For if this people shall go up and do sacrifice in the house of the LORD at Jerusalem
1Kings12:28 Whereupon the king took counsel and made two calves of gold and said unto the people: ye shall not need to go any more to Jerusalem. Behold your gods(goddes) Israel which brought you out of the land of Egypt.
1Kings12:29 And he put the one in Bethel and the other in Dan.
1Kings12:30 And that doing was a cause of sin. And the people went before the one as far as Dan.
1Kings12:31 And he made houses of hill altars
1Kings12:32 And Jeroboam made a feast the fifteenth day of the eighth month
1Kings12:33 And he offered upon the altar which he had made in Bethel
1Kings13:1 And behold
1Kings13:2 and cried against the altar at the commandment of the LORD and said: altar
1Kings13:3 And he gave them the same time a sign saying: this is the sign of that the LORD hath promised. Behold the altar shall rent and the ashes that are in it shall fall out.
1Kings13:4 And when the king heard the saying of the man of God which he cried against the altar in Bethel
1Kings13:5 and the altar clave and the ashes ran out of the altar according to the token which the man of God had given at the commandment of the LORD.
1Kings13:6 And the king answered and said unto the man of God: Oh pray unto the LORD thy God and make intercession for me
1Kings13:7 Then said the king unto the man of God: come home with me and refresh thyself
1Kings13:8 But the man of God answered the king
1Kings13:9 For so was it charged me
1Kings13:10 And so he went another way and returned not by the way he came to Bethel.
1Kings13:11 And there dwelt an old prophet in Bethel
1Kings13:12 And their father said unto them: what way went he? for his sons had seen what way the man of God went which came from Juda.
1Kings13:13 Then said he to his sons: saddle mine Ass. And they saddled him an Ass.
1Kings13:14 And he gat him up thereon and went after the man of God
1Kings13:15 Then he said to him: come home with me and eat bread.
1Kings13:16 And the other said again I may not return with thee
1Kings13:17 For it was said to me by the commandment of the LORD
1Kings13:18 And the old prophet said unto him: I am a prophet as well as thou
1Kings13:19 And so the other went again with him and ate bread in his house and drank water.
1Kings13:20 And as they sat at the table
1Kings13:21 And he cried unto the man of God that came from Juda
1Kings13:22 but camest back again and hast eaten bread and drunk water in the place in which he bade that thou shouldest eat no bread nor drink water: therefore thy carcass shall not come unto the sepulchre of thy fathers.
1Kings13:23 And when he had eaten bread and drunk
1Kings13:24 And as he journeyed a Lion met him by the way and slew him
1Kings13:25 And men that passed by
1Kings13:26 And when the prophet that brought him back again from the way
1Kings13:27 And he said to his sons saddle me an Ass: so they did.
1Kings13:28 And he went and found the body cast along in the way and the Ass and the lion standing thereby. And the Lion had not eaten the carcass nor hurt the Ass.
1Kings13:29 And he took up the body of the man of God and put it upon the Ass and brought it again
1Kings13:30 And he laid his body in his own grave and lamented over him
1Kings13:31 And when he had buried him
1Kings13:32 For the saying which he cried at the bidding of the LORD against the altar in Bethel
1Kings13:33 How be it for all that
1Kings13:34 And this doing was sin unto the house of Jeroboam
