1Kings22:2 And the third year Jehosaphat king of Juda came to the king of Israel.
1Kings22:3 Then said the king of Israel unto his servants
1Kings22:4 And he said unto Jehosaphat: wilt thou go with me to battle
1Kings22:5 And Jehosaphat said unto the king of Israel: ask counsel
1Kings22:6 And then the king of Israel gathered of the Prophets together
1Kings22:7 And Jehosaphat said: is there never a Prophet of the LORD's here more
1Kings22:8 And the king of Israel said to Jehosaphat: there is yet one
1Kings22:9 Then the king of Israel called unto one of his chamberlains and said: fetch Micheah the son of Jemlah hither at once.
1Kings22:10 And the king of Israel and Jehosaphat the king of Juda sat either in his seat and their apparel on them
1Kings22:11 And Zedekiah the son of Canaanah made horns of iron and said
1Kings22:12 And all the Prophets prophesied even so
1Kings22:13 And the messenger that was gone to call Micheah
1Kings22:14 And Micheah said: as surely as the LORD liveth
1Kings22:15 And when he was come to the king
1Kings22:16 And the king said unto him
1Kings22:17 Then he said
1Kings22:18 Then said the king of Israel to Jehosaphat: did I not tell thee
1Kings22:19 And Micheah said: hear therefore the word of the LORD. I saw the LORD sit on his seat and all his company of heaven standing about him
1Kings22:20 And the LORD said: who shall deceive Ahab that he may go and be slain at Ramoth in Gilead? and one said on this manner and another on that.
1Kings22:21 Then came forth a spirit and stood before the LORD and said: I will deceive him. And the LORD said: wherewith?
1Kings22:22 And he said: I will go out and be a false spirit in the mouths of all Prophets. And the LORD said
1Kings22:23 Now therefore behold
1Kings22:24 Then Zedechiah the son of Canaanah went and smote Micheah on the cheek
1Kings22:25 And Michaeah said: behold
1Kings22:26 And the king of Israel said: take Micheah and carry him back again into Amon the governor of the city
1Kings22:27 and say: thus sayeth the king. Put ye this fellow in the prison house and feed him with bread and water straightly
1Kings22:28 Then Micheah said
1Kings22:29 And so the king of Israel and Jehosaphat the king of Juda went up to Ramoth in Gilead.
1Kings22:30 And the king of Israel said to Jehosaphat change thee and get thee to war: but put on thine own apparel. And the king of Israel changed himself
1Kings22:31 But the king of Siria had commanded the captains of his chariots of which he had thirty two saying: fight neither with small nor great
1Kings22:32 And when the captains of the chariots saw Jehosaphat
1Kings22:33 But Jehosaphat cried out. And when the captains of the chariots saw that he was not the king of Israel
1Kings22:34 And a certain man drew a bow ignorantly and smote the king of Israel between the ribs of his harness. Wherefore the king said unto the driver of his chariot
1Kings22:35 And the battle increased that day
1Kings22:36 And there went a proclamation thorowout the host after the son was down
1Kings22:37 And when the king of Israel was dead
1Kings22:38 And while they washed the chariot in the pool of Samaria
1Kings22:39 The rest of the deeds of Ahab
1Kings22:40 And when Ahab was laid to rest with his fathers
1Kings22:41 Jehosaphat the son of Asa began to reign upon Juda
1Kings22:42 And his mother's name was Azubah the daughter of Salahi.
1Kings22:43 And he walked in all the ways of Asa his father and bowed not therefrom. But did that was right in the eyes of the LORD. Only he did not put the hillaltars out of the way: for the people offered and burnt their sacrifices yet
1Kings22:44 And he had peace with the king of Israel.
1Kings22:45 And the rest of the deeds of Jehosaphat
1Kings22:46 And the remnant of the stews of the males
1Kings22:47 There was then no king in Edom
1Kings22:48 And Jehosaphat made ships in the sea to go to Ophir for gold
1Kings22:49 Then said Ohoziah the son of Ahab unto Jehosaphat: let my servants go with thine in the ships. But Jehosaphat would not.
1Kings22:50 And Jehosaphat laid him to sleep with his fathers
1Kings22:51 Ohoziah the son of Ahab began to reign upon Israel in Samaria
1Kings22:52 and did evil in the sight of the LORD
1Kings22:53 And he served Baal and bowed himself to him and angered the LORD God of Israel
2Kings1:1 And the Moabites rebelled against Israel after the death of Ahab.
2Kings1:2 And Ohoziah fell thorow a lattice window out of an upper chamber that he had in Samaria
2Kings1:3 But the Angel of the LORD spake to Eliah the Thesbite
2Kings1:4 Therefore thus sayeth the LORD: thou shalt not come down from the bed on which thou art gone up
2Kings1:5 And the messengers turned back again unto him. And he said unto them: why are ye thus come again?
2Kings1:6 And they said unto him there came a man against us and said unto us: go and turn again unto the king that sent you
2Kings1:7 And he said to them: what manner a man was that which met you and told you these words?
2Kings1:8 And they said unto him: it was an hairy man and girded with a girdle of a skin about his loins. And he said unto them: it is Eliah the Thesbite.
2Kings1:9 Then the king sent unto him a captain over fifty with his fifty. And when the captain was come to him
2Kings1:10 And Eliah answered and said to the captain over the fifty: if I be a man of God
2Kings1:11 And the king went again and sent to him another captain over fifty with his fifty
2Kings1:12 And Eliah answered and said unto them: if I be a man of God
2Kings1:13 And the king sent again the third captain over fifty. And when the third captain over fifty was come up to him
2Kings1:14 Behold there came fire down from heaven and burnt up the two fore captains over fifty with their fifties. But let my life now be precious in thy sight.
2Kings1:15 Then said the Angel of the LORD unto Eliah: go down with him
2Kings1:16 And he said unto the king
2Kings1:17 And so he died according to the word of the LORD which Eliah spake
2Kings1:18 The rest of the deeds of Ohoziah which he did
2Kings2:1 And it chanced when the LORD would take up Eliah to heaven in a whirlwind
2Kings2:2 And Eliah said to Eliseus
2Kings2:3 the children of the Prophets that were at Bethel came out to Eliseus and said to him: knowest thou not how that the LORD will take away thy master from thee this day? And he said
2Kings2:4 Then said Eliah to Eliseus: tarry here a fellowship
2Kings2:5 And the children of the Prophets that were at Jericho came to Eliseus and said unto him: art thou not ware that the LORD will take away thy master from thee this day? And he answered: I know it also
2Kings2:6 And Eliah said to him: tarry I pray thee here
2Kings2:7 And fifty men of the sons of the Prophets went and stood in sight afar off
2Kings2:8 And then Eliah took his mantle and wrapped it together and smote the water
2Kings2:9 And as soon as they were over
2Kings2:10 And he said: thou hast asked a hard thing. Nevertheless if thou see me when I am taken away from thee
2Kings2:11 And as they went walking and talking: behold
2Kings2:12 And Eliseus saw and cried: O my father
2Kings2:13 And then he took up the mantle of Eliah that fell from him
2Kings2:14 and took the mantle of Eliah that fell from him
2Kings2:15 And the children of the Prophets of Jericho which saw from afar
2Kings2:16 And they said unto him: see
2Kings2:17 Nevertheless yet they lay upon him
2Kings2:18 And they came again to him
2Kings2:19 Then the men of Jericho said to Eliseus: behold the city standeth pleasantly as my lord seeth
2Kings2:20 And he said: bring me a new cruse and put salt therein. And they brought it to him.
2Kings2:21 And he went unto the spring of the water and cast the salt in thither
2Kings2:22 And the water was healthsome ever after according to the saying of Eliseus which he spake.
2Kings2:23 And he went from thence up to Bethel. And as he was going up in the way
2Kings2:24 And he turned back and looked on them and cursed them in the name of the LORD. And there came two Bears out of the wood and tare forty two of the boys.
2Kings2:25 And he went from thence to mount Carmel
2Kings3:1 Jehoram the son of Ahab began to reign upon Israel in Samaria the eighteenth year of Jehosaphat king of Juda
2Kings3:2 And he wrought that was evil in the sight of the LORD: but not like his father or his mother
2Kings3:3 Nevertheless he continued in the sin of Jeroboam the son of Nabat which made Israel sin
2Kings3:4 And Mesa king of Moab which was rich of sheep (and was wont to render unto the king of Israel an hundredth thousand lambs and as many rams with the wool)
2Kings3:5 rebelled against the king of Israel after the death of Ahab.
2Kings3:6 And king Jehoram went out of Samaria the same season
2Kings3:7 And then went and sent to Jehosaphat king of Juda saying: the king of Moab hath rebelled against me
2Kings3:8 And he said: what way shall we go? And the other answered: the way thorow the wilderness of Edom.
2Kings3:9 And the king of Israel took his journey with the king of Juda and the king of Edom. And when they had compassed the way seven days
2Kings3:10 Then said the king of Israel: Alas
2Kings3:11 But Jehosaphat said: is there here no prophet of the LORD's
2Kings3:12 the word of the LORD is with him. And so the king of Israel
2Kings3:13 And Eliseus said to the king of Israel: what have I to do with thee? Get thee to the prophets of thy father and of thy mother. And the king of Israel said unto him: Oh nay
2Kings3:14 And Eliseus said
2Kings3:15 But now bring me a minstrel. And as the minstrel played
2Kings3:16 And he said: thus biddeth the LORD
2Kings3:17 For thus sayeth the LORD: ye shall see neither wind nor rain
2Kings3:18 And this is yet but a small thing in the sight of the LORD: But he will give the Moabites into your hands also.
2Kings3:19 And ye shall destroy all strong towns and all goodly cities
2Kings3:20 And in the morning about offering time: there came such a water that way from Edom that the country was filled with water.
2Kings3:21 And when all the Moabites heard that the kings were come to fight against them
2Kings3:22 And they being up early in the morning
2Kings3:23 they said: it is blood
2Kings3:24 And when they came to the Host of Israel
2Kings3:25 And they overthrew the cities
2Kings3:26 And when the king of Moab saw
2Kings3:27 And then he took his eldest son that should have reigned in his stead
2Kings4:1 And there cried a certain woman of the wives of the children of the prophets unto Eliseus saying: thy servant my husband is dead
2Kings4:2 And Eliseus said to her: What shall I do for thee? Tell me
2Kings4:3 And he said: Go and borrow thee in other places
2Kings4:4 And then go and shut the door to thee and to thy sons
2Kings4:5 And she went from him
2Kings4:6 And when the vessels were full
2Kings4:7 Then she came and told the man of God. And he said: go and sell the Oil and pay thy creditor. And live thou and thy children of the rest.
2Kings4:8 And it fell on a day
2Kings4:9 And she said unto her husband: see
2Kings4:10 Let us make him a chamber with a little wall
2Kings4:11 And it fortuned on a time that he came thither and turned into the chamber and lay there.
2Kings4:12 Then he said to Gihezi his servant: call this Sunamite. And he called her
2Kings4:13 And Eliseus said to him
2Kings4:14 Then he said
2Kings4:15 And he said: call her. And he called her. And she came and stood at the door.
2Kings4:16 Then he said: by such a time
2Kings4:17 And the wife conceived and bare a son the same season that Eliseus had said unto her
2Kings4:18 And when the lad was great
2Kings4:19 And there he complained unto his father
2Kings4:20 And he took him and brought him to his mother. And he sat on her knees till noon
2Kings4:21 And she carried him up and laid him on the bed of the man of God
2Kings4:22 and came to her husband and said: send me one of the young men and an Ass that I may run to the man of God.
2Kings4:23 And he said: wherefore wilt thou go to him today
2Kings4:24 Then she saddled an Ass and said to the lad: lead away and make me not cease riding
2Kings4:25 And so she went and came unto the man of God
2Kings4:26 Now run against her
2Kings4:27 Then she went to the man of God up to the hill and caught him by the feet. And Gihezi went to her
2Kings4:28 Then she said: did I desire a son of my lord? did I not say
2Kings4:29 Then he said to Gihezi: gird up thy loins
2Kings4:30 Notwithstanding the mother of the child said: as sure as the LORD liveth
2Kings4:31 Gihezi went before them and put the staff upon the lad. But there was neither voice nor attending. And then he went again against his master and told him saying: it hath not awaked the lad.
2Kings4:32 And when Eliseus was come to the house: Behold
2Kings4:33 And he went in and shut the door to the lad and him
2Kings4:34 And he went up and lay upon the lad
2Kings4:35 And went again and walked once up and down in the house
2Kings4:36 And he called Gihezi and said: Call for this Sunamite. And he called her. And when she was come to him
2Kings4:37 Then she went and fell at his feet and bowed herself to the ground
2Kings4:38 When Eliseus was come to Gilgal again
2Kings4:39 And one went out into the field
2Kings4:40 And they poured out for the men to eat. And when they had tasted of the pottage
2Kings4:41 Then he said: bring meal. And he cast it into the pot and said: fill for the people that they may eat
2Kings4:42 There came a man from Baalsalisa and brought the man of God bread of first fruits
2Kings4:43 what should I set this before an hundredth men? And he said: set it before the people and let them eat. For thus sayeth the LORD: they shall eat and leave.
2Kings4:44 And he set it before them
2Kings5:1 Naaman captain of the Host to the king of Siria
2Kings5:2 And there had gone a company of Soldiers out of Siria a running
2Kings5:3 And the Damsel said unto her lady: I would my master were with a prophet that is in Samaria: he would deliver him of his leprosy.
2Kings5:4 And she went and told her husband saying: thus and thus sayeth the maid that is out of the land of Israel.
2Kings5:5 And the king of Siria said: go thy way
2Kings5:6 And he brought the letter to the king of Israel containing this tenor: Now when this letter is come up to thee: Behold I have sent Naaman my servant to thee
2Kings5:7 And when the king of Israel had read the letter
2Kings5:8 And when Eliseus the man of God
2Kings5:9 And Naaman came with his horses and his chariot
2Kings5:10 Then Eliseus sent a messenger saying: go and wash thee in Jordan seven times
2Kings5:11 And Naaman was wroth and went away
2Kings5:12 Are not Abanah and Pharphar
2Kings5:13 Then came his servants and communed with him and said: Father if the prophet had bid thee done some great thing
2Kings5:14 Then he went down and washed seven times in Jordan
2Kings5:15 Then he turned again to the man of God and all his company with him
2Kings5:16 But he said as surely as the LORD liveth before whom I stand
2Kings5:17 And Naaman said
2Kings5:18 But herein the LORD(Lord) be merciful to thy servant
2Kings5:19 And he said to him: go in peace. And when he was departed from him a furlong of ground
2Kings5:20 Gihezi the servant of Eliseus the man of God
2Kings5:21 And so Gihezi went after Naaman. And when Naaman saw him coming after him
2Kings5:22 And he said yea. But my master hath sent me saying: see
2Kings5:23 And Naaman said: adventure and take two talents of silver in two bags
2Kings5:24 And he went in the dark and took it of their hands and bestowed it in the house
2Kings5:25 Then he went and stood before his master. And Eliseus said to him: whence cometh Gihezi? And he said: thy servant went no whither.
2Kings5:26 And he said: went not mine heart with thee
2Kings5:27 The leprosy therefore of Naaman cleave unto thee
2Kings6:1 Then said the children of the prophets unto Eliseus: see
2Kings6:2 Let us there take every man a beam and build us a place there
2Kings6:3 And one said: begin I pray thee
2Kings6:4 and so went with them. And when they came to Jordan
2Kings6:5 And as one was heaving of a beam
2Kings6:6 And the man of God said: Where fell it? And he shewed him the place. And he cut an helve and cast it in thither
2Kings6:7 And he said: take it up to thee. And the other stretched his hand and caught it.
2Kings6:8 And the king of Siria fought against Israel
2Kings6:9 And the man of God sent unto the king of Israel saying: Beware thou go not by such a place
2Kings6:10 And the king of Israel sent to the place which the man of God told him and warned him of
2Kings6:11 And the heart of the king of Siria was troubled therewith
2Kings6:12 And one of his servants said: nay my lord king: Eliseus the prophet that is in Israel
2Kings6:13 Then he said: go and spy where he is that I may send and fetch him. And one told the king saying: see
2Kings6:14 And he sent thither horse and chariots and a great Host. And they came thither by night and compassed the town about.
2Kings6:15 And when the servant of the men of God rose up early and was gone out: Behold: there was an Host round about the town with horses and chariots. Then said his lad to him: Alas master
2Kings6:16 And he said: fear not
2Kings6:17 And Eliseus prayed and said: LORD open the eyes of the young man
2Kings6:18 And when the soldiers came down to him
2Kings6:19 Then Eliseus said unto them this is not the way
2Kings6:20 When they were come to Samaria
2Kings6:21 And then said the king of Israel unto Eliseus when he saw them: shall I smite them
2Kings6:22 And he said: smite them not. But smite them thou takest with thine own sword and with thine own bow. But set bread and water before them
2Kings6:23 And he made great ordinance before them. And when they had eaten and drunk
2Kings6:24 After this Benhadad king of Siria gathered all the Host
2Kings6:25 And there arose great dearth in Samaria: for they had besieged it
2Kings6:26 And as the king of Israel walked upon the walls there cried a woman to him
2Kings6:27 And he said: Nay
2Kings6:28 Then said the king to her: what aileth thee? And she answered: this woman here said to me
2Kings6:29 And so we dressed my son and did eat him. And I said to her another day
2Kings6:30 When the king heard the words of the woman
2Kings6:31 Then he said: God do so and so to me: if the head of Eliseus the son of Saphat tarry on him this day.
2Kings6:32 And as Eliseus sat in his house and the elders with him
2Kings6:33 And while he yet talked with them: Behold the messenger was come unto him. And he said: behold
2Kings7:1 Then Eliseus said: hear the word of the LORD: for thus sayeth the LORD: tomorrow this time a bushel of fine flour shall be sold for a sicle and two bushels of Barley for another in the gates of Samaria.
2Kings7:2 Then a great lord on whose hand the king leaned
2Kings7:3 And there were four lepers set without the gate of Samaria. And they said each to his companion
2Kings7:4 though we thought that we might come into the city
2Kings7:5 And so they arose in the dark to go to the Host of the Sirians. And when they were come to the side of the Host of Siria: see
2Kings7:6 For the Lord(Lorde) had made the host of the Sirians hear a noise of chariots and a noise of horses
2Kings7:7 And upon that they arose and fled in the dark
2Kings7:8 And when these lepers came to the edge of the Host
2Kings7:9 Then they said one to another: it is not well that we do
2Kings7:10 And so they went and called to the porter of the city and told them saying: we came to the pavilions of the Sirians: and see
2Kings7:11 Then the porters called and told the kings house within.
2Kings7:12 And the king arose in the night and said to his servants: I will shew you
2Kings7:13 And one of his servants answered and said: Let men take five of the horses that remain and are left in the city. Behold they are as good as all the multitude that are left in the city: and as good as all the multitude of the city that are consumed
2Kings7:14 And they took two chariots of horses
2Kings7:15 And they followed after them even unto Jordan: and see
2Kings7:16 And then the people went out and robbed the tents of the Sirians. And so a bushel of flour was sold for a sicle
2Kings7:17 And the king set the lord on whose hand he leaned
2Kings7:18 And it came to pass according to the word of the man of God to the king saying: two bushels of barley for a sicle and a bushel of flour for another shall be tomorrow this time in the gates of Samaria.
2Kings7:19 And the lord answered the man of God and said: though the LORD made windows in heaven
2Kings7:20 And so it chanced: for the people trod him under feet in the gate

2Kings8:2 And the woman arose and did after the saying of the man of God
2Kings8:3 And at the seven years' end
2Kings8:4 And the king was talking with Gihezi the servant to the man of God saying: tell me I pray thee
2Kings8:5 And it chanced as he was telling the king how he restored a dead body to life again that the woman whose son he revived
2Kings8:6 And the king asked the woman
2Kings8:7 After that Eliseus went to Damasco
2Kings8:8 Then said the king to Hazael: take a present with thee and go against the man of God and ask the LORD by him whether I shall recover of this my disease or no.
2Kings8:9 And Hazael went to meet him and took presents with him
2Kings8:10 And Eliseus said to him: go and say to him
2Kings8:11 And the man of God began to look earnestly
2Kings8:12 And Hazael said: why weepeth my lord: And he answered: for I know that thou shalt do evil unto the children of Israel: their strong cities thou shalt set on fire
2Kings8:13 And Hazael said: what is thy servant which am but a dog
2Kings8:14 And so he departed from Eliseus and came to his master
2Kings8:15 And on the morrow he took a rough cloth and dipped it in the water and spread it on his face
2Kings8:16 The fifth year of Jehoram son of Ahab king of Israel
2Kings8:17 Thirty two year old was he when he began to reign
2Kings8:18 And he walked in the way of the kings of Israel
2Kings8:19 Neverthelater the LORD would not destroy Juda because of David his servant
2Kings8:20 And in his days Edom fell away from under the hand of Juda
2Kings8:21 And Jehoram went to Seir
2Kings8:22 And so the Edomites slipped away from under the hand of Juda unto this day. And then Lobnah slipped away too
2Kings8:23 The rest of the deeds of Jehoram and all he did
2Kings8:24 And Jehoram rested with his fathers and was buried with his fathers in the city of David. And Ohoziah his son reigned in his stead.
2Kings8:25 The twelfth year of Jehoram son of Ahab
2Kings8:26 Two and twenty year old was Ohoziah when he began to reign
2Kings8:27 And he walked in the way of the house of Ahab: (and did that which was evil in the sight of the LORD) for he was a son-in-law to the house of Ahab.
2Kings8:28 And Jehoram the son of Ahab went to war with Hazael king of Siria
2Kings8:29 Wherefore king Jehoram went back again

2Kings9:5 and when he came
2Kings9:6 And he arose and went into the house. And the other poured the oil on his head and said to him
2Kings9:7 that thou slay the household of Ahab thy master. For I (sayeth the LORD) will avenge the blood of my servants the prophets
2Kings9:8 (for the whole house of Ahab shall be destroyed) and I will destroy unto Ahab what pisseth against the wall
2Kings9:9 and will make the house of Ahab like the house of Jeroboam son of Nabat
2Kings9:10 And as for Jezabel the dogs shall eat her in the field of Jezrahel
2Kings9:11 And when Jehu was come out to the servants of his lord
2Kings9:12 And they said: it is not so. But tell us a fellowship. And he said
2Kings9:13 And they hasted and took every man his mantle and put under him on an high bench at the top of the steps

2Kings9:15 and was returned to be healed in Jezrahel
2Kings9:16 And Jehu rode and went to Jezrahel: for Jehoram lay there
2Kings9:17 And the watchmen that stood on the tower in Jezrahel spied the company of Jehu as he came
2Kings9:18 And there went one on horseback against him and said: thus sayeth the king: is it peace? And Jehu answered: what hast thou to do with peace? turn and come after me. And the watchman told saying: the messenger came to them but he cometh not again.
2Kings9:19 Then he sent out another on horseback
2Kings9:20 And the watchman told saying: he came to them
2Kings9:21 Then said Jehoram
2Kings9:22 And when Jehoram saw Jehu
2Kings9:23 And Jehoram turned his hand and fled

2Kings9:29 And in the eleventh year of Jehoram son of David
2Kings9:30 And when Jehu was come to Jezrahel
2Kings9:31 And as Jehu entered at the gate she said: had Zamri peace which slew his master?
2Kings9:32 And he lifted up his eyes to the window and said
2Kings9:33 And he said: Throw her down. And they threw her down. And he sprinkled of her blood upon the walls and on the horses
2Kings9:34 And then when he was come in and had eaten and drunk
2Kings9:35 And when they came to bury her
2Kings9:36 And they came again and told him. And he said: it is the word of the LORD which he spake by the hand of his servant Eliah the Thesbite saying: in the field of Jezrahel shall dogs eat the flesh of Jezabel
2Kings9:37 and the carcass of Jezabel shall be dung upon the earth
2Kings10:1 Ahab had seventy sons in Samaria. And Jehu wrote letters
2Kings10:2 ye have with you your master's sons
2Kings10:3 Therefore choose the best
2Kings10:4 And they were accordingly afraid
2Kings10:5 And the governor of the king's house and of the city and of the elders and the nurses sent to Jehu saying: we are thy servants and will do all that thou shalt bid us: we will make no man king: but do thou what seemeth good in thine eyes.
2Kings10:6 Then he wrote another letter to them saying: If ye be mine and will obey my voice
2Kings10:7 And when the letter came to them
2Kings10:8 And there came a messenger and told him saying: they have brought the heads of the king's sons. And he said: let them put them on two heaps in the entering of the gate
2Kings10:9 And in the morning he went out and stood and said to all the folk
2Kings10:10 consider now how there is nothing of the LORD's word fallen to the earth
2Kings10:11 And so Jehu slew all that remained of the house of Ahab
2Kings10:12 And he arose and departed and went to Samaria. And as Jehu was come even to the house where the shepherds bind their sheep by the high way side
2Kings10:13 he met with the brethren of Ohoziah king of Juda
2Kings10:14 And he said: take them alive. And they took them alive
2Kings10:15 And when he was departed thence he met with Jehonadab the son of Rechab coming against him. And Jehu saluted him and said to him: is thine heart right
2Kings10:16 and said: go with me and see the zeal I have to the LORD
2Kings10:17 And when he came to Samaria
2Kings10:18 After that Jehu gathered all the people together and said to them: Ahab served Baal a little: but Jehu shall serve him a good.
2Kings10:19 Now therefore call unto me all the Prophets of Baal and all his servants and all his priests that none be lacking. For I have a great sacrifice to do to Baal: and if any be missed
2Kings10:20 Wherefore Jehu said: Appoint a solempne feast for Baal
2Kings10:21 Then Jehu sent thorowout all Israel. And all the servants of Baal came
2Kings10:22 Then he said to the keeper of the vestry
2Kings10:23 And Jehu went with Jehonadab son of Rechab into the house of Baal: and said to the servants of Baal: search and look that there be none here with you of the servants of the LORD
2Kings10:24 And they went in
2Kings10:25 And as soon as he had made an end of offering of burnt sacrifice
2Kings10:26 and fetched out the image out of the house of Baal and burnt it.
2Kings10:27 And they brake the Image of Baal
2Kings10:28 And so Jehu destroyed Baal out of Israel.
2Kings10:29 But from the sins of Jeroboam the son of Nabat which made Israel sin with the golden calves in Bethel and Dan
2Kings10:30 Then the LORD said to Jehu
2Kings10:31 But Jehu cared not to walk in the law of the LORD God of Israel with all his heart: for he departed not from the sins of Jeroboam which made Israel sin.
2Kings10:32 In those days the LORD began to cut Israel short
2Kings10:33 from Jordan Eastward: even all the land of Gilead
2Kings10:34 The rest of the acts of Jehu
2Kings10:35 And when Jehu was laid to rest with his fathers
2Kings10:36 And the time that Jehu reigned upon Israel in Samaria
2Kings11:1 And Athaliah the mother of Ohoziah
2Kings11:2 But Jehosaba the daughter of king Jehoram and sister of Ohoziah
2Kings11:3 And he was with her
2Kings11:4 And the seventh year Jehoiada sent and fetched the rulers over hundreds with the captains and them of the guard
2Kings11:5 And he commanded them saying: this is that ye must do: one third part of you shall come on the Sabbath day and keep the watch of the king's house:
2Kings11:6 And another third part shall be at the gate Sur: and another third part shall be at the gate behind the guard chamber
2Kings11:7 and two portions of you: that is
2Kings11:8 and shall compass the king round about
2Kings11:9 And the captains over the hundreds did all the things as Jehoiada the priest commanded: and took every man his men
2Kings11:10 And the priest gave to the captains over hundreds the spears and shields that were king David's and had remained in the temple.
2Kings11:11 And the guard stood every man with his weapon in his hand round about the king
2Kings11:12 And he brought out the son of the king and put the crown upon him
2Kings11:13 And when Athaliah heard the noise of the running of the people
2Kings11:14 And when she saw the king stand by a pillar (as the manner was) and the singers and the trumpets by the king
2Kings11:15 And Jehoiada the priest commanded the Captains of the hundreds that had the rule of the host
2Kings11:16 And they laid hands on her
2Kings11:17 And Jehoiada made a bond both between the LORD and the king
2Kings11:18 Then all the people of the land went into the house of Baal
2Kings11:19 and took the rulers over hundreds and the captains and the guard and all the people of the land: And they brought the king from the house of the LORD and went the way of the gate of the guard of the king's house. And he sat him down on the seat of the kings.
2Kings11:20 And all the people of the Lord(land) rejoiced
2Kings11:21 Jehoas was seven year old when he was made king.
2Kings12:1 And he began to reign the seventh years of Jehu
2Kings12:2 And he did that pleased the LORD
2Kings12:3 But they took not away the hill altars
2Kings12:4 And Jehoas said to the priest: all the silver that is dedicate and brought to the house of the LORD in current money
2Kings12:5 let the priests take it to them
2Kings12:6 Neverthelater the priests had not mended unto the twenty third year of Jehoas
2Kings12:7 Then king Jehoas called for Jehoiada the priest and for the other priests also
2Kings12:8 And the priests consented to receive no more money of the people: But that it should go to the mending of the temple.
2Kings12:9 Then Jehoiada the priest took a coffer and bored a hole in the lid of it
2Kings12:10 And when they saw that there was much money in the coffer the king's scribe and the high priest came
2Kings12:11 And they gave the money by sums into the hands of the workmen that had the oversight of the house of the LORD: and they brought it out to the carpenters and builders that wrought upon the house of the LORD
2Kings12:12 and to masons and hewers of stone
2Kings12:13 howbeit there was not made in the house of the LORD
2Kings12:14 For they gave that to the workmen
2Kings12:15 Moreover they reckoned not with the men
2Kings12:16 How be it trespass money and sin money might not be brought into the house of the LORD

2Kings12:18 But Jehoas king of Juda took all the dedicate things that Jehosaphat

2Kings13:1 In the twenty third year of Jehoas son of Ohoziah king of Juda
2Kings13:2 And he wrought wickedness in the sight of the LORD: for he followed the sin of Jeroboam the son of Nabat which made Israel sin
2Kings13:3 Wherefore the LORD was angry with Israel
2Kings13:4 But Jehoahaz besought the LORD
2Kings13:5 And therefore the LORD gave Israel a deliverer and they went out from under the hands of the Sirians. And the children of Israel dwelt in their tents as before time.

2Kings14:1 The second year of Joas son of Jehoahaz king of Israel reigned Amaziah the son of Jehoas king of Juda:
2Kings14:2 he was twenty five year old when he began
2Kings14:3 And he did that pleased the LORD
2Kings14:4 Neither took they away the hill altars. But the people slew and offered still in the hill altars.
2Kings14:5 And as soon as the kingdom was settled in his hand
2Kings14:6 But the children of those murderers he slew not
2Kings14:7 And he slew of the Edomites in the salt valley ten thousand
2Kings14:8 After that Amaziah sent messengers to Joas the son of Jehoahaz son of Jehu king of Israel saying: come
2Kings14:9 But Joas king of Israel sent again to Amaziah king of Juda saying: A thistle in Libanon sent to a cypress tree in Libanon saying: give thy daughter to my son to wife. But the wild beasts in Libanon went and trod down the thistle.
2Kings14:10 Because thou hast beaten the Edomites
2Kings14:11 But Amaziah would not hear. And so Joas king of Israel went up
2Kings14:12 And Juda was put to the worse before Israel
2Kings14:13 And Joas king of Israel took Amaziah king of Juda
2Kings14:14 And he took all the gold and silver and all the vessels that were found in the house of the LORD

2Kings14:20 And they brought him on an horse and he was buried at Jerusalem with his fathers in the city of David.

2Kings14:22 And he built Ailath and brought it again to Juda
2Kings14:23 The fifteenth year of Amaziah son of Jehoas king of Juda
2Kings14:24 and wrought wickedness in the sight of the LORD: for he turned in nothing from the sin of Jeroboam the son of Nabat which made Israel sin.

2Kings14:26 For the LORD had seen how that the affliction of Israel was exceeding bitter
2Kings14:27 And the LORD had not yet said that men should put out the name of Israel from under heaven. And therefore he holp them by the hand of Jeroboam the son of Joas.

2Kings17:6 And in the ninth year of Hosea
2Kings17:7 For the children of Israel had sinned against the LORD their God which brought them out of the land of Egypt
2Kings17:8 And they walked in the ordinance of the heathen which the LORD cast out before the children of Israel
2Kings17:9 And the children of Israel wrapped themselves in things that were not well toward the LORD their God. And they built them Hillaltars in all their cities
2Kings17:10 And they made them Images and groves on every high hill and under every green tree.
2Kings17:11 And there they sacrificed in the Hillaltars
2Kings17:12 And they served Idols
2Kings17:13 And the LORD testified to Israel and to Juda
2Kings17:14 Notwithstanding they would not hear
2Kings17:15 And they refused his ordinances and his appointment that he had made with their fathers
2Kings17:16 But they left the commandments of the LORD their God and made them Images of metal
2Kings17:17 And they sacrificed their sons and their daughters in fire
2Kings17:18 Wherefore the LORD was exceeding wroth with Israel and put them out of his sight
2Kings17:19 and thereto Juda kept not the commandments of the LORD their God
2Kings17:20 Therefore the LORD cast up all the seed of Israel and vexed them and delivered them into the hands of spoilers
2Kings17:21 And Israel divided themselves
2Kings17:22 And so the children of Israel walked in all the sin of Jeroboam which he had set up

2Kings17:25 But at the beginning of their dwelling
2Kings17:26 Then men told the king of Assiria

2Kings17:28 And then one of the priests which they had carried thence
2Kings17:29 How be it every nation made their own gods and put them in the houses of the Hillaltars which the Samaritans had made
2Kings17:30 The men of Babylon made Socoth
2Kings17:31 The Evites made Nebahaz
2Kings17:32 And they feared the LORD
2Kings17:33 And so they feared the LORD
2Kings17:34 Unto this day they do after the old manner: they neither fear the LORD
2Kings17:35 and made an appointment with them and charged them saying: Fear not any other gods
2Kings17:36 But to the LORD which brought you out of the land of Egypt with great power and a stretched out arm: him fear and to him bow and to him do sacrifice.
2Kings17:37 And the ordinances
2Kings17:38 And the appointment that I have made with you
2Kings17:39 but the LORD your God ye shall fear
2Kings17:40 How be it they heard not

2Kings18:1 In the third year of Hosea son of Ela
2Kings18:2 Twenty five year old was he
2Kings18:3 and he did that pleased the LORD in all things

2Kings18:5 He trusted in the LORD God of Israel
2Kings18:6 He clave to the LORD and departed not from him
2Kings18:7 And the LORD was with him. And whatsoever he took in hand he did it wisely. And he rebelled against the king of Assiria and served him not.

2Kings18:9 And the fourth year of king Hezekiah
2Kings18:10 And they took it at the end of three years

2Kings18:18 and called to the king. And there came out to them
2Kings18:19 And then Rabsakeh said unto them. Tell ye Hezekiah I pray you: Thus sayeth the great king
2Kings18:20 thou wilt haply speak a light word
2Kings18:21 dost thou trust to the staff of this broken reed Egypt
2Kings18:22 If ye say unto me
2Kings18:23 And now join thyself to my lord the king of Assiria
2Kings18:24 (and if thou be not) how then art thou able to resist one of the least dukes of my master's servants? or trustest thou to Egypt for chariots and horsemen?
2Kings18:25 Moreover thinkest thou that I am come without the bidding of the LORD to this place to destroy it? nay: the LORD said to me: Go up to this land
2Kings18:26 Then said Eliakim the son of Helkiah and Sobnah and Joah
2Kings18:27 And Rabsakeh said unto them: hath my master sent me only to thy master and to thee
2Kings18:28 And so Rabsakeh stood and cried with a loud voice in the Jews' language
2Kings18:29 Thus sayeth the king: Let not Hezekiah beguile you
2Kings18:30 neither let Hezekiah make you trust to the LORD saying: the LORD will surely rid us
2Kings18:31 Hearken not unto Hezekiah
2Kings18:32 till I come and fetch you to as good a land as yours is: a land of corn and wine
2Kings18:33 For have the Gods of the nations delivered any God his land
2Kings18:34 Where are the Gods of Hemath and of Arphad? where are the Gods of Sepharuaim of Ana and Auah? did they deliver Samaria out of mine hands?
2Kings18:35 what God of any land hath delivered his land out of mine hand
2Kings18:36 But the people held their peace and answered him not a word: for the king had commanded saying: answer him not.
2Kings18:37 Then Eliakim the steward of household
2Kings19:1 When king Hezekiah heard that
2Kings19:2 Furthermore he sent Eliakim steward of the household and Sobnah the scribe
2Kings19:3 And they said to him
2Kings19:4 Oh that the LORD thy God would hear all the words of Rabsakeh whom the king of Assiria his master hath sent to rail on the living God
2Kings19:5 When the servants of king Hezekiah were come to Isaiah:
2Kings19:6 Isaiah said to them: So shall we say to your master. Thus sayeth the LORD: be not afraid of the words thou heardest
2Kings19:7 For I will send him a blast that he shall hear tidings

2Kings19:10 Thus say to Hezekiah king of Juda
2Kings19:11 Behold

2Kings19:13 where is the king of Hemath
2Kings19:14 When Hezekiah had received the letter of the hand of the messenger and had read it: he went into the house of the LORD and laid it abroad before the LORD.
2Kings19:15 And Hezekiah prayed before the LORD and said: LORD God of Israel which dwellest between the Cherubs
2Kings19:16 LORD
2Kings19:17 But of a truth
2Kings19:18 and have set fire on their gods. For they were no Gods
2Kings19:19 Now therefore LORD our God

2Kings19:22 whom hast thou railed on
2Kings19:23 By the hand of thy messengers thou hast railed on the Lord(Lorde) and said: with the multitude of my chariots I am come up to the height of the mountains even along by the sides of Libanon
2Kings19:24 I have digged and drunk strange waters
2Kings19:25 But hast thou not heard how I have ordained such a thing a great while ago
2Kings19:26 And the inhabiters of them shall be of little power
2Kings19:27 I wot where thou dwellest
2Kings19:28 And because thou settest up thy bristles against me
2Kings19:29 And this shall be a sign unto thee: eat this year of the fruits of the seed that fell out
2Kings19:30 For the daughter of Juda that is escaped and left
2Kings19:31 For out of Jerusalem shall go a remnant
2Kings19:32 Wherefore thus sayeth the LORD
2Kings19:33 but shall go back again the way he came
2Kings19:34 For I will defend this city and save it
2Kings19:35 And the self same night the Angel of the LORD went out and smote in the Host of the Assirians an hundredth and four score and five thousand. And when they were up early in the morning: behold
2Kings19:36 And so Sennaherib king of Assiria avoided and departed and went again
2Kings19:37 And as he was in his devotion kneeling in the house of Nisroch his God
2Kings20:1 About that time Hezekiah was sick unto the death. And the Prophet Isaiah son of Amoz came to him and said to him: Thus sayeth the LORD: put thine household in an order
2Kings20:2 And Hezekiah turned his face to the wall and besought the LORD saying:
2Kings20:3 Oh LORD
2Kings20:4 And Isaiah was scarce gone out into the middle of the city
2Kings20:5 turn again and tell Hezekiah the captain of my people. Thus sayeth the LORD God of David thy father. I have heard thy prayer and seen thy tears. Behold
2Kings20:6 And I will lengthen thy days yet fifteen year
2Kings20:7 And Isaiah said: bring a lump of figs. And they brought and put it on the sore
2Kings20:8 Then said Hezekiah to Isaiah: what is the sign
2Kings20:9 And Isaiah said: this sign shalt thou have of the LORD
2Kings20:10 And Hezekiah said: it is a light thing for the shadow to go down ten degrees. Therefore I will not that: but let the shadow go backward ten degrees.
2Kings20:11 And Isaiah the Prophet called to the LORD
2Kings20:12 The same season Berodach Baladan the son of Baladan king of Babilon sent letters and presents unto Hezekiah
2Kings20:13 And Hezekiah hearkened unto them and shewed them all that was in the spicery house
2Kings20:14 Then came Isaiah the Prophet unto king Hezekiah and said to him: What say these men and from whence come they to thee? And Hezekiah said: they be come from a far country
2Kings20:15 And he said: what have they seen in thy house? And Hezekiah said: all that is in my house have they seen: there is nothing among my treasure that I have not shewed them.
2Kings20:16 And Isaiah said to Hezekiah: hear the word of the LORD.
2Kings20:17 Behold the day shall come
2Kings20:18 And of thy sons that proceeded out of thee
2Kings20:19 And Hezekiah said to Isaiah: welcome be the word of the LORD which thou hast spoken: so that peace and truth be kept in my days.
2Kings20:20 The remnant of the deeds of Hezekiah and all his power
2Kings20:21 And Hezekiah laid him to rest with his fathers
2Kings21:1 Manasseh was twelve year old when he began to reign and reigned fifty and five year in Jerusalem: his mother's name was Haphzibah.
2Kings21:2 And he did that displeased the LORD
2Kings21:3 And he went and built the hillaltars again
2Kings21:4 And he built altars in the very house of the LORD
2Kings21:5 And he built altars unto all the Host of heaven
2Kings21:6 And he offered his son in fire
2Kings21:7 And he put an image of a grove that he had made
2Kings21:8 Neither will I make the feet of Israel move any more out of the land which I gave their fathers: so that they will be diligent to do all I have commanded them
2Kings21:9 But they hearkened not: for Manasseh had led them out of the way
2Kings21:10 And the LORD spake by his servants the Prophets
2Kings21:11 because Manasseh king of Juda hath done such abominations
2Kings21:12 Therefore thus sayeth the LORD God of Israel. Behold
2Kings21:13 And I will stretch over Jerusalem the squaring line of Samaria and the plummet of the house of Ahab. And I will wipe out Jerusalem
2Kings21:14 And though I leave a remnant of mine inheritance
2Kings21:15 even because they have done wickedly and have angered me
2Kings21:16 And thereto Manasseh shed innocent blood exceeding abundantly
2Kings21:17 The rest of the acts of Manasseh and all he did and his sin that he sinned
2Kings21:18 And Manasseh laid him to sleep with his fathers
2Kings21:19 Amon was twenty two year old when he began to reign
2Kings21:20 And he did that displeased the LORD as his father Manasseh did.
2Kings21:21 And he walked in all the way that his father walked in
2Kings21:22 And he forsook the LORD God of his fathers and walked not in the way of the LORD.
2Kings21:23 And the servants of Amon conspired against him
2Kings21:24 But the people of the land slew all that conspired against king Amon
2Kings21:25 The rest of the acts of Amon which he did are written in the Chronicle of the kings of Juda.
2Kings21:26 And they buried him in his sepulchre in the garden of Oza
2Kings22:1 Josiah was eight year old when he began to reign
2Kings22:2 And he did that seemed right in the sight of the LORD
2Kings22:3 And the eighteenth year of his reign king Josiah sent Saphan the son of Azaliah the son of Mesulam the scribe to the house of the LORD
2Kings22:4 and let him sum the silver that is brought into the house of the LORD
2Kings22:5 and let them deliver it into the hands of the workmen that have the oversight of the house of the LORD
2Kings22:6 even unto carpenters and masons
2Kings22:7 How be it let no reckoning be made with them of the money that is delivered into their hands
2Kings22:8 And Helkiah the high priest said to Saphan the scribe: I have found the book of the law in the temple of the LORD
2Kings22:9 And then Saphan the scribe went to the king and brought him word again
2Kings22:10 Furthermore Saphan the scribe shewed the king saying: Helkiah the priest hath delivered me here a book. And Saphan read it before the king.
2Kings22:11 And the king as soon as he had heard the words of the book of the law
2Kings22:12 and commanded Helkiah the priest and Ahikam the son of Saphan and Achobor the son of Michaiah
2Kings22:13 go ye and seek of the LORD for me and the people and for all Juda
2Kings22:14 And Helkiah the high priest and Ahikam
2Kings22:15 And she said to them: thus sayeth the LORD God of Israel. Tell the man that sent you to me:
2Kings22:16 thus sayeth the LORD: behold I will bring evil upon this place and on the dwellers therein: even all the words of the book which the king of Juda hath read
2Kings22:17 because they have forsaken me and have brent offerings unto other gods
2Kings22:18 But to the king of Juda which sent you to enquire of the LORD
2Kings22:19 Because thine heart did melt and thou meekedest thyself before me the LORD
2Kings22:20 And therefore see
2Kings23:1 And then the king sent and gathered unto him all the elders of Juda and of Jerusalem.
2Kings23:2 And the king went up into the house of the LORD
2Kings23:3 And the king stood by a pillar and made a covenant before the LORD that they should walk after the LORD
2Kings23:4 And the king commanded Helkiah the high priest and the inferior priests and the keepers of the door
2Kings23:5 And he put down the Camarites which the kings of Juda had set to burn offerings in the hillaltars in the cities of Juda round about Jerusalem and also them that burnt sacrifices unto Baal to the son and to the moon and to the planets
2Kings23:6 And he brought out the grove from the temple of the LORD without Jerusalem unto the brook Cedron
2Kings23:7 And he brake down the cells of the male whores that were in the house of the LORD
2Kings23:8 And he brought all the priests out of the cities of Juda
2Kings23:9 Nevertheless the priests of the hillaltars might not come at the altar of the LORD in Jerusalem
2Kings23:10 And he defiled Topheth also
2Kings23:11 he put down the horses that the kings of Israel had given to the son at the entering of the house of the LORD
2Kings23:12 And the altars that were on the top of the parlour of Ahaz which the king of Juda had made
2Kings23:13 And the hillaltars that before Jerusalem on the right hand of the mount Nashith
2Kings23:14 and brake the images and cut down the groves and filled the places with the bones of men.
2Kings23:15 Moreover the altar that was at Bethel
2Kings23:16 And as Josiah turned himself
2Kings23:17 Then the king said: what meaneth yonder grave stone that I see? And the men of the city told him
2Kings23:18 And he said let him be: see that no man move his bones. And so his bones escaped with the bones of a Prophet that came out of Samaria.
2Kings23:19 And thereto all the houses of the hillaltars of the cities of Samaria which the kings of Israel had made
2Kings23:20 And he sacrificed all the priests of the hillaltars that were there
2Kings23:21 And the king commanded all the people saying: keep the feast of passover unto the LORD your God
2Kings23:22 For there was no passover held like that from the days of the judges that judged Israel
2Kings23:23 And in the eighteenth year of king Josiah was this passover holden to the LORD in Jerusalem.
2Kings23:24 And thereto workers with spirits
2Kings23:25 like unto him was there no king before him
2Kings23:26 Notwithstanding the LORD turned not from his fierce and great wrath wherewith he was angry against Juda upon all the provocations that Manasseh provoked him.
2Kings23:27 But the LORD said: I will put Juda too
2Kings23:28 The rest of the deeds of Josiah and all he did are written in the book of the stories of the kings of Juda.
2Kings23:29 In his days Pharao Necoh king of Egypt went against the king of Assiria to the river of Euphrates. And king Josiah went against him
2Kings23:30 And his servants carried him dead from Magedo and brought him to Jerusalem and buried him in his own sepulchre. And the people of the land took Jehoahaz the son of Josiah and anointed him and made him king in his father's room.
2Kings23:31 And Jehoahaz was twenty three year old when he began to reign and reigned three months in Jerusalem. His mother's name was Namital the daughter of Jeremiah of Lobnah.
2Kings23:32 And he did that displeased the LORD
2Kings23:33 And Pharao Necoh put him in bonds at Reblah in the land of Hemath in the time of his reign in Jerusalem
2Kings23:34 And Pharao Necoh made Eliakim the son of Josiah king in the room of Josiah his father
2Kings23:35 And Jehoakim gave the silver and the gold to Pharao: how be it he taxed the land
2Kings23:36 Jehoakim was twenty five year old when he began to reign
2Kings23:37 And he did that was evil favoured in the sight of the LORD
2Kings24:1 In his days came Nabuchodonozor king of Babylon
2Kings24:2 And the LORD sent upon him men of war out of Galdey
2Kings24:3 Only at the bidding of the LORD happened it so to Juda
2Kings24:4 and for the innocent blood that he shed and filled Jerusalem with innocent blood
2Kings24:5 The rest of the deeds of Jehoakim and all he did
2Kings24:6 And Jehoakim laid him to sleep with his fathers
2Kings24:7 But the king of Egypt came now no more out of his land: for the king of Babylon had taken from the river of Euphrates
2Kings24:8 Jehoacin was eighteen year old when he began to reign
2Kings24:9 And he did that displeased the eyes of the LORD in all points as his father did.
2Kings24:10 In his time came the servants of Nabuchodonozor king of Babylon
2Kings24:11 And Nabuchodonozor came to the city as his servants were yet a besieging of it.
2Kings24:12 And Jehoacin the king of Juda came out to the king of Babylon
2Kings24:13 And he carried out thence all the treasure of the house of the LORD
2Kings24:14 And he carried away all Jerusalem
2Kings24:15 And he carried away Jehoacin to Babylon
2Kings24:16 And all the men of activity to the number of seven thousand
2Kings24:17 And the king of Babylon made Mathaniah his father's brother
2Kings24:18 Zedekiah was twenty one year old when he began to reign and he reigned an eleven year in Jerusalem. His mother's name was Hamital the daughter of Jeremiah of Lobnah.
2Kings24:19 And he did evil in the sight of the LORD
2Kings24:20 For through the wrath of the LORD it so chanced to Jerusalem and Juda
2Kings25:1 Wherefore in the ninth year of his reign
2Kings25:2 And the city continued besieged until the eleventh year of king Zedekiah.
2Kings25:3 And the ninth day of the fourth month of that year
2Kings25:4 And thereto the city was broken up: wherefore all the men of Arms fled by night
2Kings25:5 And the host of the Caldeyes followed after him
2Kings25:6 And when they had taken him
2Kings25:7 And they slew the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes
2Kings25:8 And the seventh day of the fifth month which was in the nineteenth year of king Nabuchodonozor king of Babylon
2Kings25:9 and burnt the house of the LORD and the king's house and all the houses of Jerusalem
2Kings25:10 And all the host of the Caldeyes that were with the chief Marshal brake down the walls of Jerusalem round about.
2Kings25:11 And the rest of the people that were left in the city
2Kings25:12 Nabusaradan the chief Marshal carried away
2Kings25:13 And the pillars of brass that were in the house of the LORD and the bottoms
2Kings25:14 And the lavers
2Kings25:15 And the firepans
2Kings25:16 with the two pillars and the sea of brass and the bottoms which Salomon had made in the house of the LORD. The brass of all these vessels was without weight.
2Kings25:17 The height of the one pillar was eighteen cubits
2Kings25:18 And the chief Marshal took Saraiah the chief priest
2Kings25:19 And out of the city he took a certain chamberlain that had the oversight of the men of war
2Kings25:20 And Nabusaradan the chief Marshal took them and brought them to the king of Babylon to Reblah.
2Kings25:21 And the king of Babylon smote them and slew them at Reblah in the land of Hemath. And so Juda was carried away out of all their land.
2Kings25:22 And over the people that remained in the land of Juda
2Kings25:23 And when all the captains of the men of war and the men heard that the king of Babylon had made Godoliah governor: there came to Godoliah to Mazphah: Ismael the son of Nathaniah and Johanan the son of Kareah and Saraiah the son of Thanehumeth the Netophathite and Jezoniah the son of Maachati and their men.
2Kings25:24 And Godoliah sware to them and to their men
2Kings25:25 But it chanced the seventh month after
2Kings25:26 Thereupon all the people both small and great and the captains of the men of war arose and went to Egypt: for they were afraid of the Caldeyes.
2Kings25:27 Notwithstanding yet the thirty seventh year after Jehoacin king of Juda was carried away
2Kings25:28 and spake kindly to him and set his seat above the seat of all the kings that were with him at Babilon
2Kings25:29 and changed his prison garments. And he did ever eat bread before him all the days of his life:
2Kings25:30 And a continual portion was assigned him of the king

1Chronicles5:2 For unto Juda which was mighty among his brethren
1Chronicles5:3 The sons then of Ruben the eldest son of Israel
1Chronicles5:4 The sons of Joel: Samaiah
1Chronicles5:5 and his son Micah
1Chronicles5:6 and Beerah was his son. Which Beerah Thiglath Phalneser
1Chronicles5:7 But unto his brethren in their kindreds
1Chronicles5:8 And Bala the son of Azan the son of Sema the son of Joel
1Chronicles5:9 And eastward he inhabited until the wilderness
1Chronicles5:10 And in the days of Saul they warred with the Hagarites which were overthrown by their hands. And they dwelt in their tents thorowout all the east land of Gilead.
1Chronicles5:11 And the Children of Gad dwelt over against them in the land of Basan
1Chronicles5:12 And in Basan Joel was the chiefest and Sapham the next
1Chronicles5:13 And their brethren in the ancient households of them
1Chronicles5:14 These were the children of Abihail the son of Hurl the son of Jeroah Gilead the son of Michael the son of Jesisai the son of Jahado the son of Buz.
1Chronicles5:15 Ahi was the son of Abdiel the son of Guni the head of an ancient household among them.
1Chronicles5:16 And they dwelt in Gilead and in Basan and her towns
1Chronicles5:17 And they were all reckoned by kindreds in the days of Jotham king of Juda
1Chronicles5:18 The sons of Ruben of Gad and of half the tribe of Manasse
1Chronicles5:19 And they fought with the Hagarities and with Jesur
1Chronicles5:20 And they were holp against them
1Chronicles5:21 And they took of their cattle fifty thousand camels
1Chronicles5:22 for there fell many dead because the war was of God. And they dwelt there in their steads until the time that they were carried away.
1Chronicles5:23 And the children of half the tribe of Manasse dwelt in the land
1Chronicles5:24 And these were the heads of the ancient households of them: Epher
1Chronicles5:25 But when they had transgressed against the God of their fathers and had gone awhoring after the gods of the people of the land
1Chronicles5:26 God stirred up the spirit of Phul king of Assiria and the spirit of Thiglath Phalneser king of Assiria

1Chronicles6:2 The sons of Cahath: Amram
1Chronicles6:3 The children of Amram: Aaron

1Chronicles10:1 And the Philistines fought against Israel. And the men of Israel fled before the Philistines and were overthrown and slain in mount Gelboe.
1Chronicles10:2 And the Philistines followed Saul and his sons and slew Jehonathan
1Chronicles10:3 And the battle went sore against Saul
1Chronicles10:4 Then said Saul to his weapon bearer draw thy sword and thrust me thorow therewith
1Chronicles10:5 And when his harness bearer saw that Saul was dead
1Chronicles10:6 And thus Saul and his three sons and all his house died together.
1Chronicles10:7 And when all the men that dwelt in the valleys
1Chronicles10:8 And on the morrow when the Philistines came to strip the dead bodies
1Chronicles10:9 And when they had stripped him they took his head and his harness and sent them into the land of the Philistines round about to shew tidings unto their gods(Idols) and to the people.
1Chronicles10:10 And they put his harness in the houses of their gods. But his skull they hanged in the house of Dagon.
1Chronicles10:11 And when all they of Jabes in Gilead heard all that the Philistines had done to Saul
1Chronicles10:12 all the men of war arose and fetched away the body of Saul
1Chronicles10:13 And so Saul died for his trespass that he trespassed against the LORD
1Chronicles10:14 and asked not of the LORD. And therefore the Lord(he) killed him and turned the kingdom unto David the son of Jesse.(Iesai)
1Chronicles11:1 Then all Israel gathered themselves to David unto Hebron

1Chronicles11:3 And when all the elders of Israel were come to the king to Hebron
1Chronicles11:4 And David and all Israel went to Jerusalem which is Jebus: for there dwelt the Jebusites the inhabiters of the land.
1Chronicles11:5 And the enhabiters of Jebus said to David
1Chronicles11:6 Then said David: whosoever smite the Jebusites first
1Chronicles11:7 And because David dwelt in the castle
1Chronicles11:8 And he built the city on every side even from Mello round about. But Joab repaired the rest of the city.
1Chronicles11:9 And David prospered and waxed great
1Chronicles11:10 These are the principal men of power that clave to David in his kingdom with all Israel
1Chronicles11:11 And this is the number of the mighty men which David had: Isabaam the son of Hachamoni the chief of thirty
1Chronicles11:12 And after him Eleazar the son of Dodo an Ahothite which was one of the three mightiest.
1Chronicles11:13 He was with David at Phasdamin where the Philistines were gathered to battle. And there was there a parcel of ground full of barley
1Chronicles11:14 And they stepped forth into the midst of the furlong and saved it and slew the Philstines. And the LORD gave a great victory.
1Chronicles11:15 And the three of the thirty chiefs went to a rock to David at the cave Odolam. And the Host of the Philistines had pitched in the valley of Raphaim.
1Chronicles11:16 And David was in an hold. And there was a garrison of the Philistines at Bethlehem that same time.
1Chronicles11:17 And David longed and said: Oh that one would give me of the water of the well of Bethlehem that is in the gate
1Chronicles11:18 And the three brake thorow the Host of the Philistines and drew of the water of the well of Bethlehem that was in the gate
1Chronicles11:19 and said: God forbid it me
1Chronicles11:20 And Abisai the brother of Joab was captain of the three
1Chronicles11:21 And he was much more nobler than any of the three
1Chronicles11:22 Banaiah the son of Jehoiada the son of a strong man that had done great deeds and was of Cabzeel: he slew two strong lions of Moab
1Chronicles11:23 And he slew an Egyptian which was a man of size
1Chronicles11:24 Such things did Banaiah the son of Jehoihada
1Chronicles11:25 was the gloriousest of thirty: but attained not unto the three. And David made him of his secret counsel.

1Chronicles13:1 And David counselled with the Captains of thousands and of hundreds
1Chronicles13:2 and said unto all the congregation of Israel: If it seem you good and also to be of the LORD our God. Let us take and send unto the remnant of our brethren thorowout all the land of Israel
1Chronicles13:3 And let us bring again the Ark of the Lord(God) to us: for we regarded it not in the days of Saul.
1Chronicles13:4 And all the congregation was content so to do
1Chronicles13:5 And David gathered all Israel together from Sihor in Egypt unto Hemath
1Chronicles13:6 And David went up and all Israel to Baalah
1Chronicles13:7 And they carried the Ark of God in a new cart out of the house of Abinadab. And Oza and his brother drave the cart.
1Chronicles13:8 And David and all Israel played before God with all their might
1Chronicles13:9 And when they came on the threshing floor of Chidon
1Chronicles13:10 And the LORD was wroth with Oza and smote him
1Chronicles13:11 And there he died before God. And David was displeased
1Chronicles13:12 And David was so afraid of God that day
1Chronicles13:13 And so David brought not the Ark home to him in the city of David: But turned it in to the house of Obed Edom o Gethite
1Chronicles13:14 and the Ark of God was with Obed Edom in his house

1Chronicles14:13 And the Philistines came again and ran abroad in the valley.

1Chronicles14:15 And when thou hearest a sound go in the tops of the pear trees
1Chronicles14:16 And David did as God commanded him. And they smote the host of the Philistines all the way from Gabaon to Gazer.
1Chronicles14:17 And the fame of David went out into all lands

1Chronicles16:1 When they had brought in the Ark of God they set it in the tent that David had pitched for it. And they brought burnt sacrifice and peaceofferings before God.
1Chronicles16:2 And when David had made an end of offering of burntofferings and of peaceofferings
1Chronicles16:3 And he dealt all Israel both man and woman a cracknel of bread
1Chronicles16:4 And he appointed certain of the Levites to minister before the LORD
1Chronicles16:5 even Asaph the chief
1Chronicles16:6 and Banaiah and Jahaziel priests with trumpets continually before the Ark of the covenant of God.
1Chronicles16:7 And that same time David did appoint chiefly to thank the LORD by Asaph and his brethren.
1Chronicles16:8 Thank the LORD
1Chronicles16:9 Sing unto him and play unto him: and record all his wonderful deeds.
1Chronicles16:10 Praise his holy name
1Chronicles16:11 Seek the LORD and his strength: Seek his presence always.
1Chronicles16:12 Remember his marvels which he did
1Chronicles16:13 The seed of Israel are his servants: the children of Jacob are his chosen.
1Chronicles16:14 He is the LORD our God: in all lands are his judgements.
1Chronicles16:15 Think on his appointment ever
1Chronicles16:16 Of his covenant to Abraham
1Chronicles16:17 which he set before Jacob for a decree
1Chronicles16:18 to thee I will give the land of Canaan
1Chronicles16:19 When you were a small company in number
1Chronicles16:20 And they went from people to people
1Chronicles16:21 He suffered no man to do them wrong: But rebuked kings for their sakes.
1Chronicles16:22 Touch not mine anointed
1Chronicles16:23 Sing unto the LORD all the earth: and shew from day to day his victories.
1Chronicles16:24 Tell among the heathen of his glory: and among all nations of his wonderful deeds.
1Chronicles16:25 For great is the LORD and to be praised exceedingly: and terrible is he above all Gods.
1Chronicles16:26 For all the gods of the heathen are Idols: But the LORD made heaven.(the heavens)
1Chronicles16:27 Praise and honour are in his presence: strength and gladness are in his place.
1Chronicles16:28 Ascribe to the LORD ye kindreds of people; Ascribe to the LORD Glory and strength.
1Chronicles16:29 Give honour unto the LORD's name: bring presents and come before him
1Chronicles16:30 All the earth fear him
1Chronicles16:31 The heaven rejoice and the earth be glad
1Chronicles16:32 Let the sea thunder and his fulness
1Chronicles16:33 Moreover let the trees of the wood ring at the presence of the LORD: for he cometh to judge the earth.
1Chronicles16:34 Thank the LORD for he is good
1Chronicles16:35 and say: save us o God our saviour and gather us together and deliver us from among the heathen
1Chronicles16:36 Blessed be the LORD God of Israel for ever and ever
1Chronicles16:37 And he left there before the ark of the LORD's covenant
1Chronicles16:38 And Obed Edom with his brethren three score and eight
1Chronicles16:39 And Zadock the priest with his brethren the priests set he before the tabernacle of the LORD at the hillaltar of Gabaon
1Chronicles16:40 to offer burntofferings unto the LORD upon the burntoffering altar perpetually at morning and evening
1Chronicles16:41 And with them Heman and Iduthun
1Chronicles16:42 And with the said Heman and Iduthun were trumpets and sounding cymbals
1Chronicles16:43 And then all the people departed
1Chronicles17:1 And as David dwelt in his house he said to Nathan the prophet: lo
1Chronicles17:2 And Nathan said to David: do all that is in thine heart
1Chronicles17:3 But the same night the word of God came to Nathan saying:
1Chronicles17:4 go and tell David my servant
1Chronicles17:5 For I have dwell in no house since I brought out the children of Israel unto this day: But have gone from tent to tent and from one tabernacle to another.
1Chronicles17:6 Neither in any place wheresoever I have walked thorowout all Israel
1Chronicles17:7 Now therefore thus say unto my servant David: thus sayeth the LORD of Hosts: I took thee out of the pasture from after sheep
1Chronicles17:8 And I have been with thee in all thou tookest in hand
1Chronicles17:9 And I will ordain a place for my people Israel
1Chronicles17:10 and since the time I commanded judges to be over my people Israel
1Chronicles17:11 But when thy days be expired
1Chronicles17:12 He shall build me an house
1Chronicles17:13 I will be his father and he shall be my son
1Chronicles17:14 But I will set him in mine house and in my kingdom for ever
1Chronicles17:15 When Nathan had told David according to all these words
1Chronicles17:16 and sat him before the LORD and said: what am I O LORD God and what is mine household
1Chronicles17:17 And yet this seemed little in thine eyes O God. But that thou shouldest speak of thy servant's house for a great while to come: and hast looked upon me as upon a man of high degree O LORD God.
1Chronicles17:18 What can David desire more of thee
1Chronicles17:19 And LORD for thy servant's sake
1Chronicles17:20 to shew all bounteousness LORD
1Chronicles17:21 Moreover what nation on the earth is like thy people Israel
1Chronicles17:22 for ever
1Chronicles17:23 And now LORD the thing thou hast spoken concerning thy servant and his house be true for ever
1Chronicles17:24 And thy name continue and wax great for ever
1Chronicles17:25 For thou Lord(God) hast told thy servant
1Chronicles17:26 And now LORD thou art the very God
1Chronicles17:27 Now therefore go to and bless the house of thy servant

1Chronicles27:24 And Joab the son of Zaruiah began to number. And though he finished it not
1Chronicles27:25 Over the king's treasure was Azmoth the son of Adiel. And over the treasure of the fields
1Chronicles27:26 And over the workmen in the fields that tilled the ground
1Chronicles27:27 And the oversight of the vineyards had Semeiah the Ramathite. Over the winecellars and treasure of wine was Sabdi the Saphonite.
1Chronicles27:28 And over the olivetrees and mulberry trees that were in the valleys was Baal Hanan the Gadarite. And over the treasure of oil was Joas.
1Chronicles27:29 And over the oxen that fed in Saron was Setari the Saronite. And over the oxen in the valleys was Saphat the son of Adlai.
1Chronicles27:30 And over the camels Ubil the Ismaelite. And over the asses was Johadia the Merothite.
1Chronicles27:31 And over the sheep was Jazis the Hagarite. All these were the rulers of the substance of king David.
1Chronicles27:32 And Jehonathan David's brother's son
1Chronicles27:33 And Ahitophel was of the kings counsel. And Husai the Arachite was the king's companion.
1Chronicles27:34 And next to Ahitophel was Jehoiada the son of Banaiah
1Chronicles28:1 And David gathered together all the lords of Israel: the lords of the tribes
1Chronicles28:2 And king David stood upon his feet and said: Hear me my brethren and my people: I had in mine heart to build an house of rest for the Ark of the covenant of the LORD
1Chronicles28:3 But God said to me: thou shalt not build an house for my name
1Chronicles28:4 Moreover the LORD God of Israel chose me of all the house of my father
1Chronicles28:5 And of all my sons (for the LORD hath given me many sons) he hath chosen Salomon my son
1Chronicles28:6 And he said unto me: Salomon thy son
1Chronicles28:7 and will stablish his kingdom for ever. If he will harden himself to do my commandments
1Chronicles28:8 And now I say unto you before all Israel the congregation of the LORD
1Chronicles28:9 And thou Salomon my son: know God thy father and serve him with a pure heart and lust of soul. For the LORD searcheth all hearts and understandeth all the Imaginations of thoughts. If thou shalt seek him
1Chronicles28:10 Take heed now
1Chronicles28:11 And David gave Salomon his son the pattern of the porch and of the houses that longed thereto
1Chronicles28:12 and the example of all that was in his mind
1Chronicles28:13 and of the companies of the priests and Levites that waited by course
1Chronicles28:14 And appointed him what weight of gold should serve for things of gold thorowout all vessels for whatsoever use it served: and what weight of silver should suffice for all manner of vessels of silver
1Chronicles28:15 And the weight of the golden candlesticks and of their lamps of gold
1Chronicles28:16 And the weight of gold for the tables of shew bread
1Chronicles28:17 And for fleshhooks
1Chronicles28:18 And for the altar of incense of tried gold by weight. And the similitude of the seat of the Cherubs that stretched out their wings and covered the Ark of the covenant of the LORD.
1Chronicles28:19 All was given me by writing of the hand of the LORD
1Chronicles28:20 And David said to Salomon his son: harden thyself and be strong and do it
1Chronicles28:21 Behold the priests and Levites divided in companies
1Chronicles29:1 And David the king said unto all the congregation: God hath specially chosen Salomon my son which is yet young and tender
1Chronicles29:2 Moreover I have prepared with all my might for the house of God: gold for things of gold
1Chronicles29:3 And yet because I have lust to the house of my God: I have several gold and silver which I give to the house of my God above all I have prepared for the holy house:
1Chronicles29:4 even three thousand talents of gold of Ophir
1Chronicles29:5 to sile the walls of the house with gold where it becometh
1Chronicles29:6 And the ancient lords and the lords of the tribes of Israel
1Chronicles29:7 and gave for the service of the house of God
1Chronicles29:8 And they that had precious stones gave them for the treasure of the house of the LORD
1Chronicles29:9 And the people rejoiced that they were so willing: for with a pure heart they were willing unto the LORD. And thereto David the king rejoiced with great gladness.
1Chronicles29:10 And David blessed the LORD before all the congregation and said: Blessed art thou LORD God of Israel our father
1Chronicles29:11 Thine (O LORD) is the greatness
1Chronicles29:12 And riches and honour come of thee
1Chronicles29:13 And now our God
1Chronicles29:14 But all is of thee
1Chronicles29:15 For we be but strangers before thee and tenants
1Chronicles29:16 O LORD our God
1Chronicles29:17 I wot my God
1Chronicles29:18 O LORD God of Abraham
1Chronicles29:19 And give(grant) unto Salomon my son
1Chronicles29:20 And David said to all the congregation: bless the LORD your God. And all the congregation blessed the LORD God of their fathers
1Chronicles29:21 And they offered offerings unto the LORD. And on the morrow after the said day
1Chronicles29:22 And they slew plenteously thorowout all Israel
1Chronicles29:23 And so Salomon sat on the seat of the LORD
1Chronicles29:24 And all the lords and men of power
1Chronicles29:25 And the LORD magnified Salomon on high
1Chronicles29:26 Now David the son of Isai was king over all Israel.
1Chronicles29:27 And the space he reigned over Israel was forty year: seven years in Hebron and thirty three year in Jerusalem.
1Chronicles29:28 And he died in a good age: old

2Chronicles1:7 And the same night God appeared unto Salomon and said to him
2Chronicles1:8 And Salomon said unto God: thou hast shewed great mercy unto David my father
2Chronicles1:9 And now LORD God

2Chronicles1:11 Then God said to Salomon
2Chronicles1:12 wisdom and knowledge shall be given thee
2Chronicles1:13 And so Salomon came from the hillaltar that was at Gabaon to Jerusalem from the tabernacle of witness and reigned at Jerusalem.

2Chronicles2:1 And Salomon determined to build an house for the name of the LORD
2Chronicles2:2 and told out three score and ten thousand men to bear burthens and four score thousand to hew in the mountain
2Chronicles2:3 And Salomon sent to Hiram king of Tire saying: As thou didst deal with David my father and didst send him Cedar wood
2Chronicles2:4 that I may build an house for the name of the LORD my God
2Chronicles2:5 And the house which I build is great: for great is our God above other gods.
2Chronicles2:6 So that who can be able to build him an house: when that heaven
2Chronicles2:7 send me now therefore a cunning man to work in gold
2Chronicles2:8 And send me also Cedar trees
2Chronicles2:9 that they may prepare me timber enough. For the house which I build is great and wonderful.
2Chronicles2:10 And behold

2Chronicles2:12 And Hiram said moreover: blessed be the LORD God of Israel which hath made both heaven and earth

2Chronicles3:1 And Salomon began to build the house of the LORD at Jerusalem in mount Moriah
2Chronicles3:2 And he began to build the second day of the second month the fourth year of his reign.
2Chronicles3:3 And this is the foundation of Salomon in building the house of God. The length was three score cubits after the old cubit
2Chronicles3:4 And the porch at the end was as large as the house breadth twenty cubits: and the height was an hundredth and twenty cubits. And he overlaid it on the inner side with pure gold.
2Chronicles3:5 And the great house he sealed with fir tree
2Chronicles3:6 And he paved the house with precious stone goodly. And it was gold of Pharuiam.
2Chronicles3:7 And he overlaid the house: both beams
2Chronicles3:8 And he made the house most holy
2Chronicles3:9 And the weight of the nails of gold was thereto fifty sicles. And he overlaid the upper chambers with gold.
2Chronicles3:10 And he made in the house most holy two Cherubs of Image work
2Chronicles3:11 And the wings of the Cherubs were twenty cubits long. The one wing was five cubits and touched the wall of the house
2Chronicles3:12 And the one wing of the other Cherub was five cubits
2Chronicles3:13 So that the wings of the said Cherubs raught twenty cubits. And they stood on their feet and looked inwards.
2Chronicles3:14 And he made a forehanging of Jacinct colour
2Chronicles3:15 And he made before the house two pillars of thirty five cubits long. And the head that was above on the top of one of them was five cubits.
2Chronicles3:16 And he made such chains as was in the quere
2Chronicles3:17 And he reared up the pillars before the temple: one on the right hand and another on the left
2Chronicles4:1 And he made an altar of brass twenty cubits long and twenty cubits broad and ten cubits high.
2Chronicles4:2 And he cast a brazen sea of ten cubits from brim to brim
2Chronicles4:3 And the fashion of oxen did compass it round about under it: that is to wete
2Chronicles4:4 And it stood also upon twelve oxen: of which three looked North
2Chronicles4:5 And the thickest of it was an handbreadth
2Chronicles4:6 And he made ten lavers: and put five on the right hand and five on the left
2Chronicles4:7 And he made ten candlesticks of gold in their fashions
2Chronicles4:8 And he made also ten tables and put them in the temple: five on the right side and five on the left. And he made an hundredth basins of gold.
2Chronicles4:9 And he made the court of the priests
2Chronicles4:10 And he set the sea in the right side of the East end
2Chronicles4:11 And Hiram made pots
2Chronicles4:12 The two pillars with their scalps of the two heads that were on the tops of the pillars: and the two wreaths to cover the two scalps of the heads that were on the tops of the pillars:
2Chronicles4:13 and four hundredth pomegranates for the two wreaths
2Chronicles4:14 And he made bottoms
2Chronicles4:15 and the sea with twelve oxen under it.
2Chronicles4:16 And thereto pots
2Chronicles4:17 In the plain of Jordan did the king cast them
2Chronicles4:18 And made of all these vessels so mighty great abundance
