2Chronicles5:1 And so was all the work that Salomon made for the house of the LORD finished. And when the work that Salomon made in the house of the LORD was finished: then Salomon brought in the gifts dedicate by David his father
2Chronicles5:2 Then Salomon gathered the elders of Israel together and all the heads of the tribes and ancient lords among the children of Israel
2Chronicles5:3 And all the men of Israel resorted unto the king in the feast of the seventh month.
2Chronicles5:4 And when all the elders of Israel were come
2Chronicles5:5 And the priests and the Levites brought away the Ark and the tabernacle of witness
2Chronicles5:6 And king Salomon and all the congregation of Israel that were assembled unto him before the Ark
2Chronicles5:7 And the priests brought the Ark of the appointment of the LORD unto his place in the quere of the temple and place most holy: even under the wings of the Cherubs
2Chronicles5:8 that the Cherubs stretched out their wings over the place of the Ark and covered the Ark and her staves above on high.
2Chronicles5:9 And the staves of the Ark
2Chronicles5:10 Moreover there was nothing in the Ark save the two tables which Moses put therein at Horeb
2Chronicles5:11 And when the priests were come out of the holy place (for all the priests that were found sanctified themselves and did not wait by course).
2Chronicles5:12 But the Levites every one of them that were under Asaph
2Chronicles5:13 And the trumpet blowers and the singers so agreed
2Chronicles5:14 so that the priests could not endure to minister by the reason of the cloud. For the glory of the LORD had filled the house of God.
2Chronicles6:1 Then Salomon said: the LORD hath spoken it
2Chronicles6:2 And I have built an habitation for thee and a place to dwell in for ever.
2Chronicles6:3 And the king turned his face and blessed the whole congregation of Israel
2Chronicles6:4 And he said: blessed be the LORD God of Israel which spake with his mouth to my father David and hath fulfilled it with his hands
2Chronicles6:5 since the time I brought my people out of the land of Egypt
2Chronicles6:6 But now I have chosen Jerusalem to have my name there
2Chronicles6:7 And it was in the heart of David my father to build an house for the name of the LORD God of Israel:
2Chronicles6:8 But the LORD said to David my father: for as much as it was in thine heart
2Chronicles6:9 Notwithstanding thou shalt not build the house
2Chronicles6:10 And the LORD hath made good his saying that he hath spoken. For I arose in the room of David my father
2Chronicles6:11 And therein I have put the Ark wherein the covenant of the LORD made with the children of Israel
2Chronicles6:12 And he stepped forth before the altar of the LORD in the presence of all the congregation of Israel
2Chronicles6:13 now Salomon had made a Brazen pulpit of five cubits long and five cubits broad
2Chronicles6:14 and said: LORD God of Israel there is no God like thee either in heaven or in earth
2Chronicles6:15 Which hast kept with David my father that thou promisedest him: thou saidest it with thy mouth and hast fulfilled it with thine hand
2Chronicles6:16 Now LORD God of Israel keep with thy servant David my father
2Chronicles6:17 Now LORD God of Israel let thy saying be true
2Chronicles6:18 which thou saidest unto thy servant David. How be it in very deed
2Chronicles6:19 But turn to the prayer of thy servant and to his supplication (O LORD my God) to hearken unto the voice and prayer which thy servant maketh before thee.
2Chronicles6:20 That thine eyes be open over this house day and night
2Chronicles6:21 Hearken therefore unto the prayers of thy servant and of thy people Israel
2Chronicles6:22 If a man sin against his neighbour
2Chronicles6:23 then hear thou from heaven
2Chronicles6:24 Likewise if thy people Israel be put to the worse before their enemies
2Chronicles6:25 Then hear thou from heaven and be merciful unto the sin of thy people Israel
2Chronicles6:26 If heaven be shut up
2Chronicles6:27 Then hear thou from heaven and be merciful unto the sin of thy servants and of thy people Israel
2Chronicles6:28 If there chance dearth in the land
2Chronicles6:29 Then all the supplications and prayers that shall be made of all men among all thy people Israel
2Chronicles6:30 thou shalt hear from heaven thy dwelling place
2Chronicles6:31 that they may fear thee and walk in thy ways as long as they live upon the earth
2Chronicles6:32 Thereto a stranger which is not of thy people Israel
2Chronicles6:33 thou shalt hear him from heaven thy dwelling place
2Chronicles6:34 When thy people shall go out to war
2Chronicles6:35 then hear from heaven
2Chronicles6:36 If they shall sin against thee (as there is no man
2Chronicles6:37 yet if their hearts come to them again in the land where they be in captivity
2Chronicles6:38 and turn again to thee
2Chronicles6:39 Then hear from heaven thy dwelling place
2Chronicles6:40 So now my God let thine eyes be open and thine ears attent unto the prayers made in this place.
2Chronicles6:41 And now up LORD God in thy resting place: both thou and the Ark of thy strength
2Chronicles6:42 And LORD God turn not away the face of thine anointed: But remember the mercies promised to David thy servant.

2Chronicles7:6 And the priests waited on their offices

2Chronicles7:8 And Salomon kept a feast the same season of seven days
2Chronicles7:9 And the eighth day they made a gathering. For they kept the hallowing of the altar seven days and the feast seven days.

2Chronicles7:22 And it shall be answered then

2Chronicles9:5 And then she said to the king: the saying which I heard in mine own land
2Chronicles9:6 But I believed not the words of them
2Chronicles9:7 happy are thy men

2Chronicles9:13 The sum of gold that came to Salomon in one year
2Chronicles9:14 besides that which chapmen and merchants brought

2Chronicles9:16 and three hundredth shields of Beaten gold

2Chronicles9:19 And twelve lions stood one side and on the other
2Chronicles9:20 And all the drinking vessels of king Salomon were gold
2Chronicles9:21 For the king's ships went to Tharsis with the servants of Hiram every three years once. And the ships of Tharsis brought gold
2Chronicles9:22 And king Salomon passed all the kings of the earth in riches and wisdom.

2Chronicles9:25 And Salomon had four thousand mangers of horses and chariots for them

2Chronicles10:1 And Rehoboam went to Sichem: for to Sichem were all Israel come to make him king.
2Chronicles10:2 And when Jeroboam the son of Nabat
2Chronicles10:3 And they sent and called him. And so Jeroboam and all Israel came and communed with Rehoboam and said:
2Chronicles10:4 Thy father made us a grievous yoke: but remit thou somewhat of the grievous service of thy father and of his heavy yoke that he put upon us
2Chronicles10:5 And he said to them: come again after three days to me. And the people departed.
2Chronicles10:6 And king Rehoboam counselled with elders that stood before Salomon his father
2Chronicles10:7 And they told him saying: if thou shalt be kind to this people and please them and shalt speak loving words to them
2Chronicles10:8 But he left the counsel which the elders gave him
2Chronicles10:9 and said to them: what advice give ye that I may answer this people which have communed with me saying: Abate somewhat of the yoke which thy father did put upon us.
2Chronicles10:10 And the young men that were nursed up with him talked with him saying: thus answer the people that spake to thee saying: Thy father made our yoke heavy: But make thou our yoke somewhat lighter. Thus wise answer them: my little finger shall be heavier than thy father's loins.
2Chronicles10:11 For where my father put a heavy yoke upon you I will put more thereto
2Chronicles10:12 And when Jeroboam and all Israel were come the third day
2Chronicles10:13 The king answered them cruelly
2Chronicles10:14 and answered them after the advice of the young men saying: If my father made your yoke grievous
2Chronicles10:15 And so the king hearkened not unto the people
2Chronicles10:16 And when all Israel saw that the king would not hear them
2Chronicles10:17 so that Rehoboam reigned over no more of the children of Israel than dwelt in the cities of Juda.
2Chronicles10:18 Then king Rehoboam sent to them Haburam that was over the tribute
2Chronicles10:19 And so Israel departed from the house of David unto this day.

2Chronicles12:6 Whereupon the lords of Israel and the king humbled themselves and said: the LORD is righteous.
2Chronicles12:7 And when the LORD saw that they submitted themselves
2Chronicles12:8 Neverthelater they shall be his servants
2Chronicles12:9 And Sesak king of Egypt came to Jerusalem and took away the treasures of the house of the LORD
2Chronicles12:10 Instead of which king Rehoboam made shields of brass
2Chronicles12:11 And as oft as the king went to the house of the LORD
2Chronicles12:12 And so because he humbled himself
2Chronicles12:13 And king Rehoboam waxed mighty in Jerusalem and reigned. And Rehoboam was forty one year old when he was made king
2Chronicles12:14 And he did evil: for he prepared not his heart to seek the LORD.

2Chronicles26:12 And the whole number of the ancient heads of the men of might were two thousand and six hundredth
2Chronicles26:13 and under the hands of them was the army of the host
2Chronicles26:14 And Oziah provided them thorowout all the host
2Chronicles26:15 And he made engines in Jerusalem by the craft of artificers to be on the towers and corners
2Chronicles26:16 And in his greatness his heart arose
2Chronicles26:17 But Asariah the priest went in after him with four score priests of the LORD that were bold men.
2Chronicles26:18 And they stepped to Oziah the king and said to him: it pertaineth not to thee Oziah to burn cense unto the LORD
2Chronicles26:19 And Oziah was wroth and had cense in his hand to offer
2Chronicles26:20 And Asariah the chief priest with all the other priests looked upon him: and behold he was a leper in his forehead
2Chronicles26:21 And Oziah the king continued a leper unto the day of his death and dwelt in an house at liberty: how be it
2Chronicles26:22 The rest of the acts of Oziah both first and last
2Chronicles26:23 And when Oziah was laid to rest with his fathers
2Chronicles27:1 Joatham was twenty five year old when he began to reign
2Chronicles27:2 And he did that pleased the LORD in all points as did his father Oziah: save
2Chronicles27:3 He built the high gate of the temple of the LORD
2Chronicles27:4 Moreover he built cities in the mountains of Juda
2Chronicles27:5 And he fought with the king of the children of Ammon
2Chronicles27:6 And Joatham became mighty
2Chronicles27:7 The rest of the acts of Joatham and all his wars and his ways are written in the book of the kings of Israel and Juda.
2Chronicles27:8 He was twenty five year old when he began to reign
2Chronicles27:9 And when Joatham was laid to rest with his fathers
2Chronicles28:1 Ahaz was twenty year old when he began to reign
2Chronicles28:2 but walked in the ways of the kings of Israel
2Chronicles28:3 And he offered cense in the valley of the children of Hennon
2Chronicles28:4 And he offered burnt cense in hillaltars and on mountains and under every green tree.
2Chronicles28:5 Wherefore the LORD his God delivered him into the hand of the king of the Sirians: which beat him and carried away a great multitude of his captive into Damasco. And thereto he was delivered into the hand of the king of Israel which slew of his a mighty slaughter.
2Chronicles28:6 For Phakeh the son of Romeliah slew in Juda an hundredth and twenty thousand in one day
2Chronicles28:7 And Zechri a mighty man in mount Ephraim slew Maasiah the king's son and Asrican the lord steward of household
2Chronicles28:8 And the children of Israel took prisoners of their brethren two hundredth thousand wives
2Chronicles28:9 But there was a prophet of the LORD's named Obed which went out to the host which came to Samaria and said to them: see
2Chronicles28:10 And now ye purpose to keep under the children of Juda and Jerusalem and to make them bondmen and bondwomen. What other thing do ye (ye unhappy) than offend the LORD your God.
2Chronicles28:11 But now hear me
2Chronicles28:12 And certain of the heads of the children of Ephraim
2Chronicles28:13 and said unto them: ye shall not bring in the captives hither. For where we have offended the LORD already
2Chronicles28:14 And upon that the men of arms left the captives and the spoil before the lords and all the congregation.
2Chronicles28:15 And there arose certain appointed thereto by name
2Chronicles28:16 And at that same time king Ahaz sent unto the kings of Assur to have help.
2Chronicles28:17 And the Edomites came yet again and slew of Juda and carried away some captives.
2Chronicles28:18 And the Philistines invaded the cities in the low country and the south of Juda: and took Bethsames
2Chronicles28:19 For the LORD brought Juda low
2Chronicles28:20 And Thiglah Palneser king of Assur came upon him and besieged him
2Chronicles28:21 For Ahaz took away out of the house of the LORD and out of the king's house and out of the Lord's houses
2Chronicles28:22 And thereto in the very time of his tribulation did king Ahaz trespass yet more against the LORD.
2Chronicles28:23 For he offered unto the gods of them of Damasco which beat him and said
2Chronicles28:24 And Ahaz gathered together the vessels of the house of God and brake them
2Chronicles28:25 And in all the cities of Juda city by city he made hillaltars to burn incense unto other gods
2Chronicles28:26 The rest of his acts and all his ways both first and last are written in the book of the kings of Juda and Israel.
2Chronicles28:27 And when Ahaz was laid to rest with his fathers
2Chronicles29:1 Hezekiah began to reign when he was twenty five year old
2Chronicles29:2 And he did that was right in the sight of the LORD in all points as did David his father.
2Chronicles29:3 He opened the doors of the house of the LORD in the first year and first month of his reign
2Chronicles29:4 And he brought in the priests and the Levites and gathered them together into the east street:
2Chronicles29:5 and said unto them. Hear me ye Levites: purify yourselves and hallow the house of the LORD God of your fathers
2Chronicles29:6 For our fathers have trespassed and done evil favouredly in the eyes of the LORD our God: and have forsaken him
2Chronicles29:7 And beside that they have shut up the doors of the porch and quenched the lamps and have neither burned cense nor offered burntofferings in the holy place unto the God of Israel.
2Chronicles29:8 Wherefore the wrath of the LORD fell on Juda and Jerusalem: and he scattered them and made them so thin that men hiss at it
2Chronicles29:9 For see
2Chronicles29:10 Now have I in my heart to stike a covenant with the LORD God of Israel: that his wrath may cease.
2Chronicles29:11 Now therefore my sons be not negligent: for the LORD had chosen you to stand before him
2Chronicles29:12 Then the Levites arose: Nahath the son of Amasai and Joel the son of Asariah being of the children of the Cahathites: and the sons of Merari
2Chronicles29:13 and of the sons of Elizaphan
2Chronicles29:14 and of the sons of Heman
2Chronicles29:15 And they gathered their brethren and purified themselves
2Chronicles29:16 And the priests went into the house of the LORD to cleanse it
2Chronicles29:17 They began the first day of the first month to purify
2Chronicles29:18 And then they went into Hezekiah the king and said: we have cleansed all the house of the LORD
2Chronicles29:19 and thereto all the vessels which king Ahaz did cast aside when he reigned and transgressed
2Chronicles29:20 And Hezekiah the king rose early and gathered the lords of the city and went up to the house of the LORD.
2Chronicles29:21 And there were brought seven oxen
2Chronicles29:22 And they slew the oxen: and the priests received the blood and sprinkled it on the altar: and they slew the rams and sprinkled the blood upon the altar: and they slew the lambs and sprinkled the blood upon the altar.
2Chronicles29:23 And then they brought forth the kids of the sin offering before the king and the congregation which put their hands upon them.
2Chronicles29:24 And the priests slew them and offered the blood of them upon the altar
2Chronicles29:25 And they set the Levites in the house of the LORD with cymbals
2Chronicles29:26 And so the Levites stood with the instruments of David
2Chronicles29:27 And Hezekaih commanded to offer the burntoffering upon the altar. And when the burntoffering began
2Chronicles29:28 And all the congregation bowed themselves
2Chronicles29:29 And when they had made an end of the burntoffering
2Chronicles29:30 And Hezekiah the king and the lords bade the Levites to praise the LORD with the words of David and of Asaph the sear of visions. And the Levites praised that they rejoiced again
2Chronicles29:31 And Hezekiah answered and said: now that ye have filled your hands to the LORD
2Chronicles29:32 And the number of the burntofferings which the congregation brought in was seventy oxen and an hundredth rams and two hundredth lambs: and all for burntofferings to the LORD.
2Chronicles29:33 And beside that they dedicated six hundredth oxen and three thousand sheep.
2Chronicles29:34 But the priests were too few
2Chronicles29:35 And thereto the burntofferings were many with the fat of the peaceofferings and the drinkofferings that belonged to the burntofferings. And so the service pertaining to the house of the LORD went forward.
2Chronicles29:36 And Hezekiah rejoiced and all the people
2Chronicles30:1 And Hezekiah sent to all Israel and Juda: and thereto wrote letters to Ephraim and Manasseh
2Chronicles30:2 And the king held a counsel with his lords and all the congregation at Jerusalem to keep the feast of passover in the second month.
2Chronicles30:3 For they could not keep it at that time: for there were not priests enough sanctified
2Chronicles30:4 And the thing pleased the king and all the congregation.
2Chronicles30:5 And they decreed that it should be proclaimed thorowout all Israel from Bersabe to Dan
2Chronicles30:6 And the messengers went with letters of the hand of the king and of his lords thorowout all Israel and Juda
2Chronicles30:7 And be ye not like your fathers and your brethren which trespassed against the LORD of your fathers
2Chronicles30:8 Wherefore be not stiff necked like unto your fathers
2Chronicles30:9 For if ye turn unto the LORD
2Chronicles30:10 And the posts went from city to city thorowout the land of Ephraim and Manasseh and even unto Zabulon. But they laughed them to scorn and mocked them.
2Chronicles30:11 Nevertheless yet divers of Asser
2Chronicles30:12 And thereto the hand of God was in Juda
2Chronicles30:13 And so there assembled to Jerusalem much people and a mighty great congregation
2Chronicles30:14 And they arose and put away the altars that were in Jerusalem. And thereto all the incense they did away and cast them into the brook Cedron.
2Chronicles30:15 And they slew passover the fourteenth day of the second month. And the priests and Levites sanctified themselves for shame
2Chronicles30:16 And they stood in their standing after their manner according to the law of Moses the man of God. And the priests sprinkled the blood receiving it of the hands of the Levites.
2Chronicles30:17 And because there were many in the congregation that were not sanctified: and therefore did the Levites slay passover for all that were not clean to sanctify them to the LORD.
2Chronicles30:18 There was very much people out of Ephraim
2Chronicles30:19 the good LORD be merciful to all that set their hearts to seek the God that is the LORD God of their fathers
2Chronicles30:20 And the LORD heard Hezekiah and healed the people.
2Chronicles30:21 And so the children of Israel that were found at Jerusalem
2Chronicles30:22 And Hezekiah spake heartily unto the Levites that had good understanding of the LORD. And they did eat that feast seven days long and offered peaceofferings and thanked the LORD God of their fathers.
2Chronicles30:23 And the whole assembly took counsel to keep seven days more: and they held those seven days with gladness.
2Chronicles30:24 For Hezekiah king of Juda gave for heaveofferings to the congregation a thousand oxen and seven thousand sheep. And the lords gave to the congregation for heaveofferings a thousand oxen and ten thousand sheep. And the priests sanctified themselves
2Chronicles30:25 And all the congregation of Juda with the priests and Levites
2Chronicles30:26 and there was great joy in Jerusalem. For since the time of Salomon the son of David king of Israel it happened not so in Jerusalem.
2Chronicles30:27 And the priests and the Levites arose and blessed the people
2Chronicles31:1 And when they had finished all this: then all Israel that were found in cities of Juda
2Chronicles31:2 And Hezekiah set the priests and the Levites in their order to wait by course
2Chronicles31:3 And the king gave a portion of his substance for burntofferings at morning and even
2Chronicles31:4 And he bade the people that dwelt in Jerusalem to give the part of the priests and Levites
2Chronicles31:5 And as soon as the word came abroad the children of Israel brought abundance of first fruits of corn
2Chronicles31:6 And the children of Israel and Juda that dwelt in the cities of Juda
2Chronicles31:7 In the third month they began to lay the heaps and finished them the seventh.
2Chronicles31:8 And when Hezekiah and the lords came and saw the heaps
2Chronicles31:9 And Hezekiah questioned with the priests and the Levites concerning the heaps.
2Chronicles31:10 And Azariah the chief priest of the house of Zadock answered him and said since they began to bring the heaveofferings into the house of the LORD
2Chronicles31:11 And Hezekiah bade dress up the store houses about the house of the LORD. And so they did
2Chronicles31:12 and carried in the heaveofferings and the tithes and the dedicate gifts even of fidelity. Over which Chonaniah the Levite had the rule with Semei his brother next to him.
2Chronicles31:13 And Jehiel
2Chronicles31:14 And Chore the son of Jemma the Levite porter of the East door had the oversight of the freewill offerings of God
2Chronicles31:15 And under him were Eden
2Chronicles31:16 And to the males also that were reckoned from three years and above among all that went into the house of the LORD day by day
2Chronicles31:17 And to the priests that were reckoned in the households of their fathers from twenty year and above
2Chronicles31:18 And to them that were reckoned thorowout all their babes
2Chronicles31:19 For to the fidelity of them did men commit their sanctified gifts. And thereto among the children of Aaron the priests were men named by name in the fields of the suburbs of all their cities
2Chronicles31:20 And of this manner did Hezekiah thorowout all Juda
2Chronicles31:21 And in all the works that he began in the service of the house of God
2Chronicles32:1 After these deeds and truth
2Chronicles32:2 And when Hezekiah saw that Sennaherib was come and that he was purposed to fight against Jerusalem:
2Chronicles32:3 he took counsel with his captains and men of might
2Chronicles32:4 And so there gathered much people together and stopped all the wells and the brook that ran thorow the midst of the land
2Chronicles32:5 And he went to lustily and built up the wall where it was broken
2Chronicles32:6 And he set Captains of war over the people and gathered them together unto the large street of the gate of the city and spake gently to them saying:
2Chronicles32:7 Pluck up your hearts and be strong: Be not afraid or in any wise discouraged for dread of the king of Assur
2Chronicles32:8 With him is an arm of flesh: But with us is the LORD our God for to help us and to fight our battles. And the people were well couraged with the words of Hezekiah king of Juda.
2Chronicles32:9 After that Sennaherib king of Assur sent of his servants to Jerusalem (he himself lying before Lachis
2Chronicles32:10 Thus sayeth Sennaherib king of Assur: wherein do ye trust
2Chronicles32:11 Hezekiah deceiveth you
2Chronicles32:12 Is it not that Hezekiah that put down his hillaltars and his other altars
2Chronicles32:13 Moreover have ye not heard what I and my fathers have done unto the people of all lands? were the gods of the people of other lands able to save their lands out of my hand?
2Chronicles32:14 which of all the Gods of those nations that my fathers destroyed was it
2Chronicles32:15 Wherefore now let not Hezekiah deceive you either persuade you of this fashion
2Chronicles32:16 And yet more did his servants speak against the LORD God and against his servant Hezekiah.
2Chronicles32:17 And thereto he wrote a letter to rail on the LORD God of Israel and spake therein saying: as the gods of the nations(Heythen) of other lands have not delivered their people out of mine hand
2Chronicles32:18 And they cried with a loud voice in the Jews' speech unto the people of Jerusalem that were on the walls
2Chronicles32:19 And they spake against the God of Jerusalem
2Chronicles32:20 But Hezekiah the king and the prophet Isaiah son of Amoz prayed concerning the thing and cried up to heaven.
2Chronicles32:21 And the LORD sent an angel and destroyed all the men of war and the lords and captains of the host of the king of Assur
2Chronicles32:22 And so the LORD saved Hezekiah and the inhabiters at Jerusalem out of the hands of Sennaherib king of Assur and of all other
2Chronicles32:23 In so much that many brought presents unto the LORD to Jerusalem and precious gifts to Hezekiah king of Juda: so that he was magnified in the sight of all nations from thenceforth.
2Chronicles32:24 In those days Hezekiah was sick unto the death and besought the LORD: which answered him and shewed him a wonderful miracle:
2Chronicles32:25 But Hezekiah did not according to the courtesy shewed him
2Chronicles32:26 Notwithstanding Hezekiah meekened himself for the arising of his heart
2Chronicles32:27 And Hezekiah had exceeding much riches and honour. And he gathered him treasure of silver
2Chronicles32:28 and made store houses for the fruits of corn
2Chronicles32:29 And he made him towns because he had cattle of sheep and oxen great abundance. For God had given him substance exceeding much.
2Chronicles32:30 And the said Hezekiah stopped the upper water springs of Eihon and brought them down to the West side of the city of David. And Hezekiah prospered in all his works.
2Chronicles32:31 But when the ambassadors of the lords of Babilon were sent to him to enquire of the wonder that chanced in the land
2Chronicles32:32 The rest of the deeds of Hezekiah and his goodness are written in the vision of Isaiah the prophet the son of Amoz in the book of the kings of Juda and Israel.
2Chronicles32:33 And then Hezekiah laid him to rest with his fathers
2Chronicles33:1 Manasseh was twelve year old when he was made king
2Chronicles33:2 And he did wickedly in the sight of the LORD
2Chronicles33:3 For he went to and built again the hillaltars which Hezekiah his father had broken down. And he reared up altars unto Baals and made groves and bowed himself unto all the host of heaven and served them.
2Chronicles33:4 And he built altars in the house of the LORD: of which the LORD hath said in Jerusalem shall my name be for ever.
2Chronicles33:5 And he made altars unto all the host of heaven in the two courts of the house of the LORD.
2Chronicles33:6 And he burnt his children in fire in the valley of the sons of Hennon. And he observed dismal days and occupied witchcraft and sorcery
2Chronicles33:7 And he put the carved Image of an Idol which he had made
2Chronicles33:8 and no more bring the seat of Israel from the land which I have ordained for your fathers. If so be they shall be diligent to do all I have commanded by Moses in all the law ordinances and manners.
2Chronicles33:9 But Manasseh made Juda and the inhabiters of Jerusalem to err and to do worse than the heathen which the LORD destroyed before the children of Israel.
2Chronicles33:10 And when the LORD spake to Manasseh and to his people
2Chronicles33:11 Wherefore the LORD brought upon them the Captains of the host of the king of Assur
2Chronicles33:12 And when he was in tribulation he besought the LORD his God
2Chronicles33:13 and made intercession to him: and he was entreated of him and heard his prayer and brought him again to Jerusalem into his kingdom. And then Manasseh knew how that the LORD was very God.
2Chronicles33:14 After that he built a wall without the city of David on the west side of Gihon in the brook and so forth to fish gate and round about Ophel and brought it up of a very great height
2Chronicles33:15 And he took away strange gods and the Idol out of the house of GOD
2Chronicles33:16 And he made an altar unto the LORD and sacrificed thereon peaceofferings and thankofferings
2Chronicles33:17 Nevertheless the people did offer still in the hillaltars
2Chronicles33:18 The rest of the acts of Manasseh and his prayer unto his God
2Chronicles33:19 And his prayer and how he was heard
2Chronicles33:20 And when Manasseh was laid to rest with his fathers
2Chronicles33:21 Amon was twenty two years old
2Chronicles33:22 And he did that displeased the LORD like unto Manasseh his father
2Chronicles33:23 and meekened not himself before the LORD as Manasseh his father had meekened himself: But Amon trespassed greatly.
2Chronicles33:24 Wherefore his own servants conspired against him and slew him in his own house.
2Chronicles33:25 And the people of the land slew all that had conspired against king Amon. And thereto the people of the land made Josiah his son king in his room.
2Chronicles34:1 Josiah was made king when he was eight years old
2Chronicles34:2 And he did that pleased the LORD
2Chronicles34:3 Insomuch that the eighth years of his reign
2Chronicles34:4 so that they brake down the altars of Baals even in his presence
2Chronicles34:5 And he burnt the bones of the priests upon the altars
2Chronicles34:6 And even so did he in the cities of Manasse
2Chronicles34:7 And in the wildernesses of them round about he plucked asunder the altars and the groves and did beat them and stamp them to powder
2Chronicles34:8 And in the eighteenth year of his reign when he had purged the land and the temple
2Chronicles34:9 And when they came to Helkiah the high priest
2Chronicles34:10 And they put it in the hands of the workmen that had the oversight of the house of the LORD. which gave it to the labourers that wrought on the house of the LORD
2Chronicles34:11 and to masons and carpenters to buy hewed stone and timber for to make couples and beams for the houses which the kings of Juda had destroyed:
2Chronicles34:12 And the men wrought in the work of fidelity. And the overseers of them to courage them forward
2Chronicles34:13 And over the bearers of burthens and over all that wrought
2Chronicles34:14 And as they brought out the money that was brought into the house
2Chronicles34:15 And Helkiah answered and said to Saphan the scribe: I have found the book of the law in the house of the LORD
2Chronicles34:16 And Saphan carried the book to the king
2Chronicles34:17 And they have poured out the money that was found in the house of the LORD and have delivered into the hands of the overseers of the workmen.
2Chronicles34:18 And then Saphan the scribe shewed the king
2Chronicles34:19 And when the king had heard the words of the law
2Chronicles34:20 and commanded Helkiah and Ahikam the son of Saphan and Abadon the son of Micah and the said Saphan the scribe and Asaia a servant of the king's
2Chronicles34:21 go and enquire of the LORD for me and for them that are left in Israel and Juda concerning the words of the book that is found. For great is the wrath of the LORD that is fallen upon us
2Chronicles34:22 And Helkiah with them that pertained to the king went to Holdah a prophetess wife of Selum the son of Thekohath the son of Masarah keeper of the wardrobe (which prophetess dwelt in Jerusalem in the second ward) and they communed so with her.
2Chronicles34:23 And she said unto them: thus sayeth the LORD God of Israel
2Chronicles34:24 Even thus sayeth the LORD: see
2Chronicles34:25 because they have forsaken me and have offered unto other gods to anger me with all manner works of their hands
2Chronicles34:26 And as for the king of Juda which sent you to enquire of the LORD
2Chronicles34:27 Because thine heart did melt and thou didst meek thyself before God
2Chronicles34:28 Behold I will take thee to thy fathers and thou shalt be put in thy grave in peace
2Chronicles34:29 Then the king sent and gathered together all the elders of Juda and Jerusalem.
2Chronicles34:30 And the king went up into the house of the LORD
2Chronicles34:31 And the king stood at his standing and made a covenant before the LORD
2Chronicles34:32 And he made to come forth all that were found in Jerusalem and Benjamin
2Chronicles34:33 And Josiah put away all manner of abominations out of all lands that pertained to the children of Israel
2Chronicles35:1 And Josiah held the feast of passover unto the LORD in Jerusalem

2Chronicles35:5 And stand in the holy place according to the divisions of the ancient households of your brethren the children of the people

2Chronicles35:9 And Conaniah with Semeiah and Nathaneel his brethren

2Chronicles35:17 And so the children of Israel that could be found
2Chronicles35:18 And there was no passover like to that
2Chronicles35:19 And this passover was holden in the eighteenth year of the reign of Josiah.

2Chronicles35:21 And the other sent messengers to him saying: what have I to do with thee thou king of Juda? I came not against thee now at this time
2Chronicles35:22 Nevertheless Josiah turned not his face from him
2Chronicles35:23 the shooters shot against the king Josiah. And the king said to his servants: carry me away for I am sore hurt.

2Chronicles36:1 And the people of the land took Jehoahaz the son of Josiah and made him king for his father in Jerusalem.
2Chronicles36:2 And Jehoahaz was twenty three year old when he began to reign
2Chronicles36:3 For the king of Egypt put him down at Jerusalem and merced the land in an hundredth talents of silver and a talent of gold.
2Chronicles36:4 And the king of Egypt made Eliakim his brother king upon Juda and Jerusalem
2Chronicles36:5 And Jehoakim was twenty five year old when he began to reign
2Chronicles36:6 Against him came Nabuchodonozor king of Babylon and bound him in fetters to carry him to Babylon.
2Chronicles36:7 Thereto the king Nabuchodonozor carried off the vessels of the house of the LORD to Babylon and put them in his temple at Babylon.

2Chronicles36:9 Jehoiacin was eight years old when he began to reign
2Chronicles36:10 And when the year was out
2Chronicles36:11 And Zedekiah was twenty one year old when he began to reign
2Chronicles36:12 And he did that displeased the LORD his God
2Chronicles36:13 And thereto he rebelled against Nabuchodonozor which had received an oath of him by God
2Chronicles36:14 Thereto all the rulers of the priests with the people trespassed apace after all manner of abominations of the heathen and polluted the house of the LORD which he had hallowed in Jerusalem.
2Chronicles36:15 And the LORD God of their fathers sent to them by his messengers
2Chronicles36:16 But they mocked the messengers of God and despised their words and misused his prophets
2Chronicles36:17 And so he brought upon them the king of Caldeye and slew their young men with the sword in their holy temple
2Chronicles36:18 And all the vessels of the house of God both great and small

2Chronicles36:20 And he carried away them that had escaped the sword

2Chronicles36:23 Thus sayeth Cyrus king of Persia
Ezra1:1 In the first year of Cyrus king of Persia
Ezra1:2 Thus sayeth Cyrus the king of Persia: The LORD God of heaven hath given me all the kingdoms of the land and hath commanded me to build him an house at Jerusalem in Judah.
Ezra1:3 Whosoever now among you is of his people
Ezra1:4 And whosoever remaineth yet in any manner of place (where he is a stranger) let the men of his place help him with silver and gold
Ezra1:5 Then gat up the principal(pryncipall) fathers of Judah and Benjamin
Ezra1:6 And all they that were about them
Ezra1:7 And king Cyrus brought forth of the vessels of the house of the LORD
Ezra1:8 But Cyrus the king of Persia brought forth by Mithridates the treasurer
Ezra1:9 And this is the number of them: thirty basins of gold
Ezra1:10 thirty cups of gold
Ezra1:11 So that all the vessels both of gold and silver

Ezra3:1 And when the seventh month came
Ezra3:2 And there stood up Jesua the son of Josedec

Ezra4:1 But when the adversaries of Judah and Benjamin heard

Ezra4:5 and hired counsellors(councelers) against them and hindered their device

Ezra4:16 Therefore do we certify the king

Ezra7:5 the son of Abisua
Ezra7:6 This Esdras(which) was a quick scribe in the law of Moses
Ezra7:7 And there went up certain of the children of Israel
Ezra7:8 And they came to Jerusalem in the fifth month
Ezra7:9 For upon the first day of the first month devised he to go up from Babilon: and on the first day of the fifth month came he to Jerusalem
Ezra7:10 For Esdras prepared his heart to seek the law of the LORD
Ezra7:11 And this is the copy(summe) of the letter
Ezra7:12 Unto Esdras the priest and scribe in the law of the God of heaven
Ezra7:13 I have commanded
Ezra7:14 being sent of the king and of the seven lords of the counsel
Ezra7:15 And that thou shouldest take with thee
Ezra7:16 and all the silver and gold that thou canst find in all the country of Babilon: with it that the people and priests give of their own good will unto the house of God at Jerusalem.
Ezra7:17 Take thou the same
Ezra7:18 And look what it liketh thee and thy brethren to do with the remnant of the money
Ezra7:19 And the vessels that are given thee for the ministration in the house of thy God
Ezra7:20 And whatsoever thing more shall be needful for the house of thy God
Ezra7:21 I king Arthaxerses have commanded all the treasurers beyond the water

Ezra7:24 And known be it unto you

Ezra7:26 And whosoever will not diligently fulfill the law of thy God

Ezra7:28 and hath inclined his mercy unto me in the presence of the king

Ezra9:13 And after all this that is come upon us (because of our evil deeds and great trespass) thou our God hast spared our wickednesses
Ezra9:14 As for us
Ezra9:15 O LORD God of Israel

Nehemiah1:1 These are the acts of Nehemiah the son of Hachalia. It fortuned in the month Casleu
Nehemiah1:2 and Hanani
Nehemiah1:3 And they said unto me: The remnant of the captivity are there in the land in great misfortune and rebuke. The walls of Jerusalem are broken down
Nehemiah1:4 When I heard these words
Nehemiah1:5 and said: O LORD God of heaven
Nehemiah1:6 Let thine ears hearken
Nehemiah1:7 We have been corrupt unto thee
Nehemiah1:8 Yet call to remembrance the word that thou commandedst thy servant Moses
Nehemiah1:9 But if ye turn unto me
Nehemiah1:10 They are thy servants
Nehemiah1:11 O Lord
Nehemiah2:1 In the month Nisan in the twentieth year of king Artaxerses
Nehemiah2:2 Then said the king unto me: Why lookest thou so sadly? Thou art not sick
Nehemiah2:3 Nevertheless I was sore afraid and said unto the king: God save the king's life forever
Nehemiah2:4 Then said the king unto me: what is then thy request? Then made I my prayer to the God of heaven
Nehemiah2:5 and said unto the king: If it please the king
Nehemiah2:6 And the king said unto me
Nehemiah2:7 and said unto the king: if it please the king
Nehemiah2:8 And letters unto Asaph the lord of the king's wood
Nehemiah2:9 And when I came to the captains beyond the water
Nehemiah2:10 But when Sanabalat the Horonite and Tobiah the servant of the Ammonites heard that
Nehemiah2:11 And when I came to Jerusalem
Nehemiah2:12 I gat me up in the night season
Nehemiah2:13 And I rode by night unto the valley port before the Dragon well
Nehemiah2:14 And I went over unto the wellport
Nehemiah2:15 Then went I on in the night by the brook side
Nehemiah2:16 And the rulers knew not whither I went or what I did: for hither to had I not told the Jews and the priests
Nehemiah2:17 And I said unto them: Ye see the misery that we are in
Nehemiah2:18 And I told them of the good hand of my God which was upon me: and the king's words that he had spoken unto me. And they said: then let us get up
Nehemiah2:19 But when Sanabalat the Horonite
Nehemiah2:20 Then answered I them
Nehemiah3:1 And Eliasib the high priest gat him up with his brethren the priests
Nehemiah3:2 Next unto him builded the men of Jericho. And beside him builded Sachur the son of Amri.
Nehemiah3:3 But the fishport did the children of Asanah build
Nehemiah3:4 Next unto him builded Marimoth the son of Uriah the son of Nakoz. Next unto him builded Mosolam the son of Barachiah the son of Mesesabeel. Next unto him builded Zadoc the son of Baana.
Nehemiah3:5 Next unto him builded they of Thekua. But their great men put not their necks to the service of their lord.
Nehemiah3:6 The old gate builded Jehoiadah the son of Paseah
Nehemiah3:7 Next unto them builded Malatiah the Gabaon
Nehemiah3:8 Next unto him builded Usiel the son of Harhaiah the goldsmith. Next unto him builded Hananiah the Apotecary's son
Nehemiah3:9 Next unto him builded Raphaiah the son of Hur
Nehemiah3:10 Next unto him builded Jedaiah the son of Haremaph over against his house. Next unto him builded Hatus the son of Hasaboniah.
Nehemiah3:11 But Melchia the son of Harim
Nehemiah3:12 Next unto him builded Selum the son of Halohes the ruler of the half quarter of Jerusalem
Nehemiah3:13 The valley gate builded Hanun
Nehemiah3:14 But the Dung port builded Melchiah the son of Rechab
Nehemiah3:15 But the Wellgate builded Selum the son of Cholhosah

Nehemiah3:17 After him builded the Levites
Nehemiah3:18 After him builded their brethren

Nehemiah3:22 And after him builded the priests
Nehemiah3:23 After him builded Benjamin and Hasub over against their house. After them (builded) Asariah the son of Maasiah the son of Ananiah next to his house.

Nehemiah3:26 (as for the Nethinims they dwelt in Ophel
Nehemiah3:27 After him builded they of Thekua the other piece over against the great tower
Nehemiah3:28 But from the Horsegate forth builded the priests
Nehemiah3:29 After them builded Zadok the son of Emer over against his house. After him builded Semeiah the son of Sechaniah the keeper of the east gate.

Nehemiah3:31 After him builded Melchiah the goldsmith's son
Nehemiah3:32 And between the parler of the corner unto the Sheepgate builded the goldsmiths and the merchants.
Nehemiah4:1 But when Sanabalat heard that we builded the wall
Nehemiah4:2 and said before his brethren and the host(mighty men) of Samaria: What do the impotent Jews? shall they be thus suffered? shall they offer? shall they perform it in one day? shall they make the stones whole again that are brought to dust
Nehemiah4:3 And Tobiah the Ammonite beside him said: Let them build on
Nehemiah4:4 Hear O thou our God
Nehemiah4:5 Cover not their wickedness
Nehemiah4:6 Yet builded we the wall
Nehemiah4:7 But when Sanabalat
Nehemiah4:8 and conspired all together to come and fight against Jerusalem
Nehemiah4:9 Nevertheless we made our prayer unto our God
Nehemiah4:10 And Judah said: the strength of the bearers is too feeble
Nehemiah4:11 And our adversaries thought: they shall not know neither see
Nehemiah4:12 But when the Jews that dwelt beside them
Nehemiah4:13 then set I the people after their kindreds with their swords
Nehemiah4:14 and looked
Nehemiah4:15 Nevertheless when our enemies heard that we had gotten word of it
Nehemiah4:16 And from that time forth it came to pass
Nehemiah4:17 which builded on the wall
Nehemiah4:18 And everyone that builded
Nehemiah4:19 And I said unto the principal men
Nehemiah4:20 Look in what place now ye hear the noise of the trumpet
Nehemiah4:21 and we will be labouring in the work. And the half part of them held the spears from the morning spring
Nehemiah4:22 And at the same time said I unto the people: every one abide with his servant at Jerusalem
Nehemiah4:23 As for me and my brethren
Nehemiah5:1 And there arose a great complaint of the people
Nehemiah5:2 And there were some that said: our sons and daughters are too many
Nehemiah5:3 Some said: Let us set our lands
Nehemiah5:4 But some said: let us borrow money of the king's tribute(upon usury) for our lands and vineyards.
Nehemiah5:5 Now are our brethrens' bodies as our own bodies and their children as our children: else should we subdue our sons and daughters into bondage
Nehemiah5:6 But when I heard their complaint and such words
Nehemiah5:7 and I advised so in my mind
Nehemiah5:8 and said unto them: We (after our ability) have bought our brethren the Jews
Nehemiah5:9 Also I said: It is not good that ye do. Ought ye not to walk in the fear of God because of the rebuke of the Heathen our enemies?
Nehemiah5:10 I and my brethren
Nehemiah5:11 Therefore this same day see that ye restore them their lands again
Nehemiah5:12 Then said they: We will restore them again and will require nothing of them and will do as thou hast spoken. And I called the priests
Nehemiah5:13 And I shook my lap
Nehemiah5:14 And from the time forth that it was committed unto me to be a captain in the land of Juda
Nehemiah5:15 For the old captains that were before me
Nehemiah5:16 I laboured also in the work upon the wall
Nehemiah5:17 Moreover there were at my table an hundredth and fifty of the Jews and rulers
Nehemiah5:18 And there was prepared me daily an ox
Nehemiah5:19 Think upon me my God unto the best
Nehemiah6:1 And when Sanabalat
Nehemiah6:2 Sanabalat and Gesem sent unto me
Nehemiah6:3 Notwithstanding I sent messengers unto them
Nehemiah6:4 Howbeit they sent unto me as good as four times after the same manner. And I gave the same answer.
Nehemiah6:5 Then sent Sanabalat his servant unto me the fifth time
Nehemiah6:6 wherein was written: it is told the Heathen
Nehemiah6:7 and hast ordained the Prophets to preach of thee at Jerusalem
Nehemiah6:8 Nevertheless I sent unto him saying: there is no such thing done as thou sayest: thou hast feigned it out of thine own heart.
Nehemiah6:9 For they were all minded to make us afraid and thought: They shall withdraw their hands from the work
Nehemiah6:10 And I came unto the house of Semaiah the son of Delaiah the son of Mehetabeel
Nehemiah6:11 But I said: should any such man as I flee? Who is that
Nehemiah6:12 For I perceived
Nehemiah6:13 Therefore took he the money
Nehemiah6:14 My God think thou upon Tobiah and Sanabalat according unto these their works
Nehemiah6:15 And the wall was finished on the five and twentieth day of the month Elul
Nehemiah6:16 And when all our enemies heard thereof
Nehemiah6:17 And at the same time were there many of the chief of Juda
Nehemiah6:18 (for there were many in Judah that were sworn unto him: for he was the son-in-law of Shechaniah the son of Arah
Nehemiah6:19 and they spake good of him before me

Nehemiah7:4 As for the city

Nehemiah9:1 In the four and twenty(twentieth) day of this month came the children of Israel together with fasting
Nehemiah9:2 and separated the seed of Israel from all the strange children and stood and knowledged their sins
Nehemiah9:3 and stood up in their place
Nehemiah9:4 And the Levites stood on high
Nehemiah9:5 And the Levites
Nehemiah9:6 LORD
Nehemiah9:7 Thou art
Nehemiah9:8 and foundest his heart faithful before thee
Nehemiah9:9 and hast considered the misery of our fathers in Egypt
Nehemiah9:10 and shewed tokens and wonders upon Pharaoh
Nehemiah9:11 And the reed sea(See) didst thou divide(partedst thou) in sunder before them
Nehemiah9:12 and leddest them on the day time in a cloudy pillar; and on the night season in a pillar of fire
Nehemiah9:13 Thou camest down also upon mount Sinai
Nehemiah9:14 and declared unto them thy holy Sabbath
Nehemiah9:15 and gavest them bread from heaven when they were hungry
Nehemiah9:16 But(Nevertheless) our fathers were proud
Nehemiah9:17 and refused to hear
Nehemiah9:18 And though they made a molten calf
Nehemiah9:19 yet forsookest thou them not in the wilderness
Nehemiah9:20 And thou gavest them thy good spirit to enform them
Nehemiah9:21 Forty years long madest thou provision for them in the wilderness
Nehemiah9:22 And thou gavest them kingdoms and nations
Nehemiah9:23 And their children multipliedst thou as the stars of heaven
Nehemiah9:24 And the children went in
Nehemiah9:25 And they won(wanne) their strong cities
Nehemiah9:26 Nevertheless they were disobedient
Nehemiah9:27 Therefore gavest thou them over in to the hand of their enemies that vexed them. And in the time of their trouble they cried unto thee
Nehemiah9:28 But when they came to rest
Nehemiah9:29 and testified unto them
Nehemiah9:30 And many years didst thou forbear them
Nehemiah9:31 But for thy great mercies' sake thou hast not utterly consumed them
Nehemiah9:32 Now our God
Nehemiah9:33 Thou art righteous in all that thou hast brought upon us: for thou hast done right.
Nehemiah9:34 As for us
Nehemiah9:35 and have not served thee in their kingdom
Nehemiah9:36 Behold
Nehemiah9:37 And great is the increase of it unto the kings
Nehemiah9:38 And in all this make we a sure covenant

Nehemiah13:3 Now when they heard the Law
Nehemiah13:4 And before this had the Priest Eliasib delivered the chest of the house of our God unto his kinsman Tobiah:
Nehemiah13:5 for he had made him a great chest
Nehemiah13:6 But in all this was not I at Jerusalem: for in the two and thirtieth year of Arthaxerses king of Babilon
Nehemiah13:7 to come to Jerusalem. And I gat knowledge of the evil that Eliasib did unto Tobiah
Nehemiah13:8 and it grieved me sore
Nehemiah13:9 and commanded them to cleanse the chest. And thither brought I again the vessels of the house of God
Nehemiah13:10 And I perceived
Nehemiah13:11 Then reproved I the rulers
Nehemiah13:12 Then brought all Judah the tithes of corn
Nehemiah13:13 And I made treasurers over the treasure
Nehemiah13:14 Think upon me
Nehemiah13:15 At the same time saw I some treading wine presses on the Sabbath and bringing in clusters
Nehemiah13:16 There dwelt men of Tyre also therein
Nehemiah13:17 Then reproved I the rulers in Judah
Nehemiah13:18 Did not our fathers even thus
Nehemiah13:19 And it fortuned
Nehemiah13:20 Then remained the chapmen and merchants once or twice over night without Jerusalem with all manner of wares.
Nehemiah13:21 Then reproved I them sore
Nehemiah13:22 And I said unto the Levites which were clean
Nehemiah13:23 And at the same time saw I Jews
Nehemiah13:24 and their children spake half in the speech of Ashdod
Nehemiah13:25 And I reproved them
Nehemiah13:26 Did not Salomon the king of Israel sin therein? and yet among many Heathen was there no king like him

Nehemiah13:29 O my God
Nehemiah13:30 Thus cleansed I them from all such as were outlandish

Esther1:1 In the time of Ahaserus
Esther1:2 what time as he sat on his seat royal in the castle of Susan
Esther1:3 in the third year of his reign
Esther1:4 that he might shew the noble riches of his kingdom
Esther1:5 And when these days were expired
Esther1:6 where there hanged white
Esther1:7 And the drink was carried in vessels of gold
Esther1:8 And no man was appointed what he should drink: for the king had commanded all the officers of his house
Esther1:9 And the queen Vasthi made a feast also for the women in the palace of Ahasuerus.
Esther1:10 And on the seventh day when the king was merry of the wine
Esther1:11 to fetch the queen Vasthi with the crown regal
Esther1:12 But the queen Vasthi would not come at the king's word by his chamberlains. Then was the king very wroth
Esther1:13 And the king spake to the wise men that had understanding in the ordinances of the land (for the king's matters must be handled before all such as have knowledge of the law and judgment:)
Esther1:14 and the next unto him were Carsena
Esther1:15 What law should be execute upon the queen Vasthi
Esther1:16 Then said Mamucan before the king and the princes: the queen Vasthi hath not onely done evil against the king but also against all the princes and all the people in all the lands of king Ahasuerus
Esther1:17 for this deed of the queen shall come abroad unto all women
Esther1:18 And so shall the princesses in Persia and Media say likewise unto all the king's princes
Esther1:19 If it please the king
Esther1:20 And that this writing of the king which shall be made
Esther1:21 This pleased the king and the princes
Esther1:22 Then were there letters sent forth into all the king's lands
Esther2:1 After these acts when the displeasure of king Ahasuerus was laid he thought upon Vasthi
Esther2:2 Then said the king's servants: Let there be fair young virgins sought for the king
Esther2:3 and let the king appoint overseers in all the lands of his empire
Esther2:4 And look which damsel pleaseth the king
Esther2:5 In the castle of Susan there was a Jew
Esther2:6 which was carried away from Jerusalem
Esther2:7 and he nourished Hadasah (that is Esther)(Hester) his uncle's daughter: for she had neither father nor mother
Esther2:8 Now when the king's commandment and commission was published
Esther2:9 and the damsel pleased him
Esther2:10 But Esther shewed not her people nor her kindred: for Mardocheus had charged her
Esther2:11 And Mardocheus walked every day before the court of the women's building
Esther2:12 And when the appointed time of every damsel came that she should come to the king Ahasuerus
Esther2:13 then went there one damsel unto the king
Esther2:14 And when one came in the evening
Esther2:15 Now when the time came of Esther the daughter of Abihail Mardocheus uncle (whom he had received as his own daughter) that she should come to the king
Esther2:16 And Esther was taken unto king Ahasuerus in to the house royal
Esther2:17 And the king loved Esther above all the women
Esther2:18 And the king made a great feast unto all his princes and his servants
Esther2:19 And when the virgins were gathered together the second time
Esther2:20 And as yet had not Esther shewed her kindred and her people
Esther2:21 At the same time while Mardocheus sat in the king's gate
Esther2:22 whereof Mardocheus gat knowledge
Esther2:23 And when inquisition was made
Esther3:1 After these acts did the king promote Haman the son of Amadatha the Agagite
Esther3:2 And all the king's servants that were in the gate
Esther3:3 Then the king's servants which were in the king's gate
Esther3:4 And when they spake this daily unto him and he followed them not
Esther3:5 And when Haman saw
Esther3:6 and thought it too little to lay hands(and despyte
Esther3:7 In the first month (that is the month Nisan) in the twelfth year of king Ahasuerus they cast Phur
Esther3:8 And Haman said unto king Ahasuerus: There is a people scattered abroad and dispersed among all people in all the lands of thine empire
Esther3:9 If it please the king
Esther3:10 Then took the king his ring from his hand
Esther3:11 And the king said unto Haman: Let the silver be given thee
Esther3:12 Then were the king's scribes called on the thirteenth day of the first month
Esther3:13 And the writings were sent by posts in to all the king's lands
Esther3:14 This was the sum of the writing
Esther3:15 And the posts went in all the haste according to the king's commandment. And in the castle of Susan was the commandment devised. And the king and Haman sat and drank. But the city Susan was disquieted.
Esther4:1 When Mardocheus perceived all that was done
Esther4:2 and came before the king's gate: for there might no man enter within the king's gate
Esther4:3 And in all lands and places
Esther4:4 So Esther's damsels and her chamberlains

Esther4:6 So Hathath went forth to Mardocheus unto the street of the city
Esther4:7 And Mardocheus told him of all that had happened unto him
Esther4:8 And he gave him the copy of the commandment
Esther4:9 And when Hathath came in

Esther4:11 All the king's servants
Esther4:12 And when Mardocheus was certified of Esther's words
Esther4:13 Mardocheus bade say again unto Esther: think not to save thine own life
Esther4:14 for if thou holdest thy peace at this time
Esther4:15 Esther bade
Esther4:16 Go thou thy way then
Esther4:17 So Mardocheus went his way
Esther5:1 And on the third day put Esther on her royal apparel
Esther5:2 And when the king saw Esther the queen standing in the court
Esther5:3 Then said the king unto her: What wilt thou queen Esther? and what requirest thou? ask even the half of the empire
Esther5:4 Esther said: If it please the king
Esther5:5 The king said: Cause Haman to make haste
Esther5:6 the king said unto Esther at the banquet of wine
Esther5:7 Then answered Esther and said: My petition and desire is
Esther5:8 if I have found grace in the sight of the king
Esther5:9 Then went Haman forth the same day joyful and merry in his mind. And when he saw Mardocheus in the king's gate
Esther5:10 Nevertheless he refrained himself: and when he came home
Esther5:11 and told them of the glory of his riches
Esther5:12 Haman said moreover: Yea and Esther the queen let no man come with the king unto the banquet that she had prepared
Esther5:13 But in all this am I not satisfied as long as I see Mardocheus the Jew sitting at the king's gate.
Esther5:14 Then said Zares his wife and all his friends unto him: Let them make a gallows of fifty cubits high

Esther7:3 Esther the queen answered and said: If I have found grace in thy sight
Esther7:4 for we are sold
Esther7:5 The king Ahasuerus spake and said unto queen Esther: What is he that? Or where is he that dare presume in his mind to do such a thing after that manner?
Esther7:6 Esther said: The enemy and adversary is this wicked Haman. As for Haman
Esther7:7 And the king arose from the banquet and from the wine in his displeasure and went in to the palace garden. And Haman stood up
Esther7:8 And when the king came again out of the palace garden into the parlor where they had eaten

Esther7:10 So they hanged Haman on the gallows that he had made for Mardocheus. Then was the king's wrath pacified.(stilled)

Esther9:2 Then gathered the Jews together in their cities within all the lands of the king Ahasuerus
Esther9:3 And all the rulers in the lands
Esther9:4 For Mardocheus was great in the king's house
Esther9:5 Thus the Jews smote all their enemies with a sore slaughter

Esther9:7 and slew Pharsandatha
Esther9:8 Phoratha
Esther9:9 Pharmastha
Esther9:10 the ten sons of Haman the son of Hammadatha the enemy of the Jews: but on his goods they laid no hands.

Esther9:13 Esther said: If it please the king
Esther9:14 And the king charged to do so
Esther9:15 And the Jews gathered themselves together at Susan
Esther9:16 As for the other Jews in the king's lands
Esther9:17 This was done on the thirteenth day of the month Adar
Esther9:18 But the Jews at Susan were come together both on the thirteenth day and on the fourteenth
Esther9:19 Therefore the Jews that dwelt in the villages and unwalled towns
Esther9:20 And Mardocheus wrote these acts
Esther9:21 that they should yearly receive and hold the fourteenth and fifteenth day of the month Adar
Esther9:22 as the days wherein the Jews came to rest from their enemies
Esther9:23 And the Jews received it that they had begun to do
Esther9:24 how that Haman the son of Hamadatha
Esther9:25 and how Hester went and spake to the king
Esther9:26 For the which cause they called this day Phurim after the name of Phur
Esther9:27 And the Jews set it up
Esther9:28 how that these days are not to be forgotten
Esther9:29 And queen Hester the daughter of Abihail and Mardocheus the Jew wrote with all authority
Esther9:30 and sent the letters unto all the Jews
Esther9:31 to confirm these days of Phurim
Esther9:32 And Hester commanded to establish these acts of this Phurim
