Job1:1 In the land of Hus
Job1:2 This man had seven sons
Job1:3 His substance was seven thousand sheep
Job1:4 And his sons went
Job1:5 So when they had passed over the time of their banqueting round about
Job1:6 Now upon a time
Job1:7 And the LORD said unto Sathan: From whence comest thou? Sathan answered the LORD
Job1:8 Then said the LORD unto Sathan: hast thou not considered my servant Job
Job1:9 Sathan answered
Job1:10 hast thou not preserved him
Job1:11 But lay thine hand upon him a little
Job1:12 And the LORD said unto Sathan: lo all that he hath
Job1:13 Now upon a certain day when his sons and daughters were eating
Job1:14 there came a messenger unto Job
Job1:15 the Sabees came in violently
Job1:16 And while he was yet speaking
Job1:17 In the mean season while he was yet speaking
Job1:18 While he was speaking
Job1:19 and suddenly there came a mighty great wind out of the South
Job1:20 Then Job stood up
Job1:21 and said: Naked came I out of my mother's womb
Job1:22 In all these things did Job not offend
Job2:1 It happened also upon a time
Job2:2 And the LORD said unto Sathan: From whence comest thou? Sathan answered and said: I have gone about the land
Job2:3 Then said the LORD unto Sathan: Hast thou not considered my servant Job
Job2:4 Sathan answered the LORD
Job2:5 But lay thine hand upon him
Job2:6 Then said the LORD unto Sathan: lo
Job2:7 So went Sathan forth from the LORD
Job2:8 So that he sat upon the ground in the ashes
Job2:9 Then said his wife unto him: Dost thou (yet) continue in thy perfectness? curse God
Job2:10 But Job said unto her: Thou speakest like a foolish woman. Seeing we have received prosperity at the hand of God
Job2:11 Now when Job's friends heard of all the trouble
Job2:12 So when they lift up their eyes afar off
Job2:13 They sat them down by him also upon the ground seven days and seven nights. Neither was there any of them that spake one word unto him
Job3:1 After this opened Job his mouth
Job3:2 and said: lost be the day
Job3:3 and the night
Job3:4 The same day be turned to darkness
Job3:5 but be covered with darkness
Job3:6 Let the dark storm overcome that(the) night
Job3:7 Despised be that night
Job3:8 even those that be ready to raise up mourning give it also their curse.(geue it their curse also
Job3:9 Let the stars be dim thorow darkness of it. Let it look for light
Job3:10 because it shut not up the womb that bare me
Job3:11 Alas
Job3:12 Why set they me upon their knees? Why gave they me suck with their breasts?
Job3:13 Then should I now have layen still
Job3:14 like as the kings and lords of the earth
Job3:15 As the princes that have great substance of gold
Job3:16 O that I utterly had no being
Job3:17 There must the wicked cease from their tyranny
Job3:18 there are those let out free
Job3:19 There are small and great: the bondman
Job3:20 Wherefore is the light given to him that is in misery? and life unto them
Job3:21 Which long for death
Job3:22 and search for it more than for treasure which also would be exceedingly glad
Job3:23 That should be joy to the man whose way is hid
Job3:24 For my sighs come(This is the cause
Job3:25 For the thing that I feared
Job3:26 Was I not happy? Had I not quietness? Was I not in rest? And now cometh such misery upon me.
Job4:1 Then answered Eliphaz the Temanite and said unto him:
Job4:2 If we begin to commune(common) with thee
Job4:3 Behold
Job4:4 Thy words have set up those that were fallen
Job4:5 But now that the plague is come upon thee
Job4:6 Is not this thy fear
Job4:7 Consider (I pray thee) who ever perished being an innocent? Or
Job4:8 As I have seen them that plow vanity(wickedness) and sow malice(mischief) reap the same.
Job4:9 With the blast of God did they perish
Job4:10 The roaring of the lion
Job4:11 The (greate) lion perisheth
Job4:12 And unto me was the word hid
Job4:13 In the fantasies and thoughts of the visions of the night
Job4:14 Fear came upon me and dread and made all my bones to shake.
Job4:15 And when the wind passed by before my presence it made the hairs of my flesh stand(stode) up.
Job4:16 He stood there and I knew not his face
Job4:17 Shall man be more just than God? Or shall man be purer than his maker?(May a man be justified before God? May there any man be judged to be clean
Job4:18 Behold there is no trust to his servants
Job4:19 How much more in them(then shall they) that dwell in houses of clay
Job4:20 They shall be smitten(destroyed) from morning unto the evening: yea they shall perish everlastingly
Job4:21 Is not their dignity taken away with them
Job5:1 Name me one else
Job5:2 As for the foolish man
Job5:3 I have seen my self
Job5:4 that his children were without prosperity or health: that they were slain in the door
Job5:5 that his harvest was eaten up of the hungry: that the weaponed man had spoiled it
Job5:6 Is it not the earth that bringeth forth travail
Job5:7 but it is man
Job5:8 But now will I speak of(off) the Lord
Job5:9 which doth things
Job5:10 Which giveth rain upon the earth
Job5:11 which setteth up them of low degree
Job5:12 Which destroyeth the devices of the subtle
Job5:13 which compass the wise in their own craftiness
Job5:14 In so much that they run in to darkness by fair day
Job5:15 And so he delivereth the poor from the sword
Job5:16 that the poor may have hope
Job5:17 Behold
Job5:18 For though he make a wound
Job5:19 He delivereth thee out of six troubles
Job5:20 In the middest of hunger he saveth thee from death: and when it is war
Job5:21 He shall keep thee from the perilous tongue so that when trouble cometh
Job5:22 In destruction and dearth thou shalt be merry
Job5:23 But the castles in the land shall be confederate with thee
Job5:24 Yea thou shalt know
Job5:25 Thou shalt see also
Job5:26 Thou shalt come to thy grave in a fair age
Job5:27 Lo
Job6:1 Job answered
Job6:2 For then should it be heavier
Job6:3 This is the cause
Job6:4 For the arrows of the allmighty are in me
Job6:5 Doth the wild ass roar when he hath grass? Or crieth the ox
Job6:6 That which is unsavory
Job6:7 The things that some time I might not away withal
Job6:8 O that I might have my desire: O that God would grant me the thing
Job6:9 That he would begin and smite me: that he would let his hand go
Job6:10 Then should I have some comfort: yea I would desire him in my pain
Job6:11 What power have I to endure? Or
Job6:12 Is my strength the strength of stones? Or is my flesh made of brass?
Job6:13 Is it not so that there is in me no help: and that my substance is taken from me.(Am I able to help my self? Is not my strength gone from me
Job6:14 He that is in tribulation ought to be comforted of his neighbour: but the fear of the LorD is clean away:(like as if one withdrew a good deed from his friend
Job6:15 Mine own brethren pass over by me as the water brook
Job6:16 But they that fear the hoarfrost
Job6:17 When their time cometh
Job6:18 for the paths that they go in
Job6:19 Consider the paths of Theman
Job6:20 Confounded are they
Job6:21 Even so are ye also come unto me: but now that ye see my misery
Job6:22 Did I desire you
Job6:23 To deliver me from the enemy's hand
Job6:24 Teach me and I will hold my tongue: and if I do err
Job6:25 Wherefore blame ye then the words
Job6:26 which of you can reprove them? Saving only that ye are subtle to check men's sayings
Job6:27 Ye fall upon the fatherless
Job6:28 Wherefore look not only upon me
Job6:29 Turn into your own selves (I pray you) be indifferent judges
Job6:30 whether there be any unrighteousness in my tongue
Job7:1 Is not the life of man upon earth a very battle? Are not his days like the days of an hired servant?
Job7:2 For like as a bond servant desireth the shadow
Job7:3 Even so have I laboured whole months long (but in vain) and many a careful night have I told.
Job7:4 When I laid me down to sleep
Job7:5 My flesh is clothed with worms
Job7:6 my days pass over more speedily
Job7:7 O remember
Job7:8 yea and that none other man's eye shall see me anymore.
Job7:9 For if thou fasten thine eyes upon me
Job7:10 nor turneth again into his house
Job7:11 Therefore I will not spare my mouth
Job7:12 Am I a sea
Job7:13 When I think: my bed shall comfort me. I shall have some refreshing by talking by myself upon my couch:
Job7:14 Then troublest thou me with dreams
Job7:15 that my soul wisheth rather to be strangled
Job7:16 I can see no remedy
Job7:17 What is man
Job7:18 Thou takest diligent care for him
Job7:19 Why goest thou not from me
Job7:20 I have offended
Job7:21 Why doest thou not forgive my sin? Wherefore takest thou not away my wickedness. Behold
Job8:1 Then answered Baldad the Suhite
Job8:2 How long wilt thou talk of such things? how long shall thy mouth speak so proud words?
Job8:3 Doth God pervert the thing that is lawful? Or
Job8:4 When thy sons sinned against him
Job8:5 If thou wouldest now resort unto God by times
Job8:6 If thou wouldest live a pure and godly life: should he not wake up unto thee immediately
Job8:7 In so much
Job8:8 Enquire of them that have been before thee
Job8:9 Namely
Job8:10 They shall shew thee
Job8:11 May a rush be green without moistness? may the grass grow without water?
Job8:12 No: but (or ever it be shot forth
Job8:13 Even so goeth it with all them
Job8:14 His confidence shall be destroyed
Job8:15 He leaneth him upon his house
Job8:16 Often time a thing doth flourish
Job8:17 it taketh many roots
Job8:18 But if it be taken out of his place
Job8:19 Lo
Job8:20 Behold
Job8:21 Thy mouth shall be still with laughing
Job8:22 They that hate thee
Job9:1 Job answered
Job9:2 As for that
Job9:3 If he will argue with him
Job9:4 He is wise of heart
Job9:5 He translateth the mountains
Job9:6 He removeth the earth out of her place
Job9:7 He commandeth the Son
Job9:8 He himself alone spreadeth out the heavens
Job9:9 He maketh the vaines(wains) of heaven
Job9:10 He doth great things
Job9:11 If he came by me
Job9:12 If he be hasty to take anything away
Job9:13 He is God
Job9:14 How should I then answer him? or what words should I find out against him?
Job9:15 Yea though I be righteous
Job9:16 All be it that I call upon him
Job9:17 he troubleth me so with the tempest
Job9:18 He will not let my spirit be in rest
Job9:19 If men will speak of strength
Job9:20 If I will justify my self
Job9:21 For that I should be an innocent
Job9:22 This one thing will I say: He destroyeth both the righteous and ungodly.
Job9:23 And though he slay suddenly with the scourge
Job9:24 As for the world
Job9:25 My days have been more swift than a runner: they are gone suddenly
Job9:26 They are passed away
Job9:27 When I am purposed to forget my complainings
Job9:28 Then am I afraid of all my works
Job9:29 If I be then a wicked one
Job9:30 Though I washed my self with snow water
Job9:31 yet shouldest thou dip me in the mire
Job9:32 For he that I must give answer unto
Job9:33 Neither is there any daysman to reprove both the parts
Job9:34 Let him take his rod away from me
Job9:35 and then shall I answer him without any fear. For as long as I am in such fearfulness
Job10:1 it grieveth my soul to live. Nevertheless
Job10:2 and will say unto God: O do not condemn me: but shew me the cause
Job10:3 Thinkest thou it well done
Job10:4 Hast thou fleshly eyes then
Job10:5 Are thy days as the days of man
Job10:6 That thou makest such inquisition for my wickedness
Job10:7 Whereas (notwithstanding) thou knowest that I am no wicked person
Job10:8 Thy hands have made me
Job10:9 O remember (I beseek thee) how that thou madest me of the mould of the earth
Job10:10 Hast thou not milked me
Job10:11 Thou hast covered me with skin and flesh
Job10:12 Thou hast granted me life
Job10:13 Though thou hidest these things in thine heart
Job10:14 Wherefore didst thou keep me
Job10:15 If I do wickedly
Job10:16 Thou huntest me out (being in heaviness) as it were a Lion
Job10:17 Thou bringest fresh witnesses against me
Job10:18 Wherefore hast thou brought me out of my mother's womb? O that I had perished
Job10:19 If they had carried me to my grave
Job10:20 Shall not my short life come so soon to an end? O hold thee from me
Job10:21 afore I go thither
Job10:22 yea into that dark cloudy land and deadly shadow
Job11:1 Then answered Zophar the Naamathite
Job11:2 Should not he that maketh many words
Job11:3 Should men give ear unto thee only? Thou wilt laugh other men to scorn
Job11:4 Wilt thou say unto God: The thing that I take in hand is perfect
Job11:5 O that God would speak
Job11:6 that he might shew thee (out of his secret wisdom) how many fold his law is: then shouldest thou know
Job11:7 Wilt thou find out God with thy seeking? Wilt thou attain to the perfectness of the Almighty?
Job11:8 He is higher than heaven
Job11:9 His length exceedeth the length of the earth
Job11:10 Though he turn all things upside down
Job11:11 For it is he that knoweth the vanity of men: he seeth their wickedness also
Job11:12 A vain body exalteth himself
Job11:13 If thou hadst now a right heart
Job11:14 if thou wouldest put away the wickedness which thou hast in hand
Job11:15 Then mightest thou lift up thy face without shame
Job11:16 Then shouldest thou forget thy misery
Job11:17 Then should thy life be as clear as the noon day
Job11:18 Then mightest thou have comforth
Job11:19 Then shouldest thou take thy rest
Job11:20 As for the eyes of the ungodly
Job12:1 So Job answered
Job12:2 Then (no doubt) ye are the men alone
Job12:3 But I have understanding as well as ye
Job12:4 Thus he that calleth upon God
Job12:5 Godliness is a light despised in the hearts of the rich
Job12:6 The houses of robbers are in wealth and prosperity
Job12:7 Ask the cattle
Job12:8 Speak to the earth
Job12:9 What is he
Job12:10 In whose hand is the soul of every living thing
Job12:11 Have not the ears pleasure in hearing
Job12:12 Among old persons there is wisdom
Job12:13 Yea with God is wisdom and strength
Job12:14 If he break down a thing
Job12:15 Behold
Job12:16 With him is strength and wisdom: he knoweth both the deceiver and him that is deceived.
Job12:17 He carrieth away the wise men
Job12:18 He lowseth the girdle of kings
Job12:19 He leadeth away the Priests in to captivity
Job12:20 He taketh the verity from out of the mouth
Job12:21 He poureth out confusion upon princes
Job12:22 Look what lieth hid in darkness
Job12:23 He both increaseth the people and destroyeth them: He maketh them to multiply
Job12:24 He changeth the heart of the princes and kings of the earth
Job12:25 and grope in the dark without light
Job13:1 Lo
Job13:2 Look what ye know
Job13:3 Nevertheless I am purposed to talk with the almighty
Job13:4 As for you
Job13:5 Would God ye kept your tongue
Job13:6 Therefore hear my words
Job13:7 Will ye make an answer for God with lies
Job13:8 Will ye accept the person of God
Job13:9 Shall that help you
Job13:10 He shall punish you
Job13:11 Shall he not make you afraid
Job13:12 your remembrance shall be like unto the dust
Job13:13 Hold your tongues now
Job13:14 Wherefore do I bear my flesh in my teeth
Job13:15 Lo
Job13:16 he is even the same that maketh me whole: And why? There may no hypocrite come before him.
Job13:17 Hear my words
Job13:18 Behold
Job13:19 What is he that will go to law with me? For if I hold my tongue
Job13:20 Nevertheless grant me two things
Job13:21 Withdraw thine hand from me
Job13:22 And then send for me to the law
Job13:23 How great are my misdeeds and sins? Let me know my transgressions and offenses.
Job13:24 Wherefore hidest thou thy face
Job13:25 Wilt thou be so cruel and extreme unto a flying leaf
Job13:26 That thou layest so sharply to my charge
Job13:27 Thou hast put my foot in the stocks: thou lookest narrowly unto all my paths
Job13:28 where as I (notwithstanding) must consume like as a foul carrion
Job14:1 Man that is born of a woman
Job14:2 He cometh up and falleth away like a flower. He flyeth as it were a shadow
Job14:3 Thinkest thou it now well done
Job14:4 Who can make it clean
Job14:5 The days of man are short
Job14:6 Go from him
Job14:7 If a tree be cut down
Job14:8 For though a root be waxen old and dead in the ground
Job14:9 yet when the stock geteth the scent of water
Job14:10 But as for man
Job14:11 The floods when they be dried up
Job14:12 but when man sleepeth
Job14:13 O that thou wouldest keep me
Job14:14 May a dead man live again? All the days of this my pilgrimage am I looking
Job14:15 If thou wouldest but call me
Job14:16 For thou hast numbered all my goings
Job14:17 Thou hast sealed up mine offenses
Job14:18 The mountains fall away at the last
Job14:19 the waters pierce thorow the very stones by little and little
Job14:20 Thou prevailest against him
Job14:21 Whether his children come to worship or no
Job14:22 While he liveth
Job15:1 Then answered Eliphaz the Themanite
Job15:2 Should a wise man answer as the science of the wind
Job15:3 Thou reprovest with words
Job15:4 As for shame
Job15:5 but thy wickedness teacheth thy mouth
Job15:6 Thine own mouth condemneth thee
Job15:7 Art thou the first man
Job15:8 hast thou heard the secret counsel(councell) of God
Job15:9 What knowest thou
Job15:10 With us are old and aged men
Job15:11 Thinkest thou it a small thing of the consolations of God? and are they with the living word.(Dost thou no more regard the comfort of God? but thy wicked words wil not suffer thee.)
Job15:12 Why doth thine heart make thee so proud? Why standest thou so greatly in thine own conceit? Where unto look thine eyes
Job15:13 that thy mind is so puffed up against God and lettest such words go out of thy mouth?
Job15:14 What is man
Job15:15 Behold there is no trust to his saints:(he hath founde unfaithfulnesse amoge his owne sanctes) yea the very heavens are not clean in his sight.
Job15:16 How much more then an abominable and vile man
Job15:17 I will tell thee
Job15:18 which wise men have told
Job15:19 unto whom only the land was given
Job15:20 The ungodly despaireth all the days of his life
Job15:21 A fearful sound is ever in his ears
Job15:22 He believeth never to be delivered out of darkness
Job15:23 When he goeth forth to get his living
Job15:24 Sorrow and carefulness make him afraid
Job15:25 For he hath stretched out his hand against God
Job15:26 He runneth proudly upon him
Job15:27 where as he covereth his face with fatness
Job15:28 Therefore shall his dwelling be in desolate cities
Job15:29 He shall not be rich
Job15:30 He shall never come out of darkness
Job15:31 He will never apply himself to faithfulness and truth
Job15:32 He shall perish
Job15:33 He shall be plucked off as an untimely grape from the vine
Job15:34 For the congregation of hypocrites is unfruitful
Job15:35 He conceiveth travail
Job16:1 Job answered
Job16:2 I have oft times heard such things. Miserable givers of comfort are ye
Job16:3 Shall not thy vain words come yet to an end? Or
Job16:4 I could speak as ye do also. But would God
Job16:5 I should comfort you with my mouth
Job16:6 But what shall I do? For all my words
Job16:7 And now that I am full of pain
Job16:8 (whereof my wrinkles bear witness) there standeth up a dissembler to make me answer with lies to my face.
Job16:9 He is angry at me
Job16:10 They have opened their mouths wide upon me
Job16:11 God hath given me over to the ungodly
Job16:12 I was some time in wealth
Job16:13 He hath compassed me round about with his darts
Job16:14 He hath given me one wound upon another
Job16:15 I have sowed a sack cloth upon my skin
Job16:16 My face is swollen with weeping
Job16:17 Howbeit there is no wickedness in my hands
Job16:18 O earth
Job16:19 For lo
Job16:20 My friends laugh me to scorn
Job16:21 Though a body might plead with God
Job16:22 yet the number of my years are come
Job17:1 My breath faileth
Job17:2 I have deceived no man
Job17:3 O deliver me and set me by thee: who shall then be able to thrust my hands together?
Job17:4 Thou hast withholden their hearts from understanding
Job17:5 He promiseth his friends part of his good
Job17:6 He hath made me as it were a byword of the common people. I am his jesting stock among them.
Job17:7 My countenance is heavy for very anger
Job17:8 Virtuous men therefore shall well consider this
Job17:9 The righteous will keep his way
Job17:10 As for you
Job17:11 My days are past
Job17:12 changing the night in to day
Job17:13 Though I tarry never so much
Job17:14 I call corruption my father
Job17:15 What helpeth then my long tarrying? Or
Job17:16 All that I have
Job18:1 Then answered Baldad the Suhite
Job18:2 when will ye make an end of your words? Mark well
Job18:3 Wherefore are we counted as beasts
Job18:4 Why destroyest thou thy self with anger? Shall the earth be forsaken
Job18:5 Shall not the light of the ungodly be put out? Yea the flame of his fire shall not burn.
Job18:6 The light shall be dark in his dwelling
Job18:7 His presumptious goings shall be kept in
Job18:8 For his feet shall be taken in the net
Job18:9 His foot shall be holden in the gilder
Job18:10 The snare is laid for him in the ground
Job18:11 Fearfulness shall make him afraid on every side
Job18:12 Hunger shall be his substance
Job18:13 He shall eat the strength of his skin
Job18:14 All his comfort and hope shall be rooted out of his dwelling
Job18:15 Other men shall dwell in his house (which now is none of his) and brimstone shall be scattered upon his habitation.
Job18:16 His roots shall be dried up beneath
Job18:17 His rememberance shall perish from the earth
Job18:18 he shall be driven from the light into darkness
Job18:19 He shall neither have children nor kinfolks among his people
Job18:20 young and old shall be astonied at his death.
Job18:21 Such are now the dwellings of the wicked
Job19:1 Job answered
Job19:2 How long will ye vex my mind
Job19:3 Lo
Job19:4 If I go wrong
Job19:5 But if ye will enhance yourselves against me
Job19:6 know this then
Job19:7 Behold
Job19:8 He hath hedged up my path
Job19:9 He hath spoiled me of mine honour
Job19:10 He hath destroyed me on every side
Job19:11 His wrath is kindled against me
Job19:12 His men of war came together
Job19:13 He hath put my brethren far away from me
Job19:14 Mine own kinfolk have forsaken me
Job19:15 The servants and maids of mine own house take me for a stranger
Job19:16 When I call upon my servant
Job19:17 Mine own wife may not abide my breath
Job19:18 Yea the very desert fools despise me
Job19:19 All such as were my most familiars
Job19:20 My bone hangeth to my skin
Job19:21 Have pity upon me
Job19:22 Why do ye persecute me as God
Job19:23 O that my words were written
Job19:24 would God they were graven with an iron pen in lead or in stone.
Job19:25 For I am sure
Job19:26 that I shall be clothed again with this skin
Job19:27 Yea I my self shall behold him
Job19:28 when ye say: Why do not we persecute him? We have found an occasion against him.
Job19:29 But beware of the sword
Job20:1 Then answered Sophar the Naamathite
Job20:2 For the same cause do my thoughts compel me to answer. And why? My mind is tossed here and there.
Job20:3 I have sufficiently heard thy checking and reproof
Job20:4 Knowest thou not this
Job20:5 the praise of the ungodly hath been short
Job20:6 Though he be magnified up to the heaven
Job20:7 yet he perish at the last like dung: In so much that they which have seen him
Job20:8 He vanisheth as a dream
Job20:9 So that the eye which saw him before
Job20:10 His children go a begging
Job20:11 From his youth his bones are full of vice
Job20:12 When wickedness is sweet in his mouth
Job20:13 That he favoureth
Job20:14 The meat that he eateth
Job20:15 The riches that he devoureth
Job20:16 he shall suck the serpent's head
Job20:17 so that he shall no more see the rivers
Job20:18 But labour shall he
Job20:19 And why? he hath oppressed the poor
Job20:20 His belly could never be filled
Job20:21 He devoured so greedily
Job20:22 Though he had plenteousness of everything
Job20:23 For though the wicked have never so much to fill his belly
Job20:24 so that if he flee the iron weapons
Job20:25 The arrow shall be taken forth
Job20:26 There shall no darkness be able to hide him. And unkindled fire shall consume him
Job20:27 The heaven shall declare his wickedness
Job20:28 The substance that he hath in his house
Job20:29 This is the portion that the wicked shall have of God
Job21:1 Job answered
Job21:2 O hear my words
Job21:3 Suffer me a little
Job21:4 Is it with a man
Job21:5 Mark me well
Job21:6 For when I ponder and consider this
Job21:7 Wherefore do wicked men live in health and prosperity
Job21:8 Their children's children live in their sight
Job21:9 Their houses are safe from all fear
Job21:10 Their bullock gendereth
Job21:11 They send forth their children by flocks
Job21:12 They bear with them taberetts and harps
Job21:13 They spend their days in wealthiness: but suddenly they go down to hell.
Job21:14 They say unto God: go from us
Job21:15 What manner of fellow is the Almighty
Job21:16 Lo
Job21:17 How oft shall the candle of the wicked be put out? How oft cometh their destruction upon them? Oh what sorrow shall God give them for their part in his wrath.
Job21:18 Yea they shall be even as chaff before the wind
Job21:19 And though God save their children from such sorrow
Job21:20 Their own destruction and misery shall they see with their eyes
Job21:21 For what careth he
Job21:22 In as much then as God hath the highest(hyest) power of all
Job21:23 One dieth now when he is mighty and at his best
Job21:24 even when his bowels are at the fattest
Job21:25 An other dieth in sorrow and heaviness
Job21:26 Now sleep they both alike in the earth
Job21:27 But I know what ye think
Job21:28 For ye say: Where is the prince's palace? where is the dwelling of the ungodly:
Job21:29 Ask any man that goeth by the way
Job21:30 that the wicked is kept unto the day of destruction
Job21:31 Who dare reprove him for his ways to his face? Who rewardeth him for the ungraciousness that he doth?
Job21:32 Yet shall he be brought to his grave
Job21:33 Then shall he be fain to be buried among the stones by the brook side. All men must follow him
Job21:34 O how vain is the comfort that ye give me? Are not your answers clean contrary to right and truth?
Job22:1 So Eliphas the Themanite gave answer
Job22:2 May a man be compared unto God in wisdom
Job22:3 What pleasure hath God in that thou art righteous? Or what doth it profit him
Job22:4 Is he afraid to reprove thee
Job22:5 Cometh not this for that(the) great wickedness
Job22:6 Thou hast taken the pledge from thy brethren for naught
Job22:7 To such as were weary
Job22:8 Should such one then as useth violence
Job22:9 Thou hast sent widows away empty and oppressed the poor fatherless.
Job22:10 Therefore art thou compassed about with snares on every side
Job22:11 Shouldest thou then see no darkness? Should not the water flood run over thee?
Job22:12 Now because that God is higher than the heavens
Job22:13 wilt thou therefore say: Tush
Job22:14 Tush
Job22:15 Well
Job22:16 both old and young
Job22:17 which sayeth unto God: go from us
Job22:18 where as he (notwithstanding) filleth their houses with all good. Which meaning of the ungodly be far from me.
Job22:19 For with joy shall the godly
Job22:20 that their increase shall be hewn down
Job22:21 Therefore reconcile thee unto God
Job22:22 Receive the law at his mouth
Job22:23 For if thou wilt turn to the Almighty
Job22:24 He shall give thee an harvest
Job22:25 Yea the Almighty his own self shall be thine harvest
Job22:26 Then shalt thou have thy delight(delyte) in the Almighty
Job22:27 Then shalt thou make thy prayer unto him
Job22:28 Then
Job22:29 For who so humbleth himself
Job22:30 If thou be innocent
Job23:1 Job answered
Job23:2 My saying is yet this day in bitterness
Job23:3 O that I might see him and find him: O that I might come before his seat
Job23:4 to plead my cause before him
Job23:5 That I might know
Job23:6 Will he plead against me with his great power and strength
Job23:7 Oh no
Job23:8 For though I go before
Job23:9 If I go on the left side to ponder his works
Job23:10 But as for my way
Job23:11 Nevertheless my feet keep his path
Job23:12 I have not forsaken the commandment of his lips
Job23:13 It is he him self alone
Job23:14 He rewardeth me in to my bosom
Job23:15 This is the cause
Job23:16 For in so much as he is God
Job23:17 Thus can not I get out of darkness
Job24:1 Considering then that there is no time hid from the Almighty
Job24:2 For some men there be
Job24:3 that drive away the ass of the fatherless: that take the widow's ox for a pledge:
Job24:4 that thrust the poor out of the way
Job24:5 Behold
Job24:6 They reap the corn field that is not their own: and gather the grapes out of his vineyard
Job24:7 They are the cause that so many men are naked and bare
Job24:8 So that when the showers in the mountains have rained upon them
Job24:9 They spoil the sucking fatherless children
Job24:10 In so much that they let them go naked without clothing
Job24:11 The poor are fain to labour in their oil mills
Job24:12 The whole city crieth unto the Lord(LORDE) with sighing
Job24:13 where as they (notwithstanding) are rebellious and disobedient enemies: which seek not his light and way
Job24:14 Timely in the morning do they arise
Job24:15 The eye of the ungodly is like the advouterer
Job24:16 In the night season they search the houses
Job24:17 For as soon as the day breaketh
Job24:18 The ungodly is very swift: O that his portion also upon earth were swifter than the running water
Job24:19 O that they (for the wickedness which they have done) were drawn to the hell
Job24:20 O that all compassion upon them were forgotten: that their dainties were worms
Job24:21 For they maintain the barren
Job24:22 They pluck down the mighty with their power
Job24:23 And though they might be safe
Job24:24 They are exalted for a little
Job24:25 Is it not so? Who will then reprove me as a liar
Job25:1 Then answered Baldad the Suhite
Job25:2 Power and fear is with him above
Job25:3 whose men of war(warre) are innumerable
Job25:4 But how may a man compared unto God be justified? Or
Job25:5 Behold
Job25:6 How much more than man
Job26:1 Job answered
Job26:2 O how helpest thou the weak? what comfort givest thou unto him that hath no strength?
Job26:3 Where is the counsel that thou shouldest give him
Job26:4 Before whom hast thou spoken those words? Who made the breath to come out of the mouth:
Job26:5 The giants and worthies that are slain
Job26:6 yea and all they which dwell beneath in the hell are not hid from him
Job26:7 He stretcheth out the north over the empty
Job26:8 He bindeth the water in his clouds
Job26:9 He holdeth back his stole
Job26:10 He hath compassed the waters with certain bounds
Job26:11 The very pillars of heaven tremble and quake at his reproof.
Job26:12 He filleth the sea with his power
Job26:13 With his spirit hath he garnished the heavens
Job26:14 This is now a short sum of his doings. But who is able sufficiently to rehearse his works? Who can perceive and understand the thunder of his power.
Job27:1 And Job(Iob also) proceeded and went(wete) forth in his communication
Job27:2 As truly as God liveth (which hath taken away my power from me) and the Almighty
Job27:3 My lips shall talk of no vanity
Job27:4 while my breath is in me
Job27:5 God forbid
Job27:6 My righteous dealing keep I fast
Job27:7 Therefore mine enemy shall be found as the ungodly
Job27:8 What hope hath the hypocrite
Job27:9 Doth God hear him the sooner
Job27:10 Hath he such pleasure and delight in the Almighty
Job27:11 I will teach you in the name of God? and the thing that I have of the Almighty
Job27:12 Behold
Job27:13 saying: This is that portion that the wicked shall have of God
Job27:14 If he get many children
Job27:15 Look whom he leaveth behind him
Job27:16 Though he have as much money as the dust of the earth
Job27:17 he may well prepare it: but the godly shall put it upon him
Job27:18 His house shall endure as the moth
Job27:19 When the rich man dieth
Job27:20 Destruction taketh hold upon him as a water flood
Job27:21 A vehement wind carrieth him hence
Job27:22 It rusheth in upon him
Job27:23 Then clap men their hands at him
Job28:1 There are places were silver is molten
Job28:2 where iron is digged out of the ground
Job28:3 The darkness shall once come to an end
Job28:4 With the river of water parteth he a sunder the strange people
Job28:5 He bringeth food out of the earth
Job28:6 There is found a place
Job28:7 There is a way also that the birds know not
Job28:8 wherein the proud and high minded walk not
Job28:9 There putteth he his hand upon the stony rocks
Job28:10 Rivers flow out of the rocks
Job28:11 Out of drops bringeth he great floods together
Job28:12 How cometh a man then by wisdom? Where is the place that men find understanding?
Job28:13 Verily no man can tell how worthy a thing she is
Job28:14 The deep sayeth: she is not in me. The sea sayeth: she is not with me.
Job28:15 She can not be gotten for the most fine gold
Job28:16 No wedges of gold of Ophir
Job28:17 No
Job28:18 There is nothing so worthy
Job28:19 The Topaz that cometh out of Inde
Job28:20 From where then cometh wisdom? and where is the place of understanding?
Job28:21 She is hid from the eyes of all men
Job28:22 Destruction and death say: We have heard tell of her with our ears.
Job28:23 But God seeth her way
Job28:24 For he holdeth the ends of the world and looketh upon all that is under the heaven.
Job28:25 When he weighed the winds
Job28:26 When he set the rain in order
Job28:27 Then did he see her
Job28:28 And unto man he said: Behold
Job29:1 So Job proceeded and went forth in his communication
Job29:2 Oh that I were as I was in the months by past
Job29:3 When his light shined upon my head: when I went after the same light and shining even thorow the darkness.
Job29:4 As it stood with me
Job29:5 When the Almighty was with me: when my household folks stood about me:
Job29:6 When my ways ran over with butter
Job29:7 When I went thorow the city unto the gate
Job29:8 When the young men (as soon as they saw me) hid themselves
Job29:9 when the princes left of their talking
Job29:10 when the mighty kept still their voice
Job29:11 When all they that heard me
Job29:12 For I delivered the poor when he cried
Job29:13 He that should have been lost
Job29:14 And why? I put upon me righteousness
Job29:15 I was an eye unto the blind
Job29:16 I was a father unto the poor
Job29:17 I brake the chafes of the unrighteous
Job29:18 Therefore
Job29:19 For my root was spread out by the water's side
Job29:20 My honor increased(encreased) more and more
Job29:21 Unto me men gave ear
Job29:22 If I had spoken
Job29:23 They waited for me
Job29:24 When I laughed
Job29:25 When I agreed unto their way
Job30:1 But now they that are mine inferiors and younger than I
Job30:2 The power and strength of their hands might do me no good
Job30:3 For very misery and hunger
Job30:4 plucking up herbs from among the bushes
Job30:5 And when they were driven forth
Job30:6 Their dwelling was beside foul brooks
Job30:7 Upon the dry heath went they about crying
Job30:8 They were children of fools and villains
Job30:9 Now am I their song
Job30:10 they abhor me
Job30:11 for the Lord(LORDE) hath opened his quiver
Job30:12 Upon my right hand they rose together against me
Job30:13 and my path have they clean marred. It was so easy for them to do me harm
Job30:14 They fell upon me
Job30:15 Fearfulness is turned against me. Mine honour vanisheth away more swiftly than wind
Job30:16 Therefore is my mind poured full of heaviness
Job30:17 My bones are pierced thorow in the night season
Job30:18 With all their power have they changed my garment
Job30:19 I am even as it were clay
Job30:20 When I cry unto thee
Job30:21 Thou art become mine enemy
Job30:22 In times past thou didst set me up on high
Job30:23 Sure I am
Job30:24 Now use not me to do violence unto them
Job30:25 Did not I weep in the time of trouble? Had not my soul compassion upon the poor?
Job30:26 Yet nevertheless where as I looked for good
Job30:27 My bowels seeth within me
Job30:28 Meekly and lowly came I in
Job30:29 But now I am a companion of dragons
Job30:30 My skin upon me is turned to black
Job30:31 my harp is turned to sorrow
Job31:1 I made a covenant with mine eyes
Job31:2 For how great a portion shall I have of God from above? and what inheritance from the almighty on high?(Almightie on hie)
Job31:3 As for the ungodly and he that joineth himself to the company of wicked doers shall not destruction and misery come upon him?
Job31:4 Doth not he see my ways
Job31:5 If I have cleaved unto vanity
Job31:6 Let me be weighed in an even balance
Job31:7 If so be that I have withdrawn my foot out of the right way
Job31:8 O then is it reason that I sow
Job31:9 If my heart hath lusted after my neighbour's wife
Job31:10 O then let my wife be another man's harlot and let other lie with her.
Job31:11 For this is a wickedness and sin
Job31:12 yea a fire that utterly should consume
Job31:13 Did I ever think scorn to do right unto my servants and maidens
Job31:14 But seeing that God will sit in judgement what shall I do? And for so much as he will needs visit me
Job31:15 He that fashioned me in my mother's womb
Job31:16 When the poor desireth anything at me
Job31:17 Have I eaten my portion alone
Job31:18 For mercy grew up with me from my youth
Job31:19 Have I seen any man perish thorow nakedness and want of clothing? Or any poor man for lack of raiment
Job31:20 whose sides thanked me not
Job31:21 Did I ever lift up my hand to hurt the fatherless? Yea in the gates where I saw myself to be in authority:
Job31:22 then let mine arm fall from my shoulder
Job31:23 For I have ever feared the vengeance and punishment of God
Job31:24 Have I put my trust in gold? Or
Job31:25 Have I rejoiced because my substance was great
Job31:26 Did I ever greatly regard the rising of the son? Or
Job31:27 Hath my heart meddled privily with any deceit? Or
Job31:28 That were a wickedness worth to be punished
Job31:29 Have I ever rejoiced at the hurt of mine enemy? Or was I ever glad
Job31:30 I never suffered my mouth to do such a sin
Job31:31 Yet they of mine own household say: who shall let us to have our belly full of his flesh?
Job31:32 I have not suffered a stranger to lie without
Job31:33 Have I ever done any wicked deed where thorow I shamed myself before men: or any abomination
Job31:34 For if I had feared any great multitude of people: or if I had been despised of the simple
Job31:35 O that I had one which would hear me. Lo
Job31:36 Then shall I take it upon my shoulder
Job31:37 I have told the number of my goings
Job31:38 But if cause be that my land cry against me
Job31:39 if I have eaten the fruits thereof unpaid for
Job31:40 then let thistles grow in stead of my wheat
Job32:1 So these three men would strive no more with Job
Job32:2 But Eliu the son of Barachell the Bussite of the kindred of Ram
Job32:3 And with Job's three friends he was angry also
Job32:4 Now tarried Eliu till they had ended their communication with Job
Job32:5 So when Eliu the son of Barachell the Bussite saw
Job32:6 so that he gave answer himself
Job32:7 for I thought thus within myself: It becometh old men to speak
Job32:8 Every man (no doubt) hath a mind
Job32:9 All men are not wise
Job32:10 Therefore will I speak also (in so far as I may be heard) and will shew you mine opinion.
Job32:11 For when I had waited till ye made an end of your talking
Job32:12 Yea when I had diligently pondered what ye said
Job32:13 Lest ye should praise yourselves
Job32:14 Nevertheless
Job32:15 (for they where so abashed that they could not make answer
Job32:16 but in so much as ye will not speak
Job32:17 I have a good hope for my part to shape him an answer
Job32:18 For I am full of words
Job32:19 Behold I am as the new wine
Job32:20 Therefore will I speak
Job32:21 I will regard no manner of person
Job32:22 For if I would go about to please men

Job33:2 Behold
Job33:3 My heart shall order my words aright
Job33:4 The spirit of God hath made me
Job33:5 If thou canst
Job33:6 Behold
Job33:7 Therefore
Job33:8 Now hast thou spoken in mine ears
Job33:9 I am clean without any fault
Job33:10 But lo
Job33:11 He hath put my foot in the stocks
Job33:12 Behold
Job33:13 Why dost thou then strive against him? because he giveth thee no accomptes of all his doings?
Job33:14 For when God doth once command a thing
Job33:15 In dreams and visions of the night season (When slumbering cometh upon men
Job33:16 he roundeth(rowneth) them in the ears
Job33:17 that it is he
Job33:18 keepeth his soul from destruction
Job33:19 He chasteneth him with sickness
Job33:20 so that his life may away with no bread
Job33:21 In so much that his body is clean consumed away
Job33:22 His soul draweth on to destruction
Job33:23 Now if there be a messenger(angel) (one among a thousand) sent for to speak unto man
Job33:24 then the Lord(LORDE) is merciful unto him
Job33:25 Then his flesh (which hath been in misery and trouble) shall be as it was in his youth.
Job33:26 For if he submit himself unto God
Job33:27 Such a respect hath he unto men. Therefore let a man confess (and say) I offended
Job33:28 Yea he hath delivered my soul from destruction
Job33:29 Lo
Job33:30 that he keepeth his soul from perishing

Job33:32 But if thou hast any thing to say
Job33:33 If thou hast nothing
Job34:1 Eliu proceeding forth in his communication
Job34:2 Hear my words
Job34:3 For like as the mouth tasteth the meats
Job34:4 As for the judgment
Job34:5 And why? Job hath said: I am righteous
Job34:6 I must needs be a liar
Job34:7 where is there such one as Job
Job34:8 Which goeth in company of wicked doers
Job34:9 For he sayeth: Though a man be good
Job34:10 Therefore hearken unto me
Job34:11 but he rewardeth the works of man
Job34:12 For sure it is
Job34:13 Who ruleth the earth in his stead? Or whom hath he set to govern the whole world?
Job34:14 To whom hath he given his heart
Job34:15 All flesh shall come together unto naught
Job34:16 If thou now have understanding
Job34:17 May he be made whole
Job34:18 For he is even the same
Job34:19 Ungodly men are ye. He hath none respect unto the persons of the lordly
Job34:20 In the twinkling of an eye shall they be slain: and at midnight
Job34:21 And why? his eyes look upon the ways of man
Job34:22 There is no darkness nor thick shadow
Job34:23 For no man shall be suffered to go into judgement with God.
Job34:24 Many one
Job34:25 For he knoweth their evil and dark works
Job34:26 They that were in the stead of Sears
Job34:27 Therefore turned they back traitorously and unfaithfully from him
Job34:28 In so much that they have caused the voice of the poor to come unto him
Job34:29 If he deliver and grant pardon
Job34:30 From the wickedness and sin of the people
Job34:31 For so much as I have begun to talk of God
Job34:32 If I have gone amiss
Job34:33 Wilt thou not give a reasonable answer? Art thou afraid of anything
Job34:34 For else the men of understanding and wisdom
Job34:35 As for Job he hath neither spoken to the purpose nor wisely.
Job34:36 O father let Job be well tried
Job34:37 yea above his sins he hath blasphemed
Job35:1 Eliu spake moreover
Job35:2 Thinkest thou it right
Job35:3 Seeing thou sayest so
Job35:4 Therefore will I give answer unto thee and thy friends:
Job35:5 Look unto the heaven
Job35:6 If thou sinnest
Job35:7 If thou be righteous
Job35:8 Of such an ungodly person as thou
Job35:9 there is a great cry and complaint made by them that are oppressed with violence
Job35:10 For such one never sayeth: Where is God that made me? and that shineth upon us
Job35:11 Which giveth us more understanding than he doth the beasts of the earth
Job35:12 If any such complain
Job35:13 But if a man call upon God
Job35:14 When thou speakest then
Job35:15 Then useth he no violence in his wrath
Job35:16 Therefore hath Job opened his mouth but in vain
Job36:1 Eliu proceeded forth in his talking and said:
Job36:2 Hold thee still a little
Job36:3 I will open unto thee yet more of mine understanding
Job36:4 True are my words
Job36:5 Behold
Job36:6 As for the ungodly
Job36:7 He turneth not his eyes away from the righteous
Job36:8 But if they be laid in prison and chains
Job36:9 then sheweth he them their works and deeds and the sins where with they have used cruel violence.
Job36:10 He with punishing and nurturing of them
Job36:11 If they now will take heed and be obedient
Job36:12 But if they will not obey
Job36:13 As for such as be fained
Job36:14 Thus their soul perisheth in foolishness
Job36:15 The poor delivereth he out of his straitness
Job36:16 Even so shall he keep thee (if thou wilt be content) from the bottomless pit that is beneath: and if thou wilt hold the quiet
Job36:17 Nevertheless thou hast condemned the judgement of the ungodly
Job36:18 For then shall not thy cause be stilled with cruelty
Job36:19 Hath God ordained(ordened) then
Job36:20 Prolong not thou the time
Job36:21 But beware that thou turn not aside to wickedness and sin
Job36:22 Behold
Job36:23 Who will reprove him of his way? Who will say unto him: thou hast done wrong?
Job36:24 O consider how great and excellent his works be
Job36:25 yea(yee) wonder at him
Job36:26 Behold
Job36:27 He turneth the water to small drops
Job36:28 together for to rain
Job36:29 He can spread out the clouds (a covering of his tabernacle)
Job36:30 and cause his light to shine upon them
Job36:31 By these things governeth he his people
Job36:32 In the turning of a hand he hideth the light
Job36:33 The rising up thereof sheweth he to his friends and to the cattle.
Job37:1 At this
Job37:2 Hear then the sound of his voice
Job37:3 He governeth everything under the heaven
Job37:4 A roaring voice followeth him: for his glorious majesty giveth such a thunder clap
Job37:5 when God sendeth out his voice: great things doth he
Job37:6 When he commandeth the snow
Job37:7 He sendeth fear upon every man
Job37:8 The beasts creep into their dens
Job37:9 Out of the south cometh the tempest
Job37:10 At the breath of God
Job37:11 The clouds do their labour in getting moistness
Job37:12 He distributeth also on every side
Job37:13 whether it be to punish any land

Job37:15 Art thou of counsel with God
Job37:16 Art thou of his counsel
Job37:17 and how thy clothes are warm

Job38:1 Then spake the LORD unto Job out of the storm
Job38:2 What is he that hideth his mind with foolish words?
Job38:3 Gird up thy loins like a man
Job38:4 Where wast thou
Job38:5 Who hath measured it
Job38:6 Where upon stand(stode) the pillars of it? Or
Job38:7 Where wast thou
Job38:8 Who shut the sea with doors
Job38:9 When I made the clouds to be a covering for it
Job38:10 When I gave it my commandment
Job38:11 saying: Hither to shalt thou come
Job38:12 Hast thou given the morning his charge (as soon as thou wast born) and shewed the day spring his place
Job38:13 that it might take hold of the corners of the earth
Job38:14 Their tokens and weapons hast thou turned like clay
Job38:15 Yea thou hast spoiled the ungodly of their light
Job38:16 Camest thou ever into the ground of the sea: or hast thou walked in the low corners of the deep?
Job38:17 Have the gates of death been open unto thee
Job38:18 Hast thou also perceived how broad the earth is? Now if thou hast knowledge of all
Job38:19 then shew me where light dwelleth
Job38:20 that thou mayest bring us unto their quarters
Job38:21 Knowest thou (when thou wast born) how old thou shouldest be?
Job38:22 Wentest thou ever into the treasures of the snow? or hast thou seen the secret places of the hail
Job38:23 which I have prepared against the time of trouble
Job38:24 By what way is the light parted
Job38:25 Who divideth the abundance of waters into rivers
Job38:26 that it watereth and moistureth the dry and barren ground:
Job38:27 to make the grass grow in places where no body dwelleth
Job38:28 Who is the father of rain? Or who hath begotten the drops of dew?
Job38:29 Out of whose womb came the ice? Who hath gendered the coldness of the air?
Job38:30 That the waters are as hard as stones
Job38:31 Hast thou brought the seven stars together? Or art thou able to break the Circle of heaven?
Job38:32 Canst thou bring forth the morning star or the evening star at convenient time
Job38:33 Knewest thou the course of heaven
Job38:34 Moreover
Job38:35 Canst thou thunder also that they may go their way
Job38:36 Who giveth sure wisdom or steadfast understanding?
Job38:37 Who numbereth the clouds in wisdom? Who stilleth the vehement waters of heaven?
Job38:38 Who turneth the clots to dust
Job38:39 Huntest thou the prey from the lion
Job38:40 or feedest thou his whelps living in their dens
Job38:41 Who provideth meat for the raven
Job39:1 Knowest thou the time when the wild goats bring forth their young among the stony rocks? Or layest thou wait when the hinds use to fawn?
Job39:2 Reckonest thou the months after they engender
Job39:3 Or when they lie down when they cast their young ones
Job39:4 How their young ones grow up and wax great thorow good feeding?
Job39:5 who letteth the wild ass go free
Job39:6 Unto whom I have given the wilderness to be their house
Job39:7 That they may give no force for the multitude of people in the cities
Job39:8 but (to) seek their pasture about the mountains and (to) follow (upon) the green grass.
Job39:9 Will the unicorn be so tame as to do the service
Job39:10 Canst thou bind the yoke about him in thy furrows
Job39:11 Mayest thou trust him (because he is strong) or commit thy labour unto him?
Job39:12 Mayest thou believe him
Job39:13 The Ostrich (whose feathers are fairer than the wings of the sparrow hawk)
Job39:14 when he hath laid his eggs upon the ground
Job39:15 and forgetteth them: so that they might be trodden upon with feet
Job39:16 So hard is he to his young ones
Job39:17 And that because God hath taken wisdom from him
Job39:18 When his time is
Job39:19 Hast thou given the horse his strength? or learned him to bow down his neck with fear:
Job39:20 that he letteth himself be driven forth like a grasshopper
Job39:21 he breaketh the ground with the hoofs of his feet cheerfully in his strength
Job39:22 He layeth aside all fear
Job39:23 Though the quivers rattle upon him
Job39:24 yet rusheth he in fearlessly
Job39:25 but as soon as he heareth the shawmes blow
Job39:26 Cometh it thorow thy wisdom
Job39:27 Doth the Aegle mount up
Job39:28 He abideth in the stony rocks
Job39:29 From thence may he behold his pray
Job39:30 His young ones are fed with blood
Job40:1 Moreover
Job40:2 Can he that striveth with the almighty
Job40:3 Job answered the LORD
Job40:4 Behold
Job40:5 Once or twice have I spoken
Job40:6 Then spake the LORD unto Job out of the storm
Job40:7 Gird up thy loins like a man
Job40:8 Wilt thou disannul my judgment? Or wilt thou condemn me
Job40:9 Is thine arm then like the arm of God? Maketh thy voice such a sound as his doth?
Job40:10 Then arm thyself with thine own power
Job40:11 pour out thy indignation of thy wrath: see that thou cast down all the proud
Job40:12 look well
Job40:13 cast them down into the mire
Job40:14 Then will I confess also
Job40:15 Behold Behemoth(the cruell beaste) (whom I made with thee) which eateth hay as an ox:
Job40:16 lo
Job40:17 He spreadeth out his tail like a Cedar tree
Job40:18 His shins are like pipes of brass
Job40:19 First when God made him
Job40:20 that the mountains should give him grass
Job40:21 He lieth among the reeds in the Mosses
Job40:22 the fens hide him with their shadow
Job40:23 Lo
Job40:24 Who dare lay a hand on him openly
Job41:1 Darest thou draw out Leviathan with an angle? or bind his tongue with a snare?
Job41:2 Canst thou put a ring in the nose of him? or bore his chaftes thorow with a nawl?(naule)
Job41:3 Will he make many fair words with thee (thinkest thou) or flatter thee?
Job41:4 Will he make a covenant with thee? Or art thou able for to compel him to do thee continual service?
Job41:5 Wilt thou take thy pastime with him as with a bird? Or give him unto thy maidens
Job41:6 that thy companions may hew him in pieces
Job41:7 Canst thou fill the net with his skin
Job41:8 Darest thou lay hand upon him? It is better for thee to consider what harm might happen thee there thorow
Job41:9 For when thou thinkest to have hold upon him
Job41:10 And why? there dare none be so bold as to raise him up. Who is able to stand before me?
Job41:11 Or
Job41:12 I fear him not
Job41:13 Who lifteth him up
Job41:14 Who openeth the doors of his face? for he hath horrible teeth round about
Job41:15 His body is covered with scales as it were with shields
Job41:16 One is so joined to another
Job41:17 Yea one hangeth so upon another
Job41:18 His nesing is like a glistering fire
Job41:19 Out of his mouth go torches and fire brands
Job41:20 out of his nostrils there goeth smoke
Job41:21 His breath maketh the coals burn
Job41:22 In his neck remaineth strength
Job41:23 The members of his body are joined so strait one to another
Job41:24 His heart is as hard as stone
Job41:25 When he goeth: the mightiest of all are afraid
Job41:26 If he draw out the sword
Job41:27 He sitteth as much by a straw as by iron
Job41:28 He starteth not away for him that bendeth the bow: and as for sling stones
Job41:29 He counteth the hammer no better than a straw
Job41:30 He treadeth the gold in the mire like the sharp potsherds.
Job41:31 He maketh the deep to seethe and boil like a pot
Job41:32 The way is light after him
Job41:33 Upon earth is there no power like unto his
Job41:34 If a man will consider all high things
Job42:1 Then Job answered the LORD
Job42:2 I know that thou hast power over(of) all things
Job42:3 For who can keep his own counsel(councell) so secret
Job42:4 O hearken thou unto me also
Job42:5 I have given diligent ear unto thee
Job42:6 Wherefore I give mine own self the blame
Job42:7 Now when the LORD had spoken these words unto Job
Job42:8 Therefore take seven oxen and seven rams
Job42:9 So Eliphas the Themanite
Job42:10 and the LORD turned him unto Job
Job42:11 And then came there unto him all his brethren
Job42:12 And the LORD made Job richer than he was before: for he had fourteen thousand sheep
Job42:13 He had children also
Job42:14 The first (he) called Day: the second Poverty: the third All plenteousness.
Job42:15 In all the land were none found so fair
Job42:16 After this lived Job forty years
Job42:17 And so he died

Psalms1:3 Such a man is like a tree planted by the water side
Psalms1:4 As for the ungodly
Psalms1:5 Therefore the ungodly shall not be able to stand in the judgment
Psalms1:6 For the LORD alloweth the way of the righteous

Psalms5:7 But as for me
Psalms5:8 Lead me (O LORD) in thy righteousness because of mine enemies
Psalms5:9 For there is no faithfulness in their mouths: they dissemble in their hearts: their throat is an open sepulchre: with their tongues they deceive.

Psalms5:12 For thou LORD givest thy blessing unto the righteous: and with thy favourable kindness thou defendest him

Psalms18:1 To the chanter of David
Psalms18:2 The LORD is my succor
Psalms18:3 I will praise the LORD
Psalms18:4 The sorrows of death compassed me
Psalms18:5 The pains of hell came about me
Psalms18:6 Yet in my trouble I called upon the LORD
Psalms18:7 Then the earth trembled and quaked
Psalms18:8 There went a smoke out of his nostrils
Psalms18:9 He bowed the heavens
Psalms18:10 He rode upon the Cherub
Psalms18:11 He made darkness his pavilion round about him
Psalms18:12 At the brightness of his presence the clouds removed
Psalms18:13 The LORD also thundered out of the heaven
Psalms18:14 He sent out his arrows and scattered them
Psalms18:15 The springs of waters were seen
Psalms18:16 He sent down from the height(heyth) to fetch me
Psalms18:17 He delivered me from my strong enemies
Psalms18:18 They prevented me in the time of my trouble
Psalms18:19 He brought me forth also into liberty: and delivered me
Psalms18:20 The LORD shall reward me after my righteous dealing
Psalms18:21 For I have kept the ways of the LORD: and have not behaved myself wickedly against my God.
Psalms18:22 I have an eye unto all his laws
Psalms18:23 Uncorrupt will I be before him
Psalms18:24 Therefore shall the LORD reward me after my righteous dealing
Psalms18:25 With the holy thou shalt be holy
Psalms18:26 With the clean thou shalt be clean
Psalms18:27 For thou shalt save the poor oppressed
Psalms18:28 Thou lightest my candle
Psalms18:29 For in thee I can discomfort an host of men: yea in my God I can leap over the wall.
Psalms18:30 The way of God is a perfect way: the words of the LORD are tried in the fire: he is a shield of defense
Psalms18:31 For who is God
Psalms18:32 It is God that hath girded me with strength
Psalms18:33 He hath made my feet like hart's feet
Psalms18:34 He teacheth my hands to fight
Psalms18:35 Thou hast given me the defense of thy health
Psalms18:36 Thou hast made room enough under me for to go
Psalms18:37 I will follow upon mine enemies
Psalms18:38 I will smite them
Psalms18:39 Thou hast girded me with strength unto the battle
Psalms18:40 Thou hast made mine enemies to turn their backs upon me
Psalms18:41 They cried
Psalms18:42 I will beat them as small as the dust clay in(before) the wind
Psalms18:43 Thou shalt deliver me from the strivings of the people
Psalms18:44 As soon as they hear of me
Psalms18:45 The strange children are waxen old
Psalms18:46 The LORD liveth: and blessed be my helper
Psalms18:47 Even the God which seeth that I be avenged
Psalms18:48 It is he that delivereth me from my cruel enemies: thou shalt lift me up from them that rise against me
Psalms18:49 For this cause I will give thanks unto thee (O LORD) among the gentiles
Psalms18:50 Great prosperity giveth he unto his king

Psalms66:5 O come hither and behold the works of God
Psalms66:6 He turned the sea into dry land
Psalms66:7 He ruleth with his power for ever
Psalms66:8 O magnify our God
Psalms66:9 Which holdeth our soul in life
Psalms66:10 For thou
Psalms66:11 Thou hast brought us into captivity
Psalms66:12 Thou hast suffered men to ride over our heads
Psalms66:13 Therefore will I go into thy house with brent offerings
Psalms66:14 which I promised with my lips

Psalms66:18 If I incline unto wickedness with my heart
Psalms66:19 Therefore God hath heard me

Psalms69:1 To the chanter upon Sosanim of David. Help me
Psalms69:2 I stick fast in the deep mire
Psalms69:3 I am weary of crying
Psalms69:4 They that hate me without a cause
Psalms69:5 God thou knowest my simpleness
Psalms69:6 Let not them that trust in thee
Psalms69:7 And why? for thy sake do I suffer reproof
Psalms69:8 I am become a stranger unto my brethren
Psalms69:9 For the zeal of thine house hath even eaten me
Psalms69:10 I wept and chastened myself with fasting
Psalms69:11 I put on a sackcloth
Psalms69:12 They that sat in the gate
Psalms69:13 But LORD I made my prayer unto thee in an acceptable time. Hear me
Psalms69:14 Take me out of the mire
Psalms69:15 Lest the water flood drown me
Psalms69:16 Hear me
Psalms69:17 Hide not thy face from thy servant
Psalms69:18 Draw nigh unto my soul
Psalms69:19 Thou knowest my reproof
Psalms69:20 The rebuke breaketh my heart
Psalms69:21 They gave me gall to eat
Psalms69:22 Let their table be made a snare to take themselves withal
Psalms69:23 Let their eyes be blinded
Psalms69:24 Pour out thine indignation upon them
Psalms69:25 Let their habitation be void
Psalms69:26 For they persecute him whom thou hast smitten
Psalms69:27 Let them fall from one wickedness to another
Psalms69:28 Let them be wiped out of the book of the living
Psalms69:29 As for me
Psalms69:30 That I may praise the name of God with a song
Psalms69:31 This shall please the LORD better than a bullock
Psalms69:32 O consider this and be glad (ye that be in adversity) seek after God and your soul shall live.
Psalms69:33 For the LORD heareth the poor
Psalms69:34 Let heaven and earth praise him
Psalms69:35 For God will save Sion
Psalms69:36 The seed of his servants shall inherit it
Psalms70:1 To the chanter
Psalms70:2 Let them be shamed and confounded that seek after my soul: let them be turned backward

Psalms70:4 But let all those that seek thee
Psalms70:5 As for me
Psalms71:1 In thee
Psalms71:2 but rid me and deliver me thorow thy righteousness: incline thine ear unto me
Psalms71:3 Be thou my stronghold
Psalms71:4 Deliver me
Psalms71:5 For thou
Psalms71:6 I have leaned upon thee ever since I was born
Psalms71:7 I am become a wonder unto the multitude
Psalms71:8 Oh let my mouth be filled with thy praise and honour all the day long.
Psalms71:9 Cast me not away in mine old age
Psalms71:10 For mine enemies speak against me
Psalms71:11 saying: God hath forsaken him
Psalms71:12 Go not far from me
Psalms71:13 Let them be confounded and perish
Psalms71:14 As for me
Psalms71:15 My mouth shall speak of thy righteousness and saving health all the day long
Psalms71:16 Let me go in (O Lord GOD)(LORDE GOD) and I will make mention of thy power and righteousness only.
Psalms71:17 Thou
Psalms71:18 Forsake me not
Psalms71:19 Thy righteousness
Psalms71:20 O what great troubles and adversities hast thou shewed me? and yet didst thou turn and refresh me: yea and broughtest me from the deep of the earth again.
Psalms71:21 Thou hast brought me to great honour
Psalms71:22 Therefore will I praise thee and thy faithfulness
Psalms71:23 My lips would fain sing praises unto thee: and so would my soul whom thou hast delivered.
Psalms71:24 My tongue talketh of thy righteousness all the day long
Psalms72:1 Of Solomon Give the king thy judgments
Psalms72:2 That he may govern thy people according to right
Psalms72:3 That the mountains may bring peace
Psalms72:4 He shall keep the simple folk by their right
Psalms72:5 Thou shalt be feared as long as the sun and moon endureth
Psalms72:6 He shall come down like the rain in to a fleece of wool
Psalms72:7 In his time shall righteousness flourish
Psalms72:8 His dominion shall be from the one sea to the other
Psalms72:9 They that dwell in the wilderness shall kneel before him
Psalms72:10 The kings of the sea and of the isles shall bring presents
Psalms72:11 All kings shall worship him
Psalms72:12 For he shall deliver the poor when he crieth
Psalms72:13 He shall be favorable to the simple and poor
Psalms72:14 He shall deliver their souls from extortion and wrong
Psalms72:15 He shall live
Psalms72:16 There shall be an heap of corn in the earth high upon the hills
Psalms72:17 His name shall endure forever
Psalms72:18 Blessed be the LORD God
Psalms72:19 And blessed be the name of his majesty forever
Psalms72:20 Here end the prayers of David the son of Jesse.
Psalms73:1 A Psalm of Asaph. O How loving is God unto Israel
Psalms73:2 Nevertheless my feet were almost gone
Psalms73:3 And why? I was grieved at the wicked
Psalms73:4 For they are in no peril of death
Psalms73:5 They come in no misfortune like other folk
Psalms73:6 And this is the cause that they be so puffed(puft) up in pride
Psalms73:7 Their eyes swell for fatness
Psalms73:8 Corrupt are they
Psalms73:9 They stretch forth their mouth unto the heaven
Psalms73:10 Therefore fall the people unto them
Psalms73:11 Tush (say they) How should God perceive it? is there knowledge in the most highest?
Psalms73:12 Lo
Psalms73:13 Should I then cleanse my heart in vain
Psalms73:14 Wherefore should I be then punished daily
Psalms73:15 Yea I had almost also said even as they do: but lo
Psalms73:16 Then thought I to understand this
Psalms73:17 Until I went into the Sanctuary of God
Psalms73:18 Namely how thou hast set them in a slippery place
Psalms73:19 O how suddenly do they consume
Psalms73:20 Yea even like as a dream when one awaketh
Psalms73:21 Thus my heart was grieved
Psalms73:22 So foolish was I and ignorant
Psalms73:23 Nevertheless I am alway by thee
Psalms73:24 Thou leadest me with thy counsel
Psalms73:25 O what is there prepared for me in heaven? there is no thing upon earth
Psalms73:26 My flesh and my heart faileth
Psalms73:27 For lo
Psalms73:28 But it is good for me
Psalms74:1 An instruction of Asaph O God
Psalms74:2 O think upon thy congregation
Psalms74:3 Tread upon them with thy feet
Psalms74:4 Thine adversaries roar in thy houses
Psalms74:5 Men may see the axes glister above
Psalms74:6 They cut down all the ceiling work of the Sanctuary with bills and axes.
Psalms74:7 They have set fire upon the(thy) Sanctuary
Psalms74:8 Yea they say in their hearts: Let us spoil them all together
Psalms74:9 We see our tokens no more
Psalms74:10 Oh God
Psalms74:11 Why withdrawest thou thy hand? Why pluckest thou not thy right hand out of thy bosom
Psalms74:12 But God is my King of old
Psalms74:13 Thou dividest(denydest) the sea thorow thy power
Psalms74:14 Thou smitest the heads of Leviathan in pieces
Psalms74:15 Thou diggest up wells and brooks
Psalms74:16 The day is thine
Psalms74:17 Thou hast set all the borders of the earth
Psalms74:18 Remember this
Psalms74:19 O deliver not the soul of thy turtle dove unto the beasts

Psalms74:21 O let not the simple go away ashamed: for the poor and needy give praises unto thy name.

Psalms75:8 For in the hand of the LORD there is a cup full of strong wine

Psalms77:3 When I was in heaviness
Psalms77:4 Thou heldest mine eyes waking

Psalms77:6 I called to remembrance my song in the night

Psalms77:8 Is his mercy clean gone? Is his promise come utterly to an end for evermore?

Psalms77:10 At the last

Psalms78:1 An instruction of Asaph. Hear my law
Psalms78:2 I will open my mouth in parables
Psalms78:3 Which we have heard and known
Psalms78:4 That we should not hide them from the children of the generations to come: but to shew the honour of the LORD
Psalms78:5 He made a covenant with Jacob
Psalms78:6 That their posterity might know it
Psalms78:7 That they also might put their trust in God
Psalms78:8 And not to be as their forefathers
Psalms78:9 Like as the children of Ephraim
Psalms78:10 They kept not the covenant of God
Psalms78:11 They forgot what he had done
Psalms78:12 Marvelous things did he in the sight of our(their) fathers in the land of Egypt
Psalms78:13 He divided the sea
Psalms78:14 In the daytime he led them with a cloud
Psalms78:15 He clave the hard rocks in the wilderness
Psalms78:16 He brought waters out of the stony rock
Psalms78:17 Yet for all this they sinned against him
Psalms78:18 They tempted God in their hearts
Psalms78:19 For they spake against God
Psalms78:20 Lo
Psalms78:21 When the LORD heard this
Psalms78:22 Because they believed not in God
Psalms78:23 So he commanded the clouds above
Psalms78:24 He rained down manna upon them for to eat
Psalms78:25 Then ate they angels' food
Psalms78:26 He caused the East wind to blow under the heaven
Psalms78:27 He made flesh to rain upon them as thick as dust
Psalms78:28 He let it fall among their tents round about their habitations.
Psalms78:29 So they ate
Psalms78:30 And they were not disappointed(dispointed) of their lust. But while their meat was yet in their mouths:
Psalms78:31 the heavy wrath of God came upon them
Psalms78:32 But for all this they sinned yet more
Psalms78:33 Therefore their days were consumed in vanity
Psalms78:34 When he slew them
Psalms78:35 They thought then that God was their succour
Psalms78:36 Nevertheless they did but flatter him with their mouths
Psalms78:37 For their heart was not whole with him
Psalms78:38 But he was so merciful
Psalms78:39 For he considered that they were but flesh: even a wind that passeth away
Psalms78:40 O how oft have they grieved him in the wilderness? How many a time have they provoked him in the desert?
Psalms78:41 They turned back
Psalms78:42 They thought not of his hand
Psalms78:43 How he had wrought his miracles in Egypt
Psalms78:44 How he turned their waters into blood
Psalms78:45 How he sent lice among them
Psalms78:46 How he gave their fruits unto the caterpillar
Psalms78:47 How he beat(bet) down their vineyards with hail stones
Psalms78:48 How he smote their cattle with hail stones
Psalms78:49 How he sent upon them the furiousness of his wrath
Psalms78:50 When he made a way to his fearful indignation
Psalms78:51 When he smote all the firstborn in Egypt
Psalms78:52 But as for his own people
Psalms78:53 He brought them out safely
Psalms78:54 He carried them unto the borders of his Sanctuary: even in to this hill
Psalms78:55 He did cast out the Heathen before them
Psalms78:56 For all this they tempted and displeased the most high God
Psalms78:57 But turned their backs
Psalms78:58 And so they grieved him with their high places
Psalms78:59 When God heard this
Psalms78:60 So that he forsook the Tabernacle in Silo
Psalms78:61 He delivered their power into captivity
Psalms78:62 He gave his people over into the sword
Psalms78:63 The fire consumed their young men
Psalms78:64 Their priests were slain with the sword
Psalms78:65 So the Lord(LORDE) awakened as one out of sleep
Psalms78:66 He smote his enemies in the hinder parts
Psalms78:67 He refused the Tabernacle of Joseph
Psalms78:68 Nevertheless
Psalms78:69 And there he builded his temple on high
Psalms78:70 He chose David also his servant
Psalms78:71 As he was following the ewes great with young
Psalms78:72 So he fed them with a faithful and true heart

Psalms79:12 And for the blasphemy wherewith our neighbours have blasphemed thee
Psalms79:13 So we that be the people and sheep of thy pasture

Psalms88:2 Oh let my prayer enter into thy presence
Psalms88:3 For my soul is full of trouble
Psalms88:4 I am counted as one of them that go down into the pit
Psalms88:5 Free among the dead
Psalms88:6 Thou hast laid me in the lowest pit
Psalms88:7 Thy indignation lieth hard upon me
Psalms88:8 Thou hast put away mine acquaintance far from me

Psalms88:11 May thy loving-kindness be shewed in the grave? or thy faithfulness in destruction?
Psalms88:12 May thy wonderous works be known in the dark? or thy righteousness in the land where all things are forgotten?

Psalms88:14 LORD
Psalms88:15 My strength is gone for very sorrow and misery

Psalms88:18 My lovers and friends hast thou put away from me
Psalms89:1 An instruction of Ethan the Ezrahite. My song shall be alway of the loving-kindness of the LORD
Psalms89:2 For I have said: Mercy shall be set up for ever

Psalms89:4 Thy seed will I stablish forever
Psalms89:5 O LORD the very heavens shall praise thy wonderous works
Psalms89:6 For who is he among the clouds
Psalms89:7 God is greatly to be feared in the council of the saints
Psalms89:8 O LORD God of Hosts
Psalms89:9 Thou rulest the pride of the sea
Psalms89:10 Thou breakest the proud
Psalms89:11 The heavens are thine
Psalms89:12 Thou hast made the north and the south
Psalms89:13 Thou hast a mighty arm
Psalms89:14 Righteousness and equity is the habitation of thy seat
Psalms89:15 Blessed is the people
Psalms89:16 Their delight is in thy name all the day long
Psalms89:17 For thou art the glory of their strength
Psalms89:18 The LORD is our defence
Psalms89:19 Thou spakest sometime in visions unto thy saints
Psalms89:20 I have found David my servant
Psalms89:21 My hand shall hold him fast
Psalms89:22 The enemy shall not overcome him
Psalms89:23 I shall smite down his foes before his face
Psalms89:24 My truth also my mercy shall be with him
Psalms89:25 I will set his hand in the sea
Psalms89:26 He shall call me: Thou art my father
Psalms89:27 And I will make him my firstborn
Psalms89:28 My mercy will I keep for him for evermore
Psalms89:29 His seed will I make to endure for ever
Psalms89:30 But if his children forsake my law
Psalms89:31 If they break mine ordinances
Psalms89:32 I will visit their offenses with the rod
Psalms89:33 Nevertheless
Psalms89:34 My covenant will I not break
Psalms89:35 I have sworn once by my holiness

Psalms146:1 Praise the everlasting. Praise the LORD
Psalms146:2 while I live will I praise the LORD: yea as long as I have any being
Psalms146:3 O put not your trust in princes
Psalms146:4 For when the breath of man goeth forth
Psalms146:5 Blessed is he that hath the God of Jacob for his help
Psalms146:6 Which made heaven and earth
Psalms146:7 Which helpeth them to right that suffer wrong
Psalms146:8 the LORD giveth sight to the blind. The LORD helpeth them up that are fallen
Psalms146:9 The LORD careth for the strangers
Psalms146:10 The LORD thy God
Psalms147:1 Praise the everlasting. Praise ye the LORD
Psalms147:2 The LORD shall build up Jerusalem
Psalms147:3 He healeth the contrite in heart
Psalms147:4 He telleth the number of the stars
Psalms147:5 Great is our Lord
Psalms147:6 The LORD setteth up the meek
Psalms147:7 O sing unto the LORD with thanksgiving
Psalms147:8 Which covereth the heaven with clouds
Psalms147:9 Which giveth fodder unto the cattle
Psalms147:10 He hath no pleasure in the strength of an horse
Psalms147:11 But the LORD's delight is in them that fear him
Psalms147:12 Praise the LORD
Psalms147:13 For he maketh fast the bars of thy gates
Psalms147:14 He maketh peace in thy borders
Psalms147:15 He sendeth forth his commandment upon earth
Psalms147:16 He giveth snow like wool
Psalms147:17 He casteth forth his ice like morsels
Psalms147:18 He sendeth out his word and melteth them
Psalms147:19 He sheweth his word unto Jacob
Psalms147:20 He hath not dealt so with all the Heathen

Proverbs1:1 The(These are the) proverbs of Solomon the son of David king of Israel:
Proverbs1:2 To learn wisdom
Proverbs1:3 righteousness
Proverbs1:4 That the very babes might have wit
Proverbs1:5 By hearing
Proverbs1:6 and by experience he shall be more apt to understand a parable
Proverbs1:7 The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom. But fools despise wisdom and instruction.(nurture)
Proverbs1:8 My son
Proverbs1:9 for that shall bring grace unto thy head
Proverbs1:10 My son
Proverbs1:11 if they entice thee
Proverbs1:12 Let us swallow them up like the hell
Proverbs1:13 So shall we find all manner of costly riches
Proverbs1:14 Cast in thy lot among us
Proverbs1:15 My son
Proverbs1:16 For their feet run to evil
Proverbs1:17 But in vain is the net laid forth before the bird's eyes.
Proverbs1:18 Yea they themselves lay wait one for another's blood
Proverbs1:19 These are the ways of all such as be covetous
Proverbs1:20 Wisdom crieth without and putteth forth her(hir) voice in the streets.
Proverbs1:21 She calleth before the congregation in the open gates
Proverbs1:22 O ye children
Proverbs1:23 O turn you unto my correction: lo
Proverbs1:24 Seeing then that I have called
Proverbs1:25 but all my counsels have ye despised and set my corrections to naught.
Proverbs1:26 Therefore shall I also laugh in your destruction
Proverbs1:27 even when the thing that ye be afraid of
Proverbs1:28 Then shall they call upon me
Proverbs1:29 And that because they hated knowledge
Proverbs1:30 but abhorred my counsel
Proverbs1:31 Therefore shall they eat the fruits of their own way
Proverbs1:32 for the turning away of the unwise shall slay them
Proverbs1:33 But who so hearkeneth unto me shall dwell safely
Proverbs2:1 My son
Proverbs2:2 that thine ear may hearken unto wisdom
Proverbs2:3 For if thou criest after wisdom
Proverbs2:4 If thou seekest after her as after money
Proverbs2:5 then shalt thou understand the fear of the LORD

Proverbs2:11 then shall counsel preserve thee

Proverbs2:13 From such as leave the high street
Proverbs2:14 Which rejoice in doing evil

Proverbs2:17 forsaketh the husband of her youth
Proverbs2:18 For her house is inclined unto death
Proverbs2:19 All they that go in unto her
Proverbs2:20 That thou mayest walk in the good way
Proverbs2:21 For the just shall dwell in the land
Proverbs2:22 But the ungodly shall be rooted out of the land
Proverbs3:1 My son
Proverbs3:2 For they shall prolong the days and years of thy life
Proverbs3:3 Let mercy and faithfulness never go from thee: bind them about thy neck
Proverbs3:4 So shalt thou find favor and good understanding in the sight of God and men.
Proverbs3:5 Put thy trust in the LORD with all thine heart
Proverbs3:6 In all thy ways have respect unto him

Proverbs3:26 For the LORD shall be beside thee

Proverbs3:34 As for the scornful
Proverbs3:35 The wise shall have honour in possession

Proverbs10:26 As vinegar is to the teeth

Proverbs10:30 The righteous shall never be overthrown
Proverbs10:31 The mouth of the just will be talking of wisdom
