Proverbs31:1 The(These are the) words of king Lemuel
Proverbs31:2 My son
Proverbs31:3 give not over thy substance and mind unto women
Proverbs31:4 O Lamuel
Proverbs31:5 Lest they being drunken forget the law
Proverbs31:6 Give strong drink unto such as are condemned to death
Proverbs31:7 that they may drink it
Proverbs31:8 Be thou an advocate and stand in judgement thyself
Proverbs31:9 With thy mouth defend the thing that is lawful and right
Proverbs31:10 Aleph. Who so findeth an honest faithful woman
Proverbs31:11 Beth. The heart of her husband may safely trust in her
Proverbs31:12 Gimel. She will do him good and not evil all the days of her life.(She rendereth him good and not evil all the days of her life.)
Proverbs31:13 Daleth. She occupieth wool and flax
Proverbs31:14 He. She is like the merchants' ship
Proverbs31:15 Vav. She is up in the night season
Proverbs31:16 Zain. She considereth land
Proverbs31:17 Heth. She girdeth her loins with strength
Proverbs31:18 Teth. And if she perceiveth that her housewifery doth good
Proverbs31:19 Yod. She layeth her fingers to the spindle
Proverbs31:20 Caph. She openeth her hand to the poor
Proverbs31:21 Lamed. She feareth not that the cold of winter shall hurt her house
Proverbs31:22 Mem. She maketh herself fair ornaments
Proverbs31:23 Nun. Her husband is much set by in the gates
Proverbs31:24 Samech. She maketh cloth of silk and selleth it
Proverbs31:25 Ain. Strength and honour is her clothing
Proverbs31:26 Phe. She openeth her mouth with wisdom
Proverbs31:27 Zade. She looketh well to the ways of her household
Proverbs31:28 Koph. Her children arise
Proverbs31:29 Res. Many daughters there be that gather riches together
Proverbs31:30 Sin. As for favor
Proverbs31:31 Thau. Give her of the fruit of her hands
Ecclesiastes1:1 These are the words of the Preacher
Ecclesiastes1:2 All is but vanity (sayeth the Preacher) all is but plain vanity.
Ecclesiastes1:3 For what else hath a man
Ecclesiastes1:4 One generation passeth away
Ecclesiastes1:5 The Son ariseth
Ecclesiastes1:6 The wind goeth toward the South
Ecclesiastes1:7 All floods run into the sea
Ecclesiastes1:8 All things are so hard
Ecclesiastes1:9 The thing that hath been
Ecclesiastes1:10 Is there any thing
Ecclesiastes1:11 The thing that is past
Ecclesiastes1:12 I myself the Preacher
Ecclesiastes1:13 applied my mind to seek out and search for the knowledge of all things that are done under heaven. Such travail and labor hath God given to the children of men
Ecclesiastes1:14 Thus I have considered all the things that come to pass under the Sun
Ecclesiastes1:15 The crooked can not be made straight
Ecclesiastes1:16 I communed(comoned) with mine own heart
Ecclesiastes1:17 for there unto I applied my mind: that I might know what were wisdom and understanding
Ecclesiastes1:18 For where much wisdom is
Ecclesiastes2:1 Then said I thus in my heart: Now go to
Ecclesiastes2:2 in so much that I said unto laughter: thou art mad
Ecclesiastes2:3 So I thought in my heart
Ecclesiastes2:4 I made gorgeous fair works. I builded me houses
Ecclesiastes2:5 I made me orchards and gardens of pleasure
Ecclesiastes2:6 I made pools of water
Ecclesiastes2:7 I bought servants and maidens
Ecclesiastes2:8 I gathered silver and gold together
Ecclesiastes2:9 Shortly
Ecclesiastes2:10 And look whatsoever mine eyes desired
Ecclesiastes2:11 But when I considered all the works that my hands had wrought
Ecclesiastes2:12 Then turned I me to consider wisdom
Ecclesiastes2:13 And I saw that wisdom excelleth foolishness
Ecclesiastes2:14 For a wise man beareth his eyes about in his head
Ecclesiastes2:15 Then thought I in my mind: If it happeneth unto the fool as it doth unto me
Ecclesiastes2:16 For the wise are ever as little in remembrance as the foolish
Ecclesiastes2:17 Thus began I to be weary of my life
Ecclesiastes2:18 Yea I was weary of all my labour
Ecclesiastes2:19 for who knoweth
Ecclesiastes2:20 So I turned me to refrain my mind from all such travail
Ecclesiastes2:21 For so much as a man should weary himself with wisdom
Ecclesiastes2:22 For what getteth a man of all the labor and travail of his mind
Ecclesiastes2:23 but heaviness
Ecclesiastes2:24 Is it not better then for a man to eat and drink
Ecclesiastes2:25 For who may eat
Ecclesiastes2:26 He giveth unto man
Ecclesiastes3:1 Every thing hath a time
Ecclesiastes3:2 There is a time to be born
Ecclesiastes3:3 A time to slay
Ecclesiastes3:4 A time to weep
Ecclesiastes3:5 A time to cast away stones
Ecclesiastes3:6 A time to win
Ecclesiastes3:7 A time to cut in pieces
Ecclesiastes3:8 A time to love
Ecclesiastes3:9 What hath a man else (that doth anything) but weariness and labour?
Ecclesiastes3:10 For as touching the travail and carefulness which God hath given unto men
Ecclesiastes3:11 All this hath he ordained marvelous goodly
Ecclesiastes3:12 So I perceived
Ecclesiastes3:13 For all that a man eateth and drinketh
Ecclesiastes3:14 I considered also that whatsoever God doth
Ecclesiastes3:15 The thing that hath been
Ecclesiastes3:16 Moreover
Ecclesiastes3:17 Then thought I in my mind: God shall separate the righteous from the ungodly
Ecclesiastes3:18 I communed(comoned) with mine own heart also concerning the children of men: how God hath chosen them
Ecclesiastes3:19 For it happeneth unto men as it doth unto beasts
Ecclesiastes3:20 They go all unto one place
Ecclesiastes3:21 Who knoweth the spirit of man that goeth upward
Ecclesiastes3:22 Wherefore I perceive
Ecclesiastes4:1 So I turned me
Ecclesiastes4:2 Wherefore I judged those that are dead
Ecclesiastes4:3 Yea him that is yet unborn to be better at ease than they both
Ecclesiastes4:4 Again
Ecclesiastes4:5 The fool foldeth his hands together
Ecclesiastes4:6 One handful (sayeth he) is better with rest
Ecclesiastes4:7 Moreover
Ecclesiastes4:8 There is one man
Ecclesiastes4:9 Therefore two are better than one
Ecclesiastes4:10 If one of them fall
Ecclesiastes4:11 Again
Ecclesiastes4:12 One may be overcome
Ecclesiastes4:13 A poor child being wise
Ecclesiastes4:14 Someone cometh out of prison
Ecclesiastes4:15 And I perceived
Ecclesiastes4:16 As for the people that have been before him
Ecclesiastes5:1 When thou comest into the house of God
Ecclesiastes5:2 Be not hasty with thy mouth
Ecclesiastes5:3 For where much carefulness is
Ecclesiastes5:4 If thou make a vow unto God
Ecclesiastes5:5 If thou promise anything
Ecclesiastes5:6 Use not thy mouth to cause thy flesh for to sin
Ecclesiastes5:7 And why? Where as are many dreams and many words
Ecclesiastes5:8 If thou seest the poor to be oppressed and wrongously dealt withal
Ecclesiastes5:9 The whole land also with the fields and all that is therein
Ecclesiastes5:10 He that loveth money
Ecclesiastes5:11 Where as many riches are
Ecclesiastes5:12 A labouring man sleepeth sweetly
Ecclesiastes5:13 Yet is there a sore plague
Ecclesiastes5:14 For oft times they perish with his great misery and trouble: and if he have a child it getteth nothing.
Ecclesiastes5:15 Like as he came naked out of his mother's womb
Ecclesiastes5:16 This is a miserable plague
Ecclesiastes5:17 All the days of his life also must he eat in the dark
Ecclesiastes5:18 Therefore me think it a better and fairer thing
Ecclesiastes5:19 For unto whom soever God giveth riches
Ecclesiastes5:20 For he thinketh not much how long he shall live
Ecclesiastes6:1 There is yet a plague under the Sun
Ecclesiastes6:2 When God giveth a man riches
Ecclesiastes6:3 If a man beget an hundredth children
Ecclesiastes6:4 For he cometh to naught
Ecclesiastes6:5 Moreover
Ecclesiastes6:6 Yea though he lived two thousand years
Ecclesiastes6:7 All the labour that man taketh
Ecclesiastes6:8 For what hath the wise more than the fool? What helpeth it the poor
Ecclesiastes6:9 The sight of the eyes is better
Ecclesiastes6:10 What is more excellent than man? Yet can he not in the law get the victory of him that is mightier than he:
Ecclesiastes6:11 A vain thing is it to cast out many words
Ecclesiastes6:12 For who knoweth what is good for man living
Ecclesiastes7:1 A good name is more worth than a precious ointment
Ecclesiastes7:2 It is better to go into an house of mourning
Ecclesiastes7:3 It is better to be sorry than to laugh
Ecclesiastes7:4 The heart of the wise is in the mourning house
Ecclesiastes7:5 It is better to give ear to the chastening of a wise man
Ecclesiastes7:6 For the laughting of fools is like the crackling of thorns under a pot. And that is but a vain thing.
Ecclesiastes7:7 Who so doeth wrong
Ecclesiastes7:8 The end of a thing is better than the beginning. The patient of spirit is better than the high minded.
Ecclesiastes7:9 Be not hastily angry in thy mind
Ecclesiastes7:10 Say not thou: What is the cause that the days of the old time were better
Ecclesiastes7:11 Wisdom is better than riches
Ecclesiastes7:12 For wisdom defendeth as well as money
Ecclesiastes7:13 Consider the work of God
Ecclesiastes7:14 Use well the time of prosperity and remember the time of misfortune: For God maketh one by the other
Ecclesiastes7:15 These two things also have I considered in the time of vanity: that the just man perisheth for his righteousness' sake
Ecclesiastes7:16 Therefore be thou neither too righteous nor over wise
Ecclesiastes7:17 be neither too unrighteous also nor too foolish
Ecclesiastes7:18 It is good for thee to take hold of this
Ecclesiastes7:19 Wisdom giveth more courage unto the wise
Ecclesiastes7:20 For there is not one just upon earth
Ecclesiastes7:21 Take not heed unto every word that is spoken
Ecclesiastes7:22 For thine own heart knoweth
Ecclesiastes7:23 All these things have I proved because of wisdom: For I thought to be wise
Ecclesiastes7:24 yea and so deep that I might not reach unto her.
Ecclesiastes7:25 I applied my mind also to knowledge
Ecclesiastes7:26 And I found
Ecclesiastes7:27 Behold (sayeth the preacher) this have I diligently searched out and proved
Ecclesiastes7:28 Among a thousand men I have found one
Ecclesiastes7:29 Lo
Ecclesiastes8:1 where as no man hath wisdom and understanding
Ecclesiastes8:2 Keep the king's commandment (I warn thee) and the oath that thou hast made unto God.
Ecclesiastes8:3 Be not hasty to go out of his sight
Ecclesiastes8:4 Like as when a king giveth a charge
Ecclesiastes8:5 Whoso keepeth the commandment
Ecclesiastes8:6 For everything will have opportunity and judgment
Ecclesiastes8:7 And why? A man knoweth not what is for to come
Ecclesiastes8:8 Neither is there any man that hath power over the spirit
Ecclesiastes8:9 All these things have I considered
Ecclesiastes8:10 For I have oft seen the ungodly brought to their graves
Ecclesiastes8:11 Because now that evil works are not hastily punished
Ecclesiastes8:12 But though an evil person offend an hundredth times
Ecclesiastes8:13 Again
Ecclesiastes8:14 Yet is there a vanity upon earth: there be just men
Ecclesiastes8:15 Therefore I commend gladness
Ecclesiastes8:16 When I applied my mind to learn wisdom
Ecclesiastes8:17 I understood of all the works of God
Ecclesiastes9:1 For all these things purposed I in my mind to seek out. The righteous and wise
Ecclesiastes9:2 It happeneth unto one as unto another: It goeth with the righteous as with the ungodly: with the good and clean as with the unclean: with him that offereth as with him that offereth not: like as it goeth with the virtuous
Ecclesiastes9:3 Among all things that come to pass under the Son
Ecclesiastes9:4 And why? As long as a man liveth
Ecclesiastes9:5 for they that be living
Ecclesiastes9:6 so that they be neither loved
Ecclesiastes9:7 Go thou thy way then
Ecclesiastes9:8 Let thy garments be alway white
Ecclesiastes9:9 Use thy self to live joyfully with thy wife whom thou lovest
Ecclesiastes9:10 Whatsoever thou takest in hand to do
Ecclesiastes9:11 So I turned me unto other things under the Son
Ecclesiastes9:12 For man knoweth not his time
Ecclesiastes9:13 This wisdom have I seen also under the Son
Ecclesiastes9:14 There was a little city
Ecclesiastes9:15 And in the city there was found a poor man
Ecclesiastes9:16 Then said I: Wisdom is better than strength. Nevertheless
Ecclesiastes9:17 A wise man's counsel that is followed in silence
Ecclesiastes9:18 For wisdom is better than harness: but one unthrifty alone destroyeth much good.
Ecclesiastes10:1 Dead flies that corrupt sweet ointment and make it to stink
Ecclesiastes10:2 A wise man's heart is upon the right hand
Ecclesiastes10:3 A doting fool thinketh
Ecclesiastes10:4 If a principal spirit be given thee to bear rule
Ecclesiastes10:5 Another plague is there
Ecclesiastes10:6 in that a fool setteth in great dignity
Ecclesiastes10:7 I see servants ride upon horses
Ecclesiastes10:8 But he that diggeth up a pit
Ecclesiastes10:9 Whoso removeth stones shall have travail withal: and he that heweth wood
Ecclesiastes10:10 When an iron is blunt
Ecclesiastes10:11 A babbler of his tongue is no better than a serpent that stingeth without hissing.
Ecclesiastes10:12 The words out of a wise man's mouth are gracious
Ecclesiastes10:13 The beginning of his talking is foolishness
Ecclesiastes10:14 A fool is so full of words
Ecclesiastes10:15 The labour of the foolish is grievous unto them
Ecclesiastes10:16 Woe be unto thee (O thou realm and land) whose king is but a child
Ecclesiastes10:17 But well is thee (O thou realm and land) whose king is come of nobles
Ecclesiastes10:18 Thorow slothfulness the balks fall down
Ecclesiastes10:19 Meat maketh men to laugh
Ecclesiastes10:20 Wish the king no evil in thy thought
Ecclesiastes11:1 Send thy vitailles(victuals) over the waters
Ecclesiastes11:2 Give it away among seven or eight
Ecclesiastes11:3 When the clouds are full
Ecclesiastes11:4 He that regardeth the wind
Ecclesiastes11:5 Now like as thou knowest not the way of the wind
Ecclesiastes11:6 Cease not thou therefore with thy hands to sow thy seed
Ecclesiastes11:7 The light is sweet
Ecclesiastes11:8 If a man live many years
Ecclesiastes11:9 Be glad then (O thou young man) in thy youth
Ecclesiastes11:10 Put away displeasure out of thine heart
Ecclesiastes12:1 Remember thy maker in thy youth
Ecclesiastes12:2 before the sun
Ecclesiastes12:3 when the keepers of the house shall tremble
Ecclesiastes12:4 when the doors in the streets shall be shut
Ecclesiastes12:5 When men shall fear in high places
Ecclesiastes12:6 Or ever the silver lace be taken away
Ecclesiastes12:7 Or dust be turned again unto earth from whence it came
Ecclesiastes12:8 All is but vanity (sayeth the preacher) all is but plain vanity.
Ecclesiastes12:9 The same preacher was not wise alone
Ecclesiastes12:10 His diligence was to find out acceptable words
Ecclesiastes12:11 For the words of the wise are like pricks and nails that go thorow
Ecclesiastes12:12 Therefore beware (my son) that above these thou make them not many and innumerable books
Ecclesiastes12:13 Let us hear the conclusion of all things: Fear God
Ecclesiastes12:14 For God shall judge all works and secret things
SongOfSongs1:1 The Ballet of Ballets of Salomon:
SongOfSongs1:2 O that thy mouth would give me a kiss
SongOfSongs1:3 and that because of the good and pleasant savour. Thy name is sweet smelling ointment
SongOfSongs1:4 Yea that same moveth me also to run(renne) after thee. The king hath brought me in to his private chamber. We will be glad and rejoice in thee
SongOfSongs1:5 I am black (O ye daughters of Jerusalem) like as the tents of the Cedarenes
SongOfSongs1:6 but yet am I fair and well favoured withal. Marvel not at me that I am so black: And why? the sun hath shined upon me. For when my mother's children had evil will at me
SongOfSongs1:7 Tell me
SongOfSongs1:8 If thou know not thyself (O thou fairest among women) then go thy way forth after the footsteps of the sheep
SongOfSongs1:9 There will I tarry for thee (my love) with mine host and with my chariots
SongOfSongs1:10 Then shall thy cheeks and thy neck be made fair
SongOfSongs1:11 a neck band of gold will we make thee with silver buttons.
SongOfSongs1:12 When the king sitteth at the table
SongOfSongs1:13 for a bundle of Myrre (O my beloved) lieth betwixt my breasts.
SongOfSongs1:14 A cluster of grapes of Cypers
SongOfSongs1:15 O how fair art thou (my love) how fair art thou? thou hast doves' eyes.
SongOfSongs1:16 O how fair art thou (my beloved) how well favored art thou? Our bed is decked with flowers
SongOfSongs1:17 the ceilings of our house are of Cedar tree
SongOfSongs2:1 I am the flower of the field
SongOfSongs2:2 as the rose among the thorns
SongOfSongs2:3 Like as the apple tree among the trees of the wood
SongOfSongs2:4 He bringeth me in to his wine cellar
SongOfSongs2:5 Refesheth me with grapes
SongOfSongs2:6 His left hand lieth under my head
SongOfSongs2:7 I charge you
SongOfSongs2:8 Me think I hear the voice of my beloved: lo
SongOfSongs2:9 My beloved is like a Roe or a young hart. Behold
SongOfSongs2:10 My beloved answered and said unto me: O Stand(stode) up my love
SongOfSongs2:11 For lo
SongOfSongs2:12 The flowers are come up in the field
SongOfSongs2:13 The fig tree bringeth forth her figs
SongOfSongs2:14 O Stand(stode) up my love
SongOfSongs2:15 Get us the foxes
SongOfSongs2:16 My love is mine
SongOfSongs2:17 until the day break
SongOfSongs3:1 By night in my bed I sought him
SongOfSongs3:2 I will get up (thought I) and go about the city
SongOfSongs3:3 The watchmen that go about the city found me. Saw ye not him whom my soul loveth?
SongOfSongs3:4 So when I was a little past them
SongOfSongs3:5 I charge you
SongOfSongs3:6 Who is this
SongOfSongs3:7 Behold
SongOfSongs3:8 They hold swords every one
SongOfSongs3:9 King Solomon hath made himself a bedstead of the wood of Libanus
SongOfSongs3:10 the pillars are of silver
SongOfSongs3:11 Go forth (O ye daughters of Sion) and behold king Solomon in the crown
SongOfSongs4:1 O how fair art thou (my love) how fair art thou? thou hast doves' eyes
SongOfSongs4:2 Thy hairy locks are like a flock of sheep that be clipped
SongOfSongs4:3 Thy lips are like a rose coloured ribbon
SongOfSongs4:4 Thy neck is like the tower of David builded with bulwarks
SongOfSongs4:5 Thy two breasts are like two twins of young roes
SongOfSongs4:6 O that I might go to the mountain of Myrre
SongOfSongs4:7 Thou art all fair
SongOfSongs4:8 Come to me from Libanus
SongOfSongs4:9 Thou hast wounded my heart
SongOfSongs4:10 O how fair and lovely are thy breasts
SongOfSongs4:11 Thy lips
SongOfSongs4:12 Thou art a well kept garden
SongOfSongs4:13 The fruits that sprout in thee
SongOfSongs4:14 as Cypress
SongOfSongs4:15 Thou art a well of gardens
SongOfSongs4:16 Up thou northwind
SongOfSongs5:1 Come into my garden O my sister
SongOfSongs5:2 As I was asleep
SongOfSongs5:3 I have put off my coat: how can I do it on again? I have washed my feet
SongOfSongs5:4 But when my love put in his hand at the hole
SongOfSongs5:5 so that I stood up to open unto my beloved. My hands dropped with Myrre
SongOfSongs5:6 Nevertheless when I had opened unto my beloved
SongOfSongs5:7 So the watchmen that went about the city found me
SongOfSongs5:8 I charge you therefore
SongOfSongs5:9 Who is thy love above other lovers
SongOfSongs5:10 As for my loved
SongOfSongs5:11 His head is the most fine gold
SongOfSongs5:12 His eyes are as the eyes of doves by the water brooks
SongOfSongs5:13 His cheeks are like a garden bed
SongOfSongs5:14 his hands are full of gold rings and precious stones. His body is as the pure ivory
SongOfSongs5:15 His legs are as the pillars of Marble
SongOfSongs5:16 His throat is sweet
SongOfSongs6:1 Whither is thy love gone then (O thou fayrest among women) whither is thy love departed
SongOfSongs6:2 My love is gone down into his garden
SongOfSongs6:3 My love is mine
SongOfSongs6:4 Thou art pleasant (O my love) even as loveliness it self
SongOfSongs6:5 (Turn away thine eyes from me
SongOfSongs6:6 Thy teeth are like a flock of sheep that be clipped
SongOfSongs6:7 Thy cheeks are like a piece of a pomegranate
SongOfSongs6:8 There are threescore Queens
SongOfSongs6:9 But one is my dove
SongOfSongs6:10 What is she this
SongOfSongs6:11 I went down into the nut garden
SongOfSongs6:12 Then the chariots of the prince of my people made me suddenly afraid.
SongOfSongs6:13 Turn again
SongOfSongs7:1 O how pleasant are thy treadings
SongOfSongs7:2 Thy navel is like a round goblet
SongOfSongs7:3 Thy two breasts are like two twins of young roes:
SongOfSongs7:4 Thy neck is as it were a tower of ivory: thine eyes are like the water pools in Hesebon
SongOfSongs7:5 That head that standeth upon thee is like Carmel: the hair of thy head is like the kings purple folden up in plates.
SongOfSongs7:6 O how fair and lovely art thou
SongOfSongs7:7 Thy stature is like a date tree
SongOfSongs7:8 I will climb up the date tree
SongOfSongs7:9 and thy throat like the best wine. This shall be pure and clear for my love
SongOfSongs7:10 There will I turn me unto my love
SongOfSongs7:11 O come on my love
SongOfSongs7:12 In the morning will we rise by times
SongOfSongs7:13 there shall the mandragoras give their smell beside our doors: there O my love

SongOfSongs8:2 if I took thee
SongOfSongs8:3 His left hand lieth under my head
SongOfSongs8:4 I charge you
SongOfSongs8:5 What is she this
SongOfSongs8:6 O set me as a seal upon thine heart
SongOfSongs8:7 so that many waters are not able to quench love

SongOfSongs8:9 If she be a wall

SongOfSongs8:12 But my vineyard
SongOfSongs8:13 Thou that dwellest in the gardens
SongOfSongs8:14 O get thee away
Isaiah1:1 The(This is the) prophesy of Esay the son of Amos
Isaiah1:2 Hear O heaven hearken O earth
Isaiah1:3 An ox knoweth his lord
Isaiah1:4 Alas for this sinful people
Isaiah1:5 Wherefore should ye be plagued any more? For ye are ever falling away. The whole head is sick
Isaiah1:6 From the sole of the foot unto the head
Isaiah1:7 Your land lieth waste
Isaiah1:8 Moreover the daughter of Sion is left alone like a cottage in a vineyard
Isaiah1:9 And except the LORD of Hosts had left us a few alive: we should have been as Sodoma
Isaiah1:10 Hear the word of the LORD ye tyrants of Sodoma: and hearken unto the law of our God
Isaiah1:11 Why offer ye so many sacrifices unto me? I am discontent for the brentofferings of wethers
Isaiah1:12 When ye appear before me
Isaiah1:13 Offer me no more oblations
Isaiah1:14 I hate your new holy days and fastings
Isaiah1:15 Though ye hold out your hands
Isaiah1:16 Wash you
Isaiah1:17 Learn to do right
Isaiah1:18 Now go to (sayeth the LORD) we will walk together. Is it not so? Though your sins be as red as scarlet
Isaiah1:19 Is it not so? If ye be loving and obedient
Isaiah1:20 But if ye be obstinate and rebellious
Isaiah1:21 How happeneth it then that the righteous city (which was full of equity) is become unfaithful as an whore? righteousness dwelt in it
Isaiah1:22 Thy silver is turned to dross
Isaiah1:23 Thy princes are traitors and companions of thieves. They love gifts altogether
Isaiah1:24 Therefore speaketh the Lord GOD(LORDE God) of Hosts the mighty one of Israel: Ah
Isaiah1:25 And therefore shall I lay my hand upon thee
Isaiah1:26 and set thy judges again as they were sometime
Isaiah1:27 But Sion shall be redeemed with equity
Isaiah1:28 For the transgressors and ungodly
Isaiah1:29 And except ye be ashamed of the oak trees wherein ye have so delighted
Isaiah1:30 Ye shall be as an oak whose leaves are fallen away
Isaiah1:31 And as for the glory of these things
Isaiah2:1 Moreover this was the word that was opened unto Esay the son of Amos
Isaiah2:2 It will be also in process of time: That the hill where the house of the LORD is builded
Isaiah2:3 speaking thus one unto another: up
Isaiah2:4 and shall give sentence among the Heathen
Isaiah2:5 It is to thee that I cry (O house of Jacob) up
Isaiah2:6 But thou art scattered abroad with thy people (O house of Jacob) for ye go far beyond your fathers
Isaiah2:7 As soon as your land was full of silver and gold
Isaiah2:8 Immediately was it full of Idols also
Isaiah2:9 There kneeleth the man
Isaiah2:10 And therefore get thee soon in to some rock
Isaiah2:11 Which casteth down the high looks of presumptuous persons
Isaiah2:12 For the day of the LORD of Hosts shall go over all pride and presumption
Isaiah2:13 upon all high stout Cedar trees of Libanus
Isaiah2:14 upon all high hills
Isaiah2:15 upon all costly towers
Isaiah2:16 upon all ships of the sea
Isaiah2:17 And it shall bring down the pride of man
Isaiah2:18 But the Idols shall utterly be rooted out.
Isaiah2:19 Men shall creep into holes of stone
Isaiah2:20 Then
Isaiah2:21 that he may the better creep into the caves and rocks
Isaiah2:22 Every man can eschew a person moved in anger
Isaiah3:1 Even so shall the LORD of Hosts take away from Jerusalem and Juda
Isaiah3:2 the captain and the soldier
Isaiah3:3 the worshipful of fifty year old
Isaiah3:4 And I shall give you children to be your princes
Isaiah3:5 One shall ever be doing violence and wrong to another. The boy shall presume against the elder
Isaiah3:6 Yea one shall take a friend of his own kindred by the bosom
Isaiah3:7 Then shall he swear and say: I can not help you. Moreover
Isaiah3:8 For Jerusalem and Juda must decay
Isaiah3:9 The changing of their countenance betrayeth them
Isaiah3:10 Then shall they say: O happy are the godly
Isaiah3:11 But woe be unto the ungodly and unrighteous for they shall be rewarded after their works.
Isaiah3:12 O my people
Isaiah3:13 The LORD is here to commune(comon) of the matter
Isaiah3:14 The LORD shall come forth to reason with the Senators and princes of his people
Isaiah3:15 Wherefore do ye oppress my people
Isaiah3:16 Moreover thus sayeth the LORD: Seeing the daughters of Sion are become so proud
Isaiah3:17 Therefore shall the Lord shave the heads of the daughters of Sion
Isaiah3:18 In that day shall the Lord take away the gorgeousness of their apparel
Isaiah3:19 and collars
Isaiah3:20 the goodly flowered wide and broidered raiment
Isaiah3:21 rings and garlands
Isaiah3:22 holy day clothes and vails
Isaiah3:23 glasses and smocks
Isaiah3:24 And instead of good smell there shall be stink among them. And for their girdles there shall be lowse bands.(bonds) And for well set hair there shall be baldness. Instead of a stomacher
Isaiah3:25 Their husbands and their mighty men shall perish with the sword in battle.
Isaiah3:26 At that time shall their gates mourn and complain
Isaiah4:1 Then shall seven wives take hold of one man
Isaiah4:2 After that time shall the branch of the LORD be beautiful and mighty
Isaiah4:3 Then shall the remnant in Sion and the remnant at Jerusalem be called holy: namely all such as are written among the living at Jerusalem:
Isaiah4:4 What time as the Lord(LORDE) shall wash away the desolation of the daughters of Sion

Isaiah59:8 But the way of peace they know not. In their goings is no equity

Isaiah63:10 But after they provoked him to wrath and vexed his holy mind
Isaiah63:11 Yet remembered he the old time of Moses and his people: How he brought them from the water of the sea

Jeremiah1:1 These are the Sermons of Jeremy the son of Helkiah the Priest
Jeremiah1:2 when the LORD had first spoken with him
Jeremiah1:3 and so during unto the time of Jehoakim the son of Josiah king of Judah
Jeremiah1:4 The word of the LORD spake thus unto me:
Jeremiah1:5 Before I fashioned thee in thy mother's womb
Jeremiah1:6 Then said I: Oh Lord GOD
Jeremiah1:7 And the LORD answered me thus: Say not so
Jeremiah1:8 Be not afraid of their faces
Jeremiah1:9 And with that
Jeremiah1:10 and this day do I set thee over the people and kingdoms: that thou mayest root out
Jeremiah1:11 After this
Jeremiah1:12 Then said the LORD: thou hast seen right
Jeremiah1:13 It happened afterward
Jeremiah1:14 Then said the LORD unto me: Out of the north shall come a plague upon all the dwellers of the land.
Jeremiah1:15 For lo
Jeremiah1:16 And thorow them shall I declare my judgment
Jeremiah1:17 And therefore gird up thy loins
Jeremiah1:18 For behold
Jeremiah1:19 They shall fight against thee
Jeremiah2:1 Moreover
Jeremiah2:2 Go thy way
Jeremiah2:3 Thou Israel wast hallowed unto the LORD
Jeremiah2:4 Hear therefore the word of the LORD
Jeremiah2:5 Thus sayeth the LORD unto you. What unfaithfulness found your fathers in me
Jeremiah2:6 They thought not in their hearts: Where have we left the LORD
Jeremiah2:7 And when I had brought you into a pleasant well builded land
Jeremiah2:8 The priests themselves said not once: Where is the LORD? They that have the law in their hands
Jeremiah2:9 Wherefore I am constrained (sayeth the LORD) to make my complaint upon you
Jeremiah2:10 Go into the Isles of Cethim
Jeremiah2:11 whether the Gentiles themselves deal so falsely and untruly with their gods
Jeremiah2:12 Be astonied (O ye heavens) be afraid
Jeremiah2:13 For my people hath done two evils. They have forsaken me the well of the water of life
Jeremiah2:14 Is Israel a bond servant
Jeremiah2:15 Why do they roar and cry then upon him
Jeremiah2:16 Yea the children of Noph and Taphnes have defiled thy neck.
Jeremiah2:17 Cometh not this unto thee
Jeremiah2:18 And what hast thou now to do in the street of Egypt? to drink foul water? Either
Jeremiah2:19 Thine own wickedness shall reprove thee
Jeremiah2:20 I have ever broken thy yoke of old
Jeremiah2:21 and among all green trees where as I planted thee out of noble grapes and good roots. How art thou turned then into a bitter
Jeremiah2:22 Yea and that so sore: that though thou washest thee with Nitrus and makest thyself to savour with that sweet smelling herb of Borith: yet in my sight thou art stained with thy wickedness
Jeremiah2:23 Say not now: I am not unclean
Jeremiah2:24 and thy wantonness is like a wild Ass
Jeremiah2:25 Thou keepest thy foot from nakedness
Jeremiah2:26 Like as a thief that is taken with the deed cometh to shame
Jeremiah2:27 For they say to a stock
Jeremiah2:28 I shall answer them: Where are now thy gods
Jeremiah2:29 Wherefore then will ye go to law with me
Jeremiah2:30 It is but lost labour
Jeremiah2:31 If ye be the people of the LORD
Jeremiah2:32 Doth a maiden forget her raiment
Jeremiah2:33 Why boastest(boostest) thou thy ways so highly (to obtain favour there thorow) when thou hast yet stained them with blasphemies?
Jeremiah2:34 Upon thy wings is found the blood of poor and innocent people
Jeremiah2:35 Yet darest thou say: I am guiltless: Tush
Jeremiah2:36 O how evil will it be for thee
Jeremiah2:37 Yea thou shalt go thy way from them
Jeremiah3:1 Commonly
Jeremiah3:2 Lift up thine eyes on every side
Jeremiah3:3 This is the cause
Jeremiah3:4 Else wouldest thou say unto me: O my father
Jeremiah3:5 Wilt thou then put me away and cast me off for ever? Or wilt thou withdraw thyself clean from me? Nevertheless
Jeremiah3:6 The LORD said also unto me: in the time of Josiah the king: Hast thou seen what that shrinking Israel hath done? how she hath run up upon all high hills
Jeremiah3:7 hast thou seen also (when she had done all this) how I said unto her: that she should turn again unto me
Jeremiah3:8 Namely
Jeremiah3:9 Yea and the noise of her whoredom hath defiled the whole land. For she hath committed her advoutry with stones and stocks.
Jeremiah3:10 Nevertheless
Jeremiah3:11 And the LORD said unto me. The backslider Israel is more righteous
Jeremiah3:12 and therefore go preach these words toward the north
Jeremiah3:13 but on this condition
Jeremiah3:14 O ye shrinking children
Jeremiah3:15 and I will give you herdmen after mine own mind
Jeremiah3:16 Moreover
Jeremiah3:17 Then shall Jerusalem be called the LORD's seat
Jeremiah3:18 Then those that be of the house of Judah shall go into the house of Israel: and they shall come together out of the North
Jeremiah3:19 I have shewed also
Jeremiah3:20 But like as a woman faileth her lover
Jeremiah3:21 And therefore the voice of the children of Israel was heard on every side
Jeremiah3:22 O ye shrinking children
Jeremiah3:23 The hills fall
Jeremiah3:24 Confusion hath devoured our fathers' labour from our youth up: yea their sheep and bullocks
Jeremiah3:25 So do we also sleep in our confusion
Jeremiah4:1 O Israel
Jeremiah4:2 And shalt swear: The LORD liveth: in truth
Jeremiah4:3 For thus sayeth the LORD
Jeremiah4:4 Be circumcised in the LORD
Jeremiah4:5 Preach in Judah and Jerusalem
Jeremiah4:6 Set up the token in Sion
Jeremiah4:7 For the spoiler of the Gentiles is broken up from his place
Jeremiah4:8 Wherefore gird yourselves about with sackcloth
Jeremiah4:9 At the same time (sayeth the LORD) the heart of the kings and of the princes shall be gone
Jeremiah4:10 Then said I
Jeremiah4:11 Then shall it be said to the people and to Jerusalem: there cometh a warm wind from the north thorow the way of my people
Jeremiah4:12 After that shall there come unto me a strong wind
Jeremiah4:13 For lo
Jeremiah4:14 O Jerusalem
Jeremiah4:15 For a voice from Dan and from the hill of Ephraim speaketh out
Jeremiah4:16 Behold the Heathen give Jerusalem warning
Jeremiah4:17 they shall give them warning in every place
Jeremiah4:18 Thy ways and thy thoughts
Jeremiah4:19 Ah my belly; Ah my belly (shalt thou cry) how is my heart so sore? my heart panteth within me
Jeremiah4:20 They cry murder upon murder
Jeremiah4:21 How long shall I see the tokens of war
Jeremiah4:22 Nevertheless this shall come upon them because my people is become foolish
Jeremiah4:23 I have looked upon the earth
Jeremiah4:24 I beheld the mountains
Jeremiah4:25 I looked about me
Jeremiah4:26 I marked well
Jeremiah4:27 For thus hath the LORD said: The whole land shall be desolate
Jeremiah4:28 And therefore let the earth mourn
Jeremiah4:29 The whole land shall flee
Jeremiah4:30 What wilt thou now do
Jeremiah4:31 For (me think) I hear a noise
Jeremiah5:1 Look thorow Jerusalem
Jeremiah5:2 For though they can say: the LORD liveth
Jeremiah5:3 Where as thou (O LORD) lookest only upon faith and truth. Thou hast scourged them
Jeremiah5:4 Therefore thought I in myself: peradventure they are so simple and foolish
Jeremiah5:5 Therefore will I go unto their heads and rulers
Jeremiah5:6 Wherefore a lion out of the wood shall hurt them
Jeremiah5:7 Should I then for all this have mercy upon thee? Thy children have forsaken me
Jeremiah5:8 In the desire of uncleanly lust they are become like the stoned horse
Jeremiah5:9 Should I not correct this? sayeth the LORD. Should I not be avenged of every people
Jeremiah5:10 Climb up upon their walls
Jeremiah5:11 For unfaithfully hath the house of Israel and Judah forsaken me
Jeremiah5:12 They have denied the LORD
Jeremiah5:13 As for the warning of the Prophets
Jeremiah5:14 Wherefore thus sayeth the LORD God of Hosts: Because ye speak such words
Jeremiah5:15 Lo
Jeremiah5:16 Their arrows are sudden death
Jeremiah5:17 This people shall eat up thy fruit and thy meat
Jeremiah5:18 Nevertheless I will not then have done with you
Jeremiah5:19 But if they say: wherefore doth the LORD our God all this unto us? Then answer them: because
Jeremiah5:20 Preach this unto the house of Jacob
Jeremiah5:21 Hear this (thou foolish and undiscrete people) ye have eyes
Jeremiah5:22 Fear ye not me? sayeth the LORD: Are ye not ashamed
Jeremiah5:23 But this people hath a false and an obstinate heart
Jeremiah5:24 They think not in their hearts: O let us fear the LORD our God
Jeremiah5:25 Nevertheless your misdeeds have turned these from you
Jeremiah5:26 For among my people are found wicked persons
Jeremiah5:27 And like as a net is full of birds
Jeremiah5:28 hereof are they fat and wealthy
Jeremiah5:29 Should I not punish these things? sayeth the LORD: should I not be avenged of all such people
Jeremiah5:30 Horrible and grievous things are done in the land.
Jeremiah5:31 The Prophets teach falsely
Jeremiah6:1 Come out of Jerusalem
Jeremiah6:2 I will liken the daughter of Sion to a fair and tender woman
Jeremiah6:3 Their tents shall they pitch round about her
Jeremiah6:4 Make battle against her (shall they say:) Arise
Jeremiah6:5 Arise
Jeremiah6:6 for thus hath the LORD of Hosts commanded. Hew down her trees
Jeremiah6:7 Like as a conduit aboundeth in water
Jeremiah6:8 Amend thee (O Jerusalem) lest I withdraw my heart from thee
Jeremiah6:9 For thus sayeth the LORD of Hosts: The residue of Israel shall be gathered
Jeremiah6:10 But unto whom shall I speak
Jeremiah6:11 And therefore I am so full of thine indignation (o LORD) that I may suffer no longer. Shed out thy wrath upon the children that are without
Jeremiah6:12 Their houses with their lands and wives shall be turned unto strangers
Jeremiah6:13 For from the least unto the most
Jeremiah6:14 And beside that
Jeremiah6:15 Therefore they must be ashamed
Jeremiah6:16 Thus sayeth the LORD: go into the streets
Jeremiah6:17 and I will set watchmen over you
Jeremiah6:18 Hear therefore ye Gentiles
Jeremiah6:19 Hear thou earth also: behold
Jeremiah6:20 Wherefore bring ye me incense from Saba
Jeremiah6:21 And therefore thus sayeth the LORD: Behold
Jeremiah6:22 Moreover thus sayeth the LORD: Behold
Jeremiah6:23 with bows and with darts shall they be weaponed: It is a rough and fierce people
Jeremiah6:24 Then shall this cry be heard: Our arms are feeble
Jeremiah6:25 No man go forth in to the field
Jeremiah6:26 Wherefore
Jeremiah6:27 Thee have I set for a prover of my hard people
Jeremiah6:28 For they are all unfaithful and fallen away
Jeremiah6:29 The bellows are brent in the fire
Jeremiah6:30 Therefore shall they be called naughty silver
Jeremiah7:1 These are the words that GOD spake unto Jeremy:
Jeremiah7:2 Stand under the gates of the LORD's house
Jeremiah7:3 Thus sayeth the LORD of Hosts the God of Israel. Amend your ways and your counsels
Jeremiah7:4 Trust not in false lying words
Jeremiah7:5 For if ye will amend your ways and counsels
Jeremiah7:6 If ye will not oppress the stranger
Jeremiah7:7 then will I let you dwell in this place
Jeremiah7:8 But take heed
Jeremiah7:9 For when ye have stolen
Jeremiah7:10 Then come ye
Jeremiah7:11 What? Think you this house that beareth my name
Jeremiah7:12 Go to my place in Siloh
Jeremiah7:13 And now
Jeremiah7:14 And therefore even as I have done to Siloh
Jeremiah7:15 And I shall thrust you out of my sight
Jeremiah7:16 Therefore thou shalt not pray for this people
Jeremiah7:17 Seest thou not what they do in the cities of Juda

Jeremiah7:19 How be it they hurt not me (sayeth the LORD) but rather confound
Jeremiah7:20 And therefore thus sayeth the Lord GOD:(LORDE God) Behold
Jeremiah7:21 Thus sayeth the LORD of Hosts

Jeremiah7:25 And this have they done
Jeremiah7:26 yet would they not hearken
Jeremiah7:27 And thou shalt now speak all these words unto them
Jeremiah7:28 Therefore shalt thou say unto them: this is the people

Jeremiah7:30 For the children of Judah have done evil in my sight
Jeremiah7:31 They have also builded an altar at Topheth
Jeremiah7:32 And therefore behold

Jeremiah7:34 And as for the voice of mirth and gladness of the cities of Judah
Jeremiah8:1 At the same time
Jeremiah8:2 and laid against the Sun
Jeremiah8:3 And all they that remain of this wicked generation

Jeremiah8:5 Wherefore then is this people and Jerusalem gone so far back
Jeremiah8:6 For I have looked
Jeremiah8:7 The Stork knoweth his appointed time
Jeremiah8:8 How dare ye say then: we are wise
Jeremiah8:9 therefore shall the wise be confounded
Jeremiah8:10 Wherefore
Jeremiah8:11 Nevertheless
Jeremiah8:12 Fie for shame

Jeremiah8:15 We looked for peace

Jeremiah8:19 for lo

Jeremiah9:2 Would God that I had a cottage some where far from folk

Jeremiah9:4 Yea one must keep himself from another
Jeremiah9:5 Yea one dissesembleth with another
Jeremiah9:6 They have set their stole in the middest of deceit
Jeremiah9:7 Therefore thus sayeth the LORD of Hosts: behold
Jeremiah9:8 Their tongues are like sharp arrows
Jeremiah9:9 Should I not punish them for these things? sayeth the LORD: Or should I not be avenged of any such people as this?

Jeremiah9:11 I will make Jerusalem also an heap of stones

Jeremiah10:5 It standeth as still as the palm tree

Jeremiah10:7 Who would not fear thee? Or what King of the Gentiles would not obey thee? For among all the wise men of the Gentiles

Jeremiah10:9 namely

Jeremiah10:16 Nevertheless

Jeremiah44:4 How be it

Jeremiah44:9 Or have ye now forgotten the wickedness of your forefathers
Jeremiah44:10 Yet are ye not sorry this day

Jeremiah51:17 By the reason of wisdom

Jeremiah51:31 One pursuant shall meet another
Jeremiah51:32 the fords(foordes) occupied
Jeremiah51:33 For thus sayeth the LORD of Hosts the God of Israel: The daughter of Babylon hath been in her time like as a threshing floor

Jeremiah52:1 Zedekiah was twenty one year old
Jeremiah52:2 He lived wickedly before the LORD even as Jehoakim did.
Jeremiah52:3 Wherefore the LORD was angry at Jerusalem and Judah
Jeremiah52:4 But in the ninth year of his reign; In the tenth Month
Jeremiah52:5 And this besieging of the city endured unto the eleventh year of king Zedekiah.
Jeremiah52:6 And in the fourth Month
Jeremiah52:7 So all the soldiers brake away
Jeremiah52:8 And so the Caldees followed upon them
Jeremiah52:9 So they carried the king away prisoner to Riblah
Jeremiah52:10 The king of Babylon also caused Zedekiah's sons to be slain before his face
Jeremiah52:11 Moreover he put out the eyes of Zedekiah
Jeremiah52:12 Now the tenth day of the fifth Month
Jeremiah52:13 and brent up the house of the LORD. He brent up also the king's palace
Jeremiah52:14 And the whole Host of the Caldees that were with the chief Captain
Jeremiah52:15 As for the poor people and such folk as yet was left in the city
Jeremiah52:16 But the poor people of the country
Jeremiah52:17 The Caldees also brake the brasen pillars that were in the house of the LORD
Jeremiah52:18 They took away also the Caldrons
Jeremiah52:19 with the basins
Jeremiah52:20 The chief Captain took also the two pillars
Jeremiah52:21 For every pillar was eighteen cubits high
Jeremiah52:22 Now upon the rope were brasen knoppes
Jeremiah52:23 After this manner were both the pillars fashioned with the pomegranates
Jeremiah52:24 The chief Captain also took Sariah the high(hie) priest
Jeremiah52:25 He took out of the city a chamberlain which was a captain of the soldiers
Jeremiah52:26 These Nabuzaradan the chief Captain took
Jeremiah52:27 and the king of Babylon caused them to be put to death at Reblath in the land of Hemath. And thus Judah was lead away captive
Jeremiah52:28 This is the sum of the people
Jeremiah52:29 In the eighteenth year Nabuchodonosor carried away from Jerusalem eight hundredth thirty two persons.
Jeremiah52:30 In the twenty third year of Nabuchodonosor Nabuzaradan the chief Captain
Jeremiah52:31 In the thirty seventh year after that Jehoacin the king of Judah was carried away in the twenty fifth day of the twelfth Month
Jeremiah52:32 and spake lovingly to him. And set his throne above the thrones of the other kings that were with him in Babylon.
Jeremiah52:33 He changed also the clothes of his prison
Jeremiah52:34 And he had a continual living given him of the king of Babylon
Lamentations1:1 Aleph. Alas
Lamentations1:2 Beth. She weepeth sore in the night
Lamentations1:3 Gimel. Judah is taken prisoner
Lamentations1:4 Daleth. The streets of Sion mourn
Lamentations1:5 He. Her enemies are fallen upon her head
Lamentations1:6 Vau. All the beauty of the daughter of Sion is away
Lamentations1:7 Zain. Now doth Jerusalem remember the time of her misery and disobedience
Lamentations1:8 CHeth. Jerusalem sinned ever more and more
Lamentations1:9 Teth. Her skirts are defiled
Lamentations1:10 Iod. The enemy hath put his hand to all the precious things that she had
Lamentations1:11 Caph. All her people seek their bread with heaviness
Lamentations1:12 Lamed. O ye all that go fore by
Lamentations1:13 Mem. From above hath he sent down a fire into my bones and chastened me: he hath laid a net for my feet
Lamentations1:14 Nun. The yoke of my transgression is come at the last
Lamentations1:15 Samech. The Lord(LORDE) hath destroyed all the mighty men
Lamentations1:16 Ain. Therefore do I weep
Lamentations1:17 Phe. Sion casteth out her hands
Lamentations1:18 Zade. The LORD is righteous
Lamentations1:19 Koph. I called for my lovers (but they beguiled me) for my Priests and counselors
Lamentations1:20 Res. Consider (O LORD) how I am troubled
Lamentations1:21 Sin. They hear my mourning
Lamentations1:22 Thau. From thee shall come all their adversity: thou shalt pluck them away
Lamentations2:1 Aleph. Alas
Lamentations2:2 Beth. The Lord(LORDE) hath cast down all the glory of Jacob without any favour: all the strong places of the daughter Juda hath he broken in his wrath
Lamentations2:3 Gimel. In the wrath of his indignation he hath broken all the horn of Israel: he hath withdrawn his right hand from the enemy: yea a flame of fire is kindled in Jacob
Lamentations2:4 Daleth. He hath bent his bow like an enemy
Lamentations2:5 He. The Lord(LORDE) is become
Lamentations2:6 Vau. Her tabernacle (which was like a garden of pleasure) hath he destroyed: her high(hie) solempne feasts hath he put down. The LORD hath brought it so to pass
Lamentations2:7 Zain. The Lord(LORDE) hath forsaken his own alter
Lamentations2:8 CHeth. The LORD thought to break down the walls of the daughter Sion
Lamentations2:9 Teth. Her ports are cast down to the ground
Lamentations2:10 Iod. The Senators of the daughter Sion sit upon the ground in silence: they have strowed ashes upon their heads
Lamentations2:11 Caph. Mine eyes begin to fail me thorow weeping
Lamentations2:12 Lamed. Even when they spake to their mothers: where is meat and drink? for while they so said
Lamentations2:13 Mem. What shall I say of thee
Lamentations2:14 Nun. Thy prophets have looked out vain and foolish things for thee
Lamentations2:15 Samech. All they that go by thee
Lamentations2:16 Ain. All thine enemies gape upon thee
Lamentations2:17 Phe. The LORD hath fulfilled the thing
Lamentations2:18 Zade. Let thine heart cry unto the Lord
Lamentations2:19 Koph. Stand up and make thy prayer in the first watch of the night
Lamentations2:20 Res. Behold
Lamentations2:21 Sin. Young and old lie behind the streets upon the ground
Lamentations2:22 Thau. My neighbours that are round about me
Lamentations3:1 Aleph. I am the man
Lamentations3:2 He drove me forth
Lamentations3:3 Against me only he turneth his hand
Lamentations3:4 Beth. My flesh and my skin hath he made old
Lamentations3:5 He hath builded round about me
Lamentations3:6 He hath set me in darkness
Lamentations3:7 Gimel. He hath so hedged me in
Lamentations3:8 Though I cry and call piteously
Lamentations3:9 He hath stopped up my ways with four square stones
Lamentations3:10 Daleth. He layeth wait for me like a Bear
Lamentations3:11 He hath marred my ways
Lamentations3:12 He hath bent his bow
Lamentations3:13 He. The arrows of his quiver hath he shot
Lamentations3:14 I am laughed to scorn of all my people
Lamentations3:15 He hath filled me with bitterness
Lamentations3:16 Vau. He hath smitten my teeth in pieces
Lamentations3:17 He hath put my soul out of rest
Lamentations3:18 I thought in myself: I am undone
Lamentations3:19 Zain. O remember yet my misery and my trouble
Lamentations3:20 Yea thou shalt remember them
Lamentations3:21 While I consider these things in my heart
Lamentations3:22 CHeth. Namely
Lamentations3:23 His faithfulness is great and reneweth it self as the morning.
Lamentations3:24 The LORD is my portion
Lamentations3:25 Teth. O how good is the LORD unto them that put their trust in him
Lamentations3:26 O how good is it with stillness to wait and tarry
Lamentations3:27 O how good is it for a man
Lamentations3:28 Iod. He sitteth alone
Lamentations3:29 He layeth his face upon the earth
Lamentations3:30 He offereth his cheek to the smiter
Lamentations3:31 Caph. For the Lord(LORDE) will not forsake for ever.
Lamentations3:32 But though he do cast off
Lamentations3:33 For he doth not plague
Lamentations3:34 Lamed. To tread all the prisoners of the earth under his feet.
Lamentations3:35 To move the judgement of man before the most highest.
Lamentations3:36 To condemn a man in his cause; The Lord(LORDE) hath no pleasure in such things.
Lamentations3:37 Mem. What is he then that sayeth: there should something be done without the Lord's(LORDES) commandment:
Lamentations3:38 Out of the mouth of the most Highest goeth not evil and good?
Lamentations3:39 Wherefore then murmureth the living man: let him murmur at his own sin.
Lamentations3:40 Nun. Let us look well upon our own ways
Lamentations3:41 Let us lift up our heart with our hands unto the Lord(God) that is in heaven.
Lamentations3:42 We have been dissemblers
Lamentations3:43 Samech. Thou hast covered us in thy wrath
Lamentations3:44 Thou hast hid thyself in a cloud
Lamentations3:45 Thou hast made us outcasts
Lamentations3:46 Ain. All our enemies gape upon us.
Lamentations3:47 Fear and snare is come upon us
Lamentations3:48 Whole rivers of water gush out of mine eyes
Lamentations3:49 Phe. Mine eyes run
Lamentations3:50 O LORD
Lamentations3:51 Mine eye breaketh my heart
Lamentations3:52 Zade. Mine enemies hunted me out sharply
Lamentations3:53 They have put down my life into a pit
Lamentations3:54 They poured water upon my head
Lamentations3:55 Koph. I called upon thy name
Lamentations3:56 Thou hast heard my voice
Lamentations3:57 Thou hast inclined thy self unto me
Lamentations3:58 Res. Thou (O Lord)
Lamentations3:59 O LORD
Lamentations3:60 Thou hast well considered how they go about to do me harm
Lamentations3:61 Sin. Thou hast heard their despiteful words (O LORD)
Lamentations3:62 The lips of mine enemies
Lamentations3:63 Thou seest also their sitting down and their rising up
Lamentations3:64 Thau. Reward them
Lamentations3:65 Give them the thing

Lamentations4:1 Aleph. O how is the gold become so dim? How is the goodly colour of it so sore changed? and the stones of the sanctuary thus scattered in the corner of every street?
Lamentations4:2 Beth. The children of Sion that were alway in honour
Lamentations4:3 Gimel. The Lamies give their young ones suck with bare breasts: but the daughter of my people is cruel
Lamentations4:4 Daleth. The tongues of the sucking children
Lamentations4:5 He. They that were wont to fair delicately
Lamentations4:6 Vau. The sin of the daughter of my people is become greater than the wickedness of Sodom
Lamentations4:7 Zain. Her abstainers (or Nazarees) were whiter than the snow or milk: their colour was fresh red as the Corall
Lamentations4:8 CHeth. But now their faces are very black: In so much
Lamentations4:9 Teth. They that be slain with the sword
Lamentations4:10 Iod. The women (which of nature are pitiful) have sodden their own children with their hands that they might be their meat
Lamentations4:11 Caph. The LORD hath performed his heavy wrath: he hath poured out the furiousness of his displeasure. He hath kindled a fire in Sion
Lamentations4:12 Lamed. Neither the kings of the earth
Lamentations4:13 Mem. Which nevertheless is come to pass for the sins of her prophets
Lamentations4:14 Nun. So that these blind men went stumbling in the streets
Lamentations4:15 Samech. But they cried unto every man: flee the staining
Lamentations4:16 Ain. The countenance of the LORD hath banished them
Lamentations4:17 Phe. Wherefore yet our eyes fail us
Lamentations4:18 Zade. They lay so sharp wait for us
Lamentations4:19 Koph. Our persecutors are swifter than the Aegles(eagles) of the air
Lamentations4:20 Res. The very breath of our mouth: even the anointed LORD himself shall be taken in our sins
Lamentations4:21 Sin. And thou (O daughter Edom) that dwellest in the land of Huz
Lamentations4:22 Thau. Thy sin is well punished (O thou daughter Sion) he shall not suffer thee to be carried away any more. But thy wickedness (O daughter Edom) shall he visit
Lamentations5:1 (The prayer of Jeremy) Call to remembrance
Lamentations5:2 Our inheritance is turned to the strangers
Lamentations5:3 We are become careful and fatherless
Lamentations5:4 We are fain to drink our own water for money
Lamentations5:5 Our necks are under persecution
Lamentations5:6 Afore time we yielded our selves to the Egyptians
Lamentations5:7 Our fathers (which now are gone) have sinned
Lamentations5:8 Servants have the rule of us
Lamentations5:9 We must get our living with the peril of our lives because of the drouth of the wilderness.
Lamentations5:10 Our skin is as it had been brent in an oven
Lamentations5:11 The wives are ravished in Sion
Lamentations5:12 The princes are hanged up with the hand of the enemies
Lamentations5:13 they have taken young men's lives from them
Lamentations5:14 The elders sit no more under the gates
Lamentations5:15 The joy of our heart is gone
Lamentations5:16 The garland of our head is fallen: alas
Lamentations5:17 Therefore our heart is full of heaviness
Lamentations5:18 because of the hill of Sion that is destroyed. In so much
Lamentations5:19 But thou
Lamentations5:20 Wherefore wilt thou still forget us
Lamentations5:21 O LORD
Lamentations5:22 for thou hast now banished us long enough
Ezekiel1:1 It chanced in the thirtieth year the fifth day of the fourth Month
Ezekiel1:2 Now the fifth day of the Month
Ezekiel1:3 At the same time came the word of the LORD unto Ezechiel the son of Buzi the priest
Ezekiel1:4 And I looked
Ezekiel1:5 and as it were the likeness of four beasts
Ezekiel1:6 saving
Ezekiel1:7 Their legs were straight; But their feet were like bullock's feet
Ezekiel1:8 Under their wings upon all the four corners they had men's hands. Their faces and their wings were toward the four corners:
Ezekiel1:9 yet were the wings so
Ezekiel1:10 Upon the right side of these four
Ezekiel1:11 Their faces also and their wings were spread out above: so that two wings of one touched ever two wings of another
Ezekiel1:12 Every one when it went
Ezekiel1:13 The fashion and countenance of the beasts was like hot coals of fire
Ezekiel1:14 When the beasts went forward and backward
Ezekiel1:15 Now when I had well considered the beasts
Ezekiel1:16 The fashion and the work of the wheels was like the sea. The four wheels were joined and made (to look upon) as it had been one wheel in another.
Ezekiel1:17 When one went forward
Ezekiel1:18 They were large
Ezekiel1:19 And when the beasts lift themselves up from the earth
Ezekiel1:20 Whither soever the spirit went
Ezekiel1:21 When the beasts went forth
Ezekiel1:22 Above over the heads of the beasts there was a firmament
Ezekiel1:23 under the same firmament were their wings laid abroad
Ezekiel1:24 And when they went forth I heard the noise of their wings
Ezekiel1:25 Now when they stood still
Ezekiel1:26 Above the firmament that was over their heads
Ezekiel1:27 I beheld him
Ezekiel1:28 Yea the shine and glister that lightened round about

Ezekiel16:16 Yea thou hast taken thy garments of divers colors
Ezekiel16:17 The goodly ornaments and Jewels which I gave thee of mine own gold and silver

Ezekiel16:19 My meat which I gave thee
Ezekiel16:20 Thou hast taken thine own sons and daughters
Ezekiel16:21 that thou slayest my children
Ezekiel16:22 And yet in all thy abominations and whoredom
Ezekiel16:23 After all these thy wickednesses (woe
Ezekiel16:24 thou hast builded thy stewes and brodel houses in every place: yea at the head of every street hast thou builded thee an alter.
Ezekiel16:25 Thou hast made thy beauty to be abhorred
Ezekiel16:26 Thou hast committed fornication with the Egyptians thy neighbours
Ezekiel16:27 Behold
Ezekiel16:28 Thou hast played the whore also with the Assirians
Ezekiel16:29 Thus hast thou still committed thy fornication from the land of Canaan unto the Caldees
Ezekiel16:30 How should I circumcise thine heart (sayeth the Lord GOD)(LORDE God) seeing thou doest all these things
Ezekiel16:31 building thy stewes at the head of every street
Ezekiel16:32 but as a wife that breaketh wedlock
Ezekiel16:33 Gifts are given to all other whores
Ezekiel16:34 It is come to pass with thee in thy whoredoms contrary to the use of other women: yea there hath no such fornication been committed after thee
Ezekiel16:35 Therefore hear the word of the LORD
Ezekiel16:36 thus sayeth the Lord GOD:(LORDE God) For so much as thou hast spent thy money
Ezekiel16:37 Behold therefore
Ezekiel16:38 Moreover
Ezekiel16:39 I will give thee over in to their power
Ezekiel16:40 Yea they shall bring the common people upon thee
Ezekiel16:41 They shall burn up thy houses
Ezekiel16:42 Should I make my wrath to be still
Ezekiel16:43 Seeing thou rememberest not the days of thy youth
Ezekiel16:44 Behold
Ezekiel16:45 Thou art even thy mother's own daughter
Ezekiel16:46 Thine eldest sister is Samaria
Ezekiel16:47 Yet hast thou not walked after their ways
Ezekiel16:48 As truly as I live
Ezekiel16:49 Behold
Ezekiel16:50 but were proud
Ezekiel16:51 Neither hath Samaria done half of thy sins
Ezekiel16:52 (Therefore bear thine own shame
Ezekiel16:53 As for their captivity
Ezekiel16:54 that thou mayest take thine own confusion upon thee
Ezekiel16:55 Thus thy sisters (namely) Sodoma and her daughters: Samaria and her daughters with thy self and thy daughters
Ezekiel16:56 When thou wast in thy pride
Ezekiel16:57 and before thy wickedness came to light: thou wouldest not hear speak of thy sister Sodoma
Ezekiel16:58 that thou mightest bear thine own filthiness and abomination
Ezekiel16:59 For thus sayeth the Lord GOD:(LORDE God) I should (by right) deal with thee
Ezekiel16:60 Nevertheless
Ezekiel16:61 So that thou also remember thy ways
Ezekiel16:62 And so I will renew my covenant with thee
Ezekiel16:63 That thou mayest think upon it

Ezekiel18:1 The word of the LORD came unto me
Ezekiel18:2 what mean ye by this common proverb
Ezekiel18:3 As truly as I live
Ezekiel18:4 Behold
Ezekiel18:5 If a man be godly
Ezekiel18:6 he eateth not upon the hills: he lifteth not his eyes up to the Idols of Israel
Ezekiel18:7 he grieveth nobody: he giveth his debtor his pledge again
Ezekiel18:8 he lendeth nothing upon usury: he taketh nothing over: he withdraweth his hand from doing wrong: he handleth faithfully betwixt man and man:
Ezekiel18:9 he walketh in my commandments
Ezekiel18:10 If he now get a son
Ezekiel18:11 (though he do not all) he eateth upon the hills: he defileth his neighbour's wife:
Ezekiel18:12 he grieveth the poor and needy: he robbeth and spoileth: he giveth not the debtor his pledge again
Ezekiel18:13 he lendeth upon usury
Ezekiel18:14 Now if this man get a son also
Ezekiel18:15 Namely
Ezekiel18:16 he vexeth no man: he keepeth no man's pledge
Ezekiel18:17 he opresseth not the poor: he receiveth no usury
Ezekiel18:18 As for his father: because he oppressed and spoiled his brother
Ezekiel18:19 And yet say ye: Wherefore then should not this son bear his father's sin? Therefore: because the son hath done equity and right
Ezekiel18:20 The same soul that sinneth
Ezekiel18:21 But if the ungodly will turn away from all his sins that he hath done
Ezekiel18:22 As for all his sins that he did before
Ezekiel18:23 For have I any pleasure in the death of a sinner? sayeth the Lord GOD:(LORDE God) but rather that he convert and live.(For I desire not the death of a sinner (saith the Lorde Iehouah) but rather that he should turn from his way
Ezekiel18:24 Again: If the righteous turn away from his righteousness
Ezekiel18:25 And yet ye say: Tush
Ezekiel18:26 When a righteous man turneth away from his righteousness
Ezekiel18:27 Again: when the wicked man turneth away from his wickedness
Ezekiel18:28 For in so much as he remembereth himself
Ezekiel18:29 And yet sayeth the house of Israel: Tush
Ezekiel18:30 As for me
Ezekiel18:31 Cast away from you all your ungodliness
Ezekiel18:32 seeing I have no pleasure in the death of him that dieth
Ezekiel19:1 But mourn thou for the princes of Israel
Ezekiel19:2 and say: Wherefore lay thy mother that lioness among the lions
Ezekiel19:3 One of her whelps she brought up
Ezekiel19:4 The heathen heard of him
Ezekiel19:5 Now when the dame saw
Ezekiel19:6 which went among the lions
Ezekiel19:7 he destroyed their palaces
Ezekiel19:8 Then came the heathen together on every side out of all countries against him
Ezekiel19:9 So they bound him with chains
Ezekiel19:10 As for thy mother
Ezekiel19:11 her stalks were so strong
Ezekiel19:12 she was rooted out in displeasure
Ezekiel19:13 But now she is planted in the wilderness
Ezekiel19:14 And there is a fire gone out of her stalks
Ezekiel20:1 In the seventh year
Ezekiel20:2 Then came the word of the LORD unto me on this manner:
Ezekiel20:3 Thou son of man: speak unto the elders of Israel
Ezekiel20:4 I will give you no answer. Wilt thou not reprove them (thou son of man) wilt thou not reprove them? Shew them the abominations of their forefathers:
Ezekiel20:5 and tell them: Thus sayeth the Lord GOD:(LORDE God) In the day when I chose Israel
Ezekiel20:6 even in the day that I lift up mine hand over them
Ezekiel20:7 Then said I unto them: Cast away every man the abominations that he hath before him
Ezekiel20:8 But they rebelled against me
Ezekiel20:9 But I would not do it
Ezekiel20:10 Now when I had carried them out of the land of Egypt
Ezekiel20:11 I gave them my comandments
Ezekiel20:12 I gave them also my holy days
Ezekiel20:13 And yet the house of Israel rebelled against me in the wilderness
Ezekiel20:14 Yet I would not do it
Ezekiel20:15 But I swore unto them in the wilderness
Ezekiel20:16 and that because they refused my laws
Ezekiel20:17 Nevertheless mine eye spared them
Ezekiel20:18 Moreover
Ezekiel20:19 for I am the LORD your God. But walk in my statutes
Ezekiel20:20 hallow my Sabbaths: for they are a token betwixt me and you
Ezekiel20:21 Notwithstanding
Ezekiel20:22 Nevertheless
Ezekiel20:23 I lift up mine hand over them also in the wilderness
Ezekiel20:24 because they had not kept my laws
Ezekiel20:25 Wherefore I gave them also comandments not good
Ezekiel20:26 and I unhallowed them in their own gifts (when I appointed for my self all their firstborn) to make them desolate: that they might know
Ezekiel20:27 Therefore (O thou son of man) tell the house of Israel
Ezekiel20:28 For after I had brought them into the land
Ezekiel20:29 Then I ask them; What have ye to do with all
Ezekiel20:30 Wherefore
Ezekiel20:31 In all your idols
Ezekiel20:32 and as for the thing that ye go about
Ezekiel20:33 As truly as I live
Ezekiel20:34 and will bring you out of the nations and lands
Ezekiel20:35 and will bring you into the wilderness of the people
Ezekiel20:36 Like as I punished your forefathers in the wilderness
Ezekiel20:37 I will bring you under my jurisdiction
Ezekiel20:38 The forsakers also and the transgressors will I take from among you
Ezekiel20:39 Go now then (sayeth the Lord GOD)(LORDE God) ye house of Israel
Ezekiel20:40 But upon my holy hill
Ezekiel20:41 I will accept your sweet savour
Ezekiel20:42 And that ye may know
Ezekiel20:43 There shall ye call to remembrance your own ways and all your imaginations
Ezekiel20:44 And ye shall know
Ezekiel20:45 Moreover
Ezekiel20:46 Thou son of man
Ezekiel20:47 and say to the wood toward the south: Hear the word of the LORD
Ezekiel20:48 And all flesh shall see
Ezekiel20:49 Then said I: O LORD

Ezekiel21:2 Thou son of man
Ezekiel21:3 Thus sayeth the LORD God: Behold
Ezekiel21:4 Seeing then that I will root out of thee both the righteous and wicked
Ezekiel21:5 that all flesh may know (how) that I the LORD have drawn my sword out of the sheath
Ezekiel21:6 Mourn therefore (O thou son of man) that thy loins crack withal
Ezekiel21:7 And if they say
Ezekiel21:8 Again
Ezekiel21:9 Thou son of man
Ezekiel21:10 Sharpened is it for the slaughter
Ezekiel21:11 He hath put his sword to the dighting
Ezekiel21:12 Cry (O thou son of man) and howl
Ezekiel21:13 For wherefore should not the plague and staff of judgement come?(Smite therefore thou upon thy thigh
Ezekiel21:14 Prophesy thou son of man
Ezekiel21:15 to make them abashed and faint at the hearts
Ezekiel21:16 Get thee to some place alone
Ezekiel21:17 I will smite my hands together also and satisfy my wrothfull indignation: Even I the LORD have said it.
Ezekiel21:18 The word of the LORD came yet unto me again saying:
Ezekiel21:19 Thou son of man
Ezekiel21:20 Make thee a street
Ezekiel21:21 For the king of Babylon shall stand in the turning of the way
Ezekiel21:22 But the soothsaying shall point to the right side upon Jerusalem
Ezekiel21:23 Nevertheless
Ezekiel21:24 Therefore sayeth the Lord GOD:(LORDE God) for so much as ye yourselves shew your offense
Ezekiel21:25 O thou shameful wicked guide of Israel whose day is come: even the time that wickedness shall have an end:
Ezekiel21:26 Thus sayeth the Lord GOD:(LORDE God) take away the mitre
Ezekiel21:27 Punish
Ezekiel21:28 And thou (O son of man) prophesy
Ezekiel21:29 (because thou hast looked thee out vanities
Ezekiel21:30 Though it were put up again into the sheath
Ezekiel21:31 and pour my indignation upon thee
Ezekiel21:32 Thou shalt feed the fire and thy blood shall be shed in the land
Ezekiel22:1 Moreover

Ezekiel22:9 Murderers are there in thee

Ezekiel22:11 Every man hath dealt shamefully with his neighbour's wife
Ezekiel22:12 Yea gifts have been received in thee
Ezekiel22:13 Behold

Ezekiel22:15 I will scatter thee among the heathen

Ezekiel22:19 Therefore
Ezekiel22:20 like as silver

Ezekiel22:31 Therefore will I poured out my cruel displeasure upon them
Ezekiel23:1 The word of the LORD came unto me
Ezekiel23:2 Thou son of man
Ezekiel23:3 These (when they were young) began to play the harlots in Egypt. There were their breasts bruised
Ezekiel23:4 The eldest of them was called Oholah and her youngest sister Oholibah. These two were mine
Ezekiel23:5 As for Oholah she began to go a whoring
Ezekiel23:6 which had to do with her: even the princes and lords
Ezekiel23:7 Thus thorow her whoredom
Ezekiel23:8 Neither ceased she from the fornication
Ezekiel23:9 Wherefore
Ezekiel23:10 These discovered her shame
Ezekiel23:11 Her sister Aholibah saw this
Ezekiel23:12 she loved the Assyrians
Ezekiel23:13 Then I saw
Ezekiel23:14 But she increased still in whoredom: for when she saw men painted upon the wall
Ezekiel23:15 with fair girdles about them
Ezekiel23:16 immediatley
Ezekiel23:17 Now when the Babylonians came to her
Ezekiel23:18 her whoredom and shame was discovered and seen: then my heart forsook her
Ezekiel23:19 Nevertheless
Ezekiel23:20 she brent in lust upon them
Ezekiel23:21 Thus thou hast renewed the filthiness of thy youth
Ezekiel23:22 Therefore (O Oholibah) thus sayeth the Lord GOD.(LORDE God) I will raise up thy lovers (with whom thou hast satisfied thy lust) against thee
Ezekiel23:23 Namely
Ezekiel23:24 These shall come upon thee with horses
Ezekiel23:25 I will put my jealousy upon thee
Ezekiel23:26 They shall strip thee out of thy clothes
Ezekiel23:27 Thus will I make an end of thy filthiness and whoredom
Ezekiel23:28 For thus sayeth the LORD: behold
Ezekiel23:29 which shall deal cruelly with thee: All thy labour shall they take with them
Ezekiel23:30 All these things shall happen unto thee
Ezekiel23:31 Thou hast walked in the way of thy sister
Ezekiel23:32 Thus sayeth the Lord GOD:(LORDE God) Thou shalt drink of thy sister's cup
Ezekiel23:33 Thou shalt be full of drunkenness and sorrow
Ezekiel23:34 The same shalt thou drink
Ezekiel23:35 Therefore thus sayeth the Lord GOD:(LORDE God) For so much as thou hast forgotten me
Ezekiel23:36 The LORD said moreover unto me: Thou son of man
Ezekiel23:37 namely
Ezekiel23:38 Yea and this have they done unto me also: they have defiled my Sanctuary in that same day
Ezekiel23:39 For when they had slain their children for their idols
Ezekiel23:40 Beside all this
Ezekiel23:41 thou satest upon a goodly bed
Ezekiel23:42 Then was there great cheer with her: and the men that were sent from far countries over the desert
Ezekiel23:43 Then thought I: no doubt
Ezekiel23:44 And they went into her
Ezekiel23:45 O ye all that love virtue and righteousness
Ezekiel23:46 Wherefore thus sayeth the Lord GOD:(LORDE God) bring a great multitude of people upon them
Ezekiel23:47 these shall stone them and gore them with their swords. They shall slay their sons and daughters and burn up their houses with fire.
Ezekiel23:48 Thus will I destroy all such filthiness out of the land
Ezekiel23:49 And so they shall lay your filthiness upon your own selves

Ezekiel27:1 The word of the LORD came unto me

Ezekiel27:4 thy borders are in the middest of the sea
Ezekiel27:5 All thy tables have they made of Cypress trees of the mount Sanir. From Libanus have they taken Cedar trees
Ezekiel27:6 and the Oaks of Basan to make the rowers. Thy boards have they made of ivory
Ezekiel27:7 Thy sail was of white small needle work out of the land of Egypt
Ezekiel27:8 They of Sidon and Arnad were thy mariners
Ezekiel27:9 The eldest and wisest at Gebal were they
Ezekiel27:10 The Perses
Ezekiel27:11 They of Arnad were with thine host round about thy walls
Ezekiel27:12 Tharsis occupied with thee in all manner of wares
Ezekiel27:13 Javan

Ezekiel27:15 They of Dedan were thy merchants: and many other Isles that occupied with thee

Ezekiel27:17 Judah and the land of Israel occupied with thee
Ezekiel27:18 Damascus also used merchandises with thee
Ezekiel27:19 Dan
Ezekiel27:20 Dedan occupied with thee
Ezekiel27:21 Arabia and all the princes of Cedar have occupied with thee
Ezekiel27:22 The merchants of Sheba and Rema have occupied also with thee
Ezekiel27:23 Haran
Ezekiel27:24 and occupied with thee: In costly raiment
Ezekiel27:25 The ships of Tharsis were the chief of thy occupying. Thus thou art full
Ezekiel27:26 Thy mariners were ever bringing unto thee out of many waters. But the east wind shall over bear thee into the middest of the sea:
Ezekiel27:27 so that thy wares
Ezekiel27:28 The suburbs shall shake at the loud cry of thy shipmen.
Ezekiel27:29 All whirry men
Ezekiel27:30 They shall lift up their voice because of thee
Ezekiel27:31 They shall shave themselves
Ezekiel27:32 and heavy lamentation
Ezekiel27:33 When thy wares and merchandise came from the seas
Ezekiel27:34 But now art thou cast down in to the deep of the sea
Ezekiel27:35 All they that dwell in the Isles are abashed at thee
Ezekiel27:36 The merchants of the nations wonder at thee. In that thou art so clean brought to naught

Ezekiel32:1 In the twelfth year
Ezekiel32:2 thou son of man
Ezekiel32:3 Thus sayeth the Lord GOD:(LORDE God) I will spread my net over thee
Ezekiel32:4 for I will cast thee upon the land
Ezekiel32:5 Thy flesh will I cast upon the hills
Ezekiel32:6 I will water the land with the abundance of thy blood even to the mountains
Ezekiel32:7 When thou art put out
Ezekiel32:8 All the lights of heaven will I put out over thee
Ezekiel32:9 I will trouble the hearts of many people
Ezekiel32:10 Yea I will make many people with their kings so afraid thorow thee

Ezekiel32:14 Then will I make their waters clear
Ezekiel32:15 when I make the land of Egypt desolate

Ezekiel32:19 Down (how fair soever thou be) and lay thee with the uncircumcised.

Ezekiel32:21 The mighty worthies and his helpers

Ezekiel32:23 whose graves lie beside him in the low pit. His commons are buried round about his grave: all together wounded and slain with the sword

Ezekiel32:26 There is Mesech also and Tubal

Ezekiel32:30 Moreover
Ezekiel32:31 Now when Pharaoh seeth this
Ezekiel32:32 For I have given my fear in the land of the living. But Pharo and all his people shall lie among the uncircumcised
Ezekiel33:1 Again
Ezekiel33:2 Thou son of man
Ezekiel33:3 The same man (when he seeth the sword come upon the land) shall blow the trumpet
Ezekiel33:4 If a man now hear the noise of the trumpet and will not be warned
Ezekiel33:5 For he heard the sound of the trumpet
Ezekiel33:6 Again
Ezekiel33:7 And now (O thou son of man) I have made thee a watchman unto the house of Israel: that where as thou hearest anything out of my mouth
Ezekiel33:8 If I say unto the wicked: thou wicked
Ezekiel33:9 Nevertheless if thou warn the wicked of his way
Ezekiel33:10 Therefore (O thou son of man) speak unto the house of Israel. Yea say thus: Our offenses and sins lie upon us
Ezekiel33:11 Tell them: As truly as I live
Ezekiel33:12 Thou son of man
Ezekiel33:13 If I say unto the righteous

Ezekiel33:15 In so much that the same wicked man giveth the pledge again
Ezekiel33:16 Yea the sins that he hath done
Ezekiel33:17 And yet the children of thy people say: Tush
Ezekiel33:18 When the righteous turneth from his righteousness
Ezekiel33:19 But if the wicked turn from his wickedness
Ezekiel33:20 Yet ye say: The way of the Lord(LORDE) is not equal. O ye house of Israel

Ezekiel34:1 And the word of the LORD came unto me
Ezekiel34:2 Thou son of man
Ezekiel34:3 Ye have eaten up the fat
Ezekiel34:4 The weak have ye not holden up
Ezekiel34:5 Thus are they scattered here and there without a shepherd: yea all the beasts of the field devour them
Ezekiel34:6 My sheep go wandering upon all mountains
Ezekiel34:7 Therefore O ye shepherds
Ezekiel34:8 Thus sayeth the Lord GOD:(LORDE God) As truly as I live
Ezekiel34:9 Therefore hear the word of the LORD
Ezekiel34:10 Thus sayeth the Lord GOD:(LORDE God) Behold
Ezekiel34:11 For thus sayeth the Lord GOD:(LORDE God) Behold
Ezekiel34:12 Like as a shepherd among the flock seeketh after the sheep that are scattered abroad
Ezekiel34:13 I will bring them out from all people
Ezekiel34:14 I will feed them in right good pastures
Ezekiel34:15 I will feed my sheep myself
Ezekiel34:16 Such as be lost
Ezekiel34:17 And as for you (O my sheep) sayeth the Lord GOD:(LORDE God) I will put a difference among the sheep
Ezekiel34:18 Was it not enough for you
Ezekiel34:19 Thus my sheep must be fain to eat the thing
Ezekiel34:20 Therefore
Ezekiel34:21 for so much as ye have shot the weak sheep upon the sides and shoulders
Ezekiel34:22 I will help my sheep
Ezekiel34:23 I will raise up to them one only shepherd: even my servant David
Ezekiel34:24 I the LORD will be their God
Ezekiel34:25 Moreover
Ezekiel34:26 Good fortune and prosperity will I give them
Ezekiel34:27 that the trees in the wood may bring forth their fruits
Ezekiel34:28 They shall no more be spoiled of the Heathen
Ezekiel34:29 I will set up an excellent plant for them
Ezekiel34:30 Thus shall they understand
Ezekiel34:31 Ye men are my flock

Ezekiel37:6 I will give yow sinowes

Ezekiel40:1 In the twenty fifth year of our captivity

Ezekiel40:4 and said unto me: mark well with thine eyes
Ezekiel40:5 Behold
Ezekiel40:6 Then came he unto the east door
Ezekiel40:7 between the chambers were five cubits. The post of the door within the porch
Ezekiel40:8 He measured also the porche of the innermer door which contained a meterod.
Ezekiel40:9 Then measured he the entry of the door
Ezekiel40:10 The chambers of the door eastward
Ezekiel40:11 After this
Ezekiel40:12 The edge before the chambers was one cubit broad upon both the sides
Ezekiel40:13 He measured the door from the rigge of one chamber to another
Ezekiel40:14 He made pillars also sixty cubits high

Ezekiel48:1 These are the names of the tribes that lie upon the north side
Ezekiel48:2 Upon the borders of Dan from the east side unto the west
Ezekiel48:3 Upon the borders of Asher from the east part unto the west shall Nephthali have his portion.
