Ezekiel48:6 Upon the borders of Ephraim from the east part unto the west
Ezekiel48:7 Upon the borders of Reuben from the east quarter unto the west shall Judah have his portion.
Ezekiel48:8 Upon the borders of Judah from the east part unto the west
Ezekiel48:9 As for the portion
Ezekiel48:10 Which separated holy portion shall belong unto these: namely to the priests
Ezekiel48:11 Yea this same place shall be the priests
Ezekiel48:12 and this separated piece that they have of the land
Ezekiel48:13 And next unto the priests
Ezekiel48:14 Of this portion they shall sell nothing
Ezekiel48:15 The other five thousand after the breadth that lieth by the twenty five thousand shall be common: it shall belong to the city and to the suburbs for habitations
Ezekiel48:16 Let this be the measure: toward the north part five hundredth and four thousand toward the south part five hundredth and four thousand
Ezekiel48:17 The suburbs hard upon the city
Ezekiel48:18 As for the residue of the length
Ezekiel48:19 They that labour for the wealth of the city
Ezekiel48:20 All that is separated of the twenty five thousand long and twenty five thousand broad on the four parts
Ezekiel48:21 The residue upon both the sides of the Sanctuary and possession of the city
Ezekiel48:22 Moreover
Ezekiel48:23 Now of the other tribes. From the east part unto the west
Ezekiel48:24 Upon the borders of Benjamin from the east side unto the west
Ezekiel48:25 Upon the borders of Simeon from the east part unto the west
Ezekiel48:26 Upon the borders of Izakar from the east side unto the west
Ezekiel48:27 Upon the borders of Sabulon from the east part unto the west
Ezekiel48:28 Upon the borders of Gad southward
Ezekiel48:29 This is the land with his portions
Ezekiel48:30 Thus wide shall the city reach: upon the north part five hundredth and four thousand measures.
Ezekiel48:31 The ports of the city
Ezekiel48:32 Upon the east side five hundredth and four thousand measures
Ezekiel48:33 Upon the south side five hundredth and four thousand measures
Ezekiel48:34 And upon the west side five hundredth and four thousand measures
Ezekiel48:35 Thus shall it have eighteen thousand measures round about. And from that time forth
Daniel1:1 In the third year of the reign of Jehoakim king of Judah
Daniel1:2 and the Lord(LORDE) delivered Jehoakim the king of Judah into his hand
Daniel1:3 And the king spake unto Asphanaz the chief chamberlain
Daniel1:4 young springalds without any blemish
Daniel1:5 Unto these the king appointed a certain portion of his own meat
Daniel1:6 Among these now were certain of the children of Judah: namely Daniel
Daniel1:7 Unto these the chief chamberlain gave other names
Daniel1:8 But Daniel was at a point with himself
Daniel1:9 So God gave Daniel favour and grace before the chief chamberlain
Daniel1:10 that he said unto him: I am afraid of my lord the king
Daniel1:11 Then Daniel answered Melassar
Daniel1:12 O prove but ten days with thy servants
Daniel1:13 Then look upon our faces
Daniel1:14 So he consented to them in this matter
Daniel1:15 And after the ten days
Daniel1:16 Thus Melassar took away their meat and wine
Daniel1:17 God gave now these four springalds cunning and learning in all scripture and wisdom: but unto Daniel specially
Daniel1:18 Now when the time was expired
Daniel1:19 and the king communed with them. But among them all were found none such as Daniel
Daniel1:20 Therefore stood they before the king which in all wisdom and matters of understanding
Daniel1:21 And Daniel abode still
Daniel2:1 In the second year of the reign of Nabuchodnosor
Daniel2:2 Then the king commanded to call together all the soothsayers
Daniel2:3 And the king said unto them: I have dreamed a dream
Daniel2:4 Upon this the Caldees answered the king in the Syrians speech: O king
Daniel2:5 The king gave the Caldees their answer
Daniel2:6 But if ye tell me the dream and the meaning thereof
Daniel2:7 They answered again
Daniel2:8 Then the king answered
Daniel2:9 Therefore
Daniel2:10 Upon this
Daniel2:11 for it is a very hard matter
Daniel2:12 For the which cause the king was wroth with great indignation
Daniel2:13 and the proclamation went forth
Daniel2:14 Then Daniel enquired Arioch the king's steward
Daniel2:15 He answered
Daniel2:16 Upon this
Daniel2:17 and then came he home again and shewed the thing unto Ananias
Daniel2:18 that they should beseech the God of heaven for grace in this secret
Daniel2:19 Then was the mystery shewed unto Daniel in a vision by night. And Daniel praised the God of heaven.
Daniel2:20 Daniel also cried aloud
Daniel2:21 he changeth the times and ages: he putteth down kings
Daniel2:22 he openeth the deep secrets: he knoweth the thing that lieth in darkness
Daniel2:23 I thank thee
Daniel2:24 Upon this went Daniel in unto Arioch
Daniel2:25 Then Arioch brought Daniel into the king in all the haste
Daniel2:26 Then answered the king
Daniel2:27 Daniel answered the king to his face
Daniel2:28 Only God in heaven can open secrets
Daniel2:29 O king
Daniel2:30 As for me
Daniel2:31 Thou king sawest
Daniel2:32 The image head was of fine gold
Daniel2:33 his legs were of iron
Daniel2:34 This thou sawest
Daniel2:35 Then was the iron
Daniel2:36 This is the dream. And now will we shew before the king
Daniel2:37 O king
Daniel2:38 And hath delivered thee all things
Daniel2:39 After thee there shall arise another kingdom
Daniel2:40 The fourth kingdom shall be as strong as iron. For like as iron bruiseth and breaketh all things: Yea even as iron beateth everything down
Daniel2:41 Where as thou sawest the feet and toes
Daniel2:42 The toes of the feet that were part of iron and part of clay
Daniel2:43 And whereas thou sawest iron mixt with clay: they shall mingle themselves with the seed of simple people: and yet not continue one with another
Daniel2:44 In the days of these kings
Daniel2:45 And where as thou sawest
Daniel2:46 Then the king Nabuchodnosor fell down upon his face
Daniel2:47 The king answered Daniel
Daniel2:48 So the king made Daniel a great man
Daniel2:49 Now Daniel intreated the king for Sidrach

Daniel4:35 in comparison of whom all they that dwell upon the earth

Daniel8:10 It grew up to the host of heaven
Daniel8:11 Yea it grew up unto the prince of the host
Daniel8:12 And a certain season was given unto it

Daniel8:24 He shall be mighty and strong
Daniel8:25 And thorow his craftiness
Daniel8:26 And this vision that is shewed unto thee
Daniel8:27 Upon this was I Daniel very faint

Daniel11:1 And in the first year of Darius of Medea
Daniel11:2 and now will I shew thee the truth. Behold
Daniel11:3 Then shall there arise yet a mighty king
Daniel11:4 And as soon as his kingdom cometh up
Daniel11:5 And the king of the south shall be mightier
Daniel11:6 But after certain years they shall be joined together
Daniel11:7 Out of the branches of her root
Daniel11:8 he shall carry them away captives into Egypt
Daniel11:9 And when he is come into the king's realm of the south
Daniel11:10 Wherefore his sons shall be displeased
Daniel11:11 Then the king of the south shall be angry
Daniel11:12 These shall he carry away with great pride
Daniel11:13 For the king of the north shall gather (of the new) a greater heap of people than afore
Daniel11:14 At the same time there shall many stand up against the king of the south
Daniel11:15 So the king of the north shall come to lay siege
Daniel11:16 Shortly
Daniel11:17 He shall set his face with all his power to obtain his kingdom
Daniel11:18 After this shall he set his face unto the Isles
Daniel11:19 Thus shall he turn again to his own land
Daniel11:20 so that he that came upon him and did him violence
Daniel11:21 In his stead there shall arise a vile person
Daniel11:22 he shall fight against the armies of the mighty (and destroy them) yea and against the prince of the covenant.
Daniel11:23 So after that he hath take truce with him
Daniel11:24 and so with craftiness to get him to the fattest place of the land
Daniel11:25 His power and heat shall be stirred up with a great army against the king of the south: where thorow the king of the south shall be moved then unto battle
Daniel11:26 Yea they that eat of his meat
Daniel11:27 These two kings shall be minded to do mischief
Daniel11:28 Then shall he go home again into his own land with great good
Daniel11:29 At the time appointed he shall come again
Daniel11:30 And why? the ships of Cithum shall come upon him
Daniel11:31 He shall set mighty men to unhallow the Sanctuary of strength
Daniel11:32 And such as break the covenant shall he flatter with fair words. But the people that will know their God
Daniel11:33 Those also that have understanding among the people shall inform(enfourme) the multitude: and for a long season
Daniel11:34 Now when they fall
Daniel11:35 Yea some of those which have understanding shall be persecuted also: that they may be tried
Daniel11:36 The king shall do what him list
Daniel11:37 He shall not regard the God of his fathers
Daniel11:38 In his place shall he worship the mighty Idols: and the God whom his fathers knew not
Daniel11:39 This shall he do
Daniel11:40 In the latter time
Daniel11:41 he shall enter also in to the fair pleasant land. Many cities and countries shall decay
Daniel11:42 He shall stretch forth his hands upon the countries
Daniel11:43 For thorow his going in
Daniel11:44 Nevertheless the tidings out of the East and the north shall trouble him
Daniel11:45 The tents of his palace shall he pitch betwixt the two seas

Hosea7:1 When I undertake to make Israel whole

Hosea7:12 but while they be going here and there

Hosea7:16 They turn themselves
Hosea8:1 Set the horn to thy mouth
Hosea8:2 Israel can say unto me: thou art my God

Hosea8:4 They have ordained kings
Hosea8:5 Thy calf
Hosea8:6 For the calf came from Israel
Hosea8:7 They have sown wind

Hosea8:9 Since they went up to the Assyrians

Hosea8:12 Though I shew them my law never so much
Hosea8:13 Where as they do sacrifice

Hosea10:4 They commune(comon) together
Hosea10:5 They that dwell in Samaria have worshiped the calf of Bethaven: therefore shall the people mourn over them
Hosea10:6 It shall be brought to the Assyrian for a present unto king Jareb. Ephraim shall receive full punishment: Israel shall be confounded for his own imaginations
Hosea10:7 Samaria with his king shall vanish away
Hosea10:8 The high places of Aven
Hosea10:9 O Israel
Hosea10:10 I will chasten them
Hosea10:11 Ephraim was unto me
Hosea10:12 that they might sow unto righteousness
Hosea10:13 But now they have plowed them wickedness
Hosea10:14 there shall grow a sedition among thy people. All thy strong cities shall be laid waste
Hosea10:15 Even so shall it go with you (O Bethel) because of your malicious wickedness. Like as the morning goeth away
Hosea11:1 When Israel was young
Hosea11:2 But the more they were called
Hosea11:3 I learned Ephraim to go
Hosea11:4 I led them with cords of friendship
Hosea11:5 that they should not go again into Egypt. And now is Assur their king: for they would not turn unto me.
Hosea11:6 Therefore shall the sword begin in their cities
Hosea11:7 My people hath no lust to turn unto me
Hosea11:8 What great things have I given thee
Hosea11:9 therefore have I not turned me to destroy Ephraim in my wrothful displeasure. For I am God
Hosea11:10 The LORD roareth like a lion
Hosea11:11 that they may be scattered(scarred) away from Egypt
Hosea11:12 But Ephraim goeth about me with lies
Hosea12:1 Ephraim keepeth the air
Hosea12:2 The LORD hath a court to hold with Judah
Hosea12:3 He took his brother by the heel
Hosea12:4 He strove with the angel
Hosea12:5 Yea the LORD God of hosts
Hosea12:6 Then turn to thy God
Hosea12:7 But the merchant hath a false weight in his hand
Hosea12:8 Ephraim thinketh thus: Tush
Hosea12:9 Yet am I the LORD thy God
Hosea12:10 I have spoken thorow the prophets
Hosea12:11 But at Gilead is the abomination
Hosea12:12 Jacob fled into the land of Syria
Hosea12:13 By a prophet the LORD brought them out of Egypt
Hosea12:14 But Ephraim hath provoked him to displeasure thorow his abominations; therefore shall his blood be poured upon himself
Hosea13:1 The abomination of Ephraim is come also into Israel. He is gone back to Baal
Hosea13:2 And now they sin more and more: of their silver
Hosea13:3 Therefore they shall be as the morning cloud
Hosea13:4 I am the LORD thy God
Hosea13:5 I took diligent heed of thee in the wilderness
Hosea13:6 But when they were well fed and had enough
Hosea13:7 Therefore will I be unto them as a lion
Hosea13:8 I will come upon them as a she bear
Hosea13:9 O Israel
Hosea13:10 Where are thy kings now
Hosea13:11 well
Hosea13:12 The wickedness of Ephraim is bound together
Hosea13:13 Therefore shall sorrows come upon him
Hosea13:14 had not I defended him from the grave
Hosea13:15 Yet can I see no comforth
Hosea13:16 As for Samaria
Hosea14:1 O Israel
Hosea14:2 Take these words with you
Hosea14:3 Assur shall be no more our helper
Hosea14:4 O (if they would do this) I should heal their sores: yea with all my heart would I love them: so that my wrath should clean be turned away from them:(I will heal their obedience and will love them of mine own accord: for my wrath is ceased from them.)
Hosea14:5 Yea I would be unto Israel as the dew
Hosea14:6 His branches should spread out abroad
Hosea14:7 They that dwell under his shadow
Hosea14:8 O Ephraim what have I to do with Idols any more? I will graciously hear him
Hosea14:9 Who so is wise
Joel1:1 This is the word of the LORD
Joel1:2 Hear O ye elders: ponder this well
Joel1:3 Tell your childern of it
Joel1:4 Look what the caterpillar hath left
Joel1:5 Wake up ye drunkards
Joel1:6 Yea a mighty and an innumerable people shall come up into my land: these have teeth like the teeth of Lyons
Joel1:7 They shall make my vineyard waste
Joel1:8 Make thy moan as a virgin doth that girdeth herself with sack
Joel1:9 For the meat and drink offering shall be taken away from the house of the LORD: and the priests the LORD's ministers shall mourn.
Joel1:10 The field shall be wasted
Joel1:11 The husband men and the wine gardeners shall look piteously and make lamentation
Joel1:12 The grape gathers shall make great moan
Joel1:13 Gird you
Joel1:14 Proclaim a fasting
Joel1:15 alas
Joel1:16 Shall not the meats be taken away before our eyes
Joel1:17 The seed shall perish in the ground
Joel1:18 O what a fighting make the cattle? the bullocks are very evil liking
Joel1:19 O LORD
Joel1:20 Yea the wild beasts cry also unto thee

Joel2:2 a dark day

Joel2:6 The folk shall be afraid of him
Joel2:7 These shall run like giants
Joel2:8 There shall not one drive another
Joel2:9 They shall come in to the city
Joel2:10 The earth shall quake before him

Joel2:13 rent your hearts

Joel2:18 Then shall the LORD be jealous over his land
Joel2:19 yea the LORD shall answer
Joel2:20 Again

Joel2:22 Be not ye afraid neither (O ye beasts of the field) for the pastures shall be green and the trees shall bear their fruit: the fig trees and vineyards shall give their increase.
Joel2:23 Be glad then (O ye children of Sion) and rejoice in the LORD your God
Joel2:24 so that the garners shall be full of corn
Joel2:25 And as for the years that the grasshopper
Joel2:26 so that ye shall have enough to eat

Joel3:2 I shall gather all people together
Joel3:3 yea they have cast lots for my people

Joel3:5 for ye have taken away my silver and gold

Joel3:7 Behold therefore

Amos1:1 These are the sermons that were shewed unto Amos (which was one of the shepherds at Thecua) upon Israel
Amos1:2 And he said: The LORD shall roar out of Sion
Amos1:3 Thus sayeth the LORD: for three and four wickednesses of Damascus
Amos1:4 But I will send a fire into the house of Hazael
Amos1:5 Thus will I break the bars of Damascus
Amos1:6 Thus sayeth the LORD: For three and four wickednesses of Gaza
Amos1:7 Therefore will I send a fire in to the walls of Gaza
Amos1:8 I will root out them that dwell at Asdod
Amos1:9 Thus sayeth the LORD: For three and four wickednesses of the city of Tire
Amos1:10 Therefore will I send a fire into the walls of Tire
Amos1:11 Thus sayeth the LORD: For three and four wickednesses of Edom
Amos1:12 Therefore will I send a fire into Theman
Amos1:13 Thus sayeth the LORD: For three and four wickednesses of the children of Ammon
Amos1:14 Therefore I will kindle a fire in the walls of Rabbath
Amos1:15 So that their king shall go into captivity
Amos2:1 Thus sayeth the LORD: For three and four wickednesses of Moab
Amos2:2 Therefore will I send a fire into Moab
Amos2:3 I will root out the judge from among them
Amos2:4 Thus sayeth the LORD: For three and four wickednesses of Judah
Amos2:5 Therefore will I send a fire into Judah
Amos2:6 Thus sayeth the LORD: For three and four wickednesses of Israel
Amos2:7 They tread upon poor men's heads
Amos2:8 they lie beside every alter upon clothes taken to pledge
Amos2:9 Yet destroyed I the Amorite before them
Amos2:10 Again: I brought you out of the land of Egypt
Amos2:11 I raised up prophets among your children
Amos2:12 But ye gave the abstainers wine to drink
Amos2:13 Behold
Amos2:14 so that the swift shall not escape
Amos2:15 The archer shall not abide
Amos2:16 and he that is as manly of stomach as a giant
Amos3:1 Hear
Amos3:2 You only have I accepted from all the generations of the earth: therefore will I visit you in all your wickednesses.
Amos3:3 May twain walk together except they be agreed among themselves?
Amos3:4 Doth a lion roar in the woods
Amos3:5 Doth a bird fall in a snare upon the earth where no fouler is? Taketh a man his snare up from the ground
Amos3:6 Cry they out Alarum with the trumpet in the city
Amos3:7 Now doth the Lord GOD(LORDE God) no manner of thing
Amos3:8 When a lion roareth
Amos3:9 Preach in the palaces at Asded
Amos3:10 sayeth the LORD: they gather together evil gotten goods

Amos3:14 That when I begin to visit the wickedness of Israel

Amos4:3 Ye shall get you out at the gaps one after another

Amos4:5 Ye made a thank offering of leaven
Amos4:6 Therefore have I given you idle teeth in all your cities
Amos4:7 When there were but three months unto the harvest
Amos4:8 Wherefore two
Amos4:9 I have smitten you with drought and blasting: and look how many orchards
Amos4:10 Pestilence have I sent among you
Amos4:11 Some of you have I overthrown as I overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah: so that ye were as a brand plucked(plucte) out of the fire. yet will ye not turn unto me
Amos4:12 Therefore thus will I handle thee again
Amos4:13 For lo

Amos5:15 Hate the evil

Obadiah1:1 This is the vision that was shewed unto Abdy: Thus hath the Lord GOD(LORDE God) spoken upon Edom: We have heard of the LORD that there is an embassage sent among the Heathen: Up
Obadiah1:2 Behold
Obadiah1:3 The pride of thine heart hath lift thee up
Obadiah1:4 But though thou wentest up as high as the Aegle
Obadiah1:5 If the thieves and robbers came to thee by night
Obadiah1:6 But how shall they rip Esau
Obadiah1:7 Yea the men that were sworn unto thee
Obadiah1:8 Shall not I at the same time destroy the wise men of Edom
Obadiah1:9 Thy giants (O Theman) shall be afraid
Obadiah1:10 Shame shall come upon thee
Obadiah1:11 and that because of the time
Obadiah1:12 Thou shalt no more see the day of thy brother
Obadiah1:13 Thou shalt no more come in at the gates of my people
Obadiah1:14 neither shalt thou stand(stode) waiting anymore at the corners of the streets
Obadiah1:15 For the day of the LORD is hard by upon all Heathen. Like as thou hast done
Obadiah1:16 For like wise as ye have drunken upon mine holy hill
Obadiah1:17 But upon the mount Sion
Obadiah1:18 Moreover
Obadiah1:19 They of the South shall have the mount of Esau in possession: and look what lieth upon the ground
Obadiah1:20 And this host shall be the children of Israel's prisoners: Now what so lieth from Canaan unto Zareptah
Obadiah1:21 Thus they that escape upon the hill of Sion
Jonah1:1 The word of the LORD came unto the prophet Jonas the son of Amithai saying:
Jonah1:2 rise and get thee to Nineve that great city and preach unto them
Jonah1:3 And Jonas made him ready to flee to Tharsis from the presence of the LORD
Jonah1:4 But the LORD hurled a great wind in to the sea
Jonah1:5 And the mariners were afraid and cried every man unto his god
Jonah1:6 And the master of the ship came to him and said unto him
Jonah1:7 And they said one to a nother
Jonah1:8 Then they said unto him
Jonah1:9 And he answered them
Jonah1:10 Then were the men exceedingly afraid and said unto him
Jonah1:11 Then said they unto him
Jonah1:12 And he answered them
Jonah1:13 Nevertheless the men assayed with rowing to bring the ship to land: but it would not be
Jonah1:14 Wherefore they cried unto the LORD and said: O LORD let us not perish for this man's death
Jonah1:15 And then they took Jonas
Jonah1:16 And the men feared the LORD exceedingly: and sacrificed sacrifice unto the LORD: and vowed vows.
Jonah1:17 But the LORD prepared a great fish
Jonah2:1 And Jonas prayed unto the LORD his God out of the bowels of the fish.
Jonah2:2 And he said: in my tribulation I called unto the LORD
Jonah2:3 For thou hadst cast me down deep in the midst(middes) of the sea: and the flood compassed me about: and all thy waves and rolls of water went over me:
Jonah2:4 and I thought that I had been cast away out of thy sight. But I will yet again look toward thy holy temple.
Jonah2:5 The water compassed me even unto the very soul of me: the deep lay about me: and the weeds were wrapped about mine head.
Jonah2:6 And I went down unto the bottom of the hills
Jonah2:7 When my soul fainted in me
Jonah2:8 They that observe vain vanities
Jonah2:9 But I will sacrifice unto thee with the voice of thanksgiving
Jonah2:10 And the LORD spake unto the fish: and it cast out Jonas again upon the dry land.

Jonah3:7 And it was cried and commanded in Nineve by the authority of the king and of his lords
Jonah3:8 And they put on sackcloth both man and beast
Jonah3:9 who can tell whether God will turn and repent
Jonah3:10 And when God saw their works

Micah1:8 Wherefore I will mourn and make lamentation
Micah1:9 For their wound is past remedy: And why? it is come in to Judah
Micah1:10 Weep not
Micah1:11 Thou that dwellest at Sephir

Micah6:1 Harken now what the LORD sayeth: Up
Micah6:2 O hear the punishment of the LORD
Micah6:3 O my people
Micah6:4 Because I brought thee from the land of Egypt
Micah6:5 Remember (O my people) what Balach the king of Moab had imagined against thee
Micah6:6 What acceptable thing shall I offer unto the LORD? shall I bow my knee to the high God? Shall I come before him with brentofferings
Micah6:7 Hath the LORD a pleasure in many thousand rams
Micah6:8 I will shew thee
Micah6:9 that thou mayest be called a city of the LORD
Micah6:10 Should I not be displeased
Micah6:11 Or should I justify the false balances and the bag of deceitful weights
Micah6:12 among those that be full of riches unrighteously gotten: where the citizens deal with falsity
Micah6:13 Therefore I will take in hand to punish thee
Micah6:14 Thou salt eat
Micah6:15 Thou shalt sow
Micah6:16 Ye keep the ordinances of Amri

Micah7:2 There is not a godly man upon earth
Micah7:3 yet they say they do well when they do evil. As the prince will
Micah7:4 The best of them is but as a thistle
Micah7:5 Let no man believe his friend
Micah7:6 for the son shall put his father to dishonour
Micah7:7 Nevertheless I will look up unto the LORD
Micah7:8 O thou enemy of mine
Micah7:9 I will bear the punishment of the LORD (for why
Micah7:10 She that is mine enemy shall look upon it
Micah7:11 The time will come
Micah7:12 and at that time shall they come unto thee
Micah7:13 Notwithstanding the land must be wasted
Micah7:14 Therefore feed thy people with thy rod
Micah7:15 Marvelous things will I shew them
Micah7:16 This shall the Heathen see
Micah7:17 They shall lick the dust like a serpent
Micah7:18 Where is there such a God as thou? that pardonest wickedness
Micah7:19 he shall turn again

Nahum1:1 This is the heavy burthen of Ninive
Nahum1:2 The LORD is a jealous God
Nahum1:3 The LORD suffereth long
Nahum1:4 When he reproveth the sea
Nahum1:5 The mountains tremble for him
Nahum1:6 Who may endure before his wrath? Or who is able to abide his grim displeasure? His anger taketh on like fire
Nahum1:7 Full gracious is the LORD
Nahum1:8 when the flood runneth(renneth) over
Nahum1:9 What do ye imagine then against the LORD on this manner? (Tush
Nahum1:10 For like as the thorns that stick together
Nahum1:11 There come out of thee such as Imagine mischief
Nahum1:12 Therefore thus sayeth the LORD: Let them be as well prepared
Nahum1:13 And now will I break his rod from thy back
Nahum1:14 But the LORD hath given a commandment concerning thee
Nahum1:15 Behold
Nahum2:1 The scatterer shall come up against thee
Nahum2:2 for the LORD shall restore again the glory of Jacob
Nahum2:3 The shield of his giants glistereth
Nahum2:4 The chariots roll upon the streets
Nahum2:5 When he doth but warn his giants
Nahum2:6 The water ports shall be opened
Nahum2:7 The queen herself shall be led away captive
Nahum2:8 Ninive is like a pool full of water
Nahum2:9 Away with the silver
Nahum2:10 Thus must she be spoiled
Nahum2:11 Where is now the dwelling of the lions
Nahum2:12 But the lion spoiled enough for his young ones
Nahum2:13 Behold
Nahum3:1 Woe to the(that) bloodthirsty city
Nahum3:2 There a man may hear scourging
Nahum3:3 There the horsemen get up with naked swords
Nahum3:4 And that for the great and manyfold whoredom
Nahum3:5 Behold
Nahum3:6 I will cast dirt upon thee
Nahum3:7 Yea all they that look upon thee
Nahum3:8 Art thou better than the great city of Alexandria: that lay in the waters
Nahum3:9 Ethiopia and Egypt were her strength
Nahum3:10 yet was she driven away
Nahum3:11 Even so shalt thou also be drunken
Nahum3:12 All thy strong cities shall be like fig trees with ripe figs: which when a man shaketh
Nahum3:13 Behold
Nahum3:14 Draw water now against thou be besieged
Nahum3:15 yet the fire shall consume thee
Nahum3:16 Thy merchants have been numbered with(mo than) the stars of heaven: but now shall they spread abroad as the locusts
Nahum3:17 Thy lords are as the grasshoppers
Nahum3:18 Thy shepherds are asleep (O king of Assur) thy worthies are laid down: thy people is scattered abroad upon the mountains
Nahum3:19 Thy wound can not be hid
Habakkuk1:1 This is the heavy burthen

Habakkuk1:6 For lo
Habakkuk1:7 A grim and boisterous people is it
Habakkuk1:8 Their horses are swifter than the cats of the mountain
Habakkuk1:9 They come all to spoil: out of them cometh an east wind

Habakkuk1:11 Then shall they take a fresh courage unto them

Habakkuk1:13 Thine eyes are clean

Zechariah13:5 But he shall be fain to say: I am no Prophet: I am an husband man

Matthew13:44 Again the kingdom of heaven is like unto treasure hid in the field
Matthew13:45 Again
Matthew13:46 which when he had found one precious pearl
Matthew13:47 Again the kingdom of heaven is like unto a net cast in to the sea
Matthew13:48 the which when it is full
Matthew13:49 So shall it be at the end of the world. The angels shall come(go out) and sever the bad from the good
Matthew13:50 and shall cast them in to a furnace of fire

Matthew22:22 When they heard that they marveled

Matthew22:30 For in the resurrection they neither marry nor are married: but are as the angels of God in heaven.
Matthew22:31 As touching the resurrection of the dead: have ye not read what is said unto you of God
Matthew22:32 I am Abraham's God
Matthew22:33 And when the people heard that
Matthew22:34 When the pharisees had heard

Matthew22:38 This is the first and that great(chief) commandment.(the principal and greatest commandment)
Matthew22:39 And there is another like unto this. Thou shalt love thine neighbor as thyself.
Matthew22:40 In these two commandments

Matthew22:42 saying: What think ye of Christ? whose son is he? they said unto him: The son of David.
Matthew22:43 He said unto them: How then doth David in spirit call him Lord
Matthew22:44 The Lord(LORDE) said to my Lord
Matthew22:45 If David call him Lord:(LORDE) How is he then his son?
Matthew22:46 And none of them could answer him again one word. Neither durst any from that day forth

Matthew24:5 For many shall come in my name saying: I am Christ: and shall deceive many.
Matthew24:6 Ye shall hear of wars
Matthew24:7 For nation shall rise against nation
Matthew24:8 All these are the beginning of sorrows.
Matthew24:9 Then shall they put you to trouble
Matthew24:10 And then shall many fall
Matthew24:11 And many false prophets shall arise
Matthew24:12 And because iniquity shall have the upper hand
Matthew24:13 But he that endureth to the end(the same) shall be safe.
Matthew24:14 And this gospel(glad tidings) of the kingdom shall be preached in all the world
Matthew24:15 When ye then(therefore) shall see the abomination and(that betokeneth) desolation (spoken of by Daniel the prophet) stand in the holy place: whosoever(let him that) readeth it
Matthew24:16 Then let them which be in Jury(Iewry) fly into the mountains.
Matthew24:17 And let him which is on the housetop
Matthew24:18 Neither let him which is in the field
Matthew24:19 Woe(shall) be in those days to them that are with child
Matthew24:20 But pray that your flight be not in the winter
Matthew24:21 For then shall be great tribulation
Matthew24:22 Yea and except those days should be shortened
Matthew24:23 Then if any man shall say unto you: lo
Matthew24:24 for there shall arise false christs
Matthew24:25 Take heed I have told you before.
Matthew24:26 (Wherefore) If they shall say unto you: lo
Matthew24:27 For as the lightning cometh out of the east
Matthew24:28 For wheresoever a dead body(carcass) is
Matthew24:29 Immediately after the tribulations(trouble) of those days
Matthew24:30 And then shall appear the sign of the son of man in heaven. And then shall all the kindreds of the earth mourn
Matthew24:31 And he shall send his angels with the great voice of a trumpet
Matthew24:32 Learn a similitude of the fig tree: when his branches are(branch is) yet tender
Matthew24:33 So likewise (ye
Matthew24:34 Verily I say unto you
Matthew24:35 Heaven and earth shall perish: but my words shall abide.(not perish)
Matthew24:36 But of that day and hour knoweth no man
Matthew24:37 As the time of Noe was
Matthew24:38 For as in the days before the flood: they did eat and drink
Matthew24:39 and knew of nothing(regarded it not) till the flood came and took them all away. So shall also the coming of the son of man be.
Matthew24:40 Then two shall be in the fields
Matthew24:41 two (women) shall be grinding at the mill: the one shall be received
Matthew24:42 Wake(Watch) therefore
Matthew24:43 Of this be sure
Matthew24:44 Therefore be ye also ready
Matthew24:45 Who is a(If there be any) faithful servant and wise
Matthew24:46 Happy(Blessed) is that servant whom his master (when he cometh) shall find so doing.
Matthew24:47 Verily I say unto you
Matthew24:48 But and if that evil servant shall say in his heart
Matthew24:49 and begin to smite his fellows: yea and to eat and to drink with the drunken:
Matthew24:50 that servants master will come in a day when he looketh not for him: and in an hour that he is not ware of
Matthew24:51 and will divide him
Matthew25:1 Then the kingdom of heaven shall be likened unto ten virgins
Matthew25:2 five of them were foolish
Matthew25:3 The foolish took their lamps
Matthew25:4 But the wise took oil with them in their vessels with their lamps also.
Matthew25:5 While the bridegroom tarried
Matthew25:6 And even at midnight
Matthew25:7 Then all those virgins arose
Matthew25:8 And the foolish said to the wise: give us of your oil
Matthew25:9 But the wise answered
Matthew25:10 In conclusion(And) while they went to buy
Matthew25:11 Afterwards came also the other virgins
Matthew25:12 But he answered
Matthew25:13 look that ye watch therefore
Matthew25:14 Likewise as a certain man ready to take his journey to a strange country
Matthew25:15 And unto one he gave five talents
Matthew25:16 Then he that had received the five talents
Matthew25:17 Likewise he that received two gained other two.
Matthew25:18 But he that received (the) one
Matthew25:19 After a long season the lord of those servants came
Matthew25:20 Then came he that had received five talents
Matthew25:21 (Then) His master said unto him: well good servant and faithful; Thou hast been faithful in little
Matthew25:22 Also he that received two talents came
Matthew25:23 (And) His master said unto him
Matthew25:24 (Then) He which had received the one talent came also
Matthew25:25 and was (therefore) afraid
Matthew25:26 His master answered
Matthew25:27 thou oughtest(shouldest) therefore to have had my money to the changers
Matthew25:28 Take therefore the talent from him
Matthew25:29 For unto every man that hath shall be given
Matthew25:30 And cast that unprofitable servant into utter darkness
Matthew25:31 When the son of man shall come(cometh) in his majesty
Matthew25:32 and before him shall be gathered all nations.(people) And he shall sever(separate) them one from another
Matthew25:33 And he shall set the sheep on his right hand
Matthew25:34 Then shall the King say to them on his right hand: Come ye blessed children of my father
Matthew25:35 For I was an hungered
Matthew25:36 I was sick and ye visited me. I was in prison and ye came unto me.
Matthew25:37 Then shall the just(righteous) answer him saying: master
Matthew25:38 when saw we thee harborless
Matthew25:39 or when saw we thee sick
Matthew25:40 And the King shall answer and say unto them: verily I say unto you: inasmuch as ye have done it unto one of the least of these my brethren: ye have done it to me.
Matthew25:41 Then shall the King say unto them that shall be on the left hand: depart from me ye cursed
Matthew25:42 For I was an hungered
Matthew25:43 I was naked
Matthew25:44 Then shall they also answer him saying: master
Matthew25:45 then shall he answer them
Matthew25:46 And these shall go into everlasting pain: And the righteous into life eternal.

Matthew26:2 Ye know that after two days shall be ester
Matthew26:3 Then assembled together the chief priests and (the) scribes and seniors(the elders) of the people into the palace of the high priest
Matthew26:4 and held a counsel
Matthew26:5 but they said
Matthew26:6 When Jesus was in Bethany
Matthew26:7 there came unto him a woman
Matthew26:8 When his disciples saw that
Matthew26:9 This ointment might have been well sold
Matthew26:10 When Jesus understood(perceived) that
Matthew26:11 For ye shall have poor folk always with you: but me shall ye not have always.
Matthew26:12 And in that she casted(poured) this ointment on my body
Matthew26:13 Verily I say unto you
Matthew26:14 Then one of the twelve called Judas Iscariot went unto the chief priests
Matthew26:15 and said: What will ye give me
Matthew26:16 And from that time he sought opportunity to betray him.
Matthew26:17 The first day of unleavened(sweet) bread the disciples came to Jesus saying unto him: Where wilt thou that we prepare for thee to eat the ester(paschal) lamb?
Matthew26:18 And he said: Go into the city
Matthew26:19 And the disciples did as Jesus had appointed them
Matthew26:20 When the even was come
Matthew26:21 And as they did eat
Matthew26:22 And they were exceeding sorrowful
Matthew26:23 He answered and said: He that dippeth his hand with me in the dish
Matthew26:24 The son of man goeth as it is written of him: but woe be to that man
Matthew26:25 Then Judas which betrayed him
Matthew26:26 As they did ate
Matthew26:27 And he took the cup
Matthew26:28 This is my blood of the new testament
Matthew26:29 I say unto you: I will not drink henceforth of this fruit of the vine tree
Matthew26:30 And when they had said grace
Matthew26:31 Then said Jesus unto them: All ye shall fall this night because of me.(all ye shall be offended by me this night) For it is written: I will smite the shepherd
Matthew26:32 But after I am risen again
Matthew26:33 Peter answered
Matthew26:34 Jesus said unto him: Verily I say unto thee
Matthew26:35 Peter said unto him: If I should die with thee
Matthew26:36 Then went Jesus with them into a place
Matthew26:37 And he took with him Peter and the two sons of Zebede
Matthew26:38 Then said Jesus unto them: My soul is heavy even unto the death. Tarry ye here: and watch with me.
Matthew26:39 And he went away a little apart
Matthew26:40 And he came unto the disciples
Matthew26:41 Watch and pray
Matthew26:42 He went away once more
Matthew26:43 And he came
Matthew26:44 And he left and went again
Matthew26:45 Then came he to his disciples and said unto them: Sleep henceforth
Matthew26:46 Rise
Matthew26:47 While he yet spake
Matthew26:48 (And) He that betrayed him
Matthew26:49 And forthwith-all he came to Jesus
Matthew26:50 And Jesus said unto him: friend
Matthew26:51 And behold
Matthew26:52 Then said Jesus unto him: Put up thy sword into his sheath. For all they that lay hands on the sword
Matthew26:53 Either thinkest thou that I cannot now pray to my father
Matthew26:54 (But) How then should the scriptures be fulfilled
Matthew26:55 The same time said Jesus to the multitude: Ye be come out as it were unto a thief
Matthew26:56 All this was done that the scriptures of the prophets might be fulfilled. Then all the disciples forsook him and fled.
Matthew26:57 And they took Jesus and led him to Caiphas the high priest
Matthew26:58 (And) Peter followed him afar off
Matthew26:59 The chief priests
Matthew26:60 and they(but) found none: insomuch that when many false witnesses came
Matthew26:61 and said: This fellow said: I can destroy(break down) the temple of God
Matthew26:62 And the chief priest arose
Matthew26:63 But Jesus held his peace. And the chief priest answered
Matthew26:64 Jesus said to him: Thou hast said. Nevertheless I say unto you
Matthew26:65 Then the high priest rent his clothes saying? He hath blasphemed: what need we of any more witnesses? Lo
Matthew26:66 What think ye? They answered and said: He is worthy to die.(guilty of death)
Matthew26:67 Then spat they in his face
Matthew26:68 saying: Areed to us(Prophecie)(Tell us thou) Christ
Matthew26:69 Peter sat without in the palace
Matthew26:70 (but) he denied before them all saying: I wot not what thou sayest.
Matthew26:71 When he was gone out into the porch
Matthew26:72 And again he denied with an oath
Matthew26:73 And after a while came unto him they that stood by
Matthew26:74 Then began he to curse and to swear
Matthew26:75 And Peter remembered the words of Jesu
Matthew27:1 When the morning was come
Matthew27:2 and brought him bound and delivered him unto Pontius Pilate the deputy.
Matthew27:3 Then when Judas which betrayed him
Matthew27:4 saying: I have sinned betraying the innocent blood. And they said: What is that to us? see thou to that.
Matthew27:5 And he cast down the silver plates in the temple
Matthew27:6 (And) The chief priests took the silver plates and said: It is not lawful for to put them into the treasury
Matthew27:7 And they took counsel
Matthew27:8 Wherefore that field is called
Matthew27:9 Then was fulfilled
Matthew27:10 and they gave them for the potter's field
Matthew27:11 Jesus stood before the deputy: and the deputy asked him
Matthew27:12 (And) When he was accused of the chief priests and seniors
Matthew27:13 Then said Pilate unto him: Hearest thou not how many things they lay against thee?
Matthew27:14 and he answered him to never a word: insomuch that the deputy marveled very sore.(greatly.)
Matthew27:15 At that feast
Matthew27:16 He had then a notable prisoner called Barabbas.
Matthew27:17 And when they were gathered together
Matthew27:18 For he knew well
Matthew27:19 When he was set down to give judgement
Matthew27:20 (But) The chief priests and the seniors(elders) had persuaded the people
Matthew27:21 (Then) The deputy answered and said unto them: Whether of the twain will ye that I let loose unto you? And they said
Matthew27:22 Pilate said unto them: What shall I do then with Jesus
Matthew27:23 Then said the deputy: What evil hath he done? And they cried the more saying: Let him be crucified.
Matthew27:24 When Pilate saw that he prevailed nothing
Matthew27:25 Then answered all the people
Matthew27:26 Then let he Barabbas loose unto them
Matthew27:27 Then the soldiers of the deputy took Jesus unto the common hall
Matthew27:28 And they stripped him
Matthew27:29 and plaited a crown of thorns and put upon his head
Matthew27:30 and spitted upon him
Matthew27:31 And when they had mocked him
Matthew27:32 And as they came(were going) out
Matthew27:33 And (when they) came unto the place
Matthew27:34 they gave him vinegar to drink mixt(mingled) with gall. And when he had tasted thereof
Matthew27:35 When they had crucified him
Matthew27:36 And they sat and watched him there.
Matthew27:37 And they set up over his head the cause of his death written: This is Jesus the King of the Jews.
Matthew27:38 And there were two thieves(murthurers) crucified with him
Matthew27:39 They that passed(went) by
Matthew27:40 and saying: Thou that destroyest(breakest down) the temple of God
Matthew27:41 Likewise also the prelates(hye priests) mocking him(laughed him to scorn) with the scribes and seniors(elders) said:
Matthew27:42 He saved(hath helped) other
Matthew27:43 He trusted in God
Matthew27:44 That same also the thieves
Matthew27:45 From the sixth hour was there darkness over all the land unto the ninth hour.
Matthew27:46 And about the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice
Matthew27:47 Some of them that stood there
Matthew27:48 And straightway(immediately) one of them ran and took a sponge and filled it full of vinegar
Matthew27:49 Other said let be
Matthew27:50 Jesus cried again with a loud voice and yielded up the ghost.
Matthew27:51 And behold the veil of the temple was(did) rent in twain from the top to the bottom
Matthew27:52 and the stones did rent
Matthew27:53 and came out of the graves after his resurrection
Matthew27:54 When the petty captain
Matthew27:55 And many women were there
Matthew27:56 among the which was Mary Magdalen
Matthew27:57 When the even was come
Matthew27:58 He went to Pilate and begged(asked) the body of Jesus. Then Pilate commanded the body to be delivered.(that the body should be given him)
Matthew27:59 And Joseph took the body
Matthew27:60 and put(laid) it in his new tomb
Matthew27:61 And there was Mary Magdalene and the other Mary sitting over against the sepulchre.
Matthew27:62 The next day that followeth good friday
Matthew27:63 and said: Sir
Matthew27:64 command therefore that the sepulchre be made sure until the third day
Matthew27:65 Pilate said unto them: Take watchmen: Go and make it as sure as ye can.
Matthew27:66 (And) They went and made the sepulchre sure with watchmen
Matthew28:1 The Sabbath day at even which dawneth the morrow after the Sabbath
Matthew28:2 And behold there was a great earthquake. For the angel of the Lord descended from heaven: and came and rolled back the stone from the door
Matthew28:3 His countenance was like lightning
Matthew28:4 (And) For fear of him the keepers(watchmen) were astunned
Matthew28:5 The angel answered
Matthew28:6 he is not here: he is risen as he said. Come
Matthew28:7 And go quickly and tell his disciples that he is risen from death. And behold
Matthew28:8 And they departed quickly from the sepulchre with fear and great Joy. And did run to bring his disciples word.
Matthew28:9 And as they went to tell his disciples: behold
Matthew28:10 Then said Jesus unto them: Be not afraid. Go and tell my brethren
Matthew28:11 When they were gone: behold
Matthew28:12 And they gathered them together with the seniors
Matthew28:13 saying: Say that his disciples came by night
Matthew28:14 And if this come to the ruler's ears
Matthew28:15 And they took the money and did as they were taught. And this saying is noised among the jews unto this day.
Matthew28:16 Then the eleven disciples went away into Galilee
Matthew28:17 And when they saw him
Matthew28:18 (And) Jesus came and spake unto them
Matthew28:19 Go therefore and teach all nations
Matthew28:20 Teaching them to observe(keep) all things
Mark1:1 The beginning of the Gospell of Jesu Christ the son of God
Mark1:2 as it is written in the prophets
Mark1:3 The voice of one that crieth(a crier) in the wilderness: prepare ye the way of the Lord
Mark1:4 John did baptise in the wilderness
Mark1:5 And all the land of Jewry
Mark1:6 John was clothed with camel's hair
Mark1:7 and preached saying: a stronger than I cometh after me
Mark1:8 I have baptised you with water: but he shall baptise you with the holy ghost.
Mark1:9 And it came to pass in those days
Mark1:10 And immediately he came(as soon as he was come) out of the water
Mark1:11 And there came a voice from heaven: Thou art my dear son
Mark1:12 And immediately the spirit drove(drave) him into a wilderness:
Mark1:13 and he was there in the wilderness forty days
Mark1:14 After that John was taken
Mark1:15 and saying: the time is ful come
Mark1:16 As he walked by the sea of Galilee
Mark1:17 And Jesus said unto them: follow me
Mark1:18 And they straightway forsook their nets
Mark1:19 And when he had gone a little further thence
Mark1:20 And anon he called them. And they left their father Zebede in the ship with his hired servants
Mark1:21 And they entered into Capernaum
Mark1:22 And they marveled(were astonied) at his learning. For he taught them as one which(that) had power with him
Mark1:23 And there was in the(their) synagogue
Mark1:24 saying: let me alone:(be) what have we to do with thee (thou) Jesus of Nazareth? Art thou come to destroy us? I know (thee) what thou art
Mark1:25 And Jesus rebuked him
Mark1:26 And the unclean spirit tare him
Mark1:27 And they were all amazed
Mark1:28 Anon(And immediately) his name(fame) spread abroad throughout all the region bordering on Galilee.
Mark1:29 And immediately(forthwith) as soon as they were come out of the Synagogue
Mark1:30 (And) Simon's mother-in-law lay sick of a fever
Mark1:31 And he came and took her by the hand and lifted her up
Mark1:32 And at even when the sun(son) was down
Mark1:33 and all the city gathered together at the door
Mark1:34 and he healed many that were sick of divers diseases. And he cast out many devils and suffered not the devils(them not) to speak
Mark1:35 And in the morning very early
Mark1:36 And Simon and they that were with him followed after him.
Mark1:37 And when they had found him
Mark1:38 And he said unto them: let us go in to the next towns
Mark1:39 And he preached in their synagogues
Mark1:40 And there came a leper to him beseeching him
Mark1:41 (And) Jesus had compassion on him
Mark1:42 And as soon as he had spoken
Mark1:43 And he charged him and sent him away forthwith
Mark1:44 and said unto him: See that thou tell no(say nothing to any) man
Mark1:45 But he (as soon as he was departed) began to tell many things and to publish the deed
Mark2:1 After a few days he entered into Capernaum again
Mark2:2 And anon many gathered together
Mark2:3 And there came unto him that brought one sick of the palsy
Mark2:4 and because they could not come nigh unto him for press: They opened(uncovered) the roof of the house where he was. And when they had broken it open
Mark2:5 When Jesus saw their faith
Mark2:6 (And) There were certain of the scribes sitting
Mark2:7 how doth this fellow so blaspheme? Who can forgiven sins
Mark2:8 And immediately when Jesus perceived in his spirit
Mark2:9 Whether is it easier to say to the sick of the palsy
Mark2:10 That ye may know that the son of man hath power in earth to forgive sins
Mark2:11 I say unto thee
Mark2:12 And by and by he arose
Mark2:13 And he went out again unto the sea
Mark2:14 And as Jesus passed by
Mark2:15 And it came to pass
Mark2:16 And when the scribes and pharisees saw him eat with publicans and sinners
Mark2:17 When Jesus heard that
Mark2:18 And the disciples of John and of the pharisees did fast
Mark2:19 And Jesus said unto them: can the children of a wedding fast
Mark2:20 But the days will come when the bridegroom shall be taken from them and then shall they fast in those days.
Mark2:21 Also no man soweth a piece of new cloth unto an old garment
Mark2:22 In like wise
Mark2:23 And it chanced (again) that he went thorow the corn fields on the Sabbath day
Mark2:24 And the pharisees said unto him: Take heed(behold) why do they on the Sabbath day that which is unlawful?(not lawful?)
Mark2:25 And he said to them: have ye never read what David did
Mark2:26 How they(he) went into the house of God in the days of Abiathar the high priest
Mark2:27 And he said to them: the Sabbath day was made for man
Mark2:28 Wherefore is the son of man(is) Lord even of(over) the Sabbath day.
Mark3:1 And he entered again into the synagogue
Mark3:2 and they watched him to see
Mark3:3 And he said unto the man which had the withered hand: arise and stand in the midst.
Mark3:4 And he said to them: whether is it lawful to do a good deed on the Sabbath day
Mark3:5 And he looked round about on them angerly mourning on the blindness of their hearts. And said to the man: stretch forth thine hand. And he stretched it forth:(out.) And the(his) hand was restored
Mark3:6 (And) The pharisees departed
Mark3:7 And Jesus avoided with his disciples to the sea: and a great multitude followed him from Galilee and from Jewry
Mark3:8 and from Hierusalem
Mark3:9 And he commanded his disciples
Mark3:10 For he had healed many
Mark3:11 And when the unclean(foule) spirits saw him
Mark3:12 And he straightly charged them that they should not utter him.(make him known)
Mark3:13 And he went up into a mountain
Mark3:14 And he ordained the twelve that they should be with him
Mark3:15 And that they might have power to heal sicknesses and to cast out devils.

Mark3:18 And Andrew
Mark3:19 and Judas Iscarioth

Mark3:22 And the scribes which came from Jerusalem
Mark3:23 And he called them unto him
Mark3:24 For if a realm be divided against itself
Mark3:25 And(Or) if a house be divided against itself
Mark3:26 So if Satan make insurrection against himself
Mark3:27 No man can enter into a strong man's house

Mark3:29 but he that blasphemeth the holy ghost

Mark3:32 And the people sat about him
Mark3:33 And he answered them
Mark3:34 And he looked round about on his disciples
Mark3:35 For whosoever doeth the will of God
Mark4:1 And he began again to teach by the seaside. And there gathered together unto him much people
Mark4:2 And he taught them many things in similitudes
Mark4:3 Hearken to. Behold; The sower went out to sow
Mark4:4 and it fortuned(happened) as he sowed
Mark4:5 Some fell on a stony ground: where it had not much earth: and by and by sprang up
Mark4:6 and(but) as soon as the sun was up it caught heat: and because it had not rooting it withered away.
Mark4:7 And some fell among the thorns
Mark4:8 And some fell upon good ground: and did yield fruit that sprang and grew: and brought forth some thirty fold
Mark4:9 And he said unto them: He that hath ears to hear
Mark4:10 (And) When he was alone
Mark4:11 And he said unto them: To you it is given to know the mystery of the kingdom of God: But unto them that are without
Mark4:12 that when they see
Mark4:13 And he said unto them: Perceive ye not this similitude. And how ye shall know all similitudes?(how then should ye understand all other similitudes?)
Mark4:14 The sower soweth the word.
Mark4:15 These be they which(And they that) are by the ways side
Mark4:16 And these also are(And likewise) they that are sown on the stony ground: which when they have heard the word
Mark4:17 yet have no root(rotes) in themselves
Mark4:18 And these are they that are sown among the thorns
Mark4:19 and the care of this world and the deceitfulness of riches
Mark4:20 And these(those) that were sown in good ground
Mark4:21 And he said unto them: is the candle lighted
Mark4:22 For there is nothing so privy
Mark4:23 If any man have ears to hear
Mark4:24 And he said unto them: take heed what ye hear. With what measure ye mete
Mark4:25 For unto him that hath
Mark4:26 And he said: so is the kingdom of God
Mark4:27 and should sleep and rise up night and day: and the seed should spring
Mark4:28 For the earth bringeth forth fruit of herself
Mark4:29 And as soon as the fruit is brought forth
Mark4:30 And he said: whereunto shall we liken the kingdom of God? or with what comparison shall we compare it?
Mark4:31 It is like a grain of mustard seed
Mark4:32 And(but) after that it is sown it groweth up
Mark4:33 And with many such similitudes he preached the word unto them
Mark4:34 And without similitude spake he nothing unto them. But when they were apart
Mark4:35 And the same day when even was come he said unto them: let us pass over into(unto) the other side.
Mark4:36 And they late the people depart(left the people) and took him even as he was in the ship. (And) There were also with him other ships.
Mark4:37 And there arose a great storm of wind
Mark4:38 And he was in the stern asleep on a pillow. And they awoke him
Mark4:39 And he rose up and rebuked the wind
Mark4:40 and he said unto them: why are ye (so) fearful? How is it that ye have no faith?
Mark4:41 And they feared exceedingly
Mark5:1 And they came over to the other side of the sea into the country of the Gaderens.
Mark5:2 And when he was come out of the ship
Mark5:3 which had his abiding among the graves. And no man could bind him (no not) with chains
Mark5:4 because that when he was often bound with fetters and chains
Mark5:5 And always both night and day he cried in the mountains and in the graves and beat himself with stones.
Mark5:6 When he had spied Jesus afar off
Mark5:7 and cried with a loud voice and said: what have I to do
Mark5:8 For he had said unto him: Come forth(out) of the man thou foul spirit.
Mark5:9 And he asked him: what is thy name? and he answered him
Mark5:10 And he prayed him instantly
Mark5:11 (And) There was there nigh unto the mountains a great herd of swine feeding
Mark5:12 and all the devils besought him saying: send us into the herd of swine
Mark5:13 And anon Jesus gave them leave; And the unclean spirits went out and entered into the swine. And the herd startled
Mark5:14 And the swine herders fled
Mark5:15 And they came out to Jesus
Mark5:16 And they that saw it told them
Mark5:17 And they began to pray him
Mark5:18 And when he was come into the ship
Mark5:19 (Howbeit) Jesus would not suffer him but said unto him: go home into thine own house and to thy friends
Mark5:20 And he departed
Mark5:21 And when Jesus was come over(Iesus passed over) again in the(by) ship unto the other side
Mark5:22 And behold
Mark5:23 and besought him greatly saying: my daughter lieth at point of death
Mark5:24 And he went with him
Mark5:25 And there was a (certain) woman
Mark5:26 and had suffered many things of many physicians
Mark5:27 When she had heard of Jesus: she came into the press behind him
Mark5:28 For she said:(thought:) If I may but touch his clothing
Mark5:29 And straight way her fountain of blood was dried up
Mark5:30 And Jesus immediately felt in himself
Mark5:31 And his disciples said unto him: thou seest the people thrusting thee on every side
Mark5:32 And he looked round about
Mark5:33 The woman feared and trembled
Mark5:34 And he said unto her: Daughter

Mark5:36 As soon as Jesus heard that word spoken
Mark5:37 And he suffered no man to follow him more than Peter
Mark5:38 And he came unto the house of the ruler of the Synagogue
Mark5:39 And he went in and said unto them: Why make ye this ado and weep? The maiden is not dead
Mark5:40 And they laughed him to scorn. Then he put them all out
Mark5:41 And took the maiden by the hand
Mark5:42 And straight the maiden arose
Mark5:43 And he charged them straitly that no man should know of it. And commanded to give her meat.

Mark6:44 And they that ate were about five thousand men.

Mark6:52 For they remembered not of the loaves

Mark7:1 And the pharisees came together unto him
Mark7:2 And when they saw certain of his disciples eat bread with common hands (that is to say
Mark7:3 For the pharisees
Mark7:4 And when they come from the market
Mark7:5 Then asked him the pharisees and scribes: why walk not thy disciples according to the traditions of the seniors
Mark7:6 He answered and said unto them: well prophesied hath Esaias of you hypocrites as it is written: This people honoureth me with their lips
Mark7:7 In vain they worship me
Mark7:8 for ye lay(leave) the commandment of God apart
Mark7:9 And he said unto them: well
Mark7:10 For Moses said: Honour thy father and thy mother: and whosoever saith evil of his(curseth) father or mother
Mark7:11 But ye say: a man shall say to his father or mother Corban
Mark7:12 And ye suffer no more that a man do anything for his father or mother
Mark7:13 and thus have ye made the commandment(making the word) of God of none effect through your own traditions which ye have ordained. And many such things ye do.
Mark7:14 And he called all the people unto him
Mark7:15 There is nothing with out a man that can defile him when it entereth into him
Mark7:16 If any man have ears to hear
Mark7:17 And when he came into a house away from the people
Mark7:18 and he said unto them: Do ye than lack(Are ye so without) understanding: Do ye not yet perceive
Mark7:19 because it entereth not into his heart
Mark7:20 And he said that defileth a man which cometh out of a man.
Mark7:21 For from within even out of the heart of men
Mark7:22 theft
Mark7:23 All these evil things
Mark7:24 And from thence he rose and went into the borders of Tyre and Sidon
Mark7:25 For a certain woman whose daughter had a foul spirit when she heard of him
Mark7:26 The woman was a greek out of Syrophenicia
Mark7:27 (And) Jesus said unto her: let the children first be fed. (For) It is not meet
Mark7:28 She answered and said unto him: even so Master
Mark7:29 And he said unto her: for this saying go thy way
Mark7:30 And when she was come home to her house she found the devil departed
Mark7:31 And he departed again from the coasts of Tyre and Sidon
Mark7:32 And they brought unto him one that was deaf
Mark7:33 And he took him aside from the people
Mark7:34 and looked up to heaven and sighted
Mark7:35 And straightway his ears were opened
Mark7:36 And he commanded them that they should tell no man. But the more he forbade them
Mark7:37 And were beyond measure astonied
Mark8:1 In those days when there was a very great company
Mark8:2 My heart melteth(I have compassion) on this people
Mark8:3 And if I should send them away fasting to their own houses
Mark8:4 And his disciples answered him: from whence might(where should) a man suffice them with(have) bread here in the wilderness? (to satisfy these)

Luke2:1 It followed(And it chanced) in those days: that there went out a commandment from August the Emperor
Luke2:2 (And) This taxing was (the) first executed (and) when Syrenus was leftenant in Syria.

Luke2:8 And there were in the same region shepherds abiding in the field

Luke2:11 for unto you is born this day in the city of David a saviour
Luke2:12 And take this for a sign: ye shall find the child(babe) swaddled

Luke2:38 And she(the same) came forth that same hour
Luke2:39 And as soon as they had performed all things according to the law of the Lord
Luke2:40 And the child grew and waxed strong in spirit

Luke2:45 and(when they) found him not They went back again to Hierusalem

Luke6:5 And he said unto them: The son of man is Lord even of the Sabbath day.

Luke6:7 (And) The scribes

Luke6:11 And they were filled full of madness

Luke6:13 And as soon as it was day
Luke6:14 Simon
Luke6:15 Matthew and Thomas
Luke6:16 and Judas James' son
Luke6:17 And he came down with them and stood in the plain field with the company of his disciples
Luke6:18 and they also that were vexed with foul spirits
Luke6:19 And all the people pressed(sought) to touch him: for there went virtue out of him
Luke6:20 And he lifted up his eyes upon the disciples
Luke6:21 Blessed are ye that hunger: (now) for ye shall be satisfied. Blessed are ye that weep: (now) for ye shall laugh.
Luke6:22 Blessed are ye when men hate you
Luke6:23 Rejoice ye then
Luke6:24 But woe be to you that are rich: for ye have therein your consolation.
Luke6:25 Woe be to you that are full: for ye shall hunger. Woe be to you that now laugh: for ye shall wail
Luke6:26 Woe be to you when all men praise you: for so did their fathers to the false prophets.
Luke6:27 But I say unto you which hear: Love your enemies. Do good to them which hate you.
Luke6:28 Bless them that curse you. And pray for them which wrongfully trouble you.
Luke6:29 And unto him that smiteth thee on the one cheek
Luke6:30 Give to every man that asketh of thee. And if any man(of him that taketh) that take away thy goods
Luke6:31 And as ye would that men should do to you: so do ye to them likewise.
Luke6:32 If ye love them which love you: what thank are ye worthy of? seeing that(For) the very sinners love their lovers.
Luke6:33 And if ye do for them which do for you: what thank are ye worthy of? For the very sinners do even the same.
Luke6:34 If ye lend to them of whom ye hope to receive: what thank shall ye have: for the very sinners
Luke6:35 (Wherefore
Luke6:36 Be ye therefore merciful
Luke6:37 Judge not and ye shall not be judged. Condemn not: and ye shall not be condemned. Forgive
Luke6:38 Give
Luke6:39 And he put forth a similitude unto them: Can the blind lead the blind? Do they not both then fall into the ditch?
Luke6:40 The disciple is not above his master. Every man shall be perfect
Luke6:41 Why seest thou a mote in thy brother's eye
Luke6:42 Other(Either) how canst thou say to thy brother: Brother let me pull out the mote that is in thine eye: when thou perceivest not the beam that is in thine own eye? Hypocrite
Luke6:43 It is not a good tree that bringeth forth evil fruit: Neither is that an evil tree
Luke6:44 For every tree is known by his fruit. Neither of thorns gather men figs
Luke6:45 A good man out of the good treasure of his heart bringeth forth that which is good. And the(an) evil man out of the evil treasure of his heart

Luke6:47 whosoever cometh to me
Luke6:48 He is like a man which built an house: which(and) digged deep

Luke11:12 Or if he ask an egg: will he proffer(offer) him a scorpion?
Luke11:13 If ye then which are evil
Luke11:14 And he was a casting out a devil

Luke11:16 And other tempted him seeking of him a sign(token) from heaven.
Luke11:17 (But) He knew their thoughts
Luke11:18 So if Satan be at variance(divided) within himself: how shall his kingdom endure? Be cause ye say that I cast out devils by the power of Belzebub?
Luke11:19 If I by the power of Belzebub cast out devils: by whose power
Luke11:20 But if I with the finger of God cast out devils

Luke11:22 But when a stronger than he cometh upon him
Luke11:23 He that is not with me is against me. And he that gathereth not with me scattereth.

Luke11:25 And when he cometh

Luke11:34 The light of thy body is the eye. Therefore

Luke11:36 For if all thy body shall be light

Luke12:1 As there gathered together(togedther) an innumerable multitude of people (insomuch that they trod one another) he began to say unto his disciples: First of all beware of the leaven of the pharisees
Luke12:2 For there is nothing covered
Luke12:3 Wherefore(For) whatsoever ye have spoken in darkness: that same shall be heard in light. And that which ye have spoken in the ear
Luke12:4 I say unto you my friends: fear ye not(Be not afraid of) them that kill the body
Luke12:5 (But) I will shew you
Luke12:6 Are not five sparrows bought for two farthings? and none(yet not one) of them is forgotten of God.
Luke12:7 Yea(Also even) the very hairs of your heads are numbered. Fear not therefore: Ye are more of value
Luke12:8 I say unto you: Whosoever confesseth(knowledgeth) me before men
Luke12:9 And he that denieth me before men: shall be denied before the angels of God.
Luke12:10 And whosoever speaketh a word against the son of man it shall be forgiven him. But unto him that blasphemeth the holy ghost
Luke12:11 When they bring you unto their(the) synagogues
Luke12:12 For the holy ghost shall teach you in the same hour
Luke12:13 One of the company(people) said unto him: Master
Luke12:14 And he said unto him: Man
Luke12:15 Wherefore he said unto them: take heed
Luke12:16 And he put forth a similitude unto them saying: The lands(ground) of a certain (rich) man brought forth fruits plenteously
Luke12:17 and he thought in himself saying: what shall I do? because I have no room where to bestow(gather) my fruits?
Luke12:18 And he said: This will I do. I will destroy(break down) my barns
Luke12:19 and I will say to my soul: Soul thou hast much goods laid up in store for many years
Luke12:20 But God said unto him: Thou fool
Luke12:21 So is it with him that gathered riches
Luke12:22 And he spake unto his disciples: Therefore I say unto you: Take no thought for your life
Luke12:23 The life is more than meat
Luke12:24 Mark well(Consider) the ravens
Luke12:25 Which of you with taking thought can add to his stature one cubit?
Luke12:26 If ye then be not able to do that thing which is least: why take ye thought for the remnant?
Luke12:27 Consider the lilies how they grow: They labor not: They spin not: and (yet) I say unto you
Luke12:28 If God then so clothe the grass which is to day in the fields
Luke12:29 And ask not what ye shall eat
Luke12:30 for all such things the heathen people of the world seek for. Your father knoweth that ye have need of such things.
Luke12:31 Wherefore seek ye after the kingdom of heaven
Luke12:32 Fear not little flock
Luke12:33 Sell that ye have
Luke12:34 For where your treasure is; There will your hearts be also.
Luke12:35 Let your loins be girded about
Luke12:36 and ye yourselves
Luke12:37 Happy(Blessed) are those servants
Luke12:38 And if he come in the second watch
Luke12:39 This shall ye understand
Luke12:40 Be ye prepared therefore for the son of man will come at an hour when ye think
Luke12:41 Then Peter said unto him: Master tellest thou this similitude unto us
Luke12:42 And the Lord said: who is a(If there be any) faithful steward
Luke12:43 Happy(Blessed) is that servant
Luke12:44 Of a truth(Verily) I say unto you: that he will make him ruler over all that he hath.
Luke12:45 But and if the evil servant shall say in his heart: My master will defer his coming
Luke12:46 the lord of that servant will come in a day
Luke12:47 The servant that knew his master's will
Luke12:48 But he that knew not
Luke12:49 I am come to send fire on earth: and what is my desire but that it were al ready kindled?
Luke12:50 Notwithstanding I must be baptised with a baptism. And how am I pained till it be ended?
Luke12:51 Suppose(Think) ye that I am come to send(bring) peace on earth? I tell you
Luke12:52 For henceforth there shall be five in one house divided
Luke12:53 The father shall be divided against the son

Luke21:17 And hated shall ye be of all men for my name's sake.
Luke21:18 Yet there shall not one hair of your heads perish.

Luke21:27 And then shall they see the son of man come in a cloud with power and great glory.

Luke21:30 when they shoot forth their buds

John6:29 Jesus answered and said unto them: This is the work of God
John6:30 They said unto him: what sign shewest thou then? that we may see and believe thee? What dost thou work?
John6:31 our fathers did eat manna in the desert
John6:32 Jesus said unto them: verily
John6:33 For he is the bread of God
John6:34 Then said they unto him: Master(Lord) ever more give us this bread.
John6:35 And Jesus said unto them: I am that bread of life. He that cometh to me
John6:36 But I said unto you: that ye have seen me
John6:37 All that my(the) father giveth me
John6:38 For I came down from heaven: not to do mine own will: but his will which hath sent me.
John6:39 And this is my(the) fathers will which hath sent me
John6:40 And this is the will of him that sent me: That every man which seeth the son; And believeth on him
John6:41 The jews (then) murmured at it
John6:42 And they said: Is not this Jesus the son of Joseph
John6:43 Jesus answered and said unto them. Murmur not between(among) yourselves.
John6:44 No man can come to me except my(the) father which hath sent me
John6:45 It is written in the prophets: And(that) they shall all be taught of God. Every man which(therefore that) hath heard
John6:46 not that any man hath seen the father
John6:47 Verily
John6:48 I am that bread of life.
John6:49 Your fathers did eat manna in the wilderness
John6:50 This is that bread which cometh from heaven
John6:51 I am that living bread which came down from heaven. If any man eat of this bread
John6:52 (And) The jews strove among themselves saying: How can this fellow give us his flesh to eat?
John6:53 (Then) Jesus said unto them: Verily
John6:54 Whosoever eateth my flesh
John6:55 For my flesh is meat indeed: and my blood is drink indeed.
John6:56 He that eateth my flesh and drinketh my blood
John6:57 As my(the) living father hath sent me
John6:58 This is the bread which came from heaven: not as your fathers have eaten manna and are dead. He that eateth of this bread
John6:59 These things said he in the synagogue as he taught in Capernaum.
John6:60 Many (therefore) of his disciples
John6:61 Jesus knew(perceived) in himself
John6:62 what and if ye shall see the son of man ascend up where he was before?
John6:63 It is the spirit that quickeneth
John6:64 But there are some of you that believe not. For Jesus knew from the beginning
John6:65 And he said: Therefore said I unto you: that no man can come unto me
John6:66 From that time many of his disciples went away from him

John6:68 (Then) Simon Peter answered him: Master to whom shall we go? Thou hast the words of eternal(everlasting) life;
John6:69 And we have believed
John6:70 Jesus answered them: Have not I chosen you twelve? And yet one of you is the devil?
John6:71 He spake it of Judas Iscariot the son of Simon. For he it was that should betray him
John7:1 After that Jesus went about into Galilee
John7:2 The jews' tabernacle feast was at hand.
John7:3 His brethren therefore said unto him: Get thyself(thee) hence and go into Jewry that thy disciples may see thy works that thou doest.
John7:4 (For) There is no man that doeth any thing secretly
John7:5 For as yet his brethren believed not in him.
John7:6 Then Jesus said unto them: My time is not yet come
John7:7 The world cannot hate you. Me it hateth: Because I testify of it
John7:8 Go ye up unto this feast
John7:9 These words he said unto them
John7:10 (But) As soon as his brethren were gone up
John7:11 Then sought him the jews at the feast
John7:12 And much murmuring was there of him among the people. Some said: He is good. Other said nay
John7:13 (Howbeit) No man spake openly of him
John7:14 In the midst of the feast
John7:15 And the jews marveled
John7:16 Jesus answered them
John7:17 If any man will do his will
John7:18 He that speaketh of himself
John7:19 Did not Moses give you a law? And yet none of you keepeth the law? Why go ye about to kill me?
John7:20 The people answered and said: Thou hast the devil. Who goeth about to kill thee?
John7:21 Jesus answered
John7:22 Moses therefore gave unto you circumcision
John7:23 If a man on the Sabbath day receive circumcision without breaking of the law of Moses: Disdain ye at me
John7:24 Judge not after the utter appearance: but judge righteous judgement.
John7:25 Then said some of them of Jerusalem: Is not this he whom they go about to kill?
John7:26 behold he speaketh boldly
John7:27 But(Howbeit) we know this man whence he is
John7:28 Then cried Jesus in the temple as he taught saying: And me ye know
John7:29 I know him: (And if I say that I know him not
John7:30 Then sought the jews(they) to take him
John7:31 Many of the people believed on him
John7:32 The pharisees heard that the people murmured such things about him: and(Wherefore) the pharisees and scribes(hye priests) sent ministers(servants) forth to take him.
John7:33 Then said Jesus unto them: Yet am I a little while with you
John7:34 Ye shall seek me
John7:35 Then said the jews between themselves: Whither will he go? that we shall not find him. Will he go among the gentiles
John7:36 What manner of saying is this that he said: ye shall seek me
John7:37 In the last day
John7:38 Whosoever(He that) believeth on me
John7:39 This spake he of the spirit
John7:40 Many of the people
John7:41 Other said: this is Christ. Some said: shall Christ come out of Galilee?
John7:42 Saith not the scripture that Christ shall come of the seed of David: and out of the town of Bethlehem where David was?
John7:43 So was there dissension among the people for his sake.(about him.)
John7:44 And some of them would have taken him: but no man laid hands on him.
John7:45 Then came the ministers(servants) to the high priests
John7:46 The servants answered: never man spake as this man speaketh.(doeth.)

John7:48 Doth any of the rulers
John7:49 But the common people which know not the law are accursed.

John7:51 Doth our law judge any man
John7:52 They answered
John7:53 And every man went unto his own house.

John8:2 and early in the morning came again into the temple
John8:3 (And) The scribes and pharisees brought unto him a woman taken in advoutry
John8:4 Master this woman was taken in advoutry
John8:5 Moses in the law commanded us that such should be stoned: What sayest thou therefore?
John8:6 And this they said to tempt him: that they might have
John8:7 And while they continued asking him
John8:8 And again he stooped down and wrote on the ground.
John8:9 (And) As soon as they heard that
John8:10 When Jesus had lifted up himself again
John8:11 She said: Sir no man.(No man Lorde.) (And) Jesus said: Neither do I condemn thee. Go hence and sin no more.
John8:12 Then spake Jesus again unto them saying: I am the light of the world. He that followeth me shall not walk in darkness: but shall have the light of life.

John8:15 Ye judge after the flesh
John8:16 and if(though) I judge
John8:17 It is also written in your law
John8:18 I am one that bear witness of myself

John8:21 Then said Jesus again unto them: I go my way

John8:24 I said therefore unto you
John8:25 Then said they unto him
John8:26 I have many things to say
John8:27 (Howbeit) They understood not that he spake of his father.

John8:29 And he that sent me is with me. My(the) father hath not left me alone; For I do always those things that please him.
John8:30 As he spake these words
John8:31 Then said Jesus to those jews which believed on him: If ye continue in my saying
John8:32 and ye shall know the truth: And the truth shall make you free.
John8:33 They answered him: We be Abraham's seed
John8:34 Jesus answered them: verily
John8:35 And the servant abideth not in the house for ever: But the son abideth ever.
John8:36 If the son therefore shall make you free
John8:37 I know that ye are Abraham's seed: but ye seek means to kill me because my sayings have no place in you.
John8:38 I speak that I have seen with my father: and ye do that which ye have seen with your father.
John8:39 They answered and said unto him: Abraham is our father. Jesus said unto them. If ye were Abraham's children
John8:40 But now ye go about to kill me
John8:41 Ye do the deeds of your father. Then said they unto him: we were not born of fornication. We have one father that(which) is God.
John8:42 Jesus said unto them: if God were your father
John8:43 Why do ye not know my speech? (Even) Because ye cannot abide the hearing of my words.
John8:44 Ye are of your father the devil
John8:45 And because I tell you the truth
John8:46 Which of you can rebuke me of sin? If I say the truth
John8:47 He that is of God
John8:48 Then answered the jews and said unto him: Say we not well that thou art a Samaritan and hast the devil?
John8:49 Jesus answered: I have not the devil: but I honour my father
John8:50 I seek not mine own praise: (but) There is one that seeketh it and judgeth.
John8:51 Verily
John8:52 Then said the jews to him: Now know we that thou hast the devil. Abraham is dead
John8:53 Art thou greater than our father Abraham? which is dead? and the prophets are dead. Whom makest thou thyself?
John8:54 Jesus answered: If I praise(honour) myself
John8:55 And yet have ye not known him: but I know him.(your God
John8:56 Your father Abraham was glad to see my day
