John15:7 If ye bide in me
John15:8 Here in is my father glorified
John15:9 As the father hath loved me
John15:10 If ye shall keep my commandments
John15:11 These things have I spoken unto you
John15:12 This is my commandment
John15:13 Greater love than this hath no man
John15:14 Ye are my friends
John15:15 Henceforth call I you not servants: For the servant knoweth not what his lord doeth. But you have I called friends: For all things that I have heard of my father
John15:16 Ye have not chosen me
John15:17 This command I you
John15:18 If the world hate you
John15:19 If ye were of the world
John15:20 Remember the saying
John15:21 But all these things will they do unto you for my name's sake
John15:22 If I had not come and spoken unto them
John15:23 He that hateth me
John15:24 If I had not done works among them which none other man did
John15:25 Even that the saying might be fulfilled that is written in their law: they hated me without a cause.
John15:26 But when the comforter is come
John15:27 And ye shall bear witness also
John16:1 These things have I said unto you be cause ye should not be hurt in your faith.(because ye should not be offended.)
John16:2 They shall excommunicate you: yea the time shall come
John16:3 And such things will they do unto you
John16:4 (But) These things have I told you
John16:5 But now go I my way to him that sent me
John16:6 but because I have said such things unto you
John16:7 Nevertheless I tell you the truth it is expedient(better) for you that I go away. For if I go not away
John16:8 And when he is come
John16:9 Of sin
John16:10 Of righteousness
John16:11 And of judgement
John16:12 I have yet many things to say unto you: but ye cannot bear them away now.
John16:13 (Howbeit) When he is once come (I mean the spirit of verity
John16:14 He shall glorify me
John16:15 All things that my(the) father hath are mine. Therefore said I unto you that he shall take of mine and shew unto you.
John16:16 After a while ye shall not see me
John16:17 Then said some of his disciples between(among) themselves: what is this that he saith unto us
John16:18 They said therefore: what is this that he saith after a while? we cannot tell what he saith.
John16:19 Jesus perceived
John16:20 Verily
John16:21 A woman when she travaileth hath sorrow
John16:22 And ye now are in sorrow: but I will see you again
John16:23 And in that day shall ye ask me no question. Verily
John16:24 Hitherto have ye asked nothing in my name. Ask and ye shall receive it: that your joy may be full.(that our joy may be full)
John16:25 These things have I spoken unto you in proverbs. The time will come when I shall no more speak to you in proverbs: but I shall shew you plainly from my father.
John16:26 At that day shall ye ask in mine name. And I say not unto you that I will speak unto my father for you.
John16:27 For my(the) father himself loveth you
John16:28 I went out from the father
John16:29 His disciples said unto him: lo now speakest thou plainly
John16:30 Now know we that thou understandest(knowest) all things
John16:31 Jesus answered them: Now ye do believe.
John16:32 Behold the hour draweth nigh
John16:33 These words have I spoken unto you that in me ye might have peace.(For) In the world shall ye have tribulation:(trouble) but be of good cheer
John17:1 These words spake Jesus and lift(lifted) up his eyes to heaven
John17:2 As thou hast given him power over all flesh
John17:3 This is life eternal(everlasting) that they might know thee that only very God:(that thou onely art the true God) and whom thou hast sent Jesus Christ.
John17:4 I have glorified thee on the earth. I have finished the work which thou gavest me to do.
John17:5 And now glorify me thou father in(with) thine own presence
John17:6 I have declared thy name unto the men which thou gavest me out of the world. Thine they were
John17:7 Now have they known that all things whatsoever thou hast given me
John17:8 For I have given unto them the words which thou gavest me
John17:9 I pray for them.(
John17:10 and all mine are thine
John17:11 And now am I no more in the world
John17:12 While I was with them in the world
John17:13 Now come I to thee
John17:14 I have given them thy doctrine
John17:15 I desire(pray) not that thou shouldest take them out of the world: but that thou keep them from evil.
John17:16 They are not of the world
John17:17 Sanctify them in thy truth.(with thy trueth) Thy saying is verity.(trueth.)(Thy word is the trueth)
John17:18 As thou didst sent me into the world
John17:19 And for their sakes sanctify I myself
John17:20 I pray not for them alone: but for them also which shall believe on me thorow their preaching
John17:21 that they all may be one
John17:22 And that glory that thou gavest me
John17:23 I am in them and thou art in me
John17:24 Father I will that they which thou hast given me
John17:25 O righteous father the very world hath not known thee: but I have known thee
John17:26 And I have declared unto them thy name
John18:1 When Jesus had spoken these words
John18:2 (Judas also which betrayed him knew the place
John18:3 Judas then after he had received a bond of men
John18:4 Then Jesus knowing all things that should come on him
John18:5 They answered him: Jesus of Nazareth. Jesus said unto them: I am he. Judas also which betrayed him
John18:6 (But) As soon as he had said unto them I am he
John18:7 And he asked them again: whom seek ye? They said: Jesus of Nazareth.
John18:8 Jesus answered
John18:9 That the saying might be fulfilled which he spake: of them which thou gavest (me
John18:10 Simon Peter had a sword
John18:11 Then said Jesus unto Peter: put up thy sword into the sheath: shall I not drink of the cup which my father had(hath) given me?
John18:12 Then the company
John18:13 and led him away to Anna first: For he was father-in-law unto Caiphas
John18:14 Caiphas was he that gave counsel to the jews that it was expedient(it were good) that one man should die for the people.
John18:15 (And) Simon Peter followed Jesus
John18:16 But Peter stood at the door without. Then went out that other disciple which was known unto the high priest
John18:17 Then said the damsel that kept the door unto Peter: Art not thou one of this man's disciples? He said: I am not.
John18:18 The servants and the ministers(officers) stood there
John18:19 The high priest asked Jesus of his disciples
John18:20 Jesus answered him: I spake openly in the world. I ever taught in the synagogue and in the temple whither all the jews resorted: and in secret have I said nothing:
John18:21 why askest thou me? Ask them which heard me what I said unto them. Behold they can tell what I said.
John18:22 When he had thus spoken
John18:23 Jesus answered him: If I have evil spoken
John18:24 (And) Annas sent him bound unto Caiphas the high priest.
John18:25 Simon Peter stood and warmed himself
John18:26 One of the servants of the high priest (his cousin whose ear Peter smote off ) said unto him: did not I see thee in the garden with him?
John18:27 Peter denied it again: and immediately the cock crew.
John18:28 Then led they Jesus from Caiphas into the hall of judgement.(common hall) It was in the morning
John18:29 Pilate then went out unto them and said: What accusation bring ye against this man?
John18:30 They answered and said unto him: If he were not an evil doer
John18:31 Then said Pilate unto them: take(ye) him unto you
John18:32 That the words of Jesus might be fulfilled which he spake
John18:33 Then Pilate entered into the judgement house(hall) again
John18:34 Jesus answered: sayest thou that of thyself
John18:35 Pilate answered: Am I a jew? Thine own nation and high priests have delivered thee unto me. What hast thou done?
John18:36 Jesus answered: my kingdom is not of this world. If my kingdom were of this world then would my ministers surely fight
John18:37 Pilate said unto him: Art thou a King then? Jesus answered: Thou sayest that I am a King. For this cause was I born
John18:38 Pilate said unto him: what is truth?(what thinge is trueth?) And when he had said that
John18:39 Ye have a custom among you
John18:40 Then cried they all again saying: Not him
John19:1 Then Pilate took Jesus and scourged him.
John19:2 And the soldiers wound a crown of thorns and put it on his head. And they did on him a purple garment
John19:3 and said: hail King of the Jews. And they smote him on the face.
John19:4 Pilate went forth again
John19:5 Then came Jesus forth wearing a crown of thorns and a robe of purple. And Pilate said unto them: Behold the man.
John19:6 When the high priests and ministers saw him
John19:7 The jews answered him. We have a law
John19:8 When Pilate heard that saying
John19:9 and went again into the judgement house
John19:10 Then said Pilate unto him: Speakest thou not unto me? Knowest thou not that I have power to crucify thee
John19:11 Jesus answered: Thou couldest have no power at all against me
John19:12 And from thenceforth sought Pilate means to loose him: but the jews cried saying: if thou let him go
John19:13 When Pilate heard that saying he brought Jesus forth
John19:14 (It was the Sabbath even which falleth in(the day of preparing of) the ester feast
John19:15 They cried
John19:16 Then delivered he him unto them to be crucified. And they took Jesus
John19:17 And he bare his cross
John19:18 where they crucified him. And with him two other
John19:19 (And) Pilate wrote his title
John19:20 This title(superscription) read many of the jews. For the place where Jesus was crucified
John19:21 Then said the high priests of the jews to Pilate: write not
John19:22 Pilate answered: what I have written
John19:23 (Then) The soldiers
John19:24 And they said one to another: Let us not divide it: but cast lots who shall have it. That the scripture might be fulfilled which saith. They parted my raiment among them
John19:25 There stood by the cross of Jesus his mother
John19:26 When Jesus saw his mother
John19:27 Then said he to the disciple: behold thy mother. And from that hour the disciple took her for his own.
John19:28 After that when Jesus perceived that all things were performed
John19:29 There stood a vessel full of vinegar by. (And) They filled a sponge with vinegar
John19:30 As soon as Jesus had received of the vinegar
John19:31 The jews then because it was the Sabbath(the day of preparing) even that the bodies should not remain upon the cross on the Sabbath day (For that Sabbath day was an high day) besought Pilate that their legs might be broken and that they might be taken down.
John19:32 Then came the soldiers and brake the legs of the first
John19:33 (But) When they came to Jesus and saw that he was dead already
John19:34 but one of the soldiers with a spear
John19:35 And he that saw it bare record
John19:36 These things were done that the scripture should be fulfilled: Ye shall not break a bone of him.
John19:37 And again another scripture saith: They shall look on him
John19:38 After that
John19:39 And there came also Nicodemus which at the beginning came to Jesus by night
John19:40 Then took they the body of Jesu and wound it in linen clothes with those confections(the odors) as the manner of the jews is to bury.
John19:41 (And) In the place where Jesus was crucified
John19:42 There laid they Jesus because of the jews' Sabbath even

John20:9 For as yet they knew not the scriptures

John20:28 Thomas answered and said unto him: my Lord
John20:29 Jesus said unto him: Thomas

John21:1 After that Jesus shewed himself again at the sea of Tiberias. And on this wise shewed he himself.
John21:2 There were together Simon Peter and Thomas
John21:3 Simon Peter said unto them: I go a fishing. They said unto him: we also will go with thee. They went their way and entered into a ship straightway
John21:4 (But) When the morning was now come
John21:5 Jesus said unto them: Sirs
John21:6 And he said unto them: cast out your(the) net on the right side of the ship
John21:7 Then said the disciple whom Jesus loved unto Peter: It is the Lord. When Simon Peter heard that it was the Lord
John21:8 The other disciples came by ship: For they were not far from land
John21:9 As soon as they were come to land
John21:10 Jesus said unto them: bring of the fishes which ye have now caught.
John21:11 Simon Peter stepped forth and drew the net to land full of great fishes
John21:12 Jesus said unto them: come and dine. And none of the disciples durst ask him: what art thou? For they knew that it was the Lord.
John21:13 Jesus then came and took bread
John21:14 And this is now the third time that Jesus appeared to his disciples
John21:15 When they had dined
John21:16 He said to him again the second time: Simon Joanna
John21:17 He said unto him the third time: Simon Joanna
John21:18 Verily
John21:19 That spake he signifying by what death he should glorify God. And when he had said thus
John21:20 Peter turned about

John21:24 The same disciple is he
John21:25 There are also many other things which Jesus did: the which if they should be written every one

Acts4:12 Neither is there health(salvation) in any other. Nor yet also is there any other name given to men wherein we must be saved.

Acts4:17 But that it be noised no farther among the people
Acts4:18 And they called them

Acts4:20 For we cannot but speak that which we have seen and heard.

Acts4:22 For the man was above forty year old
Acts4:23 As soon as they were let go they came to their fellows

Acts4:25 which (in the holy ghost) by the mouth of thy servant David (our father) hast said: Why did the heathen grudge

Acts4:28 for to do whatsoever thy hand and thy counsel determined before to be done.

Acts5:4 Pertained it not unto thee only? And after it was sold
Acts5:5 When Ananias heard these words
Acts5:6 And the young men rose up

Acts5:20 go step forth

Acts5:39 but and if it be of God

Acts16:13 And on the Sabbath days we went out of the city besides a river where men were wont to pray
Acts16:14 And a certain(devout) woman named Lidia
Acts16:15 When she was baptised
Acts16:16 (And) It fortuned as we went to prayer
Acts16:17 The same followed Paul and us (and cried) saying: These men are the servants of the most high God
Acts16:18 And this did she many days. But Paul not content turned about and said to the spirit: I command thee in the name of Jesu Christ that thou come(depart) out of her. And he came(departed) out the same hour.
Acts16:19 (And) When her master and mastress saw that the hope of their gains(vantage) was gone
Acts16:20 and delivered(brought) them to the officers saying: These men trouble our city
Acts16:21 and shew new decrees
Acts16:22 And the people ran on them
Acts16:23 and when they had beaten them sore
Acts16:24 Which (jailer) when he had received such commandment thrust them into the inner prison
Acts16:25 At midnight Paul and Silas prayed
Acts16:26 (And) Suddenly there was a great earthquake
Acts16:27 When the keeper of the prison waked out of his sleep
Acts16:28 (But) Paul cried with a loud voice saying: Do thyself no harm
Acts16:29 (Then) He called for a light and sprang in
Acts16:30 and brought them out and said: Sirs what must I do to be saved?
Acts16:31 And they said: believe on the Lord Jesus
Acts16:32 And they preached unto him the word of the Lord
Acts16:33 And he took them the same hour of the night and washed their wounds
Acts16:34 When he had brought them into his house
Acts16:35 And when it was day the officers sent the ministers saying: Let those men go.
Acts16:36 The keeper of the prison told this saying to Paul: the officers have sent word to loose you. Now therefore get you hence and go in peace.
Acts16:37 Then said Paul unto them: They have beaten us openly uncondemned
Acts16:38 (When) The ministers told these words unto the officers and they feared when they heard that they were romans
Acts16:39 and came and besought them
Acts16:40 (And) They went out of the prison and entered into the house of Lidia
Acts17:1 As they made their journey thorow Amphipolis
Acts17:2 (And) Paul as his manner was went in unto them
Acts17:3 opening and alleging that Christ must needs have suffered and risen again from death. And that this Jesus was Christ
Acts17:4 And some of them believed and came and companied with Paul and Silas. Also of the honorable greeks a great multitude
Acts17:5 (But) The jews which believed not(stiffnecked jews) having indignation took unto them evil men which were vagabonds
Acts17:6 And(But) when they found them not
Acts17:7 which Jason hath received privily. And these all do contrary to the ordinations(elders) of Cesar
Acts17:8 And they troubled the people and the officers(rulers) of the city when they heard these things.
Acts17:9 And when they were sufficiently answered(had received a sufficient answer) of Jason
Acts17:10 (And) The brethren immediately sent away Paul
Acts17:11 These were the noblest (of birth) among them of Thessalonia
Acts17:12 And many of them believed
Acts17:13 When the jews of Thessalonia had knowledge that the word of God was preached of Paul at Berea
Acts17:14 and then by and by the brethren sent away Paul to go unto(as it were to) the sea: but Silas and Timotheus abode there still.
Acts17:15 (And) They that guided Paul brought him unto Athens
Acts17:16 While Paul waited for them at Athens
Acts17:17 Then he disputed in the synagogue with the jews
Acts17:18 Certain Philosophers of the Epicures
Acts17:19 and they took him
Acts17:20 For thou bringest strange tidings to our ears. We would know therefore what these things mean.
Acts17:21 For all the Athenians and strangers which were there gave themselves to nothing else
Acts17:22 Paul stood in the midst of Marce street(middes of the common place) and said
Acts17:23 For as I passed by and beheld the manner how ye worship your gods
Acts17:24 God that made the world
Acts17:25 neither is worshipped with men's hands
Acts17:26 and hath made of one blood all nations(generations) of men
Acts17:27 that they should seek God
Acts17:28 For in him we live
Acts17:29 Forasmuch then as we are the generation of God
Acts17:30 And the time of this ignorance God regarded not:(hath overseen) but now he biddeth all men everywhere (to) repent
Acts17:31 because he hath appointed a day
Acts17:32 When they heard of the resurrection from death
Acts17:33 So Paul departed from among them.
Acts17:34 (Howbeit) Certain men clave unto Paul and believed
Acts18:1 After that
Acts18:2 and found a certain jew named Aquila
Acts18:3 And because he was of the same craft he abode with them and wrought (their craft was to make tents.)
Acts18:4 and he preached in the synagogue every Sabbath day; (setting forth in the mean while the name of the Lorde Iesus) And exhorted the Jews and the gentiles.
Acts18:5 When Silas and Timotheus were come from Macedonia Paul was pained(constrained) by the spirit
Acts18:6 (And) When they said contrary and blasphemed
Acts18:7 and he departed thence
Acts18:8 One(Won
Acts18:9 Then spake the Lord to Paul in the night by a vision: be not afraid
Acts18:10 for I am with thee
Acts18:11 And he rested(continued) there a year and six months
Acts18:12 When Gallio was ruler of the country of Acaia. The jews made insurrection with one accord against Paul
Acts18:13 saying: This man teacheth(this fellow counceleth men) to worship God contrary to the law.
Acts18:14 And as Paul was about to open his mouth Gallio said unto the jews: if it were a matter of wrong
Acts18:15 but if it be a question of words
Acts18:16 and he drave them from his(the) seat.
Acts18:17 Then took all the greeks Sosthenes a(the chief) ruler of the Synagogue and smote him before the judge's seat. And Gallio cared for none of those things.
Acts18:18 Paul after this
Acts18:19 And he came to Ephesus and left them there: but he himself entered into the Synagogue
Acts18:20 When they desired him to tarry longer time(season) with them
Acts18:21 but bade them fare well saying: I must needs at this feast that cometh be in Jerusalem: but I will return again unto you if God will.
Acts18:22 And (he) departed from Ephesus and came unto Cesarea: and ascended and saluted the congregation
Acts18:23 and departed unto Antioche
Acts18:24 And a certain jew named Apollos
Acts18:25 The same was informed in the way of the Lord
Acts18:26 And the same began to speak boldly in the Synagogue. (And) When Priscilla and Aquila had heard him: they took him unto them; And expounded unto him the way of God more perfectly.
Acts18:27 (And) When he was disposed to go into Acaia
Acts18:28 And mightily he overcame the jews (and that) openly shewing by the scriptures that Jesus was Christ.
Acts19:1 It fortuned
Acts19:2 and said unto them: have ye received the holy ghost after(since) ye believed? And they said unto him: No
Acts19:3 And he said unto them: wherewith were ye then baptised? And they said: With John's baptism.
Acts19:4 Then said Paul: John verily baptised with the baptism of repentance
Acts19:5 When they heard that
Acts19:6 and when Paul laid his hands upon them
Acts19:7 (and) All the men were about twelve.
Acts19:8 And he went into the synagogue
Acts19:9 When divers waxed hard hearted
Acts19:10 And this continued by the space of two years: So that all they which dwelt in Asia heard the word of the Lord Jesu
Acts19:11 And God wrought not small miracles by the hands of Paul.
Acts19:12 So that from his body were brought unto the sick
Acts19:13 (Then) Certain of the vagabond jews exorcists
Acts19:14 (And) There were seven sons of one Sceva a ruler of the synagogue(a Jew and chief of the priests) which did so:
Acts19:15 and the evil spirit answered and said: Jesus I know
Acts19:16 And the man in whom the wicked devil(evil spirit) was
Acts19:17 (And) This was known to all the jews and greeks also
Acts19:18 And many that believed came
Acts19:19 Many of them which used curious crafts
Acts19:20 So mightily grew the word of God
Acts19:21 After these things were ended Paul purposed in the spirit
Acts19:22 So sent he into Macedonia two of them that ministered unto him: Timotheus and Erastus: but he himself remained in Asia. For a season.
Acts19:23 The same time there arose no little a do about that way.
Acts19:24 For a certain man named Demetrius
Acts19:25 which he called together with the workmen of like occupation
Acts19:26 Moreover ye see and hear that not alone at Ephesus: but almost thorow out all Asia
Acts19:27 so that not only this our craft cometh into peril to be set at nought: but that also the temple of (the) great (goddess) Diana should be despised. And her majesty(magnificence) should be destroyed
Acts19:28 When they heard these sayings
Acts19:29 And all the city was on a roar
Acts19:30 When Paul would have entered in unto the people
Acts19:31 Certain also of the chief of Asia which were his friends
Acts19:32 Some cried one thing
Acts19:33 Some of the company drew forth Alexander (the jews thrusting him forwards) Alexander beckoned with the hand
Acts19:34 When they knew that he was a jew
Acts19:35 When the town clerk had ceased the people he said: ye men of Ephesus: what man is it that knoweth not how that the city of the Ephesians is a worshipper of the great goddess Diana
Acts19:36 Seeing then that no man saith here against
Acts19:37 For ye have brought hither these men
Acts19:38 Wherefore if Demetrius and the craftsmen which are with him
Acts19:39 If ye go about any other thing
Acts19:40 For we are in jeopardy to be accused of this day's business.(uproar) Forasmuch as there is no cause whereby we may give a reckoning of this concourse of people.(and yet is there no man guilty
Acts19:41 And when he had thus spoken
Acts20:1 After the rage(uproar) was ceased
Acts20:2 And when he had gone over(thorow) those parts

Acts20:5 These went before

Acts20:8 (And) There were many lights in the chamber where we(they) were gathered together
Acts20:9 and there sat in a window a certain young man named Eutichus
Acts20:10 Paul went down and fell on him
Acts20:11 When he was come up again

Acts20:17 (Wherefore) From Mileton he sent to Ephesus
Acts20:18 (And) When they were come to him
Acts20:19 serving God(the Lord) with all humbleness of mind
Acts20:20 and how I kept back nothing that might be for your profit:(was profitable:) but that I have shewed you
Acts20:21 witnessing(And have testified) both to the jews
Acts20:22 And now behold I go bound in the spirit unto Jerusalem
Acts20:23 but that the holy ghost witnesseth in every city saying: that bonds and trouble abide me:
Acts20:24 but none of those things move me. Neither is my life dear unto myself
Acts20:25 And now behold
Acts20:26 Wherefore I take you to record this same day
Acts20:27 For I have kept nothing back: but have shewed you all the counsel of God.
Acts20:28 Take heed therefore unto yourselves
Acts20:29 For I am sure of this
Acts20:30 And(Moreover) of your own selves shall men arise speaking perverse things
Acts20:31 Therefore awake and remember
Acts20:32 And now dear brethren I commend you to God and to the word of his grace
Acts20:33 I have desired no man's silver
Acts20:34 Ye know well that these hands have ministered unto my necessities
Acts20:35 I have shewed you all things
Acts20:36 When he had thus spoken
Acts20:37 And they wept all abundantly
Acts20:38 sorrowing

Acts22:1 Ye men

Acts22:5 as the chief priest doth bear me witness
Acts22:6 And it fortuned that as I made my journey
Acts22:7 and I fell unto the earth

Acts22:9 And they that were with me saw verily a light and were afraid: but they heard not the voice of him that spake with me.
Acts22:10 And I said: what shall I do Lord? And the Lord said unto me: Arise and go into Damasco and there it shall be told thee of all things which are appointed for thee to do.
Acts22:11 And when I saw nothing for the brightness of that light
Acts22:12 (And) One Ananias a perfect man

Acts23:4 And they that stood by said: Revilest thou God's(Goddes) high priest?
Acts23:5 Then said Paul: I wist not brethren
Acts23:6 When Paul perceived that the one part were sadducees
Acts23:7 And when he had so said
Acts23:8 For the sadducees say that there is no resurrection
Acts23:9 And there arose a great cry
Acts23:10 And when there arose great debate

Acts23:12 When day was come

Acts24:1 After five days
Acts24:2 When Paul was called forth; Tartullus began to accuse him saying: Seeing that we live in great quietness by the means of thee and that many good things are done unto this nation thorow thy providence:
Acts24:3 that allow we ever and in all places
Acts24:4 Notwithstanding
Acts24:5 We have found this man a pestilent fellow
Acts24:6 Which(and) also hath enforced to pollute(taken in hand also to suspend) the temple
Acts24:7 but the high captain Lisias came upon us
Acts24:8 commanding his accusers to come unto thee
Acts24:9 The jews likewise affirmed
Acts24:10 Then Paul (after that the ruler himself had beckoned unto him that he should speak) answered: I shall with a more quiet mind answer for myself
Acts24:11 because that thou mayest know that there are yet but twelve days since I went up to Jerusalem for to pray.(worship)
Acts24:12 And that they neither found me in the temple disputing with any man
Acts24:13 Neither can they prove the things whereof they accuse me.
Acts24:14 But this I confess unto thee
Acts24:15 and have hope towards God
Acts24:16 And therefore study I to have a clear conscience toward God

Acts24:18 in the which they found me purified in the temple

Acts24:21 except it be for this one voice

Acts25:11 If I have hurt them

Acts25:26 Of whom I have no certain thing to write unto my lord. Wherefore I have brought him unto you
Acts25:27 For me thinketh it unreasonable for to send a prisoner

Acts26:3 namely because thou art expert in all customs
Acts26:4 My living of a child
Acts26:5 which knew me from the beginning
Acts26:6 and now I stand and am judged for the hope of the promise made of God unto our fathers
Acts26:7 unto which promise
Acts26:8 Why should it be thought a thing incredible unto you
Acts26:9 I also verily thought in myself
Acts26:10 which things I also did in Jerusalem. And(Where) many of the saints shut I (up) in prison
Acts26:11 And I punished them oft in every synagogue
Acts26:12 About the which things as I went to Damasco with authority

Acts26:15 And I said: Who art thou Lord? And he said: I am Jesus whom thou persecutest:

Acts26:17 delivering thee from the people
Acts26:18 to open their eyes that they might turn from darkness unto light
Acts26:19 Wherefore king Agrippa
Acts26:20 but shewed first unto them of Damasco

Acts26:22 Nevertheless I obtained help of God
Acts26:23 that Christ should suffer

Acts26:26 The king knoweth of these things
Acts26:27 King Agrippa believest thou the prophets? I wot well(know) thou believest.

Acts26:31 And when they were gone apart

Acts27:2 And we entered into a ship of Adramicium
Acts27:3 (And) The next day came we to Sidon
Acts27:4 And from thence launched we and sailed hard by Cypers
Acts27:5 Then sailed we over the sea of Cicill
Acts27:6 And there the under-captain found a ship of Alexandry ready to sail into Italy
Acts27:7 and when we had sailed slowly many days
Acts27:8 and with much work sailed beyond it
Acts27:9 When much time was spent and sailing was now jeopardous
Acts27:10 and said unto them: Sirs I perceive that this voyage will be with hurt and (much) damage
Acts27:11 But(Neverthelater) the under-captain believed the governor
Acts27:12 And because the haven was not commodious to winter in
Acts27:13 When the south wind blew
Acts27:14 But anon after there arose (against their purpose) a flaw of wind out of the northeast.
Acts27:15 (And) When the ship was caught
Acts27:16 (And) We came unto an isle named Clauda; And had much work to come by a boat
Acts27:17 which they took up
Acts27:18 The next day when we were tossed with an exceeding tempest
Acts27:19 and the third day we cast out with our own hands the tackling of the ship.
Acts27:20 When at the last neither sun nor star in many days appeared; And no small tempest lay upon us
Acts27:21 Then after long abstinence Paul stood forth in the midst of them and said: Sirs ye should have heard(hearkened to) me
Acts27:22 And now I exhort you to be of goad cheer
Acts27:23 For there stood by me this night the angel of God whose I am
Acts27:24 saying: fear not Paul
Acts27:25 wherefore sirs be of good cheer
Acts27:26 and(Howbeit) we must be cast into a certain island.
