Colossians1:1 Paul an apostle of Jesu Christ by the will of God
Colossians1:2 To the saints which are at Colossa
Colossians1:3 We give thanks to God the father of our Lord Jesus Christ alway praying for you in our prayers
Colossians1:4 since we heard of your faith which ye have in Christ Jesu: and of the love which ye bear to all saints
Colossians1:5 for the hope's sake which is laid up in store for you in heaven
Colossians1:6 which is come unto you
Colossians1:7 as ye learned of Epaphra our dear fellow servant
Colossians1:8 which also declared unto us your love
Colossians1:9 For this cause we also
Colossians1:10 that ye might walk worthy of the Lord in all things that please
Colossians1:11 strengthed with all might
Colossians1:12 giving thanks unto the father which hath made us meet to be partakers of the inheritance of saints in light.
Colossians1:13 Which hath delivered us from the power of darkness
Colossians1:14 in whom we have redemption thorow his blood
Colossians1:15 which is the image of the invisible God
Colossians1:16 for by him were all things created
Colossians1:17 All things are created by him
Colossians1:18 And he is the head of the body
Colossians1:19 For it pleased the father that in him should all fullness dwell
Colossians1:20 and by him to reconcile all things unto himself
Colossians1:21 And you (which were in times past strangers and enemies
Colossians1:22 in the body of his flesh thorow death
Colossians1:23 if ye continue grounded and stablished in the faith
Colossians1:24 Now joy I in my passions(sufferings) which I suffer for you
Colossians1:25 whereof am I made a minister according to the ordinance of God
Colossians1:26 that mystery hid since the world began
Colossians1:27 to whom God would make known the glorious riches of this mystery among the gentiles
Colossians1:28 whom we preach warning all men
Colossians1:29 Wherein I also labour and strive
Colossians2:1 I would ye knew what fighting I have for your sakes and for them of Laodicia
Colossians2:2 that their hearts might be comforted and knit together in love
Colossians2:3 in whom are hid all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
Colossians2:4 This I say lest any man should beguile you with enticing words.
Colossians2:5 For though I be absent in the flesh
Colossians2:6 As ye have therefore received Christ Jesu the Lord
Colossians2:7 and steadfast in the faith
Colossians2:8 Beware lest any man come and spoil you thorow philosophy and deceitful vanity
Colossians2:9 For in him dwelleth all the fullness of the Godhead bodily
Colossians2:10 and ye are full(complete) in him
Colossians2:11 in whom also ye are circumcised with circumcision made without hands
Colossians2:12 in that ye are buried with him thorow baptism
Colossians2:13 And hath with him quickened you also which were dead in sin in the uncircumcision of your flesh
Colossians2:14 and hath put out the obligation(handwriting) that was against us
Colossians2:15 and hath spoiled rule and power
Colossians2:16 Let no man therefore trouble your consciences about meat and drink: or for a piece of an holyday
Colossians2:17 which are nothing but shadows of things to come: but the body is in Christ.
Colossians2:18 Let no man make you shoot at a wrong mark
Colossians2:19 and holdeth not the head
Colossians2:20 Wherefore if ye be dead with Christ from doctrine(ordinances) of the world: Why as though ye yet lived in the world
Colossians2:21 Touch not; Taste not; Handle not:
Colossians2:22 which all perish with the using of them
Colossians2:23 which things have the similitude(a shine) of wisdom in chosen holiness
Colossians3:1 If ye be then risen again with Christ seek those things which are above
Colossians3:2 Set your affection(mind) on things that are above
Colossians3:3 For ye are dead
Colossians3:4 When Christ which is our life shall shew himself
Colossians3:5 Mortify therefore your members which are on the earth
Colossians3:6 for which thing's sakes the wrath of God falleth(cometh) on the children of unbelief.
Colossians3:7 In which things ye walked once
Colossians3:8 But now put ye also away from you all things
Colossians3:9 Lie not one to another
Colossians3:10 and have put on the new
Colossians3:11 where is neither greek(gentile) nor(ner) jew
Colossians3:12 Now therefore as elect of God
Colossians3:13 forbearing one another
Colossians3:14 Above all these things put on love
Colossians3:15 and the peace of God rule in your hearts
Colossians3:16 Let the word of God(Christ) dwell in you plenteously in all wisdom. Teach and exhort your own selves
Colossians3:17 And all things (whatsoever ye do in word or deed)(work) do in the name of the Lord Jesu
Colossians3:18 Wives submit your own selves unto your own husbands
Colossians3:19 Husbands love your wives and be not bitter unto them.
Colossians3:20 Children obey your fathers and mothers
Colossians3:21 Fathers rate not your children
Colossians3:22 Servants be obedient unto your bodily masters in all things: not with eye service as men pleasers
Colossians3:23 And whatsoever ye do
Colossians3:24 remembering(forasmuch as ye know) that of the Lord ye shall receive the reward of inheritance
Colossians3:25 But he that doth wrong shall receive for the wrong that he hath done: for there is no respect of persons. (with God)
Colossians4:1 Ye masters do unto your servants that which is just and egal
Colossians4:2 Continue in prayer and watch in the same with thanksgiving
Colossians4:3 praying also for us that God open unto us the door of utterance
Colossians4:4 that I may utter it
Colossians4:5 Walk wisely to(toward) them that are without
Colossians4:6 Let your speech alway have favour with it(be always well savoured) and be salted
Colossians4:7 The dear brother Tichicos shall tell you of all my business
Colossians4:8 whom I have sent unto you for the same purpose
Colossians4:9 with one Onesimus a faithful

Colossians4:11 and Jesus which is called Justus
Colossians4:12 Epaphras the servant of Christ
Colossians4:13 I bear him record that he hath a fervent mind toward(for) you

1Thessalonians1:1 Paul Silvanus and Timotheus. Unto the congregation of the Thessalonians
1Thessalonians1:2 We give God thanks alway(all way) for you all
1Thessalonians1:3 without ceasing
1Thessalonians1:4 because we know brethren beloved of God
1Thessalonians1:5 For our gospel(gospell) came not unto you in word only
1Thessalonians1:6 And ye counterfeited(became followers of) us
1Thessalonians1:7 so that ye were an example to all that believe in Macedonia
1Thessalonians1:8 For from you sounded(noised) out the word of the Lord
1Thessalonians1:9 for they themselves shew of you what manner of entering in we had unto you
1Thessalonians1:10 and for to look for his son from heaven
1Thessalonians2:1 For ye yourselves brethren know of our entrance in unto you
1Thessalonians2:2 but even after that we had suffered before and were shamefully entreated at Philippos (as ye well know) then were we bold in our God to speak unto you the gospel(gospell) of God
1Thessalonians2:3 Our exhortation was not to bring you to error
1Thessalonians2:4 but as we were allowed of God
1Thessalonians2:5 Neither was our conversation at any time with flattering words (as ye well know) neither in cloaked covetousness
1Thessalonians2:6 neither sought we praise of men
1Thessalonians2:7 but were tender among you
1Thessalonians2:8 so was our (heartily) affection toward you our good will was to have dealt unto you
1Thessalonians2:9 Ye remember brethren our labour and travail. For we laboured(wrought) day and night
1Thessalonians2:10 Ye are witnesses
1Thessalonians2:11 as ye know how that we exhorted and comforted
1Thessalonians2:12 that ye would walk worthy of the Lord
1Thessalonians2:13 For this cause thank we God without ceasing
1Thessalonians2:14 For ye brethren did counterfeit(became followers of) the congregations of God which in Jewry are in Christ Jesu: for ye have suffered like things of your kinsmen
1Thessalonians2:15 which as they killed the Lord Jesus and their own prophets
1Thessalonians2:16 and forbid us to preach(speak) unto the gentiles
1Thessalonians2:17 Forasmuch brethren as we are kept from you for a season
1Thessalonians2:18 and therefore we would have come unto you
1Thessalonians2:19 For what is our hope or joy
1Thessalonians2:20 Yea ye are our glory(praise) and joy.

1Thessalonians5:5 Ye are all the children of light
1Thessalonians5:6 Therefore let us not sleep as do other: but let us watch and be sober.
1Thessalonians5:7 For they that sleep
1Thessalonians5:8 But let us which are of the day be sober

2Thessalonians2:4 which is an adversary

2Thessalonians2:7 For already the mystery of iniquity worketh. Only he that holdeth

2Thessalonians2:9 even him whose coming is by(after) the working of Satan

2Thessalonians3:5 And the Lord guide your hearts unto the love of God
2Thessalonians3:6 We require you brethren in the name of our Lord Jesu Christ
2Thessalonians3:7 Ye yourselves know how ye ought to counterfeit(follow) us: For we behaved not ourselves inordinately among you.

1Timothy1:1 Paul an apostle of Jesus Christ
1Timothy1:2 Unto Timothy his(my) natural son in the faith. Grace mercy and peace from God our father
1Timothy1:3 As I besought thee to abide still in Ephesus when I departed into Macedonia
1Timothy1:4 neither give heed to fables and genealogies
1Timothy1:5 for the end(the chief sum) of the commandment is love that cometh of a pure heart and of a good conscience
1Timothy1:6 from the which things
1Timothy1:7 because they would(willing to) be doctors in(of) the scripture
1Timothy1:8 We know that the law is god
1Timothy1:9 understanding this
1Timothy1:10 and whoremongers: to them that defile themselves with mankind: to menstealers: to liars and to perjured
1Timothy1:11 according to the glorious gospel of the holy God
1Timothy1:12 And I thank him that hath made me strong in Christ Jesu our Lord:(And i thank Christ Iesus our Lorde which hath made me strong:) for he counted me true
1Timothy1:13 when before I was a blasphemer
1Timothy1:14 but(Neverthelater) the grace of our Lord was more abundant
1Timothy1:15 This is a true saying
1Timothy1:16 Notwithstanding unto me was mercy given
1Timothy1:17 So then unto God
1Timothy1:18 This commandment commit I unto thee son Timotheus
1Timothy1:19 having faith and good conscience
1Timothy1:20 Of whose number is Himeneus
1Timothy2:1 I exhort therefore that above all things prayers
1Timothy2:2 for kings
1Timothy2:3 For that is good and accepted in the sight of god our saviour
1Timothy2:4 which would(will) have all men saved
1Timothy2:5 For there is one God
1Timothy2:6 which gave himself a ransom for all men
1Timothy2:7 whereunto I am appointed(ordained) a preacher
1Timothy2:8 I woll(will) therefore that the men pray every where
1Timothy2:9 Likewise also the women that they array themselves in mannerly(comely) apparel with shamefastness
1Timothy2:10 but with such as becometh women that profess the worshipping of God thorow good works.(profess godliness thorow good works)
1Timothy2:11 Let the woman learn in silence with all subjection.
1Timothy2:12 I suffer not a woman to teach
1Timothy2:13 For Adam was first formed
1Timothy2:14 Also Adam was not deceived
1Timothy2:15 Notwithstanding they shall be saved thorow bearing of children
1Timothy3:1 This is a true saying: If a man covet the office of a bishop
1Timothy3:2 Yea and a bishop must be faultless
1Timothy3:3 not drunken
1Timothy3:4 and one that ruleth his own house honestly
1Timothy3:5 For if a man cannot rule his own house
1Timothy3:6 He may not be a young man
1Timothy3:7 He must also be well reported of among(have a good report of) them which are without forth
1Timothy3:8 Likewise must the deacons(ministers) be honest
1Timothy3:9 but having the mystery of the faith in pure conscience.
1Timothy3:10 And let them first be proved
1Timothy3:11 Even so must their wives be honest
1Timothy3:12 Let the deacons(ministers) be the husbands of one wife(every one the husband of one wife) and such as rule their children well
1Timothy3:13 For they that minister well
1Timothy3:14 These things write I unto thee
1Timothy3:15 And if I come not
1Timothy3:16 And without nay great is that mystery of godliness. God was shewed in the flesh
1Timothy4:1 The spirit speaketh evidently that in the latter times some shall depart from the faith
1Timothy4:2 and devilish doctrine of them which speak false thorow hypocrisy
1Timothy4:3 forbidding to marry
1Timothy4:4 for all the creatures of God are good: and nothing to be refused
1Timothy4:5 For it is sanctified by the word of God
1Timothy4:6 If thou shalt put the brethren in remembrance of these things
1Timothy4:7 But cast away unghostly and old wives' fables. Exercise thyself unto godliness.
1Timothy4:8 For bodily exercise profiteth little: But godliness is good(profitable) unto all things
1Timothy4:9 This is a sure saying
1Timothy4:10 For therefore we labour and suffer rebuke
1Timothy4:11 Such things command and teach.
1Timothy4:12 Let no man despise thy youth: but be unto them that believe
1Timothy4:13 Till I come give attendance to reading
1Timothy4:14 Despise not(Be not negligent) the gift that is in thee
1Timothy4:15 These things exercise
1Timothy4:16 Take heed unto thyself and unto learning
1Timothy5:1 Rebuke not a senior:(an elder:) but exhort him as a father
1Timothy5:2 the elder women as mothers
1Timothy5:3 Honour widows which are true widows.
1Timothy5:4 If any widow have children or nephews
1Timothy5:5 She that is a very widow
1Timothy5:6 but she that liveth in pleasure
1Timothy5:7 And such(these) things command
1Timothy5:8 If there be any(any man) that provideth not for his own
1Timothy5:9 Let no widow be chosen under threescore(sixty) year old
1Timothy5:10 and well reported of in good works: if she have nourished children
1Timothy5:11 The younger widows refuse. For when they have begun to wax wanton
1Timothy5:12 having damnation
1Timothy5:13 And also they learn to go(run about) from house to house idle
1Timothy5:14 I will therefore that the younger women marry and bear children
1Timothy5:15 For many of them are all ready turned back
1Timothy5:16 And if any man or woman that believeth have widows
1Timothy5:17 The seniors(elders) that rule well are worthy of double honour
1Timothy5:18 For the scripture saith: Thou shalt not muzzle(musell) the mouth of the ox that treadeth out the corn. And the laborer is worthy of his reward.
1Timothy5:19 Against an senior(elder) receive none accusation: but under two or three witnesses.
1Timothy5:20 Them that sin rebuke openly(in the presence of all) that other (also) may fear.
1Timothy5:21 I testify before God
1Timothy5:22 Lay hands suddenly on no man neither be partaker of other men's sins: Keep thyself pure.
1Timothy5:23 Drink no longer water
1Timothy5:24 Some men's sins are open beforehand and go before unto judgement: some men's sins follow after.(but some men's sins shall be manifest hereafter)
1Timothy5:25 Likewise also good works are manifest beforehand

1Timothy6:2 See that they which have believing masters despise them not because they are brethren: but so much the rather do service
1Timothy6:3 If any man teach otherwise
1Timothy6:4 he is puffed(puft) up and knoweth nothing: but wasteth his brains about questions
1Timothy6:5 superfluous disputings in scowls(and vain disputations) of men with corrupt minds
1Timothy6:6 Godliness is great riches
1Timothy6:7 For we brought nothing into the world
1Timothy6:8 When we have food and raiment
1Timothy6:9 They that will be rich
1Timothy6:10 For covetousness is the root(rote) of all evil
1Timothy6:11 But thou which art the man of God

2Timothy1:17 but when he was at Rome he sought me out very diligently

Philemon1:2 and to the beloved Appia

Philemon1:5 when(for so much as) I hear of thy love and faith
Philemon1:6 so that the fellowship that thou hast in the faith is fruitful thorow knowledge of all good things
Philemon1:7 And we have great joy and consolation over thy love: For by thee brother
Philemon1:8 Wherefore though I be bold(I have great boldness) in Christ to enjoin(command) thee
Philemon1:9 yet for love's sake I rather beseech thee
Philemon1:10 I beseech(beseek) thee for my son Onesimus
Philemon1:11 which in time passed was to thee unprofitable: but now profitable both to thee and also to me
Philemon1:12 whom I have sent home again. Thou therefore receive him
Philemon1:13 whom I would fain have retained(For I would have kept him still) with me
Philemon1:14 Nevertheless
Philemon1:15 Haply he therefore departed for a season
Philemon1:16 not now as a servant: but above a servant
Philemon1:17 If thou count me a fellow
Philemon1:18 If he have hurt thee or oweth thee ought
Philemon1:19 I Paul have written it with mine own hand. I will recompense it. So that I do not say to thee how that thou owest unto me even thine own self.
Philemon1:20 Even so brother
Philemon1:21 Trusting in thine obedience
Philemon1:22 Moreover prepare me lodging: for I trust(hope that) thorow the help of your prayers
Philemon1:23 There salute thee

Philemon1:25 The grace of our Lord Jesu Christ be with your spirits
Hebrews1:1 God in time past diversely and many ways

Hebrews1:4 and is more excellent than the angels
Hebrews1:5 For unto which of the angels said he at any time: Thou art my son
Hebrews1:6 And again when he bringeth in the first begotten son in (to) the world
Hebrews1:7 And unto(of) the angels he saith: He maketh his angels spirits
Hebrews1:8 But unto the son he saith: God thy seat shall be(thy seat endureth) for ever
Hebrews1:9 Thou hast loved righteousness and hated iniquity: Wherefore hath God
Hebrews1:10 And thou Lord in the beginning hast laid the foundation of the earth: And the heavens are the works of thy hands.
Hebrews1:11 They shall perish
Hebrews1:12 and as a vesture shalt thou change them
Hebrews1:13 Unto which of the angels said he at any time? Sit on my right hand till I make thine enemies thy footstool.
Hebrews1:14 Are they not all spirits to do service
Hebrews2:1 Wherefore we ought much more to attend unto those(to give the more heed to the) things which we have heard
Hebrews2:2 For if the word which was spoken by angels was steadfast: and(so that) every transgression and disobedience received a just recompense to reward:
Hebrews2:3 how shall we escape if we despise so great health?(salvation) which at the first began to be preached of(by) the Lord himself
Hebrews2:4 God bearing witness thereto
Hebrews2:5 He hath not unto the angels put in subjection the world to come
Hebrews2:6 but one in a certain place witnessed
Hebrews2:7 After thou hadst for a season made him lower than the angels: thou crownedst him with honour and glory
Hebrews2:8 Thou hast put all things in subjection under his feet. In that he put all things under him
Hebrews2:9 Nevertheless we yet see not all things subdued unto him: but that Jesus which for a season was made less than the angels
Hebrews2:10 For it became him
Hebrews2:11 Forasmuch as he which(that) sanctifieth
Hebrews2:12 saying: I will declare thy name unto my brethren
Hebrews2:13 And again: I will put my trust in him. And again: behold here am I and the(my) children which God hath given me.
Hebrews2:14 Forasmuch then as the children were partakers of flesh and blood
Hebrews2:15 And that he might deliver them which thorow fear of death all their life time were in danger of bondage.
Hebrews2:16 For he in no place taketh on him the angels: but the seed of Abraham taketh he on him.
Hebrews2:17 Wherefore in all things it became him to be made like unto his brethren
Hebrews2:18 For in that he himself suffered

Hebrews7:6 But he whose kindred is not counted among them
Hebrews7:7 And no man denieth but that which is less

Hebrews7:10 For he was yet in the loins of his father Abraham

Hebrews7:12 Now no doubt
Hebrews7:13 For he of whom these things are spoken
Hebrews7:14 For it is evident that our Lord sprung of the tribe of Juda
Hebrews7:15 And it is yet a more evident thing
Hebrews7:16 which is not made after the law of the carnal commandment: but after the power of the endless life.
Hebrews7:17 For he testifieth: Thou art a priest for ever
Hebrews7:18 Then the commandment that went afore
Hebrews7:19 For the law made no thing perfect: but was an introduction of a better hope
Hebrews7:20 And for this cause it is a better hope

Hebrews7:24 But this man
Hebrews7:25 Wherefore he is able also ever to save them that come unto God by him
Hebrews7:26 Such an high priest it becometh(became) us to have
Hebrews7:27 Which needeth not daily (as yonder high priests) to offer up sacrifice
Hebrews7:28 For the law maketh men priests

Hebrews8:2 and is a minister of wholy(holy) things
Hebrews8:3 For every high(hye) priest is ordained to offer gifts and sacrifices: wherefore it is of necessity
Hebrews8:4 For he were not a priest

Hebrews8:6 Now hath he obtained a more excellent office
Hebrews8:7 For if that first testament had been such a one that no man could have found fault with it:(faultless:) then should no place have been sought for the second.
Hebrews8:8 For in rebuking them he saith: Behold the days will come (saith the Lord) and I will finish upon the house of Israel

Hebrews8:10 For this is the testament that I will make with the house of Israel: After those days (saith the Lord:) I will put(give) my laws in their minds
Hebrews8:11 And they shall not teach
Hebrews8:12 For I will be merciful over their iniquities: and on their sins and on their unrighteousness

Hebrews10:2 For would not then those sacrifices have ceased to have been offered? because that the offerers once purged
Hebrews10:3 Nevertheless in those sacrifices is there mention(a rememberance) made of sins every year.
Hebrews10:4 For it is unpossible that the blood of oxen
Hebrews10:5 Wherefore when he cometh into the world

Hebrews10:8 Above when he sayeth(had said) sacrifice
Hebrews10:9 (And) then he said: Lo I am ready(come to) do thy will o God: he taketh away the first to stablish the latter.
Hebrews10:10 By the which will we are sanctified
Hebrews10:11 And every priest is ready daily ministering
Hebrews10:12 but this man after he had offered one sacrifice for sins
Hebrews10:13 and from henceforth tarrieth till his foes be made his footstool.
Hebrews10:14 For with one offering hath he made perfect for ever them that are sanctified.
Hebrews10:15 And the holy ghost also beareth us record of this
Hebrews10:16 This is the testament that I will make unto them after those days saith the Lord. And I will put my laws in their hearts
Hebrews10:17 and their sins and iniquities will I remember no more.
Hebrews10:18 And where remission of these things is
Hebrews10:19 Seeing brethren that by the means of the blood of Jesu
Hebrews10:20 by the new and living way
Hebrews10:21 And seeing also that we have an high(hye) priest which is ruler over the house of God
Hebrews10:22 let us draw nigh with a true heart in a full faith sprinkled in our hearts
Hebrews10:23 and let us keep the profession of our hope
Hebrews10:24 and let us consider one another to provoke unto love
Hebrews10:25 and let us not forsake the fellowship that we have among ourselves
Hebrews10:26 For if we sin willingly(willfully) after that we have received the knowledge of the truth
Hebrews10:27 but a fearful looking for judgment

Hebrews10:29 Of how much sorer punishment suppose ye shall he be counted worthy
Hebrews10:30 For we know him that hath said
Hebrews10:31 It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.

Hebrews10:33 partly while all men wondered and gazed at you for the shame and tribulation that was done unto you
Hebrews10:34 For ye suffered also with my bonds
Hebrews10:35 Cast not away therefore your confidence
Hebrews10:36 For ye have need of patience

Hebrews11:1 Faith is a sure confidence of things which are hoped for
Hebrews11:2 By it the elders were well reported of.
Hebrews11:3 Thorow faith we understand that the world was ordained
Hebrews11:4 By faith Abel offered unto God a more plenteous sacrifice than Cain: by which
Hebrews11:5 By faith was Enoch translated(taken away) that he should not see death: neither was he found: for God had taken him away. Before he was taken away
Hebrews11:6 but without faith it is unpossible to please him. For he that cometh to God
Hebrews11:7 By faith Noah honoured God
Hebrews11:8 By faith Abraham
Hebrews11:9 By faith he removed(was he a stranger) into the land that was promised him
Hebrews11:10 For he looked for a city having a foundation
Hebrews11:11 Thorow(By) faith Sara also received strength to be with child
Hebrews11:12 And therefore sprang there of(thereof) one (and of one which was as good as dead) so many in multitude
Hebrews11:13 And they all died in(these died according to) faith
Hebrews11:14 They that say such things
Hebrews11:15 Also if they had been mindful of that country
Hebrews11:16 But now they desire a better

Hebrews11:18 Of whom it was said
Hebrews11:19 for he considered
Hebrews11:20 In faith Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau
Hebrews11:21 By faith Jacob when he was a dying
Hebrews11:22 By faith Joseph when he died
Hebrews11:23 By faith Moses when he was born
Hebrews11:24 By faith Moses when he was of a great age
Hebrews11:25 and chose rather to suffer adversity with the people of God
Hebrews11:26 and esteemed the rebuke of Christ greater riches
Hebrews11:27 By faith he forsook Egypt
Hebrews11:28 Thorow faith he ordained the ester lamb
Hebrews11:29 By faith they passed thorow the reed sea as by dry land
Hebrews11:30 By faith the walls of Jericho fell down after they were compassed about
Hebrews11:31 By faith the harlot Rahab perished not with them that believed not
Hebrews11:32 And what shall I more say
Hebrews11:33 which thorow faith subdued kingdoms
Hebrews11:34 quenched the violence of fire
Hebrews11:35 (And) The women received their dead raised to life again. Other(others) were racked
Hebrews11:36 Other(others) tasted of mockings
Hebrews11:37 were stoned
Hebrews11:38 which the world was not worthy of: They wandered in wilderness
Hebrews11:39 And these all thorow faith obtained good report
Hebrews11:40 God providing a better thing for us
Hebrews12:1 Wherefore let us also (seeing that we are compassed with so great a multitude of witnesses) lay away all that presseth down
Hebrews12:2 looking unto Jesus
Hebrews12:3 Consider therefore how that he endured such speaking against him of sinners
Hebrews12:4 For ye have not yet resisted unto blood shedding
Hebrews12:5 And ye have forgotten the consolation which speaketh unto you
Hebrews12:6 For whom the Lord loveth
Hebrews12:7 If ye shall endure chastening
Hebrews12:8 If ye be not under correction (where of all are partakers) then are ye bastards and not sons.

Hebrews12:10 And they verily for a few days
Hebrews12:11 No manner learning for the present time(No manner chastising for the present tyme) seemeth to be joyous
Hebrews12:12 Stretch forth therefore again(Life up therefore) the hands which were let down
Hebrews12:13 and see that ye have straight steps unto your feet
Hebrews12:14 Embrace(Follow after) peace with all men
Hebrews12:15 And see(look to) that no man be destitute of the grace of God
Hebrews12:16 (and) That there be no fornicator
Hebrews12:17 Ye know how that afterward when that he would have inherited the blessing
Hebrews12:18 For ye are not come unto the mount that is(can be) touched
Hebrews12:19 neither unto the sound of a trumpet and the voice of words: which voice they that heard it
Hebrews12:20 For they were not able to abide that which was spoken. If a beast had touched the mountain
Hebrews12:21 even so terrible was the sight which appeared. Moses said I fear and quake.
Hebrews12:22 But ye are come unto the mount Sion
Hebrews12:23 and unto the congregation of the first born sons
Hebrews12:24 and to Jesus the mediator of the new testament
Hebrews12:25 See that ye despise not him that speaketh.(speaketh unto you) For if they escaped not which refused him that spake on earth: Much more shall we not escape
Hebrews12:26 whose voice then shook the earth
Hebrews12:27 No doubt the same that he sayeth
Hebrews12:28 Wherefore if we receive a kingdom which is not moved

Hebrews13:6 that we may boldly say: The Lord is my helper

Hebrews13:8 Jesus Christ yesterday and today

Hebrews13:12 Therefore Jesus

James1:2 My brethren

James1:4 and let patience have her perfect work
James1:5 If any that is among(of) you lack wisdom
James1:6 but let him ask in faith
James1:7 Neither let that man think that he shall receive any thing of God.(the Lord.)
James1:8 A wavering minded man is unstable in all his ways.
James1:9 Let the brother of low degree rejoice in that he is exalted
James1:10 and the rich in that he is made low. For even as the flower of the grass shall he vanish away.
James1:11 The son riseth with heat
James1:12 Happy is the man that endureth in temptation
James1:13 Let no man say when he is tempted that he is tempted of God: for God tempteth not unto evil: he tempteth no man:(neither tempteth he any man.)
James1:14 But every man is tempted drawn away
James1:15 Then when lust hath conceived
James1:16 Err not my dear brethren.
James1:17 Every good gift

James2:1 Brethren have not the faith of our Lord Jesus Christ the Lord of glory in respect of persons.
James2:2 If there come into your company a man with a golden ring
James2:3 and ye have a respect to him that weareth the gay clothing and say unto him: Sit thou here in a good place: and say unto the poor
James2:4 are ye not even partial in your selves
James2:5 Hearken my dear beloved brethren
James2:6 But ye have despised the poor. Are not the rich they which oppress you: and they which draw you before judges?
James2:7 Do not they speak evil of that good name that is called on over you?(of that good name after which ye be named.)
James2:8 If ye fulfil the royal law according to the scripture which saith: Thou shalt love thine neighbour as thyself
James2:9 but if ye regard one person more than another
James2:10 Whosoever shall keep the whole law
James2:11 For he that said: Thou shalt not commit fornication
James2:12 So speak ye
James2:13 For there shall be judgement merciless to him that sheweth no mercy
James2:14 What availeth it my brethren
James2:15 If a brother or a sister be naked or destitute of daily food
James2:16 and one of you say unto them: Depart in peace
James2:17 Even so faith
James2:18 But one shall(Yea and a man might) say: Thou hast faith
James2:19 Believest thou that there is one God? Thou doest well. The devils also believe and tremble.
James2:20 Wilt thou understand o thou vain man
James2:21 Was not Abraham our father justified of his deeds(thorow works) when he offered Isaac his son upon the altar?
James2:22 Thou seest how that faith wrought with in his deeds
James2:23 And the scripture was fulfilled which saith: Abraham believed God
James2:24 Ye see then how that of deeds a man is justified

James3:1 My brethren
James3:2 For in many things we sin all. If a man sin not in word
James3:3 Behold we put bits into the horses' mouths that they should obey us
James3:4 Behold also the ships
James3:5 even so the tongue is little member and boasteth great things. Behold how great a thing a little fire kindleth
James3:6 and the tongue is fire
James3:7 All the natures of beasts
James3:8 But the tongue can no man tame. It is an unruly evil full of deadly poison.
James3:9 Therewith bless we God the father
James3:10 Out of one mouth(mought) proceedeth blessing and cursing. My brethren these things ought not so to be.

James3:12 Can the fig tree

1John1:1 That which was from the beginning declare we unto you
1John1:2 For the life appeared
1John1:3 That which we have seen and heard declare we unto you that ye may have fellowship with us
1John1:4 And this write we(these things we write) unto you
1John1:5 And this is the tidings which we have heard of him
1John1:6 If we say that we have fellowship with him
1John1:7 but and if we walk in light even as he is in light
1John1:8 If we say that we have no sin
1John1:9 If we knowledge our sins
1John1:10 If we say we have not sinned
1John2:1 My little children
1John2:2 and he it is that obtaineth grace(the satisfaction) for our sins: (and) not for our sins only: but also for the sins of all the world.
1John2:3 And hereby we know that we have known him
1John2:4 He that saith I know him
1John2:5 Whosoever(He that) keepeth his word
1John2:6 He that saith he bideth in him
1John2:7 Brethren I write no new commandment unto you: but that(an) old commandment which ye heard from(had at) the beginning. The(For an) old commandment is the word which ye heard from the beginning.
1John2:8 Again
1John2:9 He that saith how that he is in the light
1John2:10 He that loveth his brother
1John2:11 He that hateth his brother is in darkness
1John2:12 Babes(little children) I write unto you how that your sins are forgiven you for his name's sake.
1John2:13 I write unto you fathers
1John2:14 I write unto you fathers
1John2:15 See that ye love not the world
1John2:16 For all that is in the world (as the lust of the flesh
1John2:17 And the world vanisheth(passeth) away
1John2:18 Little children it is (now) the last time
1John2:19 They went out from(of) us but they were not of us. For if they had been of us
1John2:20 And(But) ye have an ointment of the holy ghost
1John2:21 I wrote not unto you
1John2:22 Who is a liar: but he that denieth that Jesus is Christ? he(The same) is the antichrist that denieth the father and the son.
1John2:23 Whosoever denieth the son
1John2:24 Let therefore abide in you that same which ye heard from(at) the beginning. If that which ye heard from(at) the beginning shall remain in you
1John2:25 And this is the promise that he hath promised us
1John2:26 This(These things) have I written unto you
1John2:27 And the anointing which ye have received of him dwelleth in you. And ye need not that any man teach you: but as that(the) anointing teacheth you all things
1John2:28 And now babes(little children) abide in him
1John2:29 If ye know that he is righteous
1John3:1 Behold what love the father hath shewed on us
1John3:2 Dearly beloved
1John3:3 And every man that hath(all that have) this hope in him
1John3:4 Whosoever committeth(All that commit) sin
1John3:5 And ye know that he appeared to take away our sins
1John3:6 As many as bide(All that abide) in him
1John3:7 Babes(Little children) let no man deceive(beguile) you; He that doth(worketh) righteousness
1John3:8 He that committeth sin is of the devil: for the devil sinneth since the beginning. For this purpose(But for this cause) appeared the son of God
1John3:9 Whosoever is born of God
1John3:10 In this(And hereby) are the children(sons) of God known
1John3:11 For this is the tidings
1John3:12 (and) not (be) as Cain which was of the wicked(devil) and slew his brother. And wherefore slew he him? because(For) his own works(deeds) were evil
1John3:13 Marvel not my brethren if(though) the world hate you.
1John3:14 We know that we are translated from death unto life
1John3:15 Whosoever(All that) hateth his brother
1John3:16 Hereby perceive we(are we assured of) love: (because) that he gave(left) his life for us: And (therefore) we ought also to give our lives for the(our) brethren.
1John3:17 Whosoever(He then that) hath this world's goods(the substance of the world) and seeth his brother in necessity
1John3:18 My babes
1John3:19 And(for) thereby we know that we are of the verity
1John3:20 For(But) (if our hearts condemn us) God is greater than our hearts
1John3:21 Tenderly(Dearly) beloved
1John3:22 and whatsoever we ask
1John3:23 And this is his commandment
1John3:24 And he that keepeth his commandments dwelleth(abideth) in him
1John4:1 Dearly(Ye) beloved believe not every spirit: but prove the spirits whether they are(be) of God
1John4:2 Hereby shall ye know the spirit of God. Every spirit that confesseth that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh
1John4:3 And every spirit which(that) confesseth not that Jesus Christ is come in the flesh
1John4:4 Little children
1John4:5 They are(be) of the world
1John4:6 We are(be) of God. (and) He that knoweth God heareth(hearkeneth unto) us: (and) He that is not of God
1John4:7 Dearly beloved
1John4:8 (And) He that loveth not
1John4:9 In this(Herein) appeared the love of God to us ward
1John4:10 Herein is love
1John4:11 Dearly beloved if God so loved us
1John4:12 No man hath seen God at any time. If we love one another
1John4:13 Hereby know we
1John4:14 And we have seen and do testify that the father (hath) sent the (his) son
1John4:15 Whosoever confesseth that Jesus is the son of God
1John4:16 And we have known and believed the love that God hath to us. God is love
1John4:17 Herein(Herefor) is the love perfect in(with) us
1John4:18 There is no fear in love
1John4:19 We love him
1John4:20 If a man say
1John4:21 And this commandment have we of him: that he which loveth God
1John5:1 Whosoever believeth(All that believe) that Jesus is Christ
1John5:2 In this we know that we love the children(sons) of God
1John5:3 This is the love of God
1John5:4 For all that is born of God
1John5:5 Who is it that overcometh the world: but he which believeth that Jesus is the son of God?
1John5:6 This Jesus Christ is he that came by water and blood
1John5:7 (For there are three which(that) bear record(witness) in heaven
1John5:8 And(For) there are three which(that) bear record (in earth:) the spirit
1John5:9 If we receive the witness of men
1John5:10 He that believeth on(in) the son of God hath the witness in himself. He that believeth not God
1John5:11 And this is that record
1John5:12 He that hath the son
1John5:13 These things have I written unto you that believe on(in) the name of the son of God
1John5:14 And this is the trust(confidence)(free boldness) that we have in(toward) him: that if we ask any thing(ought) according to his will he heareth us.
1John5:15 And if we know that he hear us whatsoever we ask
1John5:16 If any man see his brother sin a sin that is not unto(to) death
1John5:17 All unrighteousness is sin
1John5:18 We know that whosoever is(all that are) born of God
1John5:19 We know that we are(be) of God
1John5:20 We know that the son of God is come
1John5:21 Babes keep yourselves from images.(Little children beware of images) Amen. [Here ends the first epistle of saint John]
2John1:1 The senior(elder) to the elect lady and her children which I love in the truth: And not I only
2John1:2 for the truth's sake
2John1:3 With you be grace
2John1:4 I rejoiced greatly
2John1:5 And now beseech(beseek) I thee lady
2John1:6 And this is the love
2John1:7 For many deceivers are entered into(are come in to) the world
2John1:8 Look on yourselves
2John1:9 Whosoever transgresseth and bideth not(abideth not) in the doctrine of Christ
2John1:10 If there come any unto you and bring not this learning
2John1:11 For he that biddeth him God speed
2John1:12 I had many things to write unto you
2John1:13 The sons(children) of thy elect sister greet(salute) thee Amen. [Here ends the second epistle of saint John]
3John1:1 The senior(elder) unto the beloved Gaius
3John1:2 Wellbeloved I wish in all things that thou prosperedest and faredest well
3John1:3 I rejoiced greatly when the brethren came and testified of the truth(trueth) that is in thee
3John1:4 I have no greater joy than for to hear how that my sons walk in verity.(trueth)
3John1:5 Dearly beloved thou dost faithfully whatsoever thou doest to the brethren
3John1:6 which bare witness of thy love before all the congregation. Which brethren when thou bringest forwards on their journey (as it beseemeth God) thou shalt do well:
3John1:7 because that for his name's sake they went forth
3John1:8 We therefore ought to receive such
3John1:9 I wrote unto the congregation: but Diotrephes which loveth to have the preeminence among them
3John1:10 wherefore if I come I will declare his deeds which he doeth jesting on us with malicious words
3John1:11 Dearly beloved counterfeit(follow) not that which is evil
3John1:12 Demetrius hath good report of all men
3John1:13 I have many things to write: But I will not with pen and ink write unto thee.
3John1:14 For I trust I (shortly see thee

Jude1:11 Woe be unto them
Jude1:12 These are spots which of your kindness feast together
Jude1:13 They are raging waves of the sea
Jude1:14 Enoch the seventh from Adam prophesied before of such saying: Behold
Jude1:15 to give judgement against all men
Jude1:16 These are murmurers

Jude1:23 and other save with fear

Jude1:25 that is to say

Revelation2:2 I know thy works
Revelation2:3 And hast suffered
Revelation2:4 Nevertheless I have somewhat against thee
Revelation2:5 Remember therefore from whence thou art fallen
Revelation2:6 But this thou hast because thou hatest the deeds of the Nicolaitans
Revelation2:7 Let him that hath ears hear
Revelation2:8 And unto the angel of the congregation of Smyrna write: These things saith he that is first
Revelation2:9 I know thy works and tribulation and poverty
Revelation2:10 Fear none of those things which thou shalt suffer. Behold
Revelation2:11 Let him that hath ears hear
Revelation2:12 And to the angel(messenger) of the congregation in Pergamos write: This saith he which hath the sharp sword with two edges.
Revelation2:13 I know thy works and where thou dwellest
Revelation2:14 But I have a few things against thee: that thou hast there
Revelation2:15 Even so hast thou them that maintain the doctrine of the Nicolaitans
Revelation2:16 But repent(be converted) or else I will come to(unto) thee shortly and will fight against them with the sword of my mouth.(mought)
Revelation2:17 Let him that hath ears hear what the spirit sayth unto the congregations: To him that overcometh will I give to eat Manna that is hid
Revelation2:18 And unto the angel(messenger) of the congregation of Thiatira write: This sayth the son of God
Revelation2:19 I know thy works and thy love
Revelation2:20 Notwithstanding(Not witstondinge) I have a few things against thee
Revelation2:21 And I gave her space to repent of her fornication and she repented not.
Revelation2:22 Behold I will cast her into a bed
Revelation2:23 And I will kill her children with death. And all the congregations shall know that I am he which searches the reins and hearts. And I will give unto everyone of you according to your works.
Revelation2:24 Unto you I say
Revelation2:25 but that which you have already. Hold fast till I come
Revelation2:26 and whosoever overcometh and keepeth my works unto the end
Revelation2:27 and he shall rule them with a rod of iron: and as the vessels of a potter
Revelation2:28 And I will(so will I) give him the morning star.
Revelation2:29 Let him that hath ears hear what the spirit says to the congregations.

Revelation9:2 And he opened the bottomless pit

Revelation9:5 and to them was commanded that they should not kill them
Revelation9:6 And in those days shall men seek death
Revelation9:7 And the similitude of the locusts was like unto horses prepared unto battle
Revelation9:8 And they had hairs as the hairs of women. And their teeth were as the teeth of lions.

Revelation9:10 And they had tails like unto scorpions

Revelation16:6 for they shed out the blood of saints

Revelation18:1 And after that I saw another angel come from heaven
Revelation18:2 And he cried mightily with a strong voice saying: Great Babylon is fallen is fallen
Revelation18:3 for all nations have drunken of the wine of the wrath of her fornication.(whoredom) And the kings of the earth have committed fornication with her
Revelation18:4 And I heard another voice from heaven say: come away from her my people
Revelation18:5 For her sins are gone up to heaven
Revelation18:6 Reward her even as she rewarded you
Revelation18:7 And as much as she glorified herself and lived wantonly
Revelation18:8 Therefore shall her plagues come at one day
Revelation18:9 And the kings of the earth shall beweep her and wail over her
Revelation18:10 and shall stand afar off
Revelation18:11 And the merchants of the earth shall weep and wail in themselves
Revelation18:12 the ware of gold and silver
Revelation18:13 and cinnamon
Revelation18:14 And the apples that thy soul lusted after
Revelation18:15 The merchants of these things which were waxed rich (by her) shall stand afar off from her
Revelation18:16 and saying: Alas alas
Revelation18:17 for at one hour so great riches is come to nought. And every ship governor
Revelation18:18 and cried
Revelation18:19 And they cast dust on their heads
Revelation18:20 Rejoice over her thou heaven
Revelation18:21 And a mighty angel took up a stone like a great millstone
Revelation18:22 And the voice of harpers
Revelation18:23 and the voice of the bridegroom and of the bride
Revelation18:24 and in her was found the blood of the prophets

Revelation21:1 And I saw a new heaven
Revelation21:2 And I John saw that holy city new Jerusalem come down from God out of heaven prepared as a bride garnished for her husband.
Revelation21:3 And I heard a great voice from the throne
Revelation21:4 And God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes. And there shall be no more death
Revelation21:5 And he that sat upon the seat
Revelation21:6 And he said unto me: it is done I am Alpha and Omega
Revelation21:7 He that overcometh shall inherit all things
Revelation21:8 But the fearful and unbelieving
Revelation21:9 And there came unto me one of the seven angels which had the seven vials full of the seven last plagues: and talked with me saying: come hither I will shew thee the bride
Revelation21:10 And he carried me away in the spirit to a great and an high mountain
Revelation21:11 having the brightness of God. And her shining was like unto a stone most precious
Revelation21:12 and had walls great and high
Revelation21:13 on the east part three gates
Revelation21:14 and the wall of the city had twelve foundations
Revelation21:15 And he that talked with me
Revelation21:16 And the city was built four square
Revelation21:17 And he measured the wall thereof an hundred (and) forty four cubits: the measure that the angel had was after the measure that man useth.
Revelation21:18 And the building of the wall of it was of jasper. And the city was pure gold like unto clear glass
Revelation21:19 and the foundations of the wall of the city was garnished with all manner of precious stones. The first foundation was jasper
Revelation21:20 the fifth sardonyx: the sixth sardeos: the seventh chrysolite: the eighth berall: the ninth a topas: the tenth a chrysoprasus: the eleventh a hyacinth:(jacinth) the twelfth an amethyst.
Revelation21:21 The twelve gates were twelve pearls
Revelation21:22 And there was no temple therein. For the Lord God almighty and the lamb are the temple of it.
Revelation21:23 And the city hath no need of the sun(son) neither of the moon to lighten it. For the brightness of God did light it: and the lamb was the light of it.
Revelation21:24 And the people which are saved shall walk in the light of it: and the kings of the earth shall bring their glory (and honour) unto it.
Revelation21:25 And the gates of it are not shut by day. For there shall be no night there.
Revelation21:26 And in to it shall they bring the glory and honour of the nations
Revelation21:27 And there shall enter into it none unclean thing: neither whatsoever worketh abomination: or maketh lies: but they only which are written in the Lamb's book of life.

Revelation22:2 In the midst(myddes) of the street of it
Revelation22:3 And there shall be no more curse
Revelation22:4 And they shall see his face
Revelation22:5 And there shall be no more night there and they need no candle
Revelation22:6 And he said unto me: these sayings are faithful
Revelation22:7 Behold I come shortly. Happy is he that keepeth the saying of the prophecy of this book.
Revelation22:8 I am John
Revelation22:9 And he said unto me: see thou do it not
Revelation22:10 And he said unto me: seal not the sayings of prophesy of this book. For the time is at hand.(tyme is at honde)
Revelation22:11 He that doth evil
Revelation22:12 And behold I come shortly
Revelation22:13 I am Alpha and Omega
Revelation22:14 Blessed are they that do his commandments
Revelation22:15 For with out shall be dogs and enchanters
Revelation22:16 I Jesus sent mine angel
Revelation22:17 And the spirit and the bride said come. And let him that heareth
Revelation22:18 I testify unto every man that heareth the words of prophecy of this book: if any man shall add unto these things
Revelation22:19 And if any man shall minish of the words of the book of this prophecy
Revelation22:20 He which testifieth these things saith: be it
